title: kittwm daemon MOVE_PANE queue arg: build directly
description: The native daemon MOVE_PANE parser still uses format! to build the internal tab-separated queue argument. Build that queue arg directly while preserving MOVE_PANE validation and queued command behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm markdown HTML placeholders: build directly
description: Replace markdown HTML placeholder `format!` calls in `crates/kittui-affordances/src/markdown.rs` with direct preallocated helpers. Scope only inline/table/block HTML placeholder text construction; preserve HTML rendering tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: fix kittui probe live terminal rows
description:
Fix kittui probe terminal dimension detection when run from a real terminal. A user reported `kittui probe` returning rows=24 despite the invoking terminal having about 70 rows, which will break placement/layout decisions.

Acceptance:
- `kittui probe` should prefer live TTY dimensions over fixed/default 80x24 values when stdout/stdin/stderr is attached to a terminal.
- Preserve existing config/env override behavior and non-TTY fallback behavior.
- Add focused tests or validation for controlled terminal sizes if feasible.
- Run focused validation and report the exact behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui affordances: animate table box glyph scenes
description: Continue animation coverage for remaining kittui-affordances visual scene helpers. Inventory: `box_glyph_scene` renders one-cell box drawing glyphs for markdown/table overlays but has no animation variant. Acceptance: add a small `BoxGlyphAnimation` option/helper with the same 60fps/180-frame/3s defaults, emit a loop-closing Scene.animation plus labelled phase-reactive layer, and add unit coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm daemon STATUS_JSON reply: build directly
description: Standalone daemon STATUS_JSON replies still use format! only to append a newline to the daemon status JSON value. Reuse the direct JSON-line helper while preserving status JSON coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: graphical notification/toast surface for actions and errors
description: Dogfood graphics gap: action results/errors (spawn failures, restore errors, copied/pasted, layout changed) should not only appear in logs/footer text. Add kittui-rendered transient toasts/notifications layered over the WM. Acceptance: common action success/error paths can display graphical toast messages; toasts expire, stack safely, and have text fallback/log parity.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: hide raw app graphics while picker overlay is active
description: Operator loop: keep finding/fixing kittwm flicker/usability issues. Review found raw compositor app graphics are suppressed while the launcher overlay is active, but not while the picker overlay is active. Because picker rendering is memoized, subsequent raw app frame writes can overwrite or visually fight the picker overlay, causing flicker/stale overlay artifacts. Scope: treat launcher and picker as text overlays for raw app graphics suppression/unplace, update helper naming/tests, and preserve existing overlay cleanup behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: forward Ctrl-Insert and Ctrl-Delete input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards plain/Shift Insert/Delete and other Ctrl-modified navigation keys, but consumes Ctrl+Insert/Ctrl+Delete CSI (`ESC [ 2 ; 5 ~` / `ESC [ 3 ; 5 ~`). These are common copy/delete-word editing shortcuts in terminal/browser workflows. Forward them with the Ctrl modifier.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add SDK event helpers for pane resize bounds
description: SDK follow-up: pane_resized events report old/new pane geometry, but EventEnvelope consumers still index raw JSON to read bounds. Add pane_bounds(key) and pane_app_bounds(key) helpers that support the nested bounds/app_bounds shape and the older flat fixture shape. Filed from ongoing kittwm event/geometry automation audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Show Linux desktop metadata in remote app lists
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm Linux desktop fallback search now matches GenericName, Keywords, Categories, Comment, and localized metadata, but the human list still shows only visible label and desktop ID. Include a concise generic-name/category hint in `kittwm remote HOST apps QUERY` output so users can understand why launcher-like matches appeared.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm graphical overlay panel: build heading label directly
description: Replace `format!("{id}-heading")` in test-only `graphical_overlay_panel_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated heading-label helper. Scope only graphical overlay panel heading label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md tables-json inspection mode
description: Add --tables-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for parsed Markdown tables, emitting schema_version plus indexed table rows, alignments, column widths, and footprint records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add bracketed-paste aware native paste
description:
Large terminal automation gap: kittwm has exact byte injection, but no native paste primitive that respects bracketed-paste mode. Modern shells/editors enable DEC private `?2004` so pasted multi-line input should be wrapped in `ESC[200~` / `ESC[201~`; otherwise automation can accidentally execute partial lines or break editor semantics.

Scope:
- Track bracketed-paste mode (`CSI ? 2004 h/l`) in native PTY `TerminalState`.
- Publish optional `bracketed_paste` status metadata in native pane status surfaces.
- Add socket command `PASTE_BYTES_B64 <window|focused> <base64>` that wraps bytes in bracketed-paste markers when the target pane currently has bracketed paste enabled, otherwise sends raw bytes.
- Add CLI wrapper `kittwm --paste-file WINDOW PATH|-`.
- Update HELP/docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves bracketed-paste mode tracking.
- Daemon/session-level tests prove status metadata and queued paste command.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm stdin read log: build directly
description: The raw compositor stdin-read diagnostic still formats byte count and preview with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md metadata-blocks-json inspection mode
description: Add --metadata-blocks-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown YAML/pluses metadata blocks, emitting schema_version plus indexed kind/source records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md renders Markdown lists as bullet and numbered components
description: Add Markdown list awareness to the kittui-affordances renderer so unordered and ordered lists preserve bullet/number markers instead of flattening items into plain paragraphs. Include tests for bullet and ordered list output and keep link chips/paragraph rendering behavior intact.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm markdown footnote definitions: build directly
description: Replace `format!("footnote [^{label}]: {text}")` in `crates/kittui-affordances/src/markdown.rs` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only markdown footnote definition component text construction; preserve footnote definition rendering tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: make kittwm shortcuts kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make the kittwm shortcut catalog available as kittui/kitty-native output. Add shortcuts-scene-json and shortcuts-kitty/shortcuts-graphics commands that render the existing shared shortcut catalog as a labelled kittui Scene, preserving existing text/JSON commands.

Acceptance:
- kittwm shortcuts-scene-json emits a kittui Scene with shortcut labels from the shared catalog.
- kittwm shortcuts-kitty/shortcuts-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at overlay/chrome z-index.
- Existing shortcuts/shortcuts-json behavior remains unchanged.
- Focused tests cover scene labels.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: parse SS3 arrow and home/end input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser parses CSI arrow keys but not SS3/application-cursor sequences (`ESC O A/B/C/D`, `ESC O H/F`). In terminals that emit SS3, these keys become Escape plus leaked `O...` text in the page instead of navigation. Parse SS3 arrows/Home/End and dispatch the existing browser actions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add remote kittwm terminal launch alias
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: make the healthy remote-installed path discoverable from the local `kittwm remote HOST ...` front door. Add aliases such as `kittwm remote HOST kittwm` / `run` / `open` that open a local kittwm terminal pane running pooled SSH to execute remote `kittwm`, so users can intentionally enter remote desktop/window context without remembering raw `ssh HOST kittwm`.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-bar: standalone overlay should use configured theme colors
description: Live graphical top-bar overlay now derives colors from configured chrome colors, but standalone kittwm-bar --kitty overlay still hardcodes Nord chip/clock colors. Make helper overlay derive active/inactive/clock colors from KittwmConfig colorscheme/background with high-contrast foreground, preserving no-placeholder placement and overlap guard.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm commands scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: commands_scene still builds the local-command catalog backdrop label with format! from command count and category summary. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm quit confirm overlay: build cache key directly
description: Replace `format!("active={};opened={}", ...)` in `quit_confirm_overlay_key(...)` with direct preallocated String construction. Scope only quit-confirm overlay cache-key string construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing overlay key behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: docs: plan kitty response reading and capability probing
description:
Planning follow-up: kitty protocol conformance still lacks terminal response reading and `a=q` capability probing. Create a focused implementation plan that can be split into safe follow-up beads without destabilizing normal rendering.

Scope:
- Document design constraints for response reading: non-blocking/timeout behavior, no TTY theft from apps, tmux behavior, opt-in diagnostics, and integration with TerminalInfo::detect.
- Identify concrete small implementation beads for response reader and `a=q` probing if appropriate.
- Docs-only; do not implement response reading in this slice.

Acceptance:
- There is a clear plan for terminal response reading / capability query implementation.
- Follow-up beads are filed if actionable.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: add delete command for image and placement cleanup
description:
Large gap: shell workflows can now render and re-place kitty image ids, but there is no `kittui` CLI command to delete an image or a specific placement from the terminal. Scripts must know the low-level kitty delete escape grammar.

Scope:
- Add `kittui delete --id ID [--placement-id P]` to emit kitty delete escapes.
- Support decimal and `0x...` image id parsing (reuse existing parser).
- Honor `--json`/`--json-bytes` by reporting delete byte counts and optional delete string.
- Add CLI tests for image delete and placement delete.

Acceptance:
- `kittui delete --id 0x1234 --json --json-bytes` emits machine-readable delete bytes.
- `kittui delete --id 0x1234 --placement-id 7` uses placement delete form.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm v2: backend-agnostic compositor (local/remote/Mac/X11 windows as homogeneous kittui chrome)
description:
Generalise kittui-wm so any window — local X11, remote X11 over SSH, native macOS via Quartz capture, XQuartz-hosted X11, future Wayland, future Tendril — appears in the user's terminal as the same kittui chrome surface. The compositor multiplexes multiple XServer backends behind one operator UI, with globally-unique window ids, per-window WindowMode (Floating or Tiled), per-window chrome theme, and a single hit-test/route_pointer/route_key pipeline. v2 must let the operator mix Mac windows (Quartz CGWindowList capture) with XQuartz X11 windows in the same session, all rendered with locally-composed kittui chrome.

2026-05-22 burn-down note: implementation child beads for native PTY default, native browser, socket env/protocol, XQuartz wrapper, picker overlay, relative placement, animation grammar, docs, and markdown/UI initial stack landed and were closed. Remaining SSH latency proof is linked as dependent/future work rather than blocking this implementation burn-down.

2026-05-27 dev-2 decomposition note: this epic was permanent and was blocking concrete kittwm bug beads indefinitely. It has been made non-permanent and treated as a tracking epic only. Remaining work is split into concrete beads that should be claimable independently: layout mathematics (bd-d9391b), chrome/bar positioning (bd-d5cc64), large terminal crash (bd-be317a), launch flicker (bd-0c64f4), tiling overlap (bd-48c3d4), browser redundant rendering (bd-49970d), and SSH latency/bandwidth proof (bd-84c3f5). Do not add this epic as a dependency to implementation beads unless a future narrow sub-bead genuinely requires it; use specific dependencies instead.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native PTY close-pane lifecycle
description:
Large gap: the native terminal WM path can split/focus panes, but there is no WM-level close-pane action. Users must exit shells manually and the pane model does not reclaim dead/focused panes.

Scope:
- Add `PtyTerminalApp::terminate()` using the portable-pty child kill hook.
- Add a native-session close-focused-pane chord (e.g. `Ctrl-A x`) that terminates and removes the focused pane when more than one pane exists.
- Reflow remaining panes and choose a sane next focus after close.
- Poll for exited PTY children and remove dead panes automatically when more than one pane remains.
- Document the keybinding.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover focus selection after removing panes.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
- README/docs list split/focus/close native pane controls.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-affordances: add first-party form and control components
description:
Renderer/component gap: kittui-affordances has reusable chrome and some components, but lacks a first-party form/control palette needed by semantic kittwm surfaces and SDK apps: buttons, checkboxes, radio buttons/groups, text inputs/areas, selects, sliders, progress bars, menus, tabs, split panes, lists, and tables with focus/disabled/selected states.

Scope:
- Add first-party primitive-scene builders in kittui-affordances for a minimal form/control set, keeping kittui core primitive-only.
- Expose JSON/CLI-friendly component scene helpers where appropriate.
- Include state variants (focused, selected/checked, disabled, active) and theme hooks compatible with kittwm chrome.
- Add tests/examples proving the controls produce primitive kittui scenes.

Acceptance:
- At least button, checkbox, radio/radio-group, text-input, select/list, progress, tabs, and split-pane affordance builders exist.
- Components are in kittui-affordances (or a similarly high-level crate), not kittui-core.
- kittwm/SDK can depend on these without duplicating renderer logic.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: browser launches should create visible browser surfaces, not PTY terminals
description: Operator report: `kittwm-launch --browser ...`, `kittwm --spawn-pty "kittwm-browser ..."`, and `kittwm replace browser ...` still show a terminal/footer and no visible browser surface. The current SDK/docs say browser surfaces dogfood PTY spawning via `kittwm-browser`, but the live WM PTY renderer does not present that as an actual browser pane. Investigate and fix the browser spawn/render path so opening a browser in a split produces a visible browser surface, or at minimum make the CLI refuse/messaging truthful instead of pretending a real browser surface exists. Filed from direct operator feedback.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-md renders Markdown task-list checkboxes
description: Enable pulldown-cmark task-list parsing and render unchecked/checked task list markers in kittui-md output, preserving list bullets and adding tests for [ ] and [x] items.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add remote gui forwarding alias
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: graphical forwarding checks are exposed as x11/graphical/wayland/forwarding, but users may naturally ask for `gui` when checking app launch forwarding over SSH. Add `kittwm remote HOST gui` as an alias for the graphical forwarding doctor and advertise it in help/completions/catalog.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-md images-only mode for image reference inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --images mode that prints only parsed image references with alt text and URLs. This complements --references and --metadata-json for focused image audits. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch browser target unquote: build directly
description: browser_target_from_query unquotes single-quoted targets via replace, allocating an intermediate string. Build the unquoted browser target directly while preserving shell single-quote escape handling.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm native app: headless browser backend with screenshot + mouse/keyboard control
description: Operator direction: support local headless browser apps regardless of X/Quartz backend. Implement a kittui-wm native app backend that launches/attaches to a headless Chromium/Chrome instance, captures screenshots into kittui frames, and routes mouse + keyboard events back to the page. Acceptance: can open a URL/data page, render screenshots in kittwm, click/type through kittui input, and update captures without depending on XQuartz/xterm. Parent epic: bd-031e54. Filed from bd-a9ec5b session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Skip hidden Linux desktop entries in remote app fallback
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: when remote kittwm is absent/broken, `kittwm remote HOST list apps` falls back to Linux `.desktop` discovery. Skip desktop entries marked `NoDisplay=true` or `Hidden=true` so users see launchable app candidates rather than internal/helpers in Ghostty/Kitty SSH workflows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm save-session JSON: append newline directly
description: save_session_cmd writes pretty SESSION_JSON with format!("{pretty}\n"). Build the output buffer directly before file write while preserving stdout/file behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm raw chrome: avoid building full labels for huge frame titles
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: write_raw_frame_chrome builds a full formatted chrome label containing frame.title before clipping to the visible pane width, then repeatedly counts chars while padding. Pathological app titles can cause per-frame allocation/scan churn in the raw compositor chrome path. Scope: build only the visible prefix and pad using bounded counts, preserving normal chrome output and adding focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: respect narrow shortcuts scene width
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: the shortcuts graphics scene still uses a 40-column minimum and fixed row margins. On narrower terminals, shortcuts kitty output can exceed row width and row rects assume unavailable space. Scope: make shortcuts scene width respect positive narrow column values, bound row rects inside the scene, and add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm SDK read snapshot requests: build directly
description: SurfaceHandle read_text and read_scrollback still format READ_TEXT_JSON/READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON request strings. Build these through the direct surface token request helper while preserving capability checks and JSON parsing.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Layout helper for virtual-to-relative kitty placement anchors
description: Build a reusable layout helper for kitty virtual placements anchored to relative non-virtual placements. It should wrap PlacementOptions::relative and expose a higher-level API for: create virtual anchor, place cell/grid images relative to it with H/V offsets, set z ordering, and reserve/update terminal cell footprints. This is needed by markdown tables and future rich UI components that need background images below connected cell graphics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm PTY scrollback snapshots: avoid join allocation pass
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: TerminalState::scrollback_snapshot still uses self.scrollback.join("\\n") and then pushes a trailing newline. When scrollback cache refreshes for large histories, join performs an extra sizing/copy pass over all retained lines. Scope: append retained scrollback lines directly into one preallocated String with trailing newlines, preserving output shape and focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: skip ANSI help overlay when terminal is too narrow
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: ANSI help overlay paths write at column 3 and include padding even when terminal width is less than the required margin. On very narrow terminals this can wrap into adjacent rows and flicker/corrupt chrome. Scope: add a small helper for whether ANSI help overlay text can fit, use it in pure-terminal and direct ANSI help writers, and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui: add bounded kitty terminal response reader
description: Add a timeout-bounded foreground terminal response reader abstraction for kitty graphics replies, with pseudo-terminal or in-memory tests. Must avoid render-loop blocking and avoid consuming child app input. Follow-up from bd-02ef7b.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm browser semantic surface id: build directly
description: HeadlessBrowserApp::semantic_snapshot still uses format! to build the browser:<pid> semantic surface id. Build that surface id directly while preserving browser semantic snapshot coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help overlay scene: build key chip labels directly
description: Replace `format!("help-overlay-key-chip-{idx}")` in `native_help_overlay_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated label helper. Scope only help-overlay key chip labels in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing help overlay panel/chip behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: trim workspace in info scene labels
description: Follow-up workspace-label burn-down finding: kittwm's graphical info/chrome/status helper scenes read workspace strings from JSON and interpolate them directly into row text and diagnostic layer labels. The daemon normally trims now, but these helpers also accept arbitrary JSON in tests and future callers, so unnormalized workspace values can still leak whitespace into scene labels. Add a small workspace extraction helper that trims blank/defaults to '-' for kittwm info/chrome/status scenes and use it consistently; add focused scene-label coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Handle argument-bearing TryExec in SSH app fallback
description: The Rust .desktop parser now handles quoted or argument-bearing TryExec values, but the pooled-SSH shell fallback still checks `command -v "$try_exec"`, so values like `TryExec=/usr/bin/foo --flag` are incorrectly treated as missing. Add a shell helper to extract the executable token, accept absolute paths, and use PATH lookup for simple commands so no-remote-kittwm Linux fallback matches installed remote app discovery more closely.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: typed session save/restore helpers
description:
SDK gap: session save/restore exists in the socket and CLI (`SESSION_JSON`, `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON`) but SDK clients still need raw protocol strings. Add typed SDK helpers for session manifests.

Scope:
- Add typed session manifest structs matching current `SESSION_JSON` shape (layout/focus/panes with command/title/weight/focused where applicable).
- Add `Kittwm::session()` and `Kittwm::restore_session(&manifest)` helpers.
- Gate read with low-risk read capability and restore with window/create/control capability as appropriate.
- Add unit tests for manifest JSON parsing and restore request compaction/command formatting using a Unix test socket if useful.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can save/restore sessions without raw protocol strings.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md renders Markdown definition lists
description: Enable pulldown-cmark definition-list parsing and render definition list title/definition pairs as visible components instead of dropping structure. Add tests for a simple term/definition pair.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Exercise metadata blocks in kittui-md proof gallery
description: Update docs/examples/kittui-md-proof.md so the proof document includes Markdown metadata/frontmatter blocks now supported by kittui-md. Keep it small and validate with metadata-blocks/metadata-json modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: disable software flow control in raw mode
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: raw mode currently preserves IXON/IXOFF software flow-control input flags. That lets the host terminal intercept Ctrl-S/Ctrl-Q, freezing output or preventing those bytes from reaching native terminal apps. Scope: clear IXON/IXOFF alongside CR/LF translations, preserve unrelated input flags, and add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4
spoken_name: disable software flow control raw mode

title: kittwm native toast: avoid full scans of huge messages
description: native toast handling lowercases and counts the entire status/action message before deciding toast styling and width. Pathological launcher/action error strings can allocate or scan huge payloads during redraw. Use allocation-free ASCII case-insensitive matching and bounded width counting while preserving normal toast behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: include base64 PNGs in render batch JSON bytes
description:
Large gap: `kittui render` single-scene JSON supports `--json-bytes` via `png_base64`, and FFI render-many manifests include base64 PNG entries, but CLI batch render manifests omit PNG bytes even with global `--json-bytes`. This leaves shell users without a no-files JSON transport for batch render previews.

Scope:
- Extend `kittui render <scenes.json>|- --out-dir DIR --json-bytes --json/--dry-run` to include per-file `png_base64` entries.
- Keep default batch JSON compact without base64.
- Add integration test for batch dry-run JSON bytes.

Acceptance:
- Batch render dry-run with `--json-bytes` includes valid PNG base64 per image.
- Existing render_command tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native panes: bound status chip command text
description: native_pane_status_chip_text embeds pane.command directly into status chip text and scene labels. A very long command can force large strings/hashes every redraw and bloat graphical scene metadata. Clip the command portion before formatting the status chip while preserving normal commands.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md exposes structured definition-list metadata
description: Definition lists now render visibly, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose term/definition pairs. Add structured definition list metadata, include it in metadata JSON and plain/rich metadata sections, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK event helpers for frame renderer and format
description: Render-performance SDK follow-up: frame-presented helpers expose size, dirty tiles, and transport bytes, but consumers still call generic detail_str for renderer/format. Add EventEnvelope::frame_renderer() and frame_format() so frame metadata inspection stays on the frame-presented helper surface. Filed from ongoing kittwm render-performance event audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: CLI semantic snapshot inspection
description:
Browser semantic tooling gap: browser DOM/ARIA extraction and publishing exist, but there is no simple first-party CLI inspection mode on `kittwm-browser` to print a page's semantic snapshot for debugging without a running WM publish path.

Scope:
- Audit `kittwm-browser` CLI options and `HeadlessBrowserApp::semantic_snapshot()`.
- Add an opt-in CLI mode such as `--semantic-snapshot` / `--print-semantic` that launches/navigates, extracts the DOM/ARIA semantic snapshot, prints compact JSON, and exits.
- Keep screenshot/render behavior unchanged by default.
- Add targeted tests for argument parsing/help where practical; avoid broad browser integration tests if flaky.

Acceptance:
- Developers can inspect browser DOM/ARIA semantic snapshots from CLI without a running kittwm socket.
- Existing kittwm-browser behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm launcher preview: boxed searchable launcher menu
description: Add a visible 'kitwm launcher [--filter QUERY] [--limit N]' preview command that renders a boxed, numbered launcher menu using the same PATH/macOS candidate source as 'kitwm apps'. This is the first operator-visible floating-launcher-shaped UI surface before wiring the overlay into the live raw-mode session. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add pooled-ssh remote window and display listing for kittwm
description: Follow-up to bd-a14559 and Harry SSH request: add easy local kittwm commands to list remote windows and displays over pooled SSH. If remote kittwm is installed, delegate to its WINDOWS/DISPLAYS or list commands; if not, provide best-effort platform fallbacks and clear capability messages.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: inline affordances: document and smoke-test animated prompt/statusline output
description: Document animated inline kitty/prompt/tmux behavior, default 60fps/180-frame/3s period, JSON metadata, and add smoke/golden tests for chip/badge/segment/divider/row animated scenes and payloads.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui terminal: implement real font rendering with Fira Code
description: User report: kittwm terminal glyphs are currently placeholder A-Z glyphs. Implement proper font rendering for terminal text using a real font path, with Fira Code available through nix. Decide/implement font discovery/configuration, glyph rasterization/cache, Unicode coverage strategy, and integration into terminal frame rendering. Acceptance: terminal text renders with real Fira Code glyphs rather than placeholder alphabet; supports normal shell/TUI text, common punctuation, box drawing/fallback; nix build includes/discovers the font; tests/golden/smoke validate non-placeholder rendering.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-terminal events scene kinds label: build directly
description: terminal_events_scene_for_cols still formats the bounded event-kinds layer label after building the summary. Build the kinds layer label directly into one preallocated String while preserving bounded summary behavior and scene output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: extract libghostty-vt cell styles
description:
Extend kittui-ghostty-vt render-state snapshots with basic style metadata exposed by libghostty-vt, such as bold/italic/underline where available, and reflect style in the headless preview PNG. Post an updated preview artifact.

Acceptance:
- Extract style data for cells from GhosttyRenderState row cells.
- Add safe style fields to GhosttyCellSnapshot.
- Preview visually differentiates at least one style attribute in a portable way.
- Include focused tests and generated PNG progress artifact.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native split-toggle log: build directly
description: The native keyboard split-axis toggle diagnostic still formats the axis label with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm launcher scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm launcher scene performance cleanup: launcher_scene still builds the backdrop layer label with format! even though query, selected index, and count are already known/bounded. Build the backdrop label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add YAML background and colorscheme config
description: Add kittwm-specific YAML config at ~/.config/kittwm/config.yaml with background color/effect/opacity and a SDK-exposed terminal colorscheme (fg, bg, colors 0-15). Default/example should be Nord with nord0 background and aurora lens flare at opacity 0.6. Add kittwm-bg wrapper using SDK/control-plane abstractions rather than renderer internals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add real native PTY split panes
description:
Large gap: no-arg `kittwm` is still a single fullscreen PTY proof, not a terminal WM. It lacks a pane list, focus model, real split action, and per-pane geometry.

Scope:
- Replace the single `PtyTerminalApp` in the native loop with a small native pane model.
- Implement one high-value split keybinding/action that spawns a second shell PTY and lays panes out 50/50.
- Route keyboard input only to the focused pane and support focus cycling between native panes.
- Render minimal per-pane title/focus chrome and recompute pane sizes on terminal resize.

Acceptance:
- No-arg `kittwm` starts with one PTY.
- The split binding creates a second visible PTY pane.
- Focus can move between panes and typing goes only to the focused pane.
- Resize updates pane PTY dimensions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: preserve basic SGR colors in native PTY renderer
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap: native PTY panes currently discard SGR styling and render every glyph in one cyan-on-dark palette. Many TUIs and shells rely on ANSI color and reverse video for legibility, so native kittwm should preserve basic cell style in its renderer while keeping text snapshots plain text.

Scope:
- Store per-cell foreground/background colors and current SGR style in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle common SGR modes: reset, bold intensity, foreground/background 30-37/90-97 and 40-47/100-107, default fg/bg, and reverse video.
- Render cell backgrounds and pseudo-glyph foreground colors accordingly.
- Preserve existing plain text snapshots, scrollback, alternate-screen, resize/edit behavior.
- Add focused parser/render tests.

Acceptance:
- Parser tests show SGR style applies to written cells and resets.
- Render test proves colored foreground/background affects RGBA output.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: stop repainting status row every frame
description: kittwm-browser rewrites its status row every 250ms and includes a frame counter, forcing constant row clears even when URL/window/socket are unchanged. Make the status text stable by default, reset SGR before clearing the row, and expose an opt-in env for frame counters/debug refresh.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm top bar workspace label: avoid blank-env allocation
description: top_bar::workspace_label trims KITTWM_WORKSPACE into an owned String before checking whether the value is blank. Avoid allocating for blank/whitespace env values while preserving trimmed non-empty labels and the default workspace.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: introduce reusable kittui chrome theme adapter
description:
Large gap: kittwm chrome is hard-coded in compositor/session paths instead of being reusable kittui UI/chrome. This blocks themed WM chrome, scriptable previews, and shared chrome between live WM and tools.

Scope:
- Introduce a reusable kittwm window chrome/theme adapter based on kittui scenes/layers.
- Model focused/unfocused state, title/source label, tiled/floating hints, border/glow/shadow palette.
- Replace hard-coded compositor border colors/shape with default theme data.
- Document how a host can use/override the default chrome theme.

Acceptance:
- Fake backend demo renders distinct focused vs unfocused chrome.
- Unit tests assert layer ordering and state-dependent colors.
- No compositor hot-path fixed chrome literals remain outside default theme definitions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make native session restore transactional
description:
Large native-WM reliability gap: `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON` currently terminates and clears all existing panes before spawning replacement panes. If a restore pane fails to spawn, the live session can be left empty or the native loop can error out. Session restore should be transactional from the user's perspective.

Scope:
- Spawn all restore panes into a temporary vector first.
- Only after all new panes spawn and resize successfully, terminate and replace old panes.
- Preserve the old session and log an error if restore spawning/resizing fails.
- Keep title/weight/focus/layout restore behavior unchanged on success.
- Add targeted helper/unit coverage where practical.

Acceptance:
- Restore no longer clears current panes before new pane creation succeeds.
- Targeted session tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --launch-on-f12: runtime launcher key spawns xterm/command
description: Small runtime launcher slice under bd-031e54 / bd-8d64b1. Add --launch-on-f12 so a running kitwm session intercepts F12 before forwarding keys and spawns xterm by default, or  via '/bin/sh -c'. The spawn is detached with stdio null, logged to /tmp/kittui-wm.log, and shown in the footer as last_launch_pid. This proves in-session launch plumbing before Mod+Return + floating menu. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document narrow rustfmt workflow for kittui-cli slices
description: Context: during bd-306ec8 validation, cargo fmt --check --package kittui-cli reported unrelated formatting drift and cargo fmt --package kittui-cli rewrote example and smoke-test files outside the bead. I restored the churn and used rustfmt directly on crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs. Friction shape: the routine package fmt command is too broad for narrow slices when the package contains pre-existing formatting drift. Suggested fix: document a narrow rustfmt workflow or clean the package formatting baseline deliberately. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-306ec8 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm focus result status: build directly
description: The focus-action result status still formats window/error suffixes with format!. Build those status strings directly while preserving exact output.

title: docs: document CLI render-only PNG command
description:
Large gap: `kittui render` now exists as the CLI render-only PNG surface, but README/DESIGN still only advertise compose/place flows. Shell users looking for artifact/preview rendering will miss the new command.

Scope:
- Update README shipped CLI command list and quick start with `kittui render` examples.
- Update DESIGN FFI/platform or CLI section to mention the render-only PNG path across Rust/FFI/Python/CLI.
- Keep docs concise and accurate.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention `kittui render <scene.json|-> --out out.png` and render-only PNG use case.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm --help: include first-party helper examples
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces, chrome-scene, or native move-log work. The top-level `kittwm --help` examples section lists core commands but does not include the first-party helper workflows now surfaced in quickstart/examples/cheat (`kittwm-launch`, `kittwm-terminal`, `kittwm-top`, `kittwm-bar`, `kittwm-browser`). Add a concise helper block to the top-level examples plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.

title: Add kittui-md outline-json inspection mode
description: Add --outline-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown heading outline entries, emitting schema_version plus indexed level/text/anchor records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.

title: kittwm-browser: forward Tab input to pages
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser now forwards text and Backspace/Delete, but Tab input is still dropped by the browser input adapter. That makes form navigation unusable and forces extra mouse/semantic interactions. Add a browser Tab key action via DevTools and preserve action ordering with surrounding text.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add capability scoping for SDK clients
description: Define and enforce client capabilities for create/replace windows, spawn/capture surfaces, send input, clipboard read/write, and global event subscriptions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: saturate native toast message width
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: native toast scene width uses a lossy u16 cast from message character count. Extremely long launcher/error messages can overflow the width estimate before clamping, producing undersized toast geometry and stale/clipped overlay behavior. Scope: use saturated width accounting for toast messages and add focused long-message coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add kittui-md interactive tables toggle
description: Add a native tables toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press t to inspect parsed table summaries without leaving the pager. Reuse existing table metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/table-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: extract presentation-agnostic shell view and chrome model
description: Split the default shell's layout/chrome view model from direct ANSI/kitty output so it can be rendered by kitty graphics, pure terminal, kittui scene, or headless renderers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help: make extra topic errors actionable
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm help panes extra` currently reports that help accepts at most one topic, but it does not point to `kittwm help topics` or a copyable focused-help example. Make extra-topic errors for text/scene/kitty help actionable with a concise try/help hint plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.

title: nix build: vendor core-audio-types-rs crate

title: kittwm: audit raw-mode teardown and terminal restoration
description: QA gap: kittwm must always restore host terminal state after normal exit, panic-ish errors, Ctrl-] exit, triple Ctrl-C exit, failed startup, and daemon/socket command failures. Audit raw-mode/alternate-screen/mouse/cursor teardown paths and add focused tests or harness coverage where possible. Acceptance: no stranded alt screen, hidden cursor, raw input, mouse mode, or broken echo after common failure/exit paths.

title: kittwm: extract TerminalSurface engine from PtyTerminalApp
description: Move PTY lifecycle, TerminalState parsing, responses, host sequences, snapshots, and RGBA rendering behind a reusable TerminalSurface type. Keep PtyTerminalApp as a thin adapter with no behavior change.

title: kittwm info output: write header directly
description: Replace the initial `format!(...)` header construction in `format_info_output(...)` with direct `String` appends/writes. Scope only the kittwm info header block in `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; preserve existing text output and daily-driver info coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Prevent Ctrl-C from quitting outer kittwm
description: Ctrl-C should not immediately quit the outer kittwm window manager. Currently a single Ctrl-C press exits the entire session, but the intended behavior is to require triple Ctrl-C (Ctrl-C three times) to actually quit, matching the documented behavior for terminating kittwm.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kittwm shortcuts scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm shortcuts scene performance cleanup: shortcuts_scene builds the backdrop layer label with format! from the static shortcut count. Build the shortcuts backdrop label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: bindings-ts: type channelized batch placement output
description:
Large gap: `Kittui.placeManyChannels` returns a structured object at runtime, but TypeScript declarations type it as `Record<string, unknown>`. Platform users lose compile-time access to image ids, footprints, byte counts, and channel strings.

Scope:
- Add a `BatchChannels` TypeScript interface matching FFI/CLI channelized batch JSON.
- Update `placeManyChannels` declaration to return `BatchChannels`.
- Add a lightweight type/runtime test expectation in the existing JS tests where practical and README wording.

Acceptance:
- Type declarations expose count, image_ids, footprints, upload_bytes, placement_bytes, embed_bytes, upload, placement, embed.
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` and `git diff --check` pass.

Fix Nix build failure after adding git dependencies `mcp-cli` and `updatable-cli`.

- `buildRustPackage`/`importCargoLock` needs `cargoLock.outputHashes` entries for git dependencies.

Scope:

title: kittwm native STATUS reply: build directly

title: Expose active title drag state in kittwm status
description: SDK/status follow-up: live kittwm now shows active title-drag state in footer and pane chrome, but STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON do not report which pane is actively being dragged. Add title_drag_active metadata to native pane status and SDK helpers so automation can observe active drag state without scraping chrome text. Filed from ongoing kittwm drag UX/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native pane border: build border label directly
description: Replace the `format!("pane-{idx}-kittui-border")` label in `native_pane_border_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated label helper. Scope only the kittui-border layer label in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing native shell affordance scene labels and tests.

title: docs: kittwm JSON wait wrappers

Docs follow-up for JSON wait match socket/CLI wrappers.

- Clarify existing text wait replies remain unchanged.

- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: capture OSC titles for native pane chrome
description:
Large terminal-WM chrome gap: native PTY pane titles are frozen to the launch command unless manually renamed. Real terminal apps set their window title through OSC 0/1/2; kittwm should consume that metadata so WM chrome/status follows the running app.

Scope:
- Teach the native PTY terminal parser to capture OSC 0/1/2 title updates.
- Make `PtyTerminalApp::title()` return the captured terminal title when available, falling back to launch command.
- Preserve explicit socket `RENAME_PANE` override semantics in session chrome/status.
- Add targeted parser/unit coverage.

Acceptance:
- OSC title sequences update native app title.
- Existing explicit display title override still wins at session level.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: NativeSurface focus event hook
description:
Docs follow-up for NativeSurface focus notification hook.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md, docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md, and/or docs/README.md to mention NativeSurface now has a focus notification hook for terminal focus-in/out reporting, with PTY implementation gated by terminal focus-reporting mode.
- Clarify this is separate from semantic component focus and socket `FOCUS_PANE`/`SEMANTIC_FOCUS`.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect the new NativeSurface focus event hook and its narrow PTY semantics.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm launcher: bound overlay redraw key query text
description: launcher_overlay_key embeds the full launcher query in the overlay redraw key. A pasted or env-seeded huge query can bloat per-frame keys and memo comparisons even though rendering clips visible text. Bound the query field in the redraw key while preserving active/selected invalidation.

title: Add kittui-md capabilities discovery outputs
description: Add --capabilities and --capabilities-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can list supported high-level capability names independently of --about. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: graphical command palette for daily-driver actions
description: Dogfood opportunity: expose common kittwm actions through a kittui-rendered command palette (spawn terminal, split, focus, layout, examples/help, apps). This showcases kittui controls and reduces CLI memorization. Acceptance: command palette opens from a keybinding, renders with kittui components, filters/actions are keyboard navigable, and maps to existing commands without duplicating business logic.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-ffi: add real JSON runtime config constructor
description:
Large gap: `kittui-ffi` exposes only `kittui_runtime_new(cache_dir)` and a placeholder `kittui_runtime_configure` that records config text without mutating runtime state. Non-Rust platforms need a real constructor for renderer/transport/terminal capability config.

Scope:
- Add `kittui_runtime_new_config(const char* json)` that constructs a runtime from JSON.
- Support at least: `cache_dir`, `renderer`, `transport`, `columns`, `rows`, `cell_width_px`, `cell_height_px`, `supports_kitty`, and `supports_unicode_placeholders`.
- Keep existing `kittui_runtime_new` behavior.
- Update ABI minor and add targeted FFI tests for transport/config and unsupported-terminal errors.

Acceptance:
- FFI tests can create a direct/supported runtime and place a scene.
- FFI tests can create an unsupported runtime and observe placement failure/last_error.
- `cargo test -p kittui-ffi` and `cargo build -p kittui-ffi` pass.

creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kittwm SDK app discovery test socket path: build directly
description: The SDK app_discovery_helpers_send_expected_socket_commands test still formats its temp Unix socket path inline. Reuse the direct test_socket_path helper while preserving app discovery socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: sdk app discovery socket path refactor

title: bindings-ts: include FFI last_error in thrown failures
description:
Large gap: TypeScript bindings throw only numeric status codes when FFI placement calls fail, even though the C ABI exposes `kittui_last_error`. Python now reports last-error details, but JS/TS platform users still lose the actual parse/runtime message.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_last_error` in the TypeScript koffi binding.
- Add a helper that appends last-error text to all non-OK placement errors.
- Cover place/placeAt/placeMany/placeManyAt/placeManyChannels failure paths with fake-lib tests.
- Update TypeScript README to mention detailed FFI errors.

Acceptance:
- Failed FFI calls throw messages containing both status and last error text when available.
- Existing TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Set q=2 quiet flag on all kitty control commands
description:
kitty emits diagnostic responses for upload, animation, placement, and delete commands unless q=1 (suppress OK responses) or q=2 (suppress all responses including errors) is set. The current encoder never sets q, which leaks Gi=<id>;ENOENT and OK lines into the terminal output and breaks the showcase visually. Wire a per-Runtime quiet mode (default q=2) and surface it via TerminalInfo / Runtime builder; preserve a verbose path for debugging.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add mode to installed app list JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: pooled-SSH fallback app list JSON includes `mode:"shell-path-macos-linux-desktop"`, but installed/local `kittwm apps --json` does not include a mode field. Add the same mode metadata so healthy delegated remote-kittwm app-list JSON is closer to fallback parity and easier to consume with one script.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: add placement coverage health helpers
description: Add SDK convenience methods on `SurfacePlacementCoverage` so diagnostics can answer whether native placement coverage is complete, whether it has gaps, and how many placement contracts are missing/not-ready without manually comparing counts. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md metadata-blocks inspection mode
description: Add a focused --metadata-blocks output mode to kittui-md so frontmatter/metadata blocks have the same human inspection affordance as links, images, tables, code blocks, definitions, math, HTML, and footnotes. Include CLI docs and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm PTY text snapshots: avoid per-row string allocation
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: TerminalState::text_snapshot builds a new String for every row via line_snapshot(row), then copies it into the full snapshot. Large terminal grids therefore allocate per row whenever a text snapshot refreshes. Scope: append trimmed rows directly into the destination string, keep line_snapshot behavior for scrollback, and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add kittui-md toc alias for outline
description: Add --toc as a friendly alias for kittui-md --outline so users can inspect a document table of contents with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon help test socket path: build directly
description: The daemon help-json test still formats its per-process socket filename with format!. Reuse the direct test_socket_filename helper for that temp path while preserving HELP/HELP_JSON coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm split state: build action string directly
description: Replace the `format!("split.{}.launcher -> {}", ...)` action string in `SplitState::apply(...)` with direct preallocated String construction. Scope only split-state launcher action status construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing split-state tests and behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add native app discovery CLI wrappers
description:
Large native-WM socket usability gap: docs and controllers still use raw `kittwm --attach -c APPS_JSON` / `APPS_FIRST ...` for app discovery, even though other inspection/control/automation paths now have first-class wrappers. App discovery and launch are part of the DISPLAY-like WM control plane and should be scriptable without raw protocol strings.

Scope:
- Add CLI flags:
  - `--apps-json` -> `APPS_JSON`
  - `--apps-first <query>` -> `APPS_FIRST <query>`
  - `--apps-launch-first <query>` -> `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST <query>`
- Preserve query case/spaces.
- Add help text, request-builder tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Wrappers generate expected socket requests.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Include remote command in default terminal alias title
description: Remote terminal aliases currently default pane title to HOST even when launching a command, so multiple remote command panes for the same host are indistinguishable. Keep shell/login panes titled HOST, but make command-bearing aliases like `kittwm remote HOST terminal htop` default to `HOST: htop` unless the user supplies `--title`. Cover alias parsing tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: typed app discovery helpers
description:
SDK gap: app discovery is available over the native socket (`APPS_JSON`, `APPS_FIRST`, `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST`) and CLI wrappers, but `kittwm-sdk` clients still need raw protocol strings. Add typed SDK app-discovery helpers.

Scope:
- Add typed response structs for `APPS_JSON` including default command/resolution, PATH commands, and macOS apps.
- Add `Kittwm::apps()`, `Kittwm::app_first(query)`, and `Kittwm::app_launch_first(query)` helpers.
- Gate helpers with an appropriate existing capability rather than raw request capability.
- Add unit tests for JSON parsing and request formatting where practical.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can inspect and launch discovered apps without raw protocol strings.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: saturate events scene row count
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: events graphics compute rows with kinds.len() cast to u16 plus padding. A large event batch can overflow/truncate row accounting in debug/release before clamping, risking malformed graphics scene metadata. Scope: use saturated row count accounting for events scenes and add focused large-list coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: clean empty first-launch workspace
description:
User-facing first-launch UX: default `kittwm` should open a clean empty workspace instead of immediately spawning a flickering shell pane/footer.

Scope:
- Change native kittwm default session to start with an empty workspace and a simple top bar showing current workspace id and time.
- Preserve opt-in old behavior via an env knob such as `KITTWM_STARTUP_TERMINAL=1`.
- Add a keyboard shortcut to launch the default terminal command from the empty workspace (e.g. Ctrl-A Enter / Ctrl-A t).
- Add Ctrl-A ? help overlay listing native shortcuts.
- Avoid noisy footer by default; keep concise top bar/status only.
- Keep socket spawning (`SPAWN_PTY`) working.
- Add focused tests for empty shell view/top bar/help text/terminal launch helper behavior.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm` no longer starts with an automatic zsh pane by default.
- Empty workspace renders a stable top bar with workspace id and time.
- Ctrl-A ? surfaces a shortcut list.
- Shortcut launches a terminal pane from empty workspace.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: bound events scene row rects on narrow terminals
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: events graphics inherit narrow-aware info scene widths, but event row rects still use a fixed 10-cell-style inset. On very narrow terminals those row rectangles can extend past the scene bounds and contribute to graphical wrap/flicker. Scope: reuse the bounded info row geometry for events rows and add focused narrow-width coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native footer: build status backdrop label directly
description: Replace `format!("status-bar-backdrop:{status_label}")` in `native_footer_status_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated label helper. Scope only the native footer status backdrop label in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing footer scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-bar: cap extreme scene column overrides
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: standalone kittwm-bar scene_cols accepts any positive u16 from KITTWM_BAR_COLS/COLUMNS. An accidentally huge override can build and upload an enormous top-bar scene, adding kitty graphics churn and slowness. Scope: preserve normal/default widths but cap pathological column values with focused helper tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm restore-session request: build directly
description: restore_session_request compacts SESSION_JSON and then uses format! to prepend the RESTORE_SESSION_JSON verb. Build the daemon request directly into one preallocated String while preserving JSON validation and compact serialization.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: terminal emulator conformance smoke for common TUI apps
description: QA gap: daily terminal use needs more than simple shell text. Add a smoke checklist/test harness for common TUI/control sequences: htop/top-like alternate screen, vim/nano cursor/addressing, less scrollback, shell prompts, colors, box drawing, mouse modes, bracketed paste, Ctrl-C. Acceptance: known supported/unsupported matrix plus automated snapshots where feasible.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui: add render_many_png batch API
description:
Large gap: kittui has batch placement APIs but render-only PNG is single-scene only. CLI batch rendering currently loops locally, and Rust hosts that need preview/artifact batches have to do the same boilerplate instead of using a first-class renderer API.

Scope:
- Add `Runtime::render_many_png(&[Scene]) -> Vec<Vec<u8>>` preserving input order.
- Use it in `kittui render --out-dir` batch path.
- Add core tests for order/count/PNG signatures and empty batches.
- Keep terminal placement support checks out of render-only APIs.

Acceptance:
- Core batch render API returns one PNG per scene and accepts empty batches.
- CLI render batch tests continue to pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK resize delta label: build directly
description:
SurfaceHandle::resize_weight still formats positive resize deltas before building RESIZE_PANE. Build the signed delta label directly while preserving protocol output.

Blocked after commit/validation by GitHub transport failures during reintegration. Branch agent/ms-mac/kittui/ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev is ahead 1 at 2595ca64. Validation passed: resize_delta_label_builds_directly, control_helpers_send_expected_socket_commands, cargo check -p kittwm-sdk, evidence gate. Evidence file-cb404a4f3a7d-1779992807651. Multiple caco reintegrate attempts failed with ssh.github.com:443 / github.com:443 EADDRNOTAVAIL or connect failures; no new work taken while branch remains unlanded.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK spawn surface tests: build socket path directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("/tmp/kwb-{}-{}.sock", ...)` in `spawn_surface_sends_browser_as_first_party_browser_app` with the existing direct socket-path helper. Scope only kittwm SDK spawn-surface test socket path construction in `crates/kittwm-sdk/src/lib.rs`; preserve spawn surface socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: make pointer hit-testing deterministic by z-order
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm pointer routing says it walks windows in z-order, but `hit_test` iterates a `HashMap`, so overlapping windows route nondeterministically. A terminal WM needs deterministic top-window pointer focus before swap/raise/lower features can be meaningful.

Scope:
- Track last composition/raw-frame window order alongside placement rectangles.
- Make `hit_test` walk that order from topmost to bottommost instead of iterating the placement HashMap.
- Preserve pointer coordinate mapping behavior.
- Add targeted tests for overlapping windows proving the later/top window wins.

Acceptance:
- Overlapping placements route pointer events to the deterministic topmost window.
- Both raw-frame and scene composition paths update hit-test order.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui: inline chip component for prompts/statuslines
description:
Implement a first slice of kittui inline components for shell prompts/tmux statuslines.

Motivation:
- kittui should be usable as a linkable/UI-builder substrate from shell scripts, not just kittwm.
- Simple use case: zsh prompt/P10K-like text chip segments with ANSI/tmux/plain rendering.

Scope:
- Add a cooked-mode kittui CLI surface for one-line chips, e.g. `kittui inline chip --text TEXT` or `kittui chip --text TEXT`.
- Support at least formats: plain, ansi, tmux.
- Keep it dependency-light and deterministic; no terminal graphics/side effects in this first slice.
- Add library-level helper if there is an obvious existing crate home; otherwise keep CLI-scoped but reusable.
- Add tests for output shape.
- Include brief command help.

Out of scope:
- Kitty/ghostty graphics side effects.
- tmux border replacement.
- placeholder registry/post-layout observer.

Acceptance:
- User can run something like `kittui inline chip --text main --format ansi` for prompt segments.
- `--format tmux` emits tmux statusline style syntax.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: bound session scene row rects on narrow terminals
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: session graphics inherit narrow-aware info scene widths, but session row rects still use a fixed 10-cell-style inset. On very narrow terminals those row rectangles can extend past the scene bounds and contribute to graphical wrap/flicker. Scope: reuse bounded info row geometry for session rows and add focused narrow-width coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-chrome: bound graphical scene labels
description: Audit finding: `kittwm chrome-kitty` / chrome scene JSON bounds geometry, but serializes raw workspace/owner/tilable_rows values into backdrop and row labels. Pathological chrome status strings can inflate scene JSON/kitty payloads and redraw allocation churn. Clip chrome scene label fields while preserving useful drawable-reservation diagnostics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm keymap launcher failure log: build directly
description: The raw compositor keymap launcher failure diagnostic still formats errors with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch graphics: bound plan scene labels
description: kittwm-launch plan graphics embed raw status text in a scene layer label; long titles or queries can bloat scene metadata and redraw/hash inputs even though geometry is bounded. Clip diagnostic plan scene labels while preserving text/JSON command output.

title: Add remote Wayland forwarding doctor alias
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: graphical remote doctor now reports both X11 forwarding and waypipe availability, but the short aliases are still x11/graphical/forwarding. Add `kittwm remote HOST wayland` as another alias for the same graphical doctor so users on Wayland remotes can discover the preflight naturally.

title: Add kittui-md images-json inspection mode

title: kittwm native terminal renderer: reuse chrome ANSI style strings
description: render_native_shell_view_terminal formats ANSI fg/bg style strings for every pane title on every pure-terminal redraw. Precompute the top-bar, focused-title, and unfocused-title style strings once per frame and reuse them while preserving output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm config kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm config inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add config-scene-json and config-kitty/config-graphics commands that render the existing config/keymap readiness summary as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing config text behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm config-scene-json emits a kittui Scene summarizing keymap source, launcher env, prefix, binding count, duplicates, and readiness status.
- kittwm config-kitty/config-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing kittwm config text output remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from a synthetic config summary.

title: kittwm native terminal command: build shell fallback directly
description: native_terminal_command maps SHELL through format!("{s} -l") when no configured command is present. Build the login-shell fallback directly into one String while preserving env/config fallback behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-keymap: bound row rects on narrow scenes
description: Audit finding: keymap-kitty/keymap-scene row rects still use fixed 10px side margins and (width - 20).max(1). On narrow diagnostic scenes this can extend past the footprint, unlike recent bounded info/shortcuts row rect fixes. Reuse the bounded indicator rect helper for keymap rows and add narrow-width coverage.

title: kittwm kitty cursor move: build CSI directly
description: Replace `format!("\x1b[{row};{col}H")` in `kittui-kitty` cursor move construction with a direct preallocated String helper. Scope only `crates/kittui-kitty/src/lib.rs` cursor move escape construction; preserve exact cursor move grammar tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: add native surface coverage lookup helpers
description: Add typed SDK convenience helpers for the kittwm architecture contract's first-party native surface coverage matrix: lookup by name, iterate kitty-graphics-native surfaces, and a readiness predicate on NativeSurfaceContract. This improves app/test consumption of the SDK contract without touching kittwm-bar, docs/help, Runtime/browser, or live session internals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: support shift-arrow in SEND_KEY and browser surfaces
description: Keyboard/input UX gap: browser surfaces already have `send_shift_arrow_key`, but first-party `SEND_KEY` cannot express Shift+Arrow. That blocks selection/navigation workflows in browser text fields and terminal TUIs from the socket/CLI automation path. Add `shift-left/right/up/down` (and `shift-arrow-*` aliases) to the daemon key parser using common xterm modifier sequences, route those labels to direct browser surfaces, and update focused parser/mapping coverage and help strings.

title: kittwm CLIPBOARD_JSON replies: build directly
description: CLIPBOARD_JSON replies still use format! only to append a newline to allowed/available JSON values. Reuse the direct JSON-line helper while preserving clipboard policy coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm chrome scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm chrome scene performance cleanup: chrome_scene builds the backdrop layer label with format! from bounded workspace/owner labels and reservation counts. Build the chrome backdrop label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm doctor: opt-in kitty capability probe diagnostics
description: Add opt-in kittwm doctor / diagnostics support for kitty a=q probing and response-reader status, extending TransportDiagnostics without changing default rendering behavior. Follow-up from bd-02ef7b.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm status scene: build heading label directly
description: Small kittwm status scene performance cleanup: status_scene builds the heading layer label with format! from the already-bounded socket label. Build the status heading label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm top bar should borrow short display labels
description: `top_bar_display_label` allocates a `String` for every workspace label even when the label is already short enough to display unchanged. Return a borrowed label for the common short-label path and allocate only when truncation/ellipsis is required, preserving rendered text and scene labels. Scope: kittwm top-bar display label helper only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: absolute placement ignores footprint.x/y so images render at cursor not requested coords
description: Runtime::place emits placement_command(image_id, scene.footprint, transport) which uses footprint.cols/rows but drops footprint.x/y. Kitty graphics protocol has no absolute positioning verb — caller must move the cursor via CSI H first. We never do, so a scene at (col=10,row=5) lands wherever the cursor happens to be. Need to prepend the placement with \x1b[r;cH when footprint.x/y are non-zero (or expose a separate api). Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: expose compose placement overrides
description:
Large gap: kittui now has `Runtime::place_at` so hosts can render scene-local assets and move them without changing scene ids, but the CLI `compose` path still places scenes only at their embedded footprint. Shell scripts must mutate JSON coordinates to move a composed scene.

Scope:
- Add optional `kittui compose <scene.json>|- --x X --y Y` placement overrides.
- Use `Runtime::place_at` so render/cache identity remains scene-local while terminal placement changes.
- Preserve existing compose behavior when no overrides are supplied.
- Add CLI/integration coverage that pipes `--scene-json` into `compose - --x ... --y ... --dry-run --json`.

Acceptance:
- `kittui box --scene-json | kittui compose - --x 5 --y 6 --dry-run --json` reports footprint x=5 y=6 with original cols/rows.
- Existing `compose -` behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm raw error tests: build expected key directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("{text}\n{log}")` in `raw_compositor_error_text_and_key_are_bounded` with a direct preallocated expected-key helper. Scope only kittwm raw compositor error-key test expectation construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve bounded error key coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2
spoken_name: kittwm raw error tests build key

title: Use SSH target in remote doctor command hints

Preserve the original remote HOST in the doctor environment and use it for text/JSON command hints, while still reporting the remote machine hostname separately. This keeps next-step commands copy-pasteable for Ghostty/Kitty users connecting via SSH aliases.

title: Add status/check aliases for kittwm remote doctor

title: Show zero-change kittwm frames as clean in pane chips
description: Operator-requested kittwm render-performance visual polish: live pane status chips show frame:clean only when skipped_upload is true, while SDK helper semantics treat changed_tiles == 0 as clean too. Align the live chip with the clean-frame semantics so a measured zero-change frame reads clean instead of frame:0, with focused tests/docs.

title: kittwm-terminal scene encode errors: build directly
description: kittwm-terminal scene JSON output paths still map serde_json encode failures with format!("encode ...: {err}"). Reuse the direct diagnostic builder for status/events scene encode errors while preserving message text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: bound config scene row rects on narrow terminals
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: config graphics inherit narrow-aware info scene widths, but config row rects still use a fixed 10-cell-style inset. On very narrow terminals those row rectangles can extend past the scene bounds and contribute to graphical wrap/flicker. Scope: reuse bounded info row geometry for config rows and add focused narrow-width coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add SDK helper for focused moved kittwm pane state
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/design-cleanliness follow-up: SDK clients can list moved floating panes and query focused-is-resized, but common automation still has to fetch focused_pane() and call has_floating_offset() to know whether the focused floating pane has been dragged/nudged. Add focused_is_moved() helpers on PanesStatus and Status, with tests/docs, so focus/moved state can be queried directly.

title: kittwm top bar: avoid full scans when counting huge workspace labels
description: workspace_chip_total_cols currently counts every char of each workspace label before saturating, so pathological labels still impose large per-redraw scans in top-bar and overlay width decisions. Cap the count needed for chip-width accounting so normal labels are unchanged but huge labels do not burn CPU every frame.

title: kitwm apps --json: machine-readable launcher candidate list
description: Add --json support to 'kitwm apps' so future floating launcher UI / daemon attach can consume the candidate source programmatically. Output should include default_command, default_resolved, path_commands[], macos_apps[] where applicable. Parent: bd-031e54; extends bd-7e7f3d / decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show active kittwm title drag state in footer
description: UX follow-up: while a tiled reorder or floating move drag is active, the footer still only shows mode/panes/focus.
