description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/design-cleanliness follow-up: live chrome now shows non-default pane weights, but SDK clients still have to hand-roll weight != 1 filtering. Add typed helpers for resized/non-default-weight panes on NativePaneDetail, PanesStatus, and Status, with docs/tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Include counts in remote app JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote app fallback JSON lists path, macOS, and Linux desktop candidates but scripts still need to count arrays themselves. Add count fields for each candidate category so local kittwm pooled-SSH app discovery is easier to consume when remote kittwm is absent or broken.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui UI primitives: textbox, headings, title/banner/header/footer, textchip
description: Add reusable kittui UI component primitives so callers do not handroll common surfaces. Components requested: textbox, h1/h2/h3, title, banner, header, footer, textchip. Each should produce kittui scenes/layers with consistent spacing, typography-like scale (within terminal constraints), padding, border/glow/theme hooks, and deterministic sizing. Include snapshot/proof tests and small CLI/example output. Should be usable by markdown renderer and kittwm overlays.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add visible chrome metadata to raw-frame path
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm sessions use the raw RGBA fast path, but raw frames carry only image bytes/footprints. The reusable kittwm chrome theme is only visible on the slower Scene compose path, so live raw sessions lack visible title/focus chrome.

Scope:
- Extend `RawFrame` with lightweight chrome metadata: title string, focused flag, and tiled/floating mode.
- Populate metadata in `Compositor::raw_frames` from focus/mode/window id.
- Render a minimal terminal title strip in `session.rs` above/over each raw frame using the metadata so focused windows are visibly marked.
- Add targeted tests for raw frame metadata.

Acceptance:
- `raw_frames` marks the focused frame and provides title/mode metadata.
- Session code renders title text without affecting image placement semantics.
- `cargo test -p kittui-wm raw_frame` and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native mouse legacy payload: build bytes directly
description: native_legacy_mouse_payload constructs the six-byte legacy terminal mouse payload with vec![...]. Build the payload directly into a fixed-capacity Vec while preserving exact bytes and bounds checks.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native-surfaces graphics: bound row rects on narrow scenes
description: kittwm native-surfaces graphics inherit narrow-aware info scene widths, but native surface rows still use fixed 10px margins and width-20 geometry. On tiny widths row rects can exceed the scene footprint. Reuse bounded row geometry for native-surface rows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Exercise link and image titles in kittui-md proof gallery
description: Update docs/examples/kittui-md-proof.md so sample links/images include Markdown title attributes, validating the newly preserved metadata through --links, --images, and --metadata-json.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon SPAWN creates tracked panes instead of detached processes
description: Next proposal: current SPAWN starts a detached process. Extend the kittwm daemon protocol/session state so socket-only spawn creates a tracked pane/window with layout/focus metadata and can be listed/attached by clients. Builds on bd-cddcf2/bd-624e22.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm base64 queued replies: build directly
description: queue_native_send_bytes_b64 and queue_native_paste_bytes_b64 still use format! for *_QUEUED command/window/bytes replies. Build the shared base64 queued reply directly while preserving bytes queue coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui inline theme tokens reusable library
description:
Extract inline component theme/style tokens into reusable library code.

Scope:
- Move Nord/glass/chrome/metal/neon inline color/style resolution out of `crates/kittui-cli/src/main.rs` into an appropriate reusable module/crate (likely `kittui-affordances`).
- Expose typed theme/style/color resolution APIs for CLI, kittwm chrome, kittwm-bar, and future prompt/status components.
- Preserve current CLI behavior and flags.
- Add library tests for default Nord glass and color overrides.
- Validate targeted tests/build and diff check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-doctor: respect narrow scene width
description: Audit finding: kittwm doctor kitty/scene output forces a minimum 32-column scene even when COLUMNS/KITTWM_DOCTOR_COLS is smaller. On narrow terminals this can overflow/wrap diagnostic graphics, unlike recent narrow-width fixes for kittwm bar/help/toast/status surfaces. Respect narrow positive widths while keeping the 120-column upper bound and default width.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: docs: update SDK plan for first-party app maturity
description:
Documentation debt: docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md still describes kittwm-terminal and kittwm-launch as skeleton-stage items even though terminal now has typed status/events and launcher maturity is landing. Update the SDK plan to reflect current first-party app status and remaining gaps.

Scope:
- Update Current state / First-party apps / Backlog mapping in docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md.
- Mention kittwm-terminal status/events and kittwm-launch matured backend/dry-run/status behavior if landed; otherwise describe terminal landed and launcher pending.
- Keep docs concise.

Acceptance:
- SDK plan no longer misleadingly describes already-landed first-party app work as only skeletons.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md modes and metadata outputs
description: Document the kittui-md CLI modes added during Markdown work: --plain, --rich, --interactive, --outline, and --metadata-json, including what metadata JSON contains and where the proof gallery lives. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser semantic publisher: skip JSON serialization for unchanged snapshots
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: BrowserSemanticPublisher still serializes each semantic snapshot to JSON solely to detect unchanged payloads before publishing. On stable pages, this allocates a potentially large String every semantic interval even though no publish occurs. Scope: compare snapshots directly, retain the last changed snapshot for equality, serialize only in the SDK publish path when the snapshot changed, and update focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Prove: kittui-wm hosts an xterm via XQuartz on macOS
description: Integration test that skips if XQuartz isn't installed. Spawns XquartzServer, launches xterm against the display, waits for it to map, calls compose() and asserts at least one non-empty capture is returned. The demo gains --features xquartz to drive this end-to-end with tendril screenshot proof. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support modified home end and page keys in SEND_KEY
description: Keyboard/input UX gap: browser surfaces already expose Shift/Ctrl/Alt Home, End, PageUp, and PageDown helpers, but first-party `SEND_KEY` only accepts unmodified home/end/page keys. That blocks common selection/navigation shortcuts in browser text fields and terminal TUIs. Add shift/alt/ctrl home/end/page-up/page-down aliases to the daemon key parser using common xterm modifier sequences, route those labels to direct browser surfaces, and update focused parser/mapping/help coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: CLI wrappers for text JSON reads
description:
CLI wrapper gap: daemon and SDK expose `READ_TEXT_JSON` / `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON`, but `kittwm` only has text wrappers.

Scope:
- Add `kittwm --read-text-json WINDOW` mapping to `READ_TEXT_JSON <window>`.
- Add `kittwm --read-scrollback-json WINDOW` mapping to `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON <window>`.
- Add help text and focused CLI request tests.
- Keep existing `--read-text` / `--read-scrollback` behavior unchanged.
- No daemon/session/SDK changes.

Acceptance:
- New wrappers preserve target token validation and emit the expected protocol commands.
- Targeted kittwm binary tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md --mode name selector
description: Add a --mode <name> selector for kittui-md so users and tools can choose any output mode by canonical flag or alias name without spelling the dedicated flag. Document it and add parser/conflict/unknown-mode tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Investigate local macOS kittwm crash report
description: Harry reported that kittwm "just crashes" on his local macOS/Ghostty machine immediately after a doctor run showed a live socket at /tmp/kittwm-harryaskham.sock and a log containing only "native terminal loop: enter". This appears separate from bd-306ec8, which is owned by dev-3 and scoped to SSH terminal-size inference. Initial evidence: `kittwm doctor` reports Direct transport, xterm-ghostty, kitty graphics likely yes, 2 displays, ScreenCaptureKit/quartz enabled, socket running; `kittwm info` shows panes=0 and events/status respond, but the operator still observes a crash. Investigate/reproduce the local crash path, improve diagnostics or fix the crash if reproducible, and coordinate with bd-306ec8 if overlap appears.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: preserve split CSI input prefixes
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser consumes incomplete CSI sequences such as `ESC [` or `ESC [ 1 ;` at the end of a stdin read. The final byte arriving in the next read is then treated as page text instead of a navigation key. Preserve incomplete CSI/SS3 prefixes across reads while keeping complete Escape key behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: skip unchanged raw compositor RGBA uploads
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: the raw compositor loop in `run_compositor_session` always calls `runtime.place_raw_frame` and writes `p.upload` for every window every frame. The comment explicitly says “Always re-upload”, so even static windows send full RGBA payloads continuously. Fix by memoizing raw frame hashes per image id and only uploading when pixels/dimensions change while still re-emitting placement/embed when the footprint changes; clear memo state when windows disappear or overlay clearing invalidates placement. Add deterministic decision coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-bar overlay: precompute repeated ANSI style strings
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: standalone kittwm-bar kitty overlay formats ANSI foreground/background escape strings inside the workspace chip loop, even though active/inactive/clock styles are stable for the frame. Scope: precompute the style strings once per overlay render, append them directly, and keep overlay rendering tests passing.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: support alt-arrow in SEND_KEY and browser surfaces
description: Keyboard/input UX gap: browser surfaces already have `send_alt_arrow_key`, but first-party `SEND_KEY` cannot express Alt+Arrow. That blocks common browser history/navigation and terminal-app shortcuts from the socket/CLI automation path. Add `alt-left/right/up/down` (and `alt-arrow-*` aliases) to the daemon key parser using common xterm modifier sequences, route those labels to direct browser surfaces, and update focused parser/mapping coverage and help strings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native failure logs: build terminate line directly
description: native_terminate_failure_log_line uses whole-line format! for native pane terminate diagnostics. Build the line directly with a preallocated buffer while preserving bounded error text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: keep raw app graphics below ANSI chrome text
description: User reports term chrome renders badly and graphics seem z-indexed over text. The raw compositor uses ANSI chrome/title/footer over kitty app images, but app placements currently use the SDK app z-index (0), which can cover terminal text. Make the raw compositor app-placement helper use a below-text z-index for this ANSI-chrome path, keeping graphical/session chrome paths on the SDK z-index contract.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser status: reuse stable status string between viewport changes
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: with KITTWM_BROWSER_STATUS_FRAMES disabled, the browser status row is stable, but the loop still formats and clips a fresh status String every frame just to compare it with last_status. Scope: cache row+cols+text and skip rebuilding stable status when row/cols are unchanged and frame counters are disabled, preserving frame-counter opt-in and resize behavior with focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm raw frame count log: build directly
description: The raw compositor periodic frame-count diagnostic still formats frame and raw frame count with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: headless Ghostty proofs should render kitty graphics placements
description: Complementary follow-up to bd-b75522. The immediate proof CLI path is owned by dev-2; this bead focuses on the headless terminal capability gap Harry called out: if kittwm emits kitty graphics / native image placements, kittui-ghostty proof PNGs must show those features rather than text placeholders. Scope: inspect libghostty-vt render-state/kitty graphics metadata exposed in kittui-ghostty-vt and add the smallest renderer/test slice that composites a captured kitty graphics placement into the headless preview, or document/file the exact missing libghostty API if not exposed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Show kittwm executable provenance in doctor output
description: During bd-2a9242, the agent initially reproduced with `kittwm` from the checkout `target/debug` because PATH shadowed the operator-reported `~/.nix-profile/bin/kittwm`. `kittwm doctor` reports version and log/socket state but not the executable path or Nix store target, so crash reports involving recently installed profile binaries require manual `command -v` / symlink inspection. Consider adding executable path/store target/provenance to `kittwm doctor` output or documenting the exact-command capture convention for kittwm crash reports. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-2a9242 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui wm chrome: add standard animation flags
description:
Continue animation coverage for kittui scene elements that are not prompt/statusline or primitive shapes. Inventory:
- `kittui wm-chrome` renders reusable kittwm window chrome as a kittui scene but is static.
- `kittui wm-session` renders one or more window chrome scenes from SESSION_JSON but is static.
Acceptance: both commands support `--animated`, `--fps`, and `--frames`, default 60fps/180 frames/3s, add labelled phase-reactive glow layers, and have CLI scene-json/batch smoke tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm launcher: avoid lowercasing candidates in selection path
description: The overlay filtering path avoids lowercasing every candidate, but first_launcher_candidate still lowercases each PATH/app candidate when launching the current query. Use the same allocation-free ASCII casefold matching helper so selecting/launching from large app/PATH lists avoids per-candidate allocations.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm PTY renderer: reduce cursor pixel index work
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: draw_terminal_cursor computes the full row-major RGBA index for every cursor pixel even though it draws contiguous scanlines inside one in-grid cell. The cursor is redrawn on every PTY frame, so this is avoidable pixel-loop overhead. Scope: compute cursor row start indexes once and increment per pixel using the in-bounds set helper, preserving cursor color/placement with focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm help: derive command tree from catalog
description: Make `kittwm --help` render its top command tree from the local command catalog rather than a drifting hand-written command list. Preserve the existing rich daily-driver/detail sections, and enrich catalog descriptions where needed so the generated overview keeps the information currently shown by help text. Scope `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs` only; avoid SSH/crash work owned by dev-3/dev-4.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-ffi: return channelized batch placement JSON
description:
Large gap: platform bindings can batch-place scenes, including at a runtime origin, but only receive one concatenated byte string. CLI users can request channelized JSON (`upload`, `placement`, `embed`, image ids, footprints), while FFI/TypeScript hosts cannot inspect, schedule, or separately write the upload/placement/embed channels.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_place_many_json_channels(runtime, scenes_json, x, y, out)` returning JSON for a batch placed at a group origin.
- Use `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin` and include `count`, `image_ids`, `footprints`, `upload_bytes`, `placement_bytes`, `embed_bytes`, and raw `upload`/`placement`/`embed` strings.
- Bump FFI ABI minor and update `kittui.h` plus ABI snapshot tests.
- Expose `Kittui.placeManyChannels(scenes, x, y)` in TypeScript and add tests.

Acceptance:
- FFI returns parseable JSON with channel strings and metadata for scene arrays.
- TypeScript returns a parsed object.
- Targeted FFI, ABI snapshot, and TypeScript tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward F11 input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards F5-F9, while F10 is owned by dev-3. F11 (`ESC [ 23 ~`) is still consumed. Browsers commonly use F11 for fullscreen-like shortcuts, so forward it through the existing DevTools function-key path.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add C-a g launcher shortcut
description: User request: `C-a g` should open the launcher. Add it to the default kittwm keymap and user-visible shortcut/help surfaces so it is discoverable and works alongside existing `C-a Enter`/`C-a d` launch bindings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm raw compositor: stop periodic footer redraw by default
description: Raw compositor footer rewrites every 30 frames even when state is unchanged, solely to update frame/fps text. This creates steady terminal churn/flicker on idle. Make periodic footer refresh configurable via env and default it off; still redraw immediately when footer state changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm bars: avoid full scans in overlay width accounting
description: Native and standalone bar overlay text-width helpers use chars().count() before saturating to u16, so pathological labels can still force full scans during redraw. Count only up to the saturation bound while preserving normal and saturated width results.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK JSON payload requests: build directly
description: Typed SDK reserve_chrome and restore_session helpers still format RESERVE_CHROME_JSON/RESTORE_SESSION_JSON payload requests. Build these request strings directly into preallocated buffers while preserving protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK reserve chrome tests: build socket path directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("/tmp/kwreserve-{}-{}.sock", ...)` in `reserve_chrome_sends_typed_drawable_reservation_request` with the existing direct socket-path helper. Scope only kittwm SDK reserve-chrome test socket path construction in `crates/kittwm-sdk/src/lib.rs`; preserve reserve chrome socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2
spoken_name: kittwm sdk reserve chrome socket path

title: Bound headless Chrome native test startup waits
description: Context: while validating bd-099358, cargo test -p kittui-wm native::tests --lib passed the PTY and terminal-state coverage but then hung until the harness timeout after headless_browser_data_url_screenshot_when_chrome_available had been running for over 60 seconds. The likely fix-shape is to make HeadlessBrowserApp::launch/read_devtools_port use a genuinely bounded startup wait and clean up the Chrome child on timeout, or gate the test with a faster availability probe. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-099358 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: avoid pre-capture shell view work in graphical mode
description: Operator loop: continue finding/fixing kittwm slow/flickery issues. After moving graphical chrome to post-capture, the native loop still builds a pre-capture shell view unconditionally before branching to pure-terminal vs graphical rendering. In graphical mode that view is unused, but it can clone pane text snapshots and metadata for every pane every frame. Scope: build the pre-capture shell view only inside the pure-terminal branch, leaving graphical mode to build its single post-capture shell view. Add targeted coverage if practical and run focused session tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm loaded keymap log: build directly
description: Replace `dbg.log(&format!("loaded keymap from {path}"))` in `load_runtime_keymap_result(...)` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only loaded-keymap diagnostic log string construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing diagnostic text and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add pooled-ssh remote terminal forwarding for kittwm-terminal
description: Operator request slice: make it easy from a local kittwm session to open a remote terminal on a host that may not have kittwm installed. `kittwm-terminal --remote HOST` should spawn a local kittwm terminal pane running SSH with ControlMaster/ControlPersist pooling; with `-- COMMAND` or `--command`, it should run that command remotely through the SSH pane. This complements bd-a14559 remote apps and bd-0686d9 remote windows/displays.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: add native inspection CLI wrappers
description:
Large native-WM usability gap: kittwm has first-class CLI wrappers for automation and session save/restore, but core inspection still requires protocol strings such as `kittwm --attach -c PANES_JSON` and `SESSION_JSON`. This keeps common controller/shell workflows unnecessarily tied to raw socket verbs.

Scope:
- Add CLI flags for native socket inspection:
  - `--status-json`
  - `--panes`
  - `--panes-json`
  - `--session-json`
- Route through default socket resolution and print replies.
- Add help text, request-builder/normalization tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Flags generate the expected socket request strings.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: pure-terminal redraw skip must flush host sequences
description: Follow-up from bd-bbdd86: the pure-terminal redraw guard skips writing the frame batch when rendered text is unchanged, but the batch may already contain host escape sequences from `pane.app.take_host_sequences()`. That can drop terminal host sequences in fallback mode. Fix by making the write decision account for pre-render pending batch bytes, so host sequences always flush even when the shell text is unchanged; add a deterministic test for that decision.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-native-surfaces: saturate scene row count
description: Audit finding: native_surfaces_scene computes rows with surfaces.len() as u16 + 5 before clamping. If the architecture contract grows/pathologically changes, the row count can wrap before clamp and produce incorrect scene metadata. Saturate/clamp using usize before casting and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm completions: collect borrowed static words
description: `completion_words` builds a `Vec<String>` by cloning command names and static flag names, but every completion word is borrowed from static command metadata or a static flag list. Return `Vec<&'static str>` instead so bash/zsh/fish completion generation can sort/dedup borrowed words without allocating one String per word. Scope: kittwm completion word collection only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm daemon stale test socket path: build directly
description: The daemon stale-pane test still formats its per-process socket filename with format!. Reuse the direct test_socket_filename helper for that temp path while preserving stale-pane cleanup coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket text injection
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: external controllers can spawn/focus/resize/move native panes, but cannot inject text into a pane through the socket. A DISPLAY-like terminal WM needs a first-class control-plane path to drive focused panes without synthesizing host TTY input.

Scope:
- Add native socket commands:
  - `SEND_TEXT <window|focused> <text>` sends UTF-8 text bytes to a pane.
  - `SEND_LINE <window|focused> <text>` sends UTF-8 text plus `\n`.
- Queue and handle commands in the native PTY session using existing pane lookup/focused semantics.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON/error text coverage and targeted tests.
- Document in README/docs if implemented.

Acceptance:
- Commands validate window/text arguments and return queued replies.
- Native loop sends bytes to the target pane.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm split launcher failure log: build directly
description: The raw compositor split launcher failure diagnostic still formats errors with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: live top-bar scene text metadata
description:
Docs follow-up for live top-bar scene text metadata.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md or docs/README.md to mention that the live kittui scene chrome path carries top-bar state/text while pure terminal has ANSI fallback.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect live top-bar scene text/status behavior.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm keymap prefix log: build directly
description: The raw compositor keymap prefix-entered diagnostic still formats the chord spec with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: interrupt idle sleeps when input arrives
description: Follow-up to adaptive browser idle capture pacing: once the browser loop enters the longer idle interval, it currently sleeps the whole slack with std::thread::sleep, so input may wait for the idle timeout before being processed. Scope: make browser frame sleep interruptible by polling stdin in bounded chunks and waking the loop as soon as input is ready; add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-help: bound daily help row rects
description: Audit finding: daily-driver/help kitty scenes still use fixed 10px row margins with (width - 20).max(1). On narrow help/example/cheat scenes these rows can escape the footprint and corrupt graphics. Reuse bounded info indicator geometry for daily help rows and add coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native top-bar clock label: build directly
description: native graphical top-bar overlay wraps the clock with format!(" {clock} "). Build the padded clock label directly while preserving overlay placement.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal SDK errors: build diagnostics directly
description: kittwm-terminal run() maps SDK/connect errors with repeated format! calls. Add a small direct diagnostic builder and reuse it for connect/status/events/spawn/replace errors while preserving message text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Emit JSON for remote app first-match selection
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST app QUERY --json` / `apps QUERY --first --json` should be scriptable like list and launch. Add first-json mode for installed remote delegation and fallback shell discovery, and make local `kittwm apps --first --json` emit a structured selected-candidate or no-candidate JSON envelope.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm unknown help topic: list focused topic examples
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. Unknown `kittwm help <topic>` errors currently say only `kittwm help topics` and `kittwm quickstart` under Available topics, even though the topic list now includes focused pages like panes/input/inspect/log/completions. Add a concise example list to the unknown-topic diagnostic plus focused assertion coverage so mistyped help topics are easier to recover from.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-bar: consume chrome reservation status
description:
kittwm-bar integration gap: after native STATUS_JSON exposes chrome reservation metadata and SDK typing is in progress, kittwm-bar should display/use that metadata when connected.

Scope:
- Update `kittwm-bar` model loading to consume typed chrome reservation metadata from SDK once available; if SDK bead is not landed yet, add runtime-safe fallback only after rebasing.
- Include top_bar_rows/tilable_rows in `--json` model if available, or add a nested optional chrome field through shared BarModel.
- Keep text output concise, maybe not showing metadata unless useful.
- Add tests for JSON/model shape with chrome metadata.
- Do not change daemon/runtime session behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-bar can reflect WM chrome reservation metadata from SDK/status.
- Text output remains stable/concise.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: document render-many platform APIs
description:
Large gap: render-many APIs now exist in Rust, FFI, Python, TypeScript, and CLI, but DESIGN's ABI shape still only lists single-scene render_json and README does not mention binding-level render-many. Docs should reflect the platform contract.

Scope:
- Update DESIGN ABI shape to list `kittui_render_many_json` and summarize manifest fields.
- Update README platform binding bullet/quick docs to mention render-only single/batch APIs across TS/Python.
- Keep concise and run diff check.

Acceptance:

Add visible-row clamping/guards to terminal fallback chrome/app rendering so rows outside the screen are skipped or clamped.

title: Infer kittwm terminal size over SSH
description: Operator request slice: when kittwm runs inside SSH from a host terminal such as Ghostty or Kitty, infer terminal rows/cols and pixel/cell dimensions from the remote shell/PTY context instead of falling back to a fixed 80x24/HiDPI default. This slice focuses on local behavior when kittwm is installed on the remote. Broader pooled-ssh remote app/window/display forwarding and the reported dynamic linker failure should be tracked separately.

title: kittwm kitty tmux passthrough: build wrapper directly

title: kittwm: graphical C-a help overlay using kittui components
description: Dogfood graphics gap: C-a ? currently reads like terminal text. Render shortcut/help overlay as kittui components: panel/card, headings, chips for key chords, grouped rows, and translucent backdrop. It should reuse the shared shortcut catalog data but present as graphics in kittwm graphics mode. Acceptance: C-a ? shows kittui-rendered overlay with key chips and daily-driver hints; text fallback and SHORTCUTS_JSON remain unchanged.

title: kittwm raw footer: reuse state labels between key and text
description: The raw compositor footer loop computes workspace/split/layout/config/focus/swap/mode labels separately for the redraw key and footer text. Cache those labels once per frame before key/text construction to reduce per-frame formatting churn while preserving footer output and throttle behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: common inspection subcommand aliases
description:
Daily-driver UX source work: add easy subcommand aliases for common running-WM inspection.

Scope:
- Implement source changes (not docs-only) for easy aliases such as:
  - `kittwm status` -> existing status behavior or STATUS_JSON-friendly status output
  - `kittwm panes` -> existing `--panes`
  - `kittwm panes-json` -> existing `--panes-json`
  - `kittwm events [ms]` -> existing `--events` / `--events-ms`
- Keep existing flags working.
- Avoid overlapping `kittwm info` implementation; focus on aliases/parse dispatch.
- Add focused tests for at least three aliases.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Users can discover/run common inspections as subcommands instead of remembering flags.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.

A prior non-X11 master can prevent graphical forwarding on the launch session. Use a separate pooled ControlPath for graphical forwarding while preserving pooling for both lanes.

description: Replace the `format!("toast-text:{}", ...)` label construction in `native_toast_scene(...)` with the existing direct toast label helper. Scope only kittwm native toast text label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing toast geometry and long-message bounds.

title: kittwm: screenshot/artifact command for full graphical WM surface
description: Dogfood QA gap: need a way to capture the full composed kittwm graphical shell surface for review/goldens. Add a command/test harness to emit a PNG/scene artifact of a representative WM state including top bar, splits, borders, pane content, overlay, and translucency. Acceptance: one command or test fixture produces reviewable artifact; useful for visual regression and showcase screenshots.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm info scene: build pane labels directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: info_scene still builds each pane row label with format! from window, focus, and title fields. Add a direct preallocated pane-label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.

title: kittwm launcher overlay: avoid full command row formatting before clipping
description: LauncherOverlay::render formats each candidate row with the full command string and only then truncates to overlay width. Build candidate row text directly into a bounded width buffer so huge command/app names cannot allocate or scan more than the visible row.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add native surface coverage fields to architecture contract
description: Strengthen the typed SDK architecture contract so first-party native surface entries explicitly state whether they are SDK-backed, kitty-graphics-native, and which kittui/kittwm rendering entry point they use. This makes the architecture artifact a more useful coverage matrix without touching live session, kittwm-bar implementation, or docs/help slices.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm info graphics: respect narrow scene width
description: kittwm info graphical scene still forces a 40-column minimum via KITTWM_INFO_COLS/COLUMNS and uses fixed 10px insets that can overflow tiny widths. Make info graphics respect narrow positive widths (cap high widths at 140) and bound internal indicator/pane rects within the scene footprint.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm attach preserves daemon command arguments
description: The attach path uppercases the entire one-shot/REPL command before sending it to the daemon. That is okay for simple verbs but corrupts argument-bearing commands such as SPAWN, paths, URLs, and case-sensitive app queries. Add a helper that normalizes only the daemon verb while preserving the rest of the line, update attach help for PANES/SPAWN, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md tables mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --tables mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Advertise singular remote app aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: parser already accepts singular `app` in remote app workflows (`kittwm remote HOST app QUERY` and `kittwm remote HOST list app QUERY`), but help/completions/catalog mostly advertise plural `apps`. Make the singular form discoverable so users can naturally list or launch remote app candidates over pooled SSH.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-md exposes structured HTML placeholder metadata
description: Inline/block HTML now renders as placeholders, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose structured HTML metadata. Add HTML metadata with kind/source, include it in JSON and metadata sections, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add reset control for dragged floating kittwm panes
description: Operator-requested kittwm floating-window UX uplift: after pointer dragging or nudge shortcuts, there is no first-class way to return a floating pane to its generated default position except manually calculating inverse deltas or restoring a session. Add a reset/recenter control for one pane/focused pane, surface it through socket help, CLI and SDK helpers, and document it with floating pane controls.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Report desktop app labels in first-match JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: installed Linux remote app selection can choose `.desktop` entries, but structured first/launch JSON currently uses the desktop id as both `candidate` and `name`. Preserve the desktop id as `candidate` and report the human desktop label as `name` so scripts and users can distinguish launcher ids from display names.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm bar: reset ANSI style before clearing overlay rows
description: Top-bar ANSI overlays clear row content with ESC[K but do not reset SGR first. If the previous write left a background/attribute active, the clear can paint the stale style across the row before new chips are drawn, causing colored tails/flicker. Prefix live and standalone kittwm-bar overlay row clears with ESC[0m and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native chrome scenes: build pane border ids directly

Scope only the native shell chrome scene id in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing native shell affordance scene ids and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md reports image references in plain and rich footers
description: Markdown image placeholders are now captured as structured metadata, but kittui-md only reports links in its trailing metadata. Add image reference sections to --plain and rich status output so alt text and URLs are visible even when placeholders are off-screen. Include viewer tests for the formatter.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: configurable tile gaps and default HiDPI rendering
description: User request: add configurable pixel gaps between tiled panes and between the tiling area and header/footer, plus configurable HiDPI mode default-on because current rendering looks poor on Retina/4K displays. Implement a narrow first slice in kittwm config/native rendering: expose env/config knobs, apply safe defaults, and add tests for gap/pixel-density calculations without overlapping active chrome-position/SSH proof beads.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm focus action log: build directly
description: The raw compositor focus-action diagnostic still formats the action message with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help panes should document split alias
description: After bd-efe4f9 added the kittwm split alias and SPLIT_PANE socket verb, top-level help/docs mention split, but the kittwm help panes topic still jumps from --spawn-pty to focus/close/layout and its socket-equivalents sentence omits SPLIT_PANE. Add the split alias and socket verb to the panes help topic so users looking at pane lifecycle help can discover directed split launch.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: wait for page readiness before first capture
description: Browser surface UX follow-up: `HeadlessBrowserApp::launch` creates a Chrome target, enables Page, sets metrics, and immediately returns. The live kittwm loop can capture before the page has reached `interactive` or `complete`, producing an initial blank/white browser pane and making the browser look broken. Add a short bounded document readiness wait during launch using DevTools `Runtime.evaluate(document.readyState)`, keep it non-fatal/bounded, and add focused parsing/decision coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native terminal resize log: build directly
description: The native terminal size-change diagnostic still formats the resize line with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact cols/rows/panes/resize_failures text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add NativePaneDetail test fixture builder
description: Context: while validating bd-ab0fe3, cargo test -p kittui-cli shortcuts failed in kittwm_terminal.rs because a test fixture constructed kittwm_sdk::NativePaneDetail exhaustively and had not been updated for newer stack/floating/title-drag fields. The immediate fixture was patched in the bd-ab0fe3 change, but this shape will recur as NativePaneDetail grows. Consider adding a small test fixture/builder helper for NativePaneDetail defaults in CLI/SDK tests so future metadata additions do not break unrelated terminal/status tests. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-ab0fe3 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4
spoken_name: add native pane detail test fixture

title: kittwm SDK: reject blank rename titles locally
description: Follow-up to CLI rename validation: SDK `SurfaceHandle::rename` still formats raw titles into `RENAME_PANE` commands. Blank/whitespace-only titles are rejected only after daemon socket I/O, and padded valid titles are sent unnormalized. Trim valid titles, reject blank titles locally, and preserve interior spaces; add focused SDK control-helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: use loaded document title for pane metadata
description: Browser surface UX issue: `HeadlessBrowserApp` initializes its title from the requested URL and never adopts `document.title` after the page loads. Native pane chrome/status therefore shows raw URLs even when the page has a useful title, making browser panes harder to identify. After the bounded readiness wait, read `document.title || location.href` via DevTools and store it when non-empty; keep the URL fallback on failure. Add focused parser coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: forward PageUp and PageDown input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards arrows, Tab, Enter, Backspace, and Delete, while Home/End are active lead work. PageUp/PageDown CSI 5~/6~ are still ignored, so scrolling/navigation in pages is broken. Add browser PageUp/PageDown key forwarding while preserving ordered text/key parsing and avoiding overlap with Home/End work.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: preserve dirty skipped-frame telemetry
description: Operator loop: continue finding kittwm flicker/performance issues. Follow-up review found the clean-frame event throttle now suppresses RGBA frames whenever no upload or placement is written, but DirtyGrid may intentionally skip a low-change upload while still reporting changed_tiles/skipped_upload. Those skipped-dirty events are useful for tuning flicker/performance thresholds and are not clean no-ops. Scope: distinguish truly clean static frames from skipped-dirty frames, publish events when dirty metrics show changes even if upload is skipped, and add targeted decision coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: prioritize custom workspace in narrow graphical overlay
description: Kittwm burn-down follow-up: live graphical top-bar overlay clips labels to avoid wrapping, but it iterates default workspace chips 1/2/3 before an appended custom active workspace. On narrow terminals the active custom workspace can still be omitted before it is considered. Scope: when overlay labels do not all fit, prioritize the active custom workspace chip before inactive numeric chips, matching the text top-bar behavior; add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-cache tests flake when run in parallel
description: cargo test -p kittui-cache --lib intermittently fails 2/8 when run alongside other crate tests, but passes deterministically with --test-threads=1 or in isolation. Likely temp-dir race in still_round_trip / animation_round_trip. File deterministic test fixtures with unique temp dirs per test.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add pluses-delimited metadata block regression coverage
description: Add targeted regression coverage that kittui-affordances/kittui-md preserve +++ pluses-delimited Markdown metadata blocks, not just YAML frontmatter. Keep changes focused to tests/docs if production code already handles it.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show moved marker in floating kittwm pane titles
description: Operator-requested kittwm visual-state polish: floating panes can be dragged/nudged and expose floating_moved in status/SDK, but the live title chrome does not visually indicate that a pane is offset from its generated floating position. Add a compact moved marker to floating pane title rows, keep alignment predictable, and document/test the title rendering.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm SDK session tests: build socket path directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("/tmp/kwsession-{}-{}.sock", ...)` in `session_helpers_send_expected_socket_commands` with the existing direct socket-path helper. Scope only kittwm SDK session-helper test socket path construction in `crates/kittwm-sdk/src/lib.rs`; preserve SESSION_JSON/RESTORE_SESSION_JSON socket command coverage.

title: Add kittui-md math-json inspection mode
description: Add --math-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown math expressions, emitting schema_version plus indexed kind/source records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm runtime swap actions: Ctrl-A hjkl visible swap direction state
description: Wire keymap swap actions into live kitwm runtime state. Ctrl-A h/j/k/l should update a visible swap direction marker in the footer and log swap.left/down/up/right transitions. This implements the operator-requested Ctrl-A l swap-right family as visible behaviour before real tile reordering lands. Parent: bd-031e54; follows bd-c7a249.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Shift-Tab input to pages
description: kittwm-browser forwards Tab but ignores the common terminal Shift-Tab sequence CSI Z, so users cannot move focus backward through browser form fields. Parse ESC [ Z and dispatch Tab with the Shift modifier via CDP.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon unknown-command reply: build directly
description: Standalone daemon unknown-command replies still use format! for ERR unknown: <cmd>. Build that reply directly while preserving daemon request/help coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-bar: size scenes from terminal width on resize
description: Resize follow-up from Harry's host-terminal resize report: `kittwm-bar` scene/kitty rendering uses `KITTWM_BAR_COLS` or static `COLUMNS`, then falls back to 80. In a hosted/resized PTY, the environment may be stale even though terminal ioctl knows the new canvas width. Make bar scene width fall back to `TerminalInfo::detect().cols` before 80 so rerendered bar/chrome uses the resized canvas without changing DPI/per-app resolution assumptions; add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm launcher candidate rows: build prefix directly
description: Replace `format!("{marker} {:>2}. [{:<5}] ", row + 1, candidate.kind_name())` in `launcher_candidate_row_text(...)` with direct bounded-prefix construction. Scope only launcher candidate row prefix construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing visible row behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm apps JSON: write default path directly
description: Small kittwm CLI performance cleanup: `kittwm apps --json` still allocates a formatted String for default_resolved via Option::map(format!(...)). Build the JSON response directly with fmt::Write, writing either the quoted path or null without the intermediate allocation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm PANES cursor label should build directly
description: `native_pane_cursor_label` renders positioned cursors with `format!("{col},{row}")` for each PANES row. Build the label directly into a small String while preserving `-` for missing cursor coordinates. Scope: daemon PANES text cursor labels only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm footer: reset ANSI style before clearing rows
description: Several kittwm footer/raw compositor row clears still emit cursor move + ESC[K without first resetting SGR. If prior chips/overlays leave a background or reverse-video style active, the cleared row tail can flash with stale styling. Reset SGR before footer/raw-compositor row clears, matching the top-bar overlay fix.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: remap DOM roles to first-class semantic roles
description:
Follow-up after SDK added first-class semantic roles: browser DOM/ARIA adapter still maps links/pixel regions/etc. to `Custom("browser.*")` in some cases. Remap obvious cases to first-class SDK roles where available.

Scope:
- Update browser semantic role mapping to use `ComponentRole::Link` for link roles and `Canvas` for pixel/canvas/video-ish opaque regions where appropriate.
- Update tests that currently expect `Custom("browser.link")`.
- Preserve custom roles for menu_item/grid or other cases without first-class variants if needed.
- Do not change screenshot rendering or publishing behavior.

Acceptance:
- Browser semantic snapshots use new first-class SDK roles for links and canvas/pixel regions where appropriate.
- Targeted browser semantic tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm launcher scene: build selected label directly
description: Small kittwm launcher scene performance cleanup: launcher_scene builds selected candidate label with Option::map(format!(kind:truncated-name)). Build selected labels directly into one bounded String to avoid the temporary format allocation while preserving scene labels.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native failure logs: build spawn line directly
description: native_spawn_failure_log_line uses whole-line format! for native pane spawn diagnostics. Build the line directly with a preallocated buffer while preserving bounded command/error text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: cap frame-budget sleep chunks for responsiveness
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported slow/flickery: the raw compositor frame loop says it sleeps out the frame budget in chunks no longer than about 16ms to preserve Ctrl-C responsiveness, but the code calls `std::thread::sleep(slack)` with the full remaining slack. At lower FPS or long frame budgets this can make input/quit feel laggy. Fix by capping each sleep chunk to 16ms and add a deterministic helper test.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: reset semantic backoff on activity
description: Audit finding: browser semantic snapshot backoff now reduces static-page CDP churn, but user input/resize does not reset the semantic publisher's idle state. After a page sits static, typing/clickless key input can wait for the idle semantic interval before publishing updated DOM/ARIA state. Reset semantic snapshot pacing on user activity so interactive edits/focus changes publish promptly.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md refs alias for references
description: Add --refs as a concise alias for kittui-md --references. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm doctor kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm doctor diagnostics available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add doctor-scene-json and doctor-kitty/doctor-graphics commands that render transport readiness as a labelled kittui scene, preserving existing doctor text/json behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm doctor-scene-json emits a kittui Scene with labelled diagnostic layers.
- kittwm doctor-kitty/doctor-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Tests cover scene labels without probing kitty or requiring a live kittwm.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm events kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make top-level kittwm event stream inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add events-scene-json and events-kitty/events-graphics commands that render bounded EVENTS JSONL batches as a labelled kittui Scene, preserving existing events text behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm events-scene-json [MS] emits a kittui Scene summarizing event count/kinds.
- kittwm events-kitty/events-graphics [MS] places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing events / --events / --events-ms behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from synthetic event lines.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal SSH ControlPath arg: build directly
description: The kittwm-terminal remote SSH command builder still formats ControlPath with format!. Build that option string directly while preserving the pooled SSH command.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: clear old raw placeholders when window footprint moves
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: after bd-2abaf4, unchanged raw compositor frames whose footprint moves use `place_uploaded_image_with_options` without deleting the old placement first. The previous always-upload path explicitly unplaced moved image ids to clear stale Unicode placeholder cells. Restore that invariant by forcing a raw upload when the footprint changes and unplacing the old id before placing at the new footprint; keep unchanged/no-move frames skipped. Update deterministic raw-frame write decision coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm docs: document expanded SEND_KEY names
description: Documentation drift: after adding Shift-Tab/function key support and updating CLI/socket help, `docs/wm.md` still describes `SEND_KEY` only with examples like `ctrl-c`, `escape`, arrows, and paging keys. Update the long-form WM guide/quickstart examples to mention Shift-Tab/backtab, page-up/down spellings, and F5-F12 so documentation matches the actual accepted key vocabulary.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: bindings: add primitive image scene helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: Python/TypeScript helpers now cover solid and gradient primitives, but embedded image scenes still require hand-authored `Node::Image` JSON. Images are a core renderer use case for shell scripts and platform previews.

Scope:
- Add Python `scene.image_layer(...)` and `scene.image_box(...)` helpers.
- Add TypeScript `scene.imageLayer(...)` and `scene.imageBox(...)` helpers.
- Support path sources and byte-array sources in JSON-compatible shape.
- Include fit and optional tint.
- Add binding tests and README examples.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS users can build valid image Scene JSON without hand-authoring schema.
- Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon status test socket path: build directly
description: The daemon status test still formats its per-process socket filename with format!. Reuse the direct test_socket_filename helper for that temp path while preserving status command coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal: bound status and events scene labels
description: Audit finding: kittwm-terminal graphical status/events scenes bound geometry but still serialize unbounded status/layout/detail/event-kind strings into scene labels. Pathological daemon data can inflate kitty/JSON payloads and redraw allocation churn. Bound diagnostic scene labels while preserving displayed/semantic intent.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-kitty: add raw RGB medium upload helper
description:
Additive kitty transport follow-up: raw RGB (`f=24`) direct uploads exist, but file/temp/shared-memory medium helpers remain documented as only future-if-needed. Add a small medium helper mirroring raw RGBA so callers that already own/write RGB files or shm can emit `f=24` with `t=f|t=t|t=s`.

Scope:
- Add `upload_still_rgb_medium` mirroring `upload_still_rgba_medium` for `UploadMedium`.
- Emit `f=24,s=W,v=H,t=<medium>` for file/temp/shared-memory and reuse direct RGB helper for direct bytes.
- Add exact grammar tests for temp file and shared-memory names.
- Do not change renderer/kittwm defaults.

Acceptance:
- kittui-kitty supports additive raw RGB medium grammar.
- Existing RGB/RGBA/PNG tests pass.
- `cargo test -p kittui-kitty --lib` and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native pane window id: build directly
description: spawn_native_pane formats native-{id} for every new native pane. Add a direct builder helper for native pane window ids while preserving DISPLAY/KITTWM_WINDOW behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md rich tables align cell text from Markdown markers
description: Use the preserved MarkdownTable alignment metadata in kittui-md rich table rendering so left/center/right aligned columns position their cell text within the table glyph cells. Add pure tests for alignment formatting and keep metadata JSON behavior unchanged.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: fix nix kittui-cli sandbox test failures
description:
Nix build regression: kittui-cli lib tests fail in sandbox.

Failures reported:
- daemon::tests::native_spawn_queue_reports_live_pane_status
- daemon::tests::native_spawn_queue_wait_text_does_not_block_ping
- session::native_pane_tests::native_pane_statuses_include_dirty_frame_metrics

Symptoms:
- WAIT_TEXT_MS timeout in nonblocking ping test.
- PTY child program `true` not found in sandbox test.

Scope:
- Make tests deterministic and sandbox-safe without weakening runtime behavior.
- Prefer direct PTY test program resolution via env/PATH fallback or test helper already used elsewhere.
- Avoid broad churn.
- Validate targeted tests and, if feasible, nix/cargo relevant build.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK helpers for focused fullscreen pane state
description: SDK/design-cleanliness follow-up after fullscreen title marker: clients can test is_fullscreen_layout and focused_pane separately, but there is no focused/fullscreen convenience helper parallel to focused_is_resized/moved/topmost/title-draggable. Add focused_is_fullscreen and fullscreen_pane helpers on PanesStatus and Status so automation can directly detect the pane represented by fullscreen title state. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add kittui-md interactive position footer
description: Add an in-pager position footer for kittui-md --interactive showing source path, current offset, maximum offset, viewport rows, and total rows. Keep reload status messages and add targeted tests for footer rendering.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: respect narrow status scene width
description: Kittwm burn-down finding:  /  uses the generic info scene width helper with a 40-column minimum. On narrower terminals the status graphics scene can exceed the row and its row rects assume a fixed 10-cell margin. Scope: give status scene width narrow-aware handling, bound row rects inside the scene, and add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-renderer: bound graphical footer status geometry
description: Audit finding: native_footer_status_scene forces a minimum 20-column footprint and still creates status chips whose x offset can exceed the scene width on tiny terminals. This can overflow graphical footer chrome and contribute to wrapping/flicker. Bound footer scene width to available columns and skip/clamp chips outside the scene footprint.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: honor terminal insert mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm implements insert-character CSI (`CSI n @`) but not terminal insert/replace mode (`CSI 4 h/l`, IRM). Editors and line-oriented TUIs can enable insert mode so printable output shifts existing cells right instead of overwriting them; without it, native pane rendering and READ_TEXT snapshots diverge from real terminals.

Scope:
- Track terminal insert mode in native `TerminalState`, default false.
- Handle `CSI 4 h` / `CSI 4 l` for set/reset insert mode (non-private mode).
- When insert mode is enabled, printable characters should insert one cell at the cursor before drawing, shifting row contents right and dropping the last cell.
- Reset insert mode during terminal resets; preserve it across resize like other modes.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test proves default replace behavior overwrites at cursor.
- Test proves insert mode shifts row contents right before drawing.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm commands: list concrete help topic examples
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm commands` lists the generic `help <topic>` command, but the human catalog does not show concrete discovery examples such as `help topics` or `help completions` even though recent UX work made those paths important. Add concise concrete help-topic entries to the local command catalog plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: trim workspace in top-bar scene labels
description: Follow-up workspace-label burn-down finding: `BarModel::scene_with_prefix` uses `self.workspace` directly in the root background diagnostic label, while workspace chips and text rendering compare against `self.workspace.trim()`. If a model is built from unnormalized status/config input, scene labels can still contain whitespace even though the visible active chip is trimmed. Use the normalized trimmed workspace for scene diagnostic labels and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Show focused pane title in kittwm footer state
description: Operator-requested kittwm UX polish slice: the footer now shows the focused pane/window id, but users still have to map native ids to human pane titles. Include a compact focused pane title alongside the id in the high-priority footer state segment, bounded so narrow terminals still keep mode first.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm launcher F12 failure log: build directly
description: The raw compositor F12 launcher failure diagnostic still formats errors with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native socket focus-next log: build directly
description: The native socket FocusNext path formats its debug diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: avoid hiding graphical top-bar clock with double gap
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: graphical `BarModel::scene_with_prefix` advances `chip_x` by `chip_w + 3.0` after each workspace chip, then passes that next-chip cursor to `top_bar_clock_chip_x`, which adds another 4px gap before deciding whether the clock fits. This double-counts spacing and can hide the clock at widths where the last chip plus intended gap plus clock would fit. Track the actual drawn chip end when placing the clock; add focused scene coverage for the marginal width.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm apps graphics: bound row rects on narrow scenes
description: kittwm apps graphics inherit narrow-aware scene widths, but path/app rows still use fixed 10px margins and width-20 sizing. On tiny widths row rects can exceed the footprint. Reuse bounded row geometry for app candidate rows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add SurfaceSpec native readiness helpers
description: Add convenience methods on `SurfaceSpec` so SDK apps can ask whether a typed terminal/browser surface request is backed by the current first-party SDK + kitty-graphics-native contract without manually constructing `ArchitectureContract`. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser, bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md mode-info discovery
description: Add --mode-info <name> and --mode-info-json <name> no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can inspect one mode's canonical flag, aliases, description, and JSON top-level keys when applicable. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native shell view: skip text snapshots for graphical chrome
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: native_shell_view always calls pane.app.text_snapshot() for every pane, even in the normal graphical renderer path where NativePaneChrome.text_snapshot is only used by the pure terminal renderer. Large terminal grids make this an avoidable per-frame allocation/copy before chrome redraw checks. Scope: make text snapshot collection explicit and skip it for graphical chrome, preserving pure terminal fallback behavior with focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser semantic publisher: store payload fingerprint instead of JSON
description: Kittwm burn-down follow-up: BrowserSemanticPublisher still retains the previous full semantic JSON string only to detect unchanged payloads. Semantic JSON can be large, and storing the full string adds idle-loop memory churn. Scope: replace last_payload with a compact (len, hash) fingerprint, preserve unchanged-payload debounce semantics, and update focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: adapt Xvfb and XQuartz backends to common Surface trait
description: Expose Xvfb/XQuartz app capture/input/resize as common kittwm Surface implementations so GUI apps can be launched and composed through the same handle model.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm toggle action log: build directly
description: The raw compositor toggle-action diagnostics still format action messages with format!. Build those log lines directly while preserving exact output for float/fullscreen toggles.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm CLI: reject blank pane rename titles
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: the daemon rejects `RENAME_PANE` when the title is blank after trimming, but the public CLI `rename_pane_request` formats the raw title and lets whitespace-only titles reach the daemon. This produces a slower socket round-trip and a less direct error than the other CLI validators. Trim the title in the CLI helper, reject blank titles before sending, and preserve interior spaces for valid titles; add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: JSON wait match socket and CLI wrappers
description:
Automation JSON surface gap: WAIT_TEXT/WAIT_OUTPUT return text `MATCH_*` lines while other read surfaces have JSON forms.

Scope:
- Add socket commands `WAIT_TEXT_JSON`, `WAIT_TEXT_JSON_MS`, `WAIT_OUTPUT_JSON`, and `WAIT_OUTPUT_JSON_MS` returning JSON on success with kind/window/bytes.
- Preserve existing text `WAIT_TEXT*` / `WAIT_OUTPUT*` behavior unchanged.
- Add CLI wrappers `--wait-text-json`, `--wait-text-json-ms`, `--wait-output-json`, and `--wait-output-json-ms`.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON/error help entries and focused tests.
- No SDK changes in this bead.

Acceptance:
- JSON wait commands return parseable JSON for matching text/output.
- Existing wait commands still return existing text form.
- Targeted kittui-cli tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK helpers for active title drag kind
description: SDK follow-up: active_title_drag_pane() exposes the target pane, but consumers still have to chain into title_drag_reorders_pane()/title_drag_repositions_pane() to learn whether the active drag is a tiled reorder or floating reposition. Add direct active_title_drag_reorders_pane()/active_title_drag_repositions_pane() helpers on Status/PanesStatus. Filed from ongoing kittwm SDK/drag UX audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-wm: add NativeSurface exact-byte input hook
description:
Surface runtime gap: `NativeSurface` has text input but no common exact-byte input hook, even though PTY surfaces and kittwm automation support exact bytes.

Scope:
- Add `send_surface_bytes(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<()>` to `NativeSurface`.
- Default implementation should accept UTF-8 by delegating to `send_surface_text` and reject non-UTF8 bytes explicitly.
- Override for `PtyTerminalApp` to preserve exact bytes through `TerminalSurface::send_bytes`.
- Capture-only adapters remain unsupported through the default text path.
- Add focused tests for PTY byte send and default non-UTF8 rejection/capture-only unsupported behavior.
- Do not change daemon/socket behavior.

Acceptance:
- NativeSurface abstraction can represent exact-byte input where supported.
- PTY preserves raw bytes; other adapters fail explicitly as appropriate.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal status scene label: build directly
description: terminal_status_scene_for_cols formats the status text layer label after bounding focus/layout/detail fields. Build that label directly into one preallocated String while preserving bounded label text and scene output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK semantic roles and event iterator status
description:
Docs follow-up after documented SDK semantic roles and bounded event iterator helper landed. Document new ComponentRole variants and `KittwmEventIter` / `events_iter_ms` / `event_iter_ms`.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-semantic-surfaces.md and docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md or docs/README.md.
- Mention adapter remapping to new roles is a follow-up where current adapters still emit Custom.
- Mention event iterator reuses existing bounded `events_ms` call and preserves daemon behavior.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect new SDK semantic role vocabulary and event iterator helper.
- `git diff --check` passes.
