title: kittwm top bar: build workspace chips without collect concat
description: BarModel::workspace_chips_text_from_labels maps every workspace label to a temporary formatted String, collects into a String, then concatenates the trailing separator. Build chips directly into one preallocated String to reduce top-bar redraw allocations while preserving active/inactive chip text and label bounds.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: mark active workspace in text top bar
description: Kittwm burn-down follow-up: BarModel::workspace_chips_text renders active and inactive workspaces identically (for example all chips look like '| 1 | 2 | 3 |'). In pure-terminal fallback or text diagnostics there is no visible active workspace indication, unlike the graphical chip scene. Scope: render the active workspace distinctly while preserving compact one-line output and update top-bar tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Show tables and metadata blocks in kittui-md rich status
description: Update the kittui-md rich/interative status line so it reports table and metadata-block counts alongside the existing component/link/image/footnote/math/html/code counts. Add targeted tests and keep changes narrow.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: plan adaptive graphics transport selection
description:
Performance architecture gap: kittui/kittwm currently choose between direct escape streaming / tmux passthrough / file-ish transport mostly from terminal probing and caller configuration. For high-rate WM surfaces we need an adaptive transport policy that chooses the fastest safe mode: direct stream for remote/ssh, file/tempfile or shared-memory for local terminals that support it, zlib for large raw frames when beneficial, and a tmux-safe pure terminal fallback unless explicitly overridden.

Scope:
- Add a durable design note or implementation plan for transport policy selection across kittui and kittwm.
- Define inputs: terminal capabilities, local vs SSH, tmux, kitty support, file/shm availability, payload size/frame rate, compression threshold.
- Define explicit env/config overrides and safe defaults.
- File/describe implementation follow-ups for shared-memory/file transport in raw-frame hot paths and optional compression thresholds.

Acceptance:
- In-repo docs capture automatic transport selection strategy and relation to zlib/raw-RGBA/tmux safeguards.
- Follow-up beads or TODO mapping exists for implementing shared-memory/local transport and compression thresholds.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-python: add package metadata and module entrypoint
description:
Large gap: the Python binding exists, but it is only importable by manually setting `PYTHONPATH`. Python platform users need minimal packaging metadata and a module entry point to discover the ABI/library path and smoke-test configuration.

Scope:
- Add `bindings/python/pyproject.toml` with package metadata for the stdlib ctypes binding.
- Add `python -m kittui` CLI entry point that prints ABI/probe JSON or library discovery information.
- Add unittest coverage for the module helper using the fake library path-discovery path where practical.
- Update README with packaging/entry-point examples.

Acceptance:
- `PYTHONPATH=bindings/python python3 -m kittui --help` works.
- `PYTHONPATH=bindings/python python3 -m unittest discover bindings/python` passes.
- No compiled shared library is required for tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: report live native panes over session socket
description:
Large gap: no-arg native `kittwm` now owns the live socket, but `STATUS`/`PANES` on that socket only report queue metadata, not the actual visible native panes. This makes the DISPLAY/socket model hard to inspect from shell scripts.

Scope:
- Extend the native session socket queue with a lightweight live pane status snapshot.
- Update the snapshot from the native session loop after split/close/socket spawn/resize/reap changes.
- Add `STATUS` and `PANES` replies that report visible native pane count and focused pane token.
- Add unit tests for status/panes formatting.

Acceptance:
- A live native session socket `STATUS` includes pending count, pane count, and focused window.
- `PANES` returns visible native pane records.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-events: bound graphical scene labels
description: Audit finding: `kittwm events-kitty` / events scene JSON bounds geometry and row count, but serializes raw event kind strings into heading and row labels. Pathological event kind values can inflate scene JSON/kitty payloads and redraw allocation churn. Clip events scene label fields while preserving useful diagnostics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add native pane resize weights
description:
Large gap: native kittwm pane layouts are always equal splits. A terminal WM needs basic pane resizing/weights so users and socket clients can give more space to a focused terminal without changing pane order or axis.

Scope:
- Add per-native-pane layout weights with default weight 1.
- Add socket command `RESIZE_PANE <window|focused> <grow|shrink|+N|-N>` queued into the native PTY loop.
- Make native pane layout allocation use weights for both columns and rows, with sane minimum/title-row constraints.
- Include weight in native pane status JSON/text and HELP/HELP_JSON.
- Add parser and weighted layout tests.

Acceptance:
- Socket resize command updates a pane weight and recomputes layout.
- Weighted native layout allocates more columns/rows to heavier panes.
- Targeted daemon/session tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm doctor scene: build readiness label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: doctor_scene still builds the doctor readiness label with format! from readiness, tmux/remote booleans, display count, and log state. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm launcher: avoid lowercasing huge query each render
description: Launcher candidate filtering now avoids lowercasing every candidate, but it still lowercases the full query on each render/selection. A pasted huge query can allocate and scan on every overlay frame. Make launcher_match_score compare ASCII case-insensitively against the original query to remove the per-render query allocation while preserving exact/prefix/contains ordering.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: add semantic scene/kitty output
description: Add kitty/kittui-native semantic inspection outputs to kittwm-browser: render a browser semantic snapshot through the existing kittui-wm semantic renderer as scene JSON or kitty graphics. Scope to kittwm_browser.rs only; avoid kittwm-terminal/launch/bar/session/ArchitectureContract areas currently handled by kittui-dev/dev-2.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm SDK events tests: build socket path directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("/tmp/kwe-{}-{}.sock", ...)` in `events_ms_parses_json_lines_until_end` with the existing direct socket-path helper. Scope only kittwm SDK events test socket path construction in `crates/kittwm-sdk/src/lib.rs`; preserve EVENTS socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm chrome: avoid joining scene labels for redraw keys
description: native_shell_chrome_scene_key joins every scene layer label into a large string even though the visual scene hash already includes labels and paint. This adds allocation/churn on every chrome redraw check. Remove the redundant label join while preserving placement/identity/visual-change invalidation.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add SDK-backed kittwm-top dual-mode process viewer
description: Operator request: add a `kittwm-top` command that shows rich output for current/default kittwm session owned processes. Add process introspection functionality to the SDK and implement the command in terms of that SDK surface. Outside a kittwm session it should be a regular terminal app; inside a kittwm session it should render natively as a first-party kittwm surface application, but not as a hardcoded special case in the WM -- all through SDK/app surfaces. Use the work to identify SDK dual-mode gaps; file and fix follow-up beads as needed until this works fully inside and outside kittwm.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-native: reset idle pacing on input
description: Audit finding: native kittwm idle pacing only resets when a frame emits output. If user input is read while the compositor is already on idle cadence but does not immediately change pixels (or is forwarded to an app before it redraws), the next frame can stay on the slower idle target. Reset idle pacing on input activity in both native renderer loops to keep typing/focus interactions responsive.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm raw footer cache key: build directly
description: The raw compositor footer cache key still uses format! across many fields. Build the cache key directly while preserving exact output and bounded note behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Complete kitty graphics protocol conformance + ratakittui coverage
description: Operator-confirmed gap: kittui was supposed to be a careful extraction of the Cacophony TUI graphics lib against https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/ but the showcase reproduces ENOENT image-not-found responses in Ghostty and produces only placeholder character grids. Audit shows multiple spec deviations: (1) unicode placeholders emit bare U+10EEEE with foreground color only, missing the (row, column, msb-of-image-id) combining diacritics that the spec requires for cell-to-pixel anchoring; (2) the q= quiet field is never set so kitty prints diagnostic responses inline; (3) declared Transport::File and Transport::Memory never adjust t= and still base64-stream via a=t; (4) animation upload uses a=t with r= instead of a=t for first frame then a=f for subsequent frames and the animation control verb shape is wrong; (5) placement command lacks p= placement-id support and X=/Y= subcell offset support; (6) tests assert substrings of the current malformed output instead of the protocol grammar. This epic tracks getting kittui to a verified spec-conformant baseline plus full ratakittui widget coverage proof. Child beads will land incrementally with grammar-pinned tests and a kittui proof CLI mode the operator can paste into Ghostty/kitty for visual confirmation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK: validate send-key tokens locally
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: SDK `SurfaceHandle::send_key` formats raw key strings into `SEND_KEY` commands. Blank or whitespace-containing key names are rejected only after daemon socket I/O, and padded valid keys are sent unnormalized. Add SDK-side token validation for send-key: trim valid keys, reject blank/multi-word keys locally, and preserve existing daemon vocabulary validation for unsupported single tokens. Add focused socket-command and local-rejection coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Validate core features against spec via Ghostty integration tests
description:
Execute integration tests using the Ghostty terminal harness to validate that implemented core features match the specification. Run real-world use cases by driving Ghostty.app and capturing screenshots for analysis.

Acceptance criteria:
- All core features tested via real Ghostty terminal usage
- Screenshots captured for each test scenario
- Test results documented and analyzed
- Integration tests pass against specification requirements
creator: helsinki:cacophony:harry

title: kittwm layout result status: build directly
description: The layout action result status still formats window-count and error suffixes with format!. Build those status strings directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: pure-terminal redraw skip must flush host sequences
description: Follow-up from bd-bbdd86: the pure-terminal redraw guard skips writing the frame batch when rendered text is unchanged, but the batch may already contain host escape sequences from `pane.app.take_host_sequences()`. That can drop terminal host sequences in fallback mode. Fix by making the write decision account for pre-render pending batch bytes, so host sequences always flush even when the shell text is unchanged; add a deterministic test for that decision.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Mention Wayland diagnostics in remote app display errors
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: Linux desktop fallback launch now detects missing DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY and suggests `kittwm remote HOST x11`. Since graphical doctor also reports waypipe availability, update the missing-display error to point users at the broader graphical/forwarding doctor path and mention X11/Wayland forwarding context.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: bound status row text to terminal width
description: kittwm-browser status text includes URL/window/socket and is written without clipping, so long socket paths or narrow terminals can wrap/corrupt adjacent rows. Clip status text to the viewport width before the SGR-reset row clear/write.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: saturate native help overlay dimensions
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: native graphical help overlay computes max line width and panel rows via lossy u16 casts. Extremely long help lines or pathological help lists can overflow/truncate before bounding, risking malformed overlay geometry. Scope: use saturated dimension accounting and add focused long-line/list coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: do not change focus on mouse wheel scroll
description: Bug-hunt finding while improving kittwm usability: `native_mouse_event_name_and_position` marks scroll-up and scroll-down as `should_focus = true`, so merely scrolling over an unfocused pane steals focus. This is surprising in multi-pane terminal workflows and can make keyboard input go to the wrong pane after a scroll. Keep click-to-focus for button presses, but route wheel events to the pane under the pointer without changing focus; add deterministic coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm XWindow key-release context: build directly
description: Replace `format!("release keysym {sym} for X window {:?}", self.window.id)` in `crates/kittui-wm/src/native.rs` with a direct preallocated helper matching the existing press context helper. Scope only XWindowSurface key-release context labels; preserve exact text.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native socket browser-key log: build directly
description: The native socket SendBytes browser-key success path formats its diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact window/key text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show focused pane id in kittwm footer state
description: Operator-requested kittwm UX polish slice: after adding mode and pane count to the high-priority footer state segment, include the focused pane/window id as `focus:<window>` so users can identify focus at a glance even when title chrome is partially clipped. Keep mode first, pane count second, and preserve narrow-terminal truncation behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm kitty upload payloads: build body wrapper directly
description: Replace formatted kitty graphics payload-with-body construction (`{ESC}_G{header};{body}{ESC}\\`) with a direct preallocated helper in `crates/kittui-kitty/src/lib.rs`. Scope only common upload payload wrapping for existing headers/bodies; preserve exact upload grammar tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-wm native app: nested PTY terminal panes
description: Operator direction: support a native terminal app independent of X/Quartz. Implement a nested PTY backend for kittui-wm so terminal sessions can be resized, moved, composed as windows, and receive keyboard input, allowing kittwm to act as a tmux-like multiplexer as well as a graphical compositor. Acceptance: launch shell in a PTY, render terminal contents into kittui frames, resize with pane geometry, route typed input, and prove with an echo hello round trip. Parent epic: bd-031e54; directly helps bd-a9ec5b when xterm/XQuartz are unavailable. Filed from bd-a9ec5b session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm bar: graphical overlay should preserve live HH:MM clock
description: native_top_bar_model_from_view only recognizes text ending in `UTC`, but native_top_bar_text/render_i3bar strips ` UTC`, so graphical top-bar scene/ANSI overlay can fall back to 00:00 instead of the live clock. Parse a trailing HH:MM token as UTC for graphical scene/overlay model reconstruction and add coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support config-selectable libghostty terminal app
description: Operator request: now that the headless Ghostty/libghostty utility exists for capturing kittwm, expose it as a launchable kittwm terminal app and make the default terminal command configurable. Desired behavior: dev-2 is working on config; once a terminal binary is configured away from the current kittwm-term/default shell path, Ctrl-A t / Ctrl-A Enter should launch that configured binary as the native pane app. This should let kittwm run the libghostty-backed terminal surface while preserving kittwm chrome/layout like any other window. Additional operator note: decide a default DPI/cell metric for virtual apps and surfaces because current defaults are inconsistent; this should keep apps from different surfaces/backends consistently scaled when mixing native PTY/libghostty/X11/Quartz panes. Scope: inspect existing kittwm terminal launch/config paths, add/adjust terminal app binary selection hook as needed, document the expected config key or env fallback, add shared default virtual cell metric helpers/constants, and add targeted tests for launch command selection/scaling without requiring a real Ghostty GUI. Filed from Harry's 2026-05-26 request.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native socket restore log: build directly
description: The native socket RestoreSession success path formats its debug diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving panes/focus text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: capability profile presets
description:
SDK capability ergonomics gap: `ClientCapabilities::restricted()` only allows `ReadText`, even though many low-risk inspection helpers now exist (status/panes/help/app catalog/session reads/events/semantic reads). Add clearer preset builders so SDK apps can choose local scopes without hand-assembling capability lists.

Scope:
- Add additive `ClientCapabilities` presets such as `none()`, `inspect_only()`, and `automation()` or similarly named helpers.
- Keep `restricted()` behavior compatible or delegate to an appropriate documented preset only if clearly safe.
- Add `allowed()`/`iter()` style accessor if useful for tests/docs.
- Update tests for preset contents and denials.
- Do not change daemon/runtime enforcement.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients have clearer local capability profiles.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Avoid allocation in SDK title drag kind checks
description: SDK quality/performance follow-up: NativePaneDetail::title_drag_reorders_pane() and title_drag_repositions_pane() currently route through parsed_title_drag_kind(), which allocates an Unknown(String) for unrecognized kinds just to return false. Compare raw title_drag_kind strings allocation-free for the known reorder/reposition checks, while preserving parsed_title_drag_kind() for callers that need typed unknowns. Filed from ongoing kittwm SDK/design cleanliness audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4
spoken_name: avoid allocation in sdk title drag

title: Align tiled title drag metadata with reorder marker
description: Follow-up after adding the visible tiled reorder marker: tiled `title_drag_col` still points at the first title cell (`x+1`), but the visible `⇄` reorder handle is rendered after the focus marker. Align tiled title-drag metadata with the visible marker cell (`x+2` when width permits, clamped for tiny titles) so SDK/automation drags the advertised handle. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm SDK spawn-pty request: build directly
description: Kittwm::spawn_surface still formats SPAWN_PTY command request strings. Build the request directly into a preallocated buffer while preserving native command construction and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm chrome inspection kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make CHROME_JSON inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add chrome-scene-json and chrome-kitty/chrome-graphics commands/flags that render chrome workspace/reservation metadata as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing --chrome-json behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm chrome-scene-json / --chrome-scene-json emits a kittui Scene summarizing workspace, reservation bands/gaps, owner, and tilable rows from CHROME_JSON.
- kittwm chrome-kitty/chrome-graphics / --chrome-kitty places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing --chrome-json behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from synthetic CHROME_JSON.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: forward OSC 52 clipboard writes from native panes
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm consumes inner PTY OSC sequences while rendering panes itself, so OSC 52 clipboard requests from apps do not reach the host terminal clipboard. A terminal WM should mediate clipboard OSC safely and transparently: inner apps should be able to set the host clipboard via Ghostty's OSC 52 support, while kittwm keeps its own pane model/rendering path.

Scope:
- Recognize OSC 52 clipboard sequences in native `TerminalState`.
- Queue sanitized OSC 52 set-clipboard sequences for host-terminal forwarding instead of rendering them or writing them back to the child PTY.
- Drain host-forwarded OSC sequences from `PtyTerminalApp` in the native session loop and write them to stdout outside pane frames.
- Preserve existing OSC title handling.
- Add focused tests for OSC 52 capture/sanitization and drain behavior.

Acceptance:
- Test proves OSC 52 with base64 payload queues a host OSC 52 sequence.
- Test proves empty/query-like payloads are ignored rather than forwarded as clipboard reads.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK surface pane-control requests: build directly
description: SurfaceHandle rename, resize_weight, and move_pane still format pane-control request strings. Build these requests through direct preallocated builders while preserving validation and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: graphical launcher and picker overlays
description: Dogfood graphics gap: launcher/picker overlays should use kittui components rather than boxed ASCII. Render candidate rows, selected state, search/filter, footer hints, and translucent backdrop as kittui scenes. Acceptance: launch/picker overlays are graphical in graphics mode, restore underlying panes on close, and retain text fallback.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-bar overlay: build fitted chip text directly
description: Standalone kittwm-bar overlay chip fitting still uses format!(" {label} ") for labels that already fit, while clipped labels build directly. Build fitted and clipped chip text through the same preallocated buffer to reduce overlay redraw allocation churn while preserving output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm X window resize context: build directly
description: XWindowSurface::resize_surface still uses format! for its resize error context label. Build that context string directly while preserving X surface resize/capture coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: honor workspace label env in chrome metadata
description:
Workspace metadata gap: native kittwm top bar, CHROME_JSON, and SDK status currently hard-code workspace "1" even though kittwm-bar already has `KITTWM_WORKSPACE` fallback.

Scope:
- Make live native session top bar and daemon chrome/status metadata use a shared/resolved workspace label from `KITTWM_WORKSPACE`, defaulting to `1`.
- Preserve current behavior when env is unset.
- Add targeted tests for env override in top-bar model and CHROME_JSON/status JSON.
- Do not implement multi-workspace runtime switching in this bead.

Acceptance:
- `KITTWM_WORKSPACE=dev kittwm` / socket status surfaces report `dev` in the top bar and chrome metadata.
- Existing default remains `1`.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: saturate architecture scene row count
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: architecture graphics compute rows by casting layer/plane/surface counts to u16 and adding them. If the architecture contract grows or is generated in tests, this can overflow/truncate before clamping, risking malformed scene metadata. Scope: use saturated row count accounting for architecture scenes and add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add kittui-md equations alias for math
description: Add --equations as a friendly alias for kittui-md --math so users can inspect Markdown math expressions with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native pane border: build focus-ring label directly
description: Replace the `format!("pane-{idx}-focus-ring")` label in `native_pane_border_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated label helper. Scope only the focus-ring layer label in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing native shell affordance scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: skip unchanged PNG native app uploads
description: Operator loop: keep finding/fixing kittwm slow/flickery issues. While inspecting the current renderer after stable placement work, RGBA native frames use DirtyGrid to skip unchanged uploads, but PNG native frames (including libghostty/browser-style surfaces) still upload every frame even when bytes and placement are unchanged. This can make libghostty default terminals unnecessarily slow/flickery. Scope: add lightweight unchanged-PNG tracking keyed by image id, reuse existing placement tracking to redraw only when placement changes, publish skipped_upload metadata, and add targeted unit coverage for the decision helper.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-bar: prioritize active workspace in narrow kitty overlay
description: Kittwm burn-down follow-up: standalone kittwm-bar --kitty now bounds workspace chip labels, but it still iterates default numeric chips before an appended/custom active workspace. On narrow bars the active workspace can be omitted before it is considered. Scope: use active-first workspace ordering in the standalone kitty overlay only when labels are constrained, preserving wide/default ordering; add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm raw frame chrome: build cache key directly
description: Replace the `format!` in `raw_frame_chrome_key(...)` with direct preallocated String construction. Scope only raw compositor frame chrome cache-key construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve key semantics and bounded-title tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: default dirty frames to skip unchanged uploads
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: `NativeDirtyFramePolicy` only skips byte-identical RGBA uploads when `KITTWM_DIRTY_FRAMES=skip-unchanged` is set. The default path still uploads every frame even when the dirty grid is clean, wasting bandwidth and increasing flicker risk. Make skip-unchanged the default and keep explicit env opt-outs for debugging; update tests to assert default enabled and opt-out disabled.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: add placement coverage gap helpers
description: Add SDK helpers to expose placement coverage gaps from `ArchitectureContract`: missing placement-contract surfaces and not-ready placement contracts. This lets diagnostics explain why native kitty-graphics coverage is incomplete, instead of only reporting counts. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native pane title keys: build directly
description: native_pane_title_key_from_text uses format! to build the pane-title chrome cache key from geometry/focus/text each frame. Build the key directly into one preallocated String while preserving exact key text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add short remote X11 forwarding aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST status --x11` preflights the graphical SSH lane used by remote app launch. Add discoverable shortcuts such as `kittwm remote HOST x11` and `kittwm remote HOST graphical` that map to the same doctor check, so users can quickly test forwarding from Ghostty/Kitty host terminals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add SDK helpers for focused frame changed percent
description: Render-performance SDK follow-up: DirtyFrameStatus exposes changed_percent(), and focused helpers expose changed tile ratio/fraction, but there is no focused_frame_changed_percent helper on Status/PanesStatus. Add focused percent helpers and NativePaneDetail::frame_changed_percent so clients can present human-friendly render dirtiness without recomputing fractions. Filed from ongoing kittwm render-performance/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-terminal: prefer Fira Code Nerd Font for glyph rendering
description: User reports terminal glyphs do not render and asks to use Fira Code Nerd Font if possible. The native terminal renderer font discovery only looks for firacode regular names and can pick non-Nerd fonts before Nerd Font installs. Prefer FiraCode Nerd Font regular/mono files when scanning font roots, while keeping KITTUI_TERMINAL_FONT override and normal Fira Code fallback.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward F5 input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards many editing/navigation keys but consumes F5 (`ESC [ 15 ~`), so users cannot trigger browser/web-app refresh shortcuts in browser panes. Forward F5 through DevTools key events.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Warn agents to quote caco bd descriptions containing shell metacharacters
description: Context: during bd-306ec8 follow-up filing, an inline caco bd create description with Markdown-style command quoting was passed through a double-quoted shell string. The shell interpreted the command text before caco received it, briefly launching kittwm and corrupting the bead description until I updated it safely. Friction shape: the profile warns about shell-safe descriptions for broken-on-main filing, but ordinary bead create/update descriptions have the same hazard. Suggested fix: add general guidance to pass descriptions with single quotes, stdin, or description files whenever they contain command snippets or shell metacharacters. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-306ec8 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native toast: avoid repeated trigger scans
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: native_should_show_footer_toast calls ascii_contains_ignore_case four times over the same footer/toast message (error/failed/denied/launcher.error). Large launcher/error messages are already bounded for rendering, but trigger classification still rescans the whole message repeatedly. Scope: classify toast trigger keywords in one ASCII case-insensitive pass and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: friendly info command
description:
Daily-driver UX: add a friendly `kittwm info` command for one-screen running-WM overview.

Scope:
- Add cooked-mode `kittwm info` subcommand.
- It should query `STATUS_JSON`, `CHROME_JSON`, and `PANES_JSON` from the selected socket/display and print a compact human-readable overview: socket path, running status, workspace/chrome rows, pane count/focus/layout, and pane ids/titles when available.
- If no daemon is reachable, print a helpful message with `kittwm` start hint and exit nonzero.
- Preserve existing `--status`, `--status-json`, `--panes`, `--panes-json`, `--chrome-json` behavior.
- Add focused formatting tests using sample JSON; avoid needing a live TUI session.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm info` is the obvious first inspection command for daily driver use.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md renders Markdown blockquotes as banner callouts
description: Fix Markdown blockquote rendering so paragraph endings inside a blockquote do not flush as normal text boxes before the blockquote closes. Track blockquote state, render quoted content as a banner/callout component, and add tests covering simple blockquotes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add HELP_JSON to standalone daemon
description:
Large gap: native kittwm sockets expose HELP_JSON, but the standalone daemon only has human-readable HELP text. This keeps the two socket/control-plane modes inconsistent for external automation and discovery.

Scope:
- Add `HELP_JSON` to the standalone daemon request handler.
- Return a machine-readable command catalog covering current standalone daemon commands (`PING`, `STATUS`, `STATUS_JSON`, `WINDOWS`, `DISPLAYS`, `APPS`, `APPS_JSON`, `APPS_FIRST`, `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST`, `SPAWN`, `PANES`, `PANES_JSON`, `QUIT`, `HELP`, `HELP_JSON`).
- Keep existing HELP text behavior unchanged except adding HELP_JSON to the list.
- Add targeted daemon tests and docs/wm.md examples.

Acceptance:
- `client_request(server.path(), "HELP_JSON")` returns parseable JSON with a command record for `SPAWN <argv>`.
- Existing text HELP still works.
- `cargo test -p kittui-cli daemon::tests -- --nocapture` and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui protocol audit: animation + absolute + relative placement reported broken; verify vs kitty spec
description: Operator report: kittui fundamentals are broken — animation, absolute placement, and relative placement all show incorrect behaviour. Audit our protocol usage end-to-end against the canonical kitty graphics protocol spec (a=t/f/d, q=2, m=, transmission, animation frames a=a, placement r/c, virtual placement U=1 + unicode placeholder, classic z-index, relative placement P/Q anchoring, deletion verbs). Compare every escape sequence we emit. Capture mismatches as child bugs and fix the worst behaviour-affecting items. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add program remote app aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote app workflows support app/apps and application/applications, but users may naturally think in terms of programs when launching/listing software over SSH. Add `kittwm remote HOST programs QUERY` as a list alias and `kittwm remote HOST program QUERY` as a first-match selection alias, including JSON support and help/catalog/parser coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm doctor scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: doctor_scene builds the doctor backdrop label with format! from readiness. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.

title: Document kittui-md math mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --math mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.

title: kittui-md preserves Markdown inline and block HTML placeholders
description: Add Markdown HTML handling so inline HTML and block HTML are not silently dropped by the renderer. Render them as tool-toned or inline placeholder text and add tests for inline and block HTML output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native surfaces scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: native_surfaces_scene still builds the native-surfaces backdrop label with format! from surface count and readiness. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: graphical focus ring and pane selection affordances
description: Dogfood graphics gap: focus should be visually clear with kittui-rendered affordances rather than only title-row inversion/text. Add graphical focus ring, active pane glow/accent, and keyboard selection affordances that respect translucency/theme. Acceptance: focused pane is obvious in screenshots; focus changes update chrome without flicker or stale images.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: avoid graphical top-bar clock overlap on narrow terminals
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: the live graphical top-bar ANSI text overlay always writes three workspace chip labels and then right-aligns the clock with . On narrow terminals, the clock can be written back over the workspace labels, causing garbled top-bar chrome/flicker. Scope: compute the occupied workspace-label width and only render the clock when it fits with spacing; add targeted helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-md code-blocks-only mode for code extraction
description: Add a kittui-md --code-blocks mode that prints only parsed code blocks with language labels and source text. This complements --components and --metadata-json for human-readable code extraction. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: stop text/image fighting with absolute native placements
description: Live kittwm native app/chrome surfaces still use unicode-placeholder placement and write placeholder text into the terminal each redraw. This can fight with ANSI/footer/chrome text and cause visible placeholder/text/image contention. Make Runtime return empty embed for non-U absolute placements, then switch live native app/chrome placements to absolute z-indexed placement where possible. Add tests for no embed/no U=1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm session scene: build row labels directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: session_scene still builds each pane row label with format! from index, window, title, command, weight, and focus fields. Add a direct preallocated session-row label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: publish native PTY mouse reporting modes
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native PTY panes do not track terminal mouse-reporting modes (`?1000`, `?1002`, `?1003`, `?1006`). kittwm needs this state before it can safely route host mouse reports into the focused/target pane, and controllers need to know whether a TUI is asking for mouse input.

Scope:
- Track native PTY mouse modes in `TerminalState`: basic mouse, button-motion, all-motion, and SGR coordinate encoding.
- Expose the state through `PtyTerminalApp`.
- Publish optional mouse mode booleans in native pane status JSON/text surfaces.
- Add parser/status tests and docs.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves `CSI ? 1000/1002/1003/1006 h/l` toggles state.
- Daemon status tests include mouse mode metadata.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK chrome tests: build socket path directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("/tmp/kwchrome-{}-{}.sock", ...)` in `chrome_helper_sends_expected_socket_command` with a direct preallocated socket-path helper. Scope only kittwm SDK chrome-helper test socket path construction in `crates/kittwm-sdk/src/lib.rs`; preserve chrome helper socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-quartz: macOS virtual-display backend (CGVirtualDisplay + ScreenCaptureKit + CGEventPost)
description: Add a sibling crate kittui-quartz that implements the kittui-xvfb XServer trait against macOS Quartz. Creates a CGVirtualDisplay via the private CoreGraphics symbols (only macOS path to a real headless display without third-party drivers), captures it with ScreenCaptureKit, and routes pointer/key events via CGEventPost + Accessibility. Gated behind a 'quartz' feature; documents the private-API trade-off in docs/wm.md. Lands the XServer trait implementation now; the CGVirtualDisplayCreate FFI itself behind unsafe extern "C" symbols, with a graceful fallback to capturing the main display when virtual display creation fails. The kittui_wm_demo learns --features quartz to drive it on macOS the same way --features xvfb drives Xvfb on Linux.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-input: parse kitty mouse + key reporting
description:
New crate kittui-input that parses the kitty pointer protocol (SGR mouse 1006, motion 1003, focus 1004, hover 1006 with extensions) and CSI key reports into typed events. Includes subcell pixel coordinates where the host reports them. Exact-grammar tests for press/release/motion/scroll/drag/hover and modifier flags.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: add kittui-ghostty command render mode
description:
Add a command-output mode to the portable kittui-ghostty CLI so agents can render real command output through libghostty-vt without desktop capture.

Acceptance:
- kittui-ghostty supports --command/-c COMMAND and captures stdout/stderr.
- Captured command bytes are fed through GhosttyVtTerminal and rendered to PNG using existing --out/--cols/--rows/--scroll options.
- Existing stdin, --demo, and --timelapse-demo modes still work.
- Generate/post a fresh command-rendered headless Ghostty image in the session.
- Run focused validation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: bounded event iterator helper
description:
SDK ergonomics gap: `Kittwm::events_ms(ms)` returns a Vec after a bounded batch. Add a small iterator/stream-like wrapper over the same bounded call for clients that want to iterate events without managing the Vec directly.

Scope:
- Add an additive `Kittwm::event_iter_ms(ms)` or `events_iter_ms(ms)` helper returning an owning iterator over `KittwmEvent` backed by the existing `events_ms` call.
- Preserve current socket behavior and `events_ms` API.
- Gate with SubscribeEvents via the existing call path.
- Add targeted SDK tests for iterator behavior and capability denial if practical.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can iterate a bounded event batch ergonomically.
- Existing event APIs remain compatible.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: cache chrome theme config colors
description: native_glass_chrome_colors loads ~/.config/kittwm/config.yaml in several chrome scene builders and can run repeatedly in the live render path. Cache the resolved InlineChipColors for the process so per-frame chrome construction does not repeatedly hit disk/parse YAML. Add a deterministic test for stable cached color resolution and keep config/background semantics unchanged.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm runtime keymap: build function key specs directly
description: Replace `format!("f{n}")` in `key_spec_for_event(...)` with direct preallocated String construction for function-key specs. Scope only runtime keymap function-key label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing key-spec behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward Delete key input to pages
description: kittwm-browser now forwards Backspace/Tab/arrows/Enter, but Delete key CSI 3~ is still ignored. Users cannot delete forward in text fields. Parse Delete from stdin and dispatch browser Delete key events while preserving ordered text input.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native weighted layouts should not clone weights
description: `native_pane_layouts_weighted` currently converts the input weights slice to a Vec on every call, even when weights are non-empty and only read by `native_weighted_spans`. Replace the clone with a borrowed slice and a static single-weight fallback for the empty case. Scope: native layout weight calculation only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add SDK event helpers for frame status labels
description: Render-performance SDK follow-up: frame-presented events now expose dirty-tile labels/percentages, but consumers still hand-compose clean/status/chip labels to match live pane status chips. Add EventEnvelope helpers frame_is_clean(), frame_status_label(), and frame_chip_label() with the same clean/skipped semantics used by DirtyFrameStatus. Filed from ongoing kittwm render-performance event audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4
spoken_name: add sdk event helpers frame status

title: kittui-cli: support stdin image bytes
description:
Large gap: `kittui image` is path-only, which weakens kittui as a shell/external-platform renderer. Scripts that generate or transform image bytes must write a temporary file before kittui can render/place them.

Scope:
- Teach `kittui image --src -` to read image bytes from stdin and build `Node::Image { src: ImageRef::Bytes { ... } }`.
- Preserve path behavior for normal paths.
- Add CLI/integration coverage that pipes a tiny PNG through stdin into `kittui image --src - --dry-run --json`.
- Update README/DESIGN examples if needed.

Acceptance:
- `printf ... | kittui image --src - -w 1 -h 1 --dry-run --json` works for valid image bytes.
- Path-based image rendering still compiles/works.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: mark raw RGB medium helper landed
description:
Docs follow-up after raw RGB medium helper landed. Update kitty protocol conformance docs to say raw RGB now covers direct and file/temp/shared-memory medium grammar, leaving visual proof/response probing as the remaining protocol work.

Scope:
- Update docs/protocol-conformance.md raw RGB/open work sections.
- Mention `upload_still_rgb_medium` and `f=24,t=f|t=t|t=s` grammar.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs match raw RGB direct + medium helper coverage.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm run-loop entry log: build directly

title: Fix source tree browser off-by-one folder expansion in TUI

The source tree browser in the TUI has an off-by-one indexing bug where clicking on a folder expands the folder below it instead of the clicked folder itself.

Acceptance criteria:

- No adjacent folders are incorrectly expanded

crates/kittui-cli/examples/kittui_wm_demo.rs that starts Xvfb at :99, launches xterm (or any X app), composites it as a floating kittui window in the current terminal with full pointer/keyboard routing, and shows the perf panel beside it. Documents the SSH-over-kittui story in docs/wm.md.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native renderer tests should not depend on exact font pixels
description: While validating bd-88b644, targeted helper coverage passed, but existing tests terminal_renderer_draws_readable_bitmap_glyphs and terminal_renderer_uses_sgr_foreground_and_background failed in this environment after terminal_font used an anti-aliased font path, because they assert exact foreground pixel values. The behavior is test-environment dependent and should be made robust to font-backed rendering or explicitly force bitmap fallback. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-88b644 session.

title: Teach remote doctor explicit fallback commands
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote doctor currently says to use local pooled-SSH forwarding when remote kittwm is missing or broken, but it does not print the concrete fallback commands we have added. Update remote doctor text output to show actionable examples such as `kittwm remote HOST fallback apps firefox`, `fallback launch firefox`, `fallback windows`, and `fallback displays`, while keeping JSON unchanged. Cover the script text in tests.

title: kittwm-browser: cap timeout environment overrides
description: Browser hang hardening follow-up: `KITTWM_BROWSER_CDP_TIMEOUT_MS` and `KITTWM_BROWSER_DEVTOOLS_TIMEOUT_MS` accept any positive integer. A typo such as a huge value can effectively disable the bounded-hang protections recently added for DevTools connect/command paths. Centralize browser timeout parsing and clamp positive overrides to a sane max (60s), while preserving defaults and ignoring zero/invalid values. Add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm graphics labels: avoid double allocation in truncate helper
description: The kittwm truncate helper is used across help, window picker, diagnostics, launcher and config/info graphical labels. It builds a bounded String, but for fitting inputs discards it and calls s.to_string(), duplicating work/allocation in redraw/status paths. Return the built buffer and preallocate the bounded width while preserving output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: pane control subcommand aliases
description:
Daily-driver UX source work: add memorable pane-control subcommands.

Scope:
- Implement source changes for aliases such as:
  - `kittwm focus WINDOW` -> `FOCUS_PANE <window>`
  - `kittwm close [WINDOW]` -> `CLOSE_PANE <window>` default focused
  - `kittwm layout columns|rows`
  - `kittwm move [WINDOW] DIR` default focused
  - `kittwm resize [WINDOW] N` default focused
  - `kittwm balance`
  - `kittwm rename WINDOW TITLE`
- Keep existing flags working.
- Avoid overlapping action aliases (`spawn/read/type/line/key/wait`) being handled by current agent.
- Add focused parse tests; no daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Users can manage panes with subcommands instead of long flags.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.

title: Capture targeted kittwm SPP graphics usability proof
description: From bd-5eedb3 user-facing analysis: current proof captures do not exercise SPP/kitty graphics transfer enough to judge end-user usability. Add a targeted kittwm graphics/SPP scenario using real output and capture it through the Ghostty/kittui proof path; classify blank, wrapped, stale, errored, or command-log-only artifacts as regression/validation rather than PASS proof.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: animation first transmit emits redundant r=1; per-frame delay grammar should use a=t/a=f z= not a=a r=N z=
description: First animation frame uses a=t and currently includes r=1, which is redundant per spec (transmit is implicitly frame 1). Per-frame delays are also emitted as a=a,r=N,z=<ms> control commands; the canonical way is to set z= on the upload (a=t/a=f) command itself. Audit + adjust. Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm config state: build action strings directly
description: Replace `format!("reload.config -> {}", self.label())` and `format!("reload.config error -> {}", self.label())` in `ConfigState` with direct preallocated String helpers. Scope only config reload action string construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing config state behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add SDK helper for focused resized kittwm pane state
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/design-cleanliness follow-up: SDK clients can list resized panes, but common focus-state automation still has to fetch focused_pane() and call is_resized() manually. Add focused_is_resized() helpers on PanesStatus and Status, with tests/docs, so focus/resize state can be queried directly like focused_is_topmost().

title: Name and test focused footer state bound

Introduce a named NATIVE_STATUS_STATE_MAX_CHARS constant and test that pathological focused status labels are clipped before lower-priority footer hints/log text. Filed from ongoing kittwm footer UX audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-renderer: bound graphical help overlay width
description: Audit finding: graphical help overlay scene forces a minimum width of 20 columns even when the terminal is narrower, so kitty placement can extend past the visible terminal and corrupt/wrap adjacent chrome. Bound graphical help overlay panel width to the available terminal columns, complementing the ANSI narrow-width guard.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm apps graphics: saturate app row count
description: kittwm apps scene computes rows by casting path/macos app list lengths to u16 before adding and clamping. Pathological app lists can wrap before clamp and produce wrong scene metadata. Use saturated row accounting before clamping.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK JSON wait helpers
description:
Docs follow-up for SDK JSON wait match helpers.

Scope:
- After source lands, document `wait_text_match_json[_ms]` and `wait_output_match_json[_ms]` alongside existing wait helpers.
- Clarify they use JSON daemon commands and return `WaitMatch` while existing helpers remain available.
- Docs-only; no source changes.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention SDK JSON wait helpers.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: use FFI batch placement for placeMany
description:
Large gap: FFI now exposes `kittui_place_many_json`, but the TypeScript binding's `placeMany()` still loops over `place(scene)`, crossing the JS/FFI boundary once per scene. JS/TS hosts cannot use the efficient batch renderer path.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_place_many_json` in `bindings/ts/src/index.js`.
- Change `Kittui.placeMany(scenes)` to call the batch FFI API once and return the concatenated batch bytes string.
- Add `placeManyList(scenes)` or equivalent only if needed for old per-scene array behavior; keep documented behavior aligned.
- Update TypeScript declarations and README.
- Add fake-library tests proving one batch FFI call is made.

Acceptance:
- `placeMany([scene, scene])` calls `kittui_place_many_json` once with a JSON array.
- Existing `place` and `placeAt` tests still pass.
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md input format discovery outputs
description: Add --input-formats and --input-formats-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can query supported input formats independently of the mode catalog. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add typed SDK title drag kind enum
description: SDK design-cleanliness follow-up: kittwm status now exposes title_drag_kind as a string (`reorder`/`reposition`), but typed SDK consumers still need to compare raw strings for exhaustive handling. Add a typed SDK enum/convenience parser for title drag kind while preserving raw string access and unknown-kind fallback. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Fix Ctrl-C handling in ghostty terminal
description:
Ctrl-C is not being passed correctly to focused applications in ghostty. Currently, triple Ctrl-C quits the terminal, but single and double Ctrl-C should be forwarded to the focused application first. Fix the key event routing to allow proper interrupt signal handling in terminal applications.

Acceptance criteria:
- Single Ctrl-C passes to focused app
- Double Ctrl-C passes to focused app
- Triple Ctrl-C quits ghostty
- Terminal applications receive SIGINT correctly
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kittwm graphical helpers: avoid unicode placeholder grids
description: Several kittwm graphical helper commands still use PlacementOptions::unicode(), emitting placeholder grids that can create visible text/image artifacts. Switch the shared scene/kitty printer to absolute no-placeholder placements, matching kittwm-terminal and kittwm-bar, while keeping z-index roles.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm PTY renderer: skip redundant font pixel bounds checks
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: draw_terminal_font_glyph already clips rasterized glyph pixels to the cell bounds, and the renderer only calls it for in-grid cells, but blend_rgba_pixel still performs a slice length bounds check for every non-transparent font pixel. Scope: use a font-glyph-specific in-bounds blend helper after clipping, keep the checked helper for generic callers, and add focused coverage for index calculation/edge safety.

title: kittwm-launch surface errors: build directly

- Implement source changes (not docs-only) in the shared shortcut/help list so `C-a ?` / `kittwm shortcuts` also point to key external commands like `kittwm info`, `quickstart`, `examples`, and `cheat` without becoming too long.

Scope only launcher path test temp directory name construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve launcher PATH matching coverage.

Extend kittui-ghostty-vt with portable libghostty-vt render-state extraction. Read rows/cells/graphemes and optional foreground/background colors through libghostty-vt render-state APIs, use that for the headless PNG preview, and post an updated artifact.

title: kitwm --launcher-query: Ctrl-A Enter launches first matching app candidate
description: Wire the searchable launcher candidate source into the live runtime launcher action. Add --launcher-query QUERY / KITTUI_WM_LAUNCH_QUERY so Ctrl-A Enter (and other launch actions) select the first matching PATH command or macOS app and launch it, falling back to KITWM_LAUNCH_CMD/xterm when unset. Log selected candidate and pid; footer still shows last launch pid. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix animation upload to use a=t/a=f frame appends and a=a control
description:
Animation upload currently uses repeated a=t,r=N which the spec does not document for appending frames. Per https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#animation the first frame uses a=t with f= and i= as usual, subsequent frames use a=f with the same i= to append, and the animation control sets gap/loop count via a=a with parameters s=, c=, z=. Audit kittui-kitty::upload_animation against the spec, fix the verb sequence, set per-frame delays via the appropriate field, add grammar tests, and ensure native looping plays without escape traffic per cycle.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix terminal tiling overlap issue
description: Terminals are currently drawing over each other instead of being properly tiled. Implement correct window tiling logic so that multiple terminal windows are displayed side-by-side or in a grid without overlapping. Acceptance criteria: Open multiple terminals and verify they tile correctly without overlapping content.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kittwm-browser: forward arrow-key input to pages
description: kittwm-browser raw input parser ignores terminal arrow escape sequences, so users cannot move cursors in text fields or navigate focused controls. Parse CSI A/B/C/D from stdin and dispatch browser ArrowUp/Down/Right/Left key events while preserving ordered text input.

title: kittwm examples: include first-party helper workflows
description:

The `kittwm examples` daily-driver page has core `kittwm` workflows, but it does not include the first-party helper workflows recently polished in help topics: `kittwm-launch --browser ...`, `kittwm-terminal --events-ms ...`, `kittwm-top --json`, `kittwm-bar --reserve --kitty`, and `kittwm-browser --semantic-snapshot ...`. Add a concise helper section plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.

title: kittwm config scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Replace the remaining `label: Some(format!("kittwm-config-backdrop:..."))` in `config_scene_for_cols(...)` with a direct preallocated label builder. Scope is only the config scene backdrop label in `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; preserve existing scene labels and add narrow coverage for the helper if needed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add select and pick remote app aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST app QUERY` selects the first app match, but “select” and “pick” are natural verbs for choosing a remote app before launching. Add `kittwm remote HOST select QUERY` and `kittwm remote HOST pick QUERY` as first-match app selection aliases, including JSON support through `--json`, and advertise them in help/catalog.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Show current kittwm layout mode in footer
description: Operator-requested kittwm UX polish slice: after adding floating drag/raise behavior, the live footer still lists shortcuts but does not surface the current layout mode, so users cannot easily tell whether they are in columns/rows/grid/floating/fullscreen unless they infer from geometry. Add an explicit `mode:<label>` indicator to the native footer/status line for non-empty workspaces, preserving empty-workspace minimalism and clipping behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-session: bound row rects on narrow scenes
description: Audit finding: session-kitty / session-scene-json rows still use fixed 10px margins with (width - 20).max(1). On narrow session scenes these manifest row rects can escape the scene footprint and corrupt graphics. Reuse bounded info indicator geometry for session rows and add coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Fix Chrome redundant rendering
description: Chrome is rendering 100 times per window update instead of once. This is causing unnecessary CPU usage and potential performance issues. Optimize the rendering pipeline to render chrome exactly once per window update cycle. Acceptance criteria: Profile rendering calls and verify only one render pass occurs per window state change.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: Compositor multiplex: many backends, one session
description:
Generalise kittui-wm::compositor::Compositor from Compositor<S: XServer> to Compositor holding Vec<Box<dyn XServer>>. Introduce a globally-unique WindowKey = (backend_id, XWindowId) so two backends can't collide. Update hit_test, route_pointer, route_key, compose to dispatch by backend. Per-window mode + chrome theme still live in the compositor. All existing tests must keep passing.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add placement role z-index helpers
description: Add SDK helpers to resolve z-index and composition plane directly from `SurfacePlacementRole` (`ArchitectureContract::z_index_for_role`, `composition_plane_for_role`). This lets SDK apps use the typed role enum without converting back to raw plane strings. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: plan accessibility-tree semantic adapter
description:
Semantic adapter gap: arbitrary native GUI apps need pixel capture today unless toolkit/browser semantics are available. Plan the first platform accessibility-tree semantic adapter (macOS AX / Linux AT-SPI shape) that can expose labels/buttons/inputs/menus through kittwm semantic surfaces while retaining pixel fallback.

Scope:
- Add an in-repo design note for accessibility-tree semantic adapter architecture.
- Cover macOS AX and Linux AT-SPI high-level mapping into SDK ComponentRole/ActionKind.
- Define event/focus/action routing, sensitive value policy, permissions, and fallback behavior.
- File concrete implementation follow-ups for one platform spike.

Acceptance:
- Docs describe accessibility adapter approach, limitations, and security policy.
- Follow-up beads exist for implementation spikes.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: expose chrome reservation in status JSON
description:
SDK/runtime gap for top-bar integration: native STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON does not report the reserved chrome band or tilable area, so SDK clients like kittwm-bar cannot reason about top-clamped chrome vs tiled area.

Scope:
- Add native socket status metadata for chrome reservation: top_bar_rows, tilable_rows if inferable, and maybe workspace id (currently 1).
- Include metadata in STATUS_JSON and PANES_JSON without changing existing fields.
- Compute tilable_rows from pane/app geometry when panes exist, otherwise expose top_bar_rows/workspace only.
- Add targeted daemon tests for JSON shape.
- Do not change live session rendering.

Acceptance:
- SDK/CLI clients can inspect chrome reservation metadata from native status JSON.
- Existing status/panes JSON remains backward-compatible.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: throttle raw compositor footer repaints
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: `run_compositor_session` rewrites the terminal footer every frame, and the footer text includes the changing frame counter/FPS. Even when all window image uploads are skipped, this forces constant ANSI text repaint at the bottom of the screen, adding flicker and terminal throughput. Throttle footer writes to state changes or periodic telemetry refreshes; clear the old footer row if its row moves; add deterministic helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: update viewport metadata only after resize succeeds
description: Browser resize/metadata issue: `HeadlessBrowserApp::resize_surface` assigns `self.width`/`self.height` before sending `Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride`. If DevTools returns an error or times out, metadata now reports the requested size even though Chrome may still be at the old viewport. Compute the requested dimensions first, send the DevTools resize, and only update stored metadata after success; add focused helper coverage for the cell-to-pixel viewport calculation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: add kittui-ghostty headless preview CLI
description:
Add a small portable kittui-ghostty CLI over kittui-ghostty-vt. It should read VT bytes from stdin (or demo content), feed libghostty-vt, extract render-state cells/styles, and write a PNG artifact. This provides a reusable command-line path for headless Ghostty evidence.

Acceptance:
- Add a bin target in kittui-ghostty-vt, e.g. `kittui-ghostty`, with --out/--cols/--rows/--demo options.
- Keep portable: no Ghostty.app, no desktop capture.
- Use render-state preview path, not formatter text only.
- Validate by rendering a PNG and post it as a caco file/progress image.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md footnotes-json inspection mode
description: Add --footnotes-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown footnote references and definitions, emitting schema_version plus references and indexed definition records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.

title: Advertise remote screen display aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `screen(s)` already route to remote display listing, but help/catalog examples focus on monitor aliases. Make screen aliases discoverable too so users can naturally ask for screens, monitors, or displays over pooled SSH.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-md preserves Markdown footnote references
description: Add Markdown footnote reference handling so [^label] references are not silently dropped. Preserve references as visible placeholders in component text and add tests for footnote reference output.

description:
Rewrite docs/wm.md to lead with the multi-backend mental model: any window is local/remote/Quartz/XQuartz, rendered as homogenous kittui chrome. Include backend matrix from the design conversation, the SSH-into-Mac story, the mixed X11+macOS session example, and the limits (no Cocoa hosting without v3 CGVirtualDisplay).
