- Validate targeted test behavior and kittui-cli lib tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add fallback flag for remote window and display listings
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote window/display listings delegate to remote kittwm when installed and fall back when it fails. For diagnostics and broken remote installs, add explicit `--fallback` support to `kittwm remote HOST windows|displays QUERY` and list aliases so users can force pooled-SSH platform fallback discovery instead of trying remote kittwm first. Cover help/catalog/parser/env behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native socket resize log: build directly
description: The native socket Resize path formats its debug diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact window/delta/weight text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render semantic component surfaces via kittui affordances
description:
Implementation gap: once semantic component surfaces exist, kittwm needs a renderer bridge that turns component trees into the same kittui primitives/chrome used by kittwm. This should let semantic SDK/toolkit surfaces render through pure terminal, kitty graphics, kittui scene, or remote/headless outputs without bespoke per-app drawing.

Scope:
- Define a kittwm semantic-surface renderer adapter that consumes component trees and produces kittui primitive scenes/chrome model output.
- Reuse first-party kittui-affordances controls instead of duplicating button/radio/text-input rendering in kittwm.
- Document/implement fallback behavior when a semantic node cannot be represented exactly.
- Add a small synthetic semantic surface test/example.

Acceptance:
- Synthetic component tree can render through kittui primitives with focus/selection state preserved.
- kittwm renderer bridge uses shared affordance components.
- No high-level UI controls are added to kittui-core.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add remote graphical forwarding doctor check
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote app launch now requests trusted X11 forwarding with `ssh -Y`, but users need an easy status check to diagnose whether graphical forwarding will actually be available before launch. Add a `kittwm remote HOST status --x11` / `--graphical` doctor path that uses the graphical SSH lane and reports remote DISPLAY/forwarding state.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm quit: debounce Ctrl-C triple and require confirmation dialog
description: User report: triple Ctrl-C to quit should use a debounce/confirmation flow, not immediately quit on any three in sequence. Add a timed confirmation dialog before kittwm exits, so accidental Ctrl-C bursts are forwarded/handled safely and the operator explicitly confirms quit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm info scene: build pane labels directly
description: Small kittwm info scene performance cleanup: info_scene builds each pane row layer label with format! from bounded window/title labels and focus state. Build pane row labels directly into preallocated Strings while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm events scene: build heading label directly
description: Small kittwm events scene performance cleanup: events_scene builds the heading layer label with format! from the already-bounded event summary. Build the events heading label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-wm: add NativeSurface exact-byte input hook
description:
Surface runtime gap: `NativeSurface` has text input but no common exact-byte input hook, even though PTY surfaces and kittwm automation support exact bytes.

Scope:
- Add `send_surface_bytes(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<()>` to `NativeSurface`.
- Default implementation should accept UTF-8 by delegating to `send_surface_text` and reject non-UTF8 bytes explicitly.
- Override for `PtyTerminalApp` to preserve exact bytes through `TerminalSurface::send_bytes`.
- Capture-only adapters remain unsupported through the default text path.
- Add focused tests for PTY byte send and default non-UTF8 rejection/capture-only unsupported behavior.
- Do not change daemon/socket behavior.

Acceptance:
- NativeSurface abstraction can represent exact-byte input where supported.
- PTY preserves raw bytes; other adapters fail explicitly as appropriate.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: send focus event after native session restore
description: Bug-hunt finding while improving kittwm usability: `NativePaneCommand::RestoreSession` swaps in restored panes and assigns `focused = new_focus` directly, unlike normal focus paths that use `native_set_focus` / focus events. If a restored terminal enables focus reporting during startup or shortly before restore completes, it may not receive the initial focus-in event for the restored focused pane. After swapping restored panes, send a focus-in through the existing native focus event path for the selected focus index; add deterministic helper coverage for whether restored panes should receive focus notification.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add kittui-md about discovery outputs
description: Add --about and --about-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can inspect the binary name, package version, default mode, and high-level capabilities. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive reload key
description: Add a native reload key to kittui-md --interactive so users can refresh the Markdown file in-place after editing it. Document the keybinding, expose it through keybinding discovery, and add targeted tests for the pager action and reload helper.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md metadata JSON includes schema version
description: Add a stable schema_version field to kittui-md --metadata-json so downstream consumers can detect future JSON shape changes. Add a test asserting the schema version.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm top bar: preallocate display label truncation
description: top_bar_display_label bounds workspace labels but collects into a String without explicit bounded capacity. Preallocate the capped label width and push chars directly so top-bar redraws avoid avoidable String growth while preserving ellipsis behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed layout focus and move helpers
description:
SDK control gap: socket/CLI expose layout and focus navigation operations (`FOCUS_NEXT`, `FOCUS_PREV`, `LAYOUT`, `BALANCE_PANES`, `MOVE_PANE`) but SDK clients still need raw protocol strings for these session-level controls. Add typed SDK helpers.

Scope:
- Add Kittwm helpers for focus_next, focus_prev, layout columns/rows or string, and balance_panes.
- Add SurfaceHandle::move_pane or typed move helper for left/right/up/down/first/last.
- Gate with ControlWindow capability.
- Add targeted SDK socket command-format and capability-denial tests.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can navigate focus/layout/move/balance without raw protocol strings.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK event helpers for frame presented metadata
description: Render-performance SDK follow-up: pane_frame_presented events carry renderer/format, pixel size, app bounds, dirty-tile counts, upload bytes, and elapsed time, but SDK consumers still index raw JSON for those render diagnostics. Add EventEnvelope frame-presented helpers for pixel size, app bounds, dirty tile ratio, upload/skipped state, bytes, and elapsed time. Filed from ongoing kittwm render-performance event audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-apps: bound launcher candidate row rects
description: Audit finding: launcher apps/candidates graphics still use fixed 10px row margins with (width - 20).max(1). On narrow app candidate scenes these path/macos row rects can escape the footprint and corrupt graphics. Reuse bounded info indicator geometry for app candidate rows and add coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-keymap: bound graphical scene labels
description: Audit finding: `kittwm keymap-kitty` / keymap scene JSON bounds geometry and row count, but serializes raw custom prefix/chord/action text into labels. A pathological custom keymap can inflate scene JSON/kitty payloads and redraw allocation churn. Clip keymap scene label fields while preserving useful diagnostics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Make remote app alias select first match
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: help now advertises `kittwm remote HOST app QUERY --json` as structured first-match selection, but the direct `app` alias still parses like plural `apps` unless `--first` is explicitly provided. Make singular `remote HOST app QUERY` default to first-match selection while keeping `remote HOST list app QUERY` as the listing alias.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Delegate remote launch JSON to installed kittwm
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: in `remote_apps_script`, fallback mode `launch-first-json` is implemented for missing/broken remote kittwm, but the healthy remote-kittwm delegation only maps json/first/launch-first and therefore runs a list instead of `--launch-first --json`. Map `launch-first-json` to remote `kittwm apps --launch-first --json` so installed remote kittwm behaves consistently with local fallback.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Preserve Markdown link and image titles
description: Preserve pulldown-cmark link/image title attributes in kittui-affordances metadata and expose them through kittui-md JSON and focused link/image modes. Add targeted tests and docs if needed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Restrict kittwm title drag start to title row
description: UX follow-up: kittwm docs say tiled/floating title rows are draggable, but mouse routing starts drag from any pane chrome cell because chrome hit-testing covers the whole outer pane. Restrict tiled/floating drag start to the title row while preserving focus on other chrome cells. Filed from ongoing kittwm drag UX audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add SDK event helpers for frame transport bytes
description: Render-performance SDK follow-up: frame-presented events carry upload_bytes plus placement_bytes/embed_bytes, but SDK only exposes upload bytes. Add EventEnvelope helpers for frame_placement_bytes(), frame_embed_bytes(), and frame_total_transport_bytes() so diagnostics can inspect complete transport cost without raw JSON indexing. Filed from ongoing kittwm render-performance event audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-sdk: typed HELP_JSON catalog helper
description:
SDK introspection gap: native socket exposes `HELP_JSON` as a machine-readable command catalog, but SDK clients need raw protocol strings to read it. Add typed SDK helpers for the help catalog.

Scope:
- Add typed structs for `HELP_JSON` response (`HelpCatalog`, `HelpCommand` or similar).
- Add `Kittwm::help()` / `help_catalog()` helper.
- Gate with a low-risk read capability rather than raw request.
- Add JSON decode, capability-denial, and Unix socket command-format tests.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can inspect socket capabilities/help without raw protocol strings.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm config scene rows should build labels directly
description: `config_scene_for_cols` builds a large `rows_data` array of formatted strings and then formats each into `kittwm-config-row` layer labels. Build those labels directly in the layer loop to avoid intermediate row strings while preserving graphical config scene labels. Scope: kittwm config scene rows only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Show resized marker in kittwm tiled pane titles
description: UX audit follow-up: live kittwm pane titles already show focus and floating state markers, while resized tiled panes only expose non-default weight in the status chip (`wt:<n>`). Add a compact title-row marker for non-default tiled pane weights so resize state remains visible in the primary pane chrome and narrow/status-chip-constrained views. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/quality audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm accessibility tests: build recorded action calls directly
description: Replace test-only `format!` calls in the kittui-wm accessibility `RecordingBackend` with direct preallocated String helpers. Scope only accessibility action recording test string construction in `crates/kittui-wm/src/accessibility.rs`; preserve accessibility action routing coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-info: respect narrow scene width
description: Audit finding: kittwm info/chrome status graphics still force a 40-column minimum and use fixed 10px side margins for row rects. On narrow terminals this can overflow scene width and wrap/corrupt diagnostics. Respect narrow KITTWM_INFO_COLS/COLUMNS values and bound internal info row rects to the scene footprint.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm --attach REPL + WINDOWS/DISPLAYS protocol verbs
description: Extend daemon protocol with WINDOWS (lists Mac windows) and DISPLAYS (lists displays), and add 'kitwm --attach' that opens an interactive line-oriented REPL against the running daemon. REPL accepts PING/STATUS/WINDOWS/DISPLAYS/QUIT/help and prints replies; exits on Ctrl-D. Makes --attach real (no longer placeholder). Parent: bd-031e54 (decomposes bd-aaea73).
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm fullscreen toggle result status: build directly
description: The fullscreen-toggle result status still formats window/fullscreen and failure suffixes with format!. Build those status strings directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Alt-PageUp and Alt-PageDown input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards Alt+Arrow/Home/End and plain/Shift/Ctrl PageUp/PageDown, but consumes Alt+PageUp/PageDown CSI (`ESC [ 5 ; 3 ~` / `ESC [ 6 ; 3 ~`). Some terminals/web apps use these modified page-navigation keys. Forward them with the Alt modifier.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward PageUp/PageDown input to pages
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: browser input routing handles text, Backspace, Tab, Enter, Delete, and arrows, while CSI PageUp/PageDown (5~/6~) are ignored. Pages and focused controls need real PageUp/PageDown key events for scrolling and navigation. Scope: parse ordered CSI 5~/6~ input, dispatch DevTools PageUp/PageDown keyDown/keyUp events, and add focused parser/key coverage. Avoid overlap with active Home/End work.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native pane move should avoid collecting window names
description: Native pane move handling saves the old focused window, reorders panes, then collects all pane window names into a temporary Vec just to find the previous focused pane index. Replace that with a direct slice scan over panes. Scope: native pane move/focus bookkeeping only; avoid browser, doctor, and active footer/status slices. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: pace idle captures after unchanged frames
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: kittwm-browser now skips unchanged uploads/placements, but it still captures the browser every fixed 250ms while idle. Headless capture can be expensive even when no terminal output is emitted. Scope: add adaptive idle capture pacing after consecutive no-output frames, with env overrides for active/idle intervals and focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm launcher graphics: bound preview row rects on narrow scenes
description: kittwm launcher preview graphics still use fixed 10px margins and width-20 sizing for candidate rows. On narrow scene widths those row rects can exceed the footprint even after apps row bounds were fixed. Reuse bounded row geometry for launcher preview rows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm browser DevTools target endpoint: build directly
description: Headless browser create_target still formats the Chrome DevTools /json/new endpoint URL. Build the endpoint directly while preserving percent-encoded URL behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: clean empty first-launch workspace
description:
User-facing first-launch UX: default `kittwm` should open a clean empty workspace instead of immediately spawning a flickering shell pane/footer.

Scope:
- Change native kittwm default session to start with an empty workspace and a simple top bar showing current workspace id and time.
- Preserve opt-in old behavior via an env knob such as `KITTWM_STARTUP_TERMINAL=1`.
- Add a keyboard shortcut to launch the default terminal command from the empty workspace (e.g. Ctrl-A Enter / Ctrl-A t).
- Add Ctrl-A ? help overlay listing native shortcuts.
- Avoid noisy footer by default; keep concise top bar/status only.
- Keep socket spawning (`SPAWN_PTY`) working.
- Add focused tests for empty shell view/top bar/help text/terminal launch helper behavior.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm` no longer starts with an automatic zsh pane by default.
- Empty workspace renders a stable top bar with workspace id and time.
- Ctrl-A ? surfaces a shortcut list.
- Shortcut launches a terminal pane from empty workspace.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add nix flake
description: Add a nix flake to the project. See tendril and other rust caco projects as examples for implementation details and best practices.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kittwm-terminal events scene: build heading label directly
description: Small kittwm-terminal scene performance cleanup: terminal_events_scene_for_cols builds the events heading layer label with format!. Build the `kittwm-terminal-events-heading:count=... ms=...` label directly into a preallocated String while preserving event scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm top bar: bound huge workspace label text rendering
description: BarModel::render_i3bar builds the full workspace-chip string before clipping, so a pathological workspace label can allocate/copy a huge string during bar redraws and contribute to UI churn. Bound workspace labels for text rendering before formatting while preserving normal labels.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support native PTY cursor save and restore
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm handles many cursor movement sequences but not cursor save/restore. Shell prompts and TUIs commonly use DECSC/DECRC (`ESC 7` / `ESC 8`) or SCO-style `CSI s` / `CSI u` around status updates; without support, subsequent output can land in the wrong cell and corrupt READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Track a saved cursor in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `ESC 7` / `ESC 8` and `CSI s` / `CSI u`.
- Preserve saved cursor sensibly across resize by clamping.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Tests demonstrate saving, moving, writing, restoring, and writing at the saved position for both ESC and CSI variants.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: kittwm text JSON read wrappers
description:
Docs follow-up for `kittwm --read-text-json` and `--read-scrollback-json`.

Scope:
- After source lands, document the stable CLI wrappers near existing read-text/read-scrollback automation docs.
- Mention they map to `READ_TEXT_JSON` / `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON`.
- Docs-only; no code changes.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention both wrappers.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward F7 input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser has started forwarding function keys but still consumes F7 (`ESC [ 18 ~`). Browsers and web apps use F7 for caret browsing and feature shortcuts. Forward F7 through DevTools key events.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: add composition plane ordering helpers
description: Add typed SDK helpers for composition-plane ordering in the kittwm architecture contract: compare plane z-order, ask if one plane is above another, and expose ordered plane names. This helps apps and tests reason about app/chrome/overlay layering without hard-coded z-index comparisons. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm record: dump N capture frames to PNG sequence for debugging
description: kitwm record [--frames N] [--out DIR] runs the chosen backend for N frames, writes each capture as PNG into DIR (default /tmp/kitwm-record-<timestamp>/), prints the directory path. Useful for: visually verifying the capture pipeline without TCC permissions on the WM terminal; bug reports; making demo gifs. Works with --capture SPEC. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md: standalone rich kitty-graphics markdown viewer
description: Operator clarification: the markdown renderer should ship as a full kittui Markdown viewer, usable both as a kittwm-native app and directly in a normal terminal without `kittwm replace`. Add a `kittui-md` (or `kittui md`) viewer binary/command that reads a Markdown file/stdin, renders it with rich kittui/kitty graphics using the UI component layer, and supports normal terminal use even outside kittwm. When launched inside kittwm it can use KITTWM_SOCKET/KITTWM_WINDOW context for future interactivity, but it must not require that context. Include navigation/scrolling plan, terminal size handling, and smoke tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Match localized desktop names in installed app parser
description: The pooled-SSH fallback includes localized `Name[...]` values in its desktop candidate rows, but the installed/local Rust parser ignores localized names. Add localized name metadata to LinuxDesktopApp matching and JSON output so healthy remote `kittwm apps` can find localized launcher names like the fallback path.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-renderer: use stable absolute placements in raw compositor
description: The raw compositor run_loop_with path still uses Runtime::place_raw_frame/place_uploaded_image plus an extra cursor move and Unicode placeholders for app frames. That keeps writing placeholder text into app cells and duplicates cursor positioning even though the native/session path now uses stable absolute z-indexed placements. Switch raw compositor app surfaces to stable_absolute placement options with the app z-index and rely on Runtime placement bytes only (no extra cursor move, no embed placeholder) to reduce flicker/text-image fighting.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm daily-help tests: build label prefixes directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("kittwm-daily-help-backdrop:{kind}:")` and heading equivalents in `daily_help_scenes_label_existing_quickstart_examples_and_cheat` with direct preallocated label-prefix helpers. Scope only kittwm daily-help test expectation construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; preserve daily guide scene label coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: [broken-on-main] native_showcase_scene_signature_matches_visual_golden failing
description: `cargo test -p kittui-cli session::native_pane_tests --lib -- --nocapture` fails in `session::native_pane_tests::native_showcase_scene_signature_matches_visual_golden`: the generated showcase signature includes the `C-a g launcher` shortcut and an extra help-overlay key row, while `crates/kittui-cli/tests/fixtures/kittwm_showcase_scene_signature.json` still expects the older text without launcher. This appears pre-existing relative to bd-f1c4d1 layout changes; no existing bead found for the test name or showcase golden launcher shortcut.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-md definitions-only mode for glossary inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --definitions mode that prints only parsed definition-list entries with term and definition text. This complements --tables and --code-blocks for focused inspections. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launcher: bound graphical scene labels
description: Audit finding: `kittwm launcher-kitty` / launcher scene JSON bounds geometry and candidate row counts, but serializes raw query and candidate names into backdrop, heading, and row labels. Pathological launcher query/candidate strings can inflate scene JSON/kitty payloads and redraw allocation churn. Clip launcher scene label fields while preserving useful selection diagnostics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Tiling layout integration: split/stack/tab + floating mix
description:
Extend LayoutNode to allow Floating windows sitting on top of a tiled tree. Compositor renders the tiled tree first (one Scene::Image per pane), then Floating windows with z-order. Add resize and move keybindings (Mod+arrow / Mod+drag) routed through the Compositor.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md html-json inspection mode
description: Add --html-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown HTML fragments, emitting schema_version plus indexed kind/source records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Centralize kittwm pooled SSH command helpers
description: During bd-0686d9 and dev-3 bd-2d4596, pooled SSH command construction started to be shared conceptually across apps, windows/displays, and terminal forwarding. The current code keeps the helper local to `kittwm.rs`, and the agents deliberately avoided extracting a broader API mid-flight to prevent overlap. Consider a follow-up cleanup that centralizes pooled SSH env/script execution conventions and test helpers once the SSH workflow stabilizes. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-0686d9 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm architecture scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: architecture_scene still builds the architecture backdrop label with format! from layer, plane, surface, and schema counts. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add indexes to remaining kittui-md metadata JSON arrays
description: Add stable index fields to remaining kittui-md metadata-json array records: outline, footnotes, definitions, math, html, metadata_blocks, code_blocks, and tables. Keep scalar footnote_references unchanged. Add targeted tests and docs.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed pane resized event
description:
SDK/docs follow-up for runtime pane resize events. Add typed SDK parsing and docs for `pane_resized` emitted by the native event stream.

Scope:
- Add `KittwmEvent::PaneResized` variant, kind mapping, and parser tests using the agreed `pane_resized` event name.
- Update docs/wm.md or docs/README.md event list.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients get typed `PaneResized` events.
- Docs list `pane_resized`.
- Targeted kittwm-sdk event tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: List and launch remote Linux desktop apps without remote kittwm
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: when the remote host lacks kittwm, `kittwm remote HOST list apps QUERY` and `kittwm remote HOST launch QUERY` should discover Linux `.desktop` launchers as well as PATH commands, and launch selected desktop entries via gtk-launch when available or their Exec command. This improves local kittwm pooled-SSH fallback behaviour for Linux remote hosts.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: shell/app launch error UX for missing commands
description: QA gap: launching a missing command or invalid app should produce clear feedback in command output/status/logs and not leave ghost panes or stale pending queue entries. Cover kittwm spawn, launch aliases, apps-launch-first, and restore-session commands that reference unavailable programs. Acceptance: failures are visible/actionable, no ghost pane remains, pending/status are consistent.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Report remote app fallback launch method
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: when local kittwm launches an app on a remote host without a working remote kittwm, the output says only kind/name/pid. Report whether the launch used PATH, macOS open, gtk-launch, gio launch, or raw desktop Exec so users can diagnose pooled-SSH graphical app forwarding behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm session scene: build row labels directly
description: Small kittwm session scene performance cleanup: session_scene builds each pane row label with format! from bounded window/title/command labels, weight, and focus state. Build session row labels directly into preallocated Strings while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: bindings-python: add ctypes platform binding
description:
Large gap: kittui can now be used from Rust, CLI, C, and TypeScript, but Python shell/platform automation still has to hand-roll ctypes calls to the C ABI. Python is a common glue layer for terminal tooling and external platforms.

Scope:
- Add a small first-party Python ctypes binding under `bindings/python`.
- Support runtime config, ABI version, probe, unplace, place, place_at, place_many, place_many_at, and place_many_channels.
- Include pure stdlib unittest coverage using a fake CDLL so the API is validated without requiring a built shared library.
- Add a README with usage examples.

Acceptance:
- `python3 -m unittest discover bindings/python` passes.
- Binding can normalize scene dicts/JSON strings and parse channel JSON.
- No changes to Rust ABI are required.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm CLI: size graphical scenes from terminal width
description: Resize follow-up: kittwm's own graphical helper scenes (`doctor`, `info`, `status`, `shortcuts`) use explicit env vars or static `COLUMNS`, then fixed defaults. In hosted/resized PTYs, `COLUMNS` can be stale even when terminal detection knows the current canvas width. Add a shared scene-width helper that falls back to `TerminalInfo::detect().columns` before fixed defaults, preserving explicit valid overrides and caps; update focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kitwm runtime keymap: Ctrl-A prefix actions for launch/quit/focus placeholders
description: Wire the keymap config foundation into the live session. Parse Ctrl-letter terminal bytes into ctrl-modified chars; load default or --keymap PATH; track prefix state; execute at least Ctrl-A Enter / Ctrl-A c / Ctrl-A | / Ctrl-A q visibly in the runtime. v1 can implement launch + quit and log not-yet-implemented actions, but the operator should see prefix mode and last action in the footer. Parent: bd-031e54; follows bd-89ae11.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Perform critical analysis of user-facing issues using Tendril
description:
Use Tendril or similar analysis tools to critically examine captured screenshots and test outputs, identifying usability issues from the user perspective. Focus on catching issues like SPP (Scalable Pixel Protocol) not being usable or functional from end-user viewpoint.

Acceptance criteria:
- Critical analysis completed on all captured test data
- User-facing issues identified and documented
- Issues like SPP usability problems clearly reported
- Analysis results feed into bug/feature prioritization
creator: helsinki:cacophony:harry

title: docs: SDK exact bytes paste and mouse helper status
description:
Docs follow-up after SDK exact byte, paste, and mouse input helpers landed. Update SDK/WM docs to mention `SurfaceHandle::send_bytes`, `send_bytes_b64`, `paste_bytes`, `paste_bytes_b64`, `send_mouse`, and `MouseEvent`, all gated by SendInput.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md and docs/wm.md or docs/README.md.
- Keep concise and docs-only.
- Mention the helpers map to existing `SEND_BYTES_B64`, `PASTE_BYTES_B64`, and `SEND_MOUSE` socket paths.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect typed SDK input automation helpers.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal events scene: build kinds label directly
description: Small kittwm-terminal scene performance cleanup: terminal_events_scene_for_cols still builds the event-kinds layer label with format!("kittwm-terminal-events-kinds:{summary}"). Build that label directly into a preallocated String while preserving bounded event summaries.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-cli: expose kittwm chrome scene command
description:
Large integration gap: kittwm has reusable `WindowChromeTheme`, but shell scripts and external render pipelines cannot ask `kittui` to build that same WM chrome as a scene/PNG. This keeps kittwm chrome separate from kittui as the renderer substrate.

Scope:
- Add `kittui wm-chrome` command that emits the kittwm window chrome scene using `kittui_wm::chrome::WindowChromeTheme`.
- Flags: `--title`, `--focused`, `--floating`, `-w/--width`, `-h/--height`.
- Support existing global modes (`--scene-json`, `--json`, render/place paths) through the normal emit pipeline.
- Add targeted CLI unit coverage.

Acceptance:
- `kittui --scene-json wm-chrome ...` produces a Scene with `wm-chrome:*:<title>` labels.
- Targeted kittui CLI tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md metadata JSON includes table layout metrics
description: Extend kittui-md --metadata-json table entries with computed column widths and footprint rows/cols from MarkdownTable. This lets downstream renderers inspect table sizing without recomputing it. Add tests for the JSON shape.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native browser path: build default directly
description: native_browser_cmd uses format! for the default screenshot path. Add a direct path builder while preserving the /tmp/kittwm-native-browser-<pid>.png default.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md counts inspection mode
description: Add --counts as a focused output mode that prints the same document counts exposed in metadata JSON, without full stats/source provenance or detailed arrays. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native idle loop should default below 10 FPS
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: the native compositor drops to idle mode after clean frames, but the default KITTWM_IDLE_FPS is still 10 FPS. On static workspaces that means continued capture/hash/status/chrome work ten times per second, contributing to slowness and flicker on idle screens. Scope: lower the default idle FPS to a calmer value while preserving the KITTWM_IDLE_FPS override and active FPS behavior; update focused pacing tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm send-mouse request: build directly
description: send_mouse_request validates event/coordinates and then formats a temporary payload before passing it to automation_request. Build the SEND_MOUSE daemon request directly into one preallocated String while preserving event validation, 1-indexed coordinate checks, and output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-status: bound graphical scene labels
description: Audit finding: `kittwm status-kitty`/status-scene-json bounds card geometry but serializes raw focus/layout/workspace/socket values into scene labels. Pathological daemon/status strings can inflate scene JSON/kitty payloads and redraw allocation churn. Clip status scene label fields while preserving readable diagnostic labels.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: machine-readable shortcut catalog
description:
First-launch shortcut discovery should have a machine-readable surface, not only text output / in-session overlay.

Scope:
- Add a structured native shortcut catalog in kittui-cli shared shortcut module.
- Add local CLI output via `kittwm shortcuts-json` and `kittwm --shortcuts-json`.
- Add native socket `SHORTCUTS_JSON` and include it in HELP / HELP_JSON / error help.
- Keep `kittwm shortcuts`, `--shortcuts`, and `C-a ?` text output unchanged.
- No live keybinding behavior changes.

Acceptance:
- JSON includes entries for terminal launch, help toggle, and exit shortcut.
- Targeted CLI/daemon/module tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm doctor scene: build display label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: doctor_scene still builds the doctor display layer label with format! from the already-computed display tuning label. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm send-key queued reply: build directly
description: queue_native_send_key still uses format! for SEND_KEY_QUEUED command/window/key/bytes replies. Build that reply directly while preserving key queue coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK scrollback wait test socket path: build directly
description: The SDK scrollback_and_wait_helpers_send_expected_commands test still formats its temp Unix socket path inline. Reuse the direct test_socket_path helper while preserving scrollback/wait socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm: flicker between frames — drop \x1b[H\x1b[J + only re-emit changed windows
description: Operator: 'image flickers between frames. should be a smooth consistent WM experience, only insides of windows are latency bound but can't cause main WM to flicker'. Cause: session::run_loop writes \x1b[H\x1b[J every frame to clear, then re-emits each window's upload+placement+placeholder. The clear wipes the previous frame before the new pixels arrive over the slow tmux passthrough, so the operator sees a black flash per frame. Fix: stop clearing the screen, kitty atomically replaces an image at the same id; only emit placement+placeholder on first frame per window or when footprint changes; never re-emit when nothing changed; track last-seen window set so removed windows get a single delete escape. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui affordances unified theme model for inline panels controls
description:
Deep audit gap: kittui now has reusable inline tokens, but broader affordance components still use separate Palette/Tone/control color logic. Create a first-class shared theme model for kittui-affordances so inline chips, panels, controls, markdown components, kittwm chrome, and kittwm-bar can all resolve from one coherent token source.

Scope:
- Define a reusable theme/token API beyond inline-only colors (surface fills, borders, highlights, focus, muted text, danger/warning/success, control states).
- Bridge existing `Palette`/`Tone`, `InlineChipColors`, panel chrome, and controls without breaking public behavior.
- Add tests proving representative components resolve from the shared theme source.
- Keep kittui-core primitive-only; this belongs in kittui-affordances or a sibling theme module.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: WM-thread architecture: per-backend Pump (capture+encode), non-blocking UI tick
description: Move capture + PNG/raw-RGB encode off the UI thread. Each attached backend becomes a Pump with its own worker thread that loops capture -> encode -> store-in-slot. MultiCompositor::tick(layout) reads slots non-blockingly and returns whatever's there. UI thread keeps running input, resize, layout, hit-test at 60fps regardless of slow windows. Slow windows just show their previous frame until they catch up.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm apps graphics: saturate app row count
description: kittwm apps scene computes rows by casting path/macos app list lengths to u16 before adding and clamping. Pathological app lists can wrap before clamp and produce wrong scene metadata. Use saturated row accounting before clamping.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm kitty single-payload headers: build directly
description: Replace formatted header construction in `kittui-kitty` single-payload upload paths with direct preallocated String assembly. Scope only `single_payload(...)` in `crates/kittui-kitty/src/lib.rs`; preserve exact file/shared-memory upload grammar tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: make fullscreen toggle affect compositor placement
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm exposes `fullscreen.toggle`, but it is still only footer state. As a terminal WM, fullscreen should change the focused window's real compositor placement/chrome, just like the recently wired focus and float actions.

Scope:
- Add compositor fullscreen state and helpers for focused-or-first fullscreen toggle/query.
- Add `Layout::bounds()` so fullscreen can occupy the tiled layout envelope when available.
- Make raw and Scene composition use the fullscreen target rect before tiled/floating mode.
- Carry fullscreen metadata in `RawFrame` and show it in the raw terminal chrome label.
- Wire `Action::FullscreenToggle` in `session.rs` to compositor fullscreen state while preserving footer text.
- Add targeted compositor tests for fullscreen target footprint and toggling.

Acceptance:
- `fullscreen.toggle` updates the focused window's compositor fullscreen state.
- Raw frames report fullscreen metadata and use layout bounds for focused fullscreen windows.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Use wmctrl class output in remote fallback listings
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm window listing fallback uses plain `wmctrl -l`, which lists titles but not app classes. Prefer `wmctrl -lx` when available so `kittwm remote HOST windows QUERY` can match app/class names on X11 remotes before falling back to xdotool.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add source to remote app JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote window/display listing JSON reports `source`, but remote app JSON does not consistently report whether output came from a healthy delegated remote `kittwm` or the pooled-SSH fallback. Add source metadata to remote app JSON so scripts can diagnose delegation vs fallback across list/first/launch modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3
spoken_name: add source to remote app json

title: kittwm-bar: add first-party SDK top bar app
description:
User-facing first-launch follow-up: the empty workspace has an internal `kittui-bar`-style top bar, but we should also provide a small first-party SDK app that can render/query the same basic bar content.

Scope:
- Add a small `kittwm-bar` first-party binary in `kittui-cli`.
- It should use `kittwm-sdk` for status when a socket is available, and gracefully fall back to env/defaults when not connected.
- Print a concise one-line bar with current workspace id (initially `1`), pane count/empty state, and time.
- Include `--once` default behavior and a simple `--json` mode for machine-readable status.
- Add targeted tests for bar model/rendering without requiring a live daemon.
- Do not wire it into live session process spawning yet.

Acceptance:
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm-bar` works.
- Tests cover offline render/model behavior.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show footer drag scene chip only when drag is active
description: UX follow-up: footer affordance scenes now have a drag chip label, but it is emitted even when no active drag is present. Make the drag chip conditional on the footer carrying an active drag segment, while keeping focus/state always available for non-empty footer state. Filed from ongoing kittwm footer visual-state audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native chrome: avoid duplicate scene-id hashing in redraw keys
description: Follow-up to bd-140437: native_shell_chrome_scene_key embeds chrome.scene.id().0 and native_shell_chrome_scene_visual_hash also recomputes/hashes scene.id().0 before hashing labels. Scene id normalization/serialization is not free, so redraw-key checks compute it twice per chrome scene. Scope: compute scene id once and hash only labels as the label component, preserving visual paint and label invalidation coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm headless browser profile path: build directly
description: HeadlessBrowserApp profile temp directory construction still uses format! for kittui-headless-chrome-PID-ID. Build that profile directory name directly while preserving uniqueness/scoping coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm raw chrome: avoid full titles in redraw keys
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: raw_frame_chrome_text was bounded, but raw_frame_chrome_key still formats the full app title every frame. Pathological titles can cause allocation/hash churn, and changes outside the visible pane width should not invalidate chrome redraws. Scope: key raw chrome from bounded visible chrome text plus state/footprint, preserving redraws for visible title/focus/mode/geometry changes and adding focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm raw frame shm names: build directly
description: Replace raw-frame shared-memory filename `format!` construction in `crates/kittui/src/lib.rs` with direct preallocated String assembly. Scope only kittui raw frame `/dev/shm` kitty transport name/path construction used by kittwm graphics transport.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: bound DevTools websocket reads
description: Browser reliability follow-up: `cdp_send_raw` writes a DevTools command and then loops on `socket.read()` until the matching id arrives. If Chrome wedges or the websocket stops producing responses, kittwm/browser capture/input/resize can hang indefinitely. Set bounded read/write timeouts on the underlying DevTools TCP socket after connect and route timeout errors as ordinary command failures. Add focused helper coverage for the timeout value.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm SDK events request: build directly
description: Typed SDK events_ms still formats the EVENTS request string after clamping. Build the request directly into a small preallocated buffer while preserving clamping and event parsing behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description:
Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process ("server") and a thin viewer process ("client"), communicating over a Unix domain socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux's $TMUX or X11's $DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux's pane sharing) — useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock).\n\nMakes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.

---

Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process (server) and a thin viewer process (client), communicating over a Unix domain socket at KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux TMUX or X11 DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux pane sharing) - useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock). Makes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session.
