## Merged duplicate: bd-fb5d9d

Title: kittui-wm daemon: socket server + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY

New binary kittui-wm-daemon (or kittui-wm serve subcommand) that listens on a Unix socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock). Owns all backends + Pumps. Wire format: length-prefixed bincode. v1 messages: CmdListWindows, CmdTick, CmdInput(InputEvent), CmdResize { cols, rows }, CmdQuit. Daemon survives client disconnects. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-aaea73

Title: kittui-wm attach: thin viewer client

kittui-wm attach (or kittui-wm with no args + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY set) connects to the daemon socket and runs the existing run_loop as a renderer: receives scene snapshots from CmdTick replies, posts InputEvents on stdin parse, never touches backends directly. Multiple clients can attach to the same session simultaneously. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-b45025

Title: Prove: detach + reattach preserves session state

Integration test: spawn daemon with a FakeServer backend, attach client A, send pointer events, detach. Spawn client B (new process), attach to same socket, verify it sees the same session state (windows, focus, layout) immediately. Asserts the daemon outlived A and B starts at the same state, not a fresh one. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-1e1b87

Title: End-to-end SSH demo: serve here, attach from another terminal

Practical demo + script: launch kittui-wm serve on host A, ssh from host B with KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:0 forwarded, run kittui-wm attach. Confirm Quartz capture pixels travel over SSH and pointer events route back. Capture screenshots from both terminals to prove same-session-different-viewers. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-keymap: avoid full string builds when clipping scene labels
description: Audit finding: recent keymap scene label clipping bounds serialized payloads, but `keymap_scene` still builds full `binding.chord_string()` and full custom action strings before clipping. A pathological custom keymap can still allocate/scan huge strings during graphical diagnostics. Build bounded keymap label fields directly.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward Shift-Backspace input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards Backspace plus Ctrl/Alt Backspace CSI-u, but consumes Shift+Backspace CSI-u (`ESC [ 8 ; 2 u` / `ESC [ 127 ; 2 u`). Forward it with the Shift modifier so modified backspace input is not swallowed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui inline chip defaults to kitty graphics
description:
Follow-up inline chip behavior: default should be kittui/kitty graphics, not ANSI text.

User expectation:
- `kittui inline chip --text abcdef` should output inline text with a unicode placeholder at the left and enough trailing space for the chip width, e.g. `Uabcdef `, while also emitting/side-effecting kitty graphics for a pill-shaped chip background sized to text width + padding.
- Existing text-only implementation should remain available under explicit formats like ascii/plain/ansi/tmux.

Scope:
- Change default inline chip format to kitty/graphics-oriented output.
- Implement an inline kitty mode that builds a one-row chip scene using existing chip chrome and text-width sizing.
- Output should include a width-bearing placeholder/text fallback so shell layout has correct width.
- Keep `--format plain`, `--format ansi`, and `--format tmux` for non-graphics use.
- Add or rename ascii/plain as needed without breaking tests.
- Validate with targeted tests and build. If full terminal placement cannot be reliable in tests, validate dry-run/JSON/bytes shape plus fallback output.

Out of scope:
- tmux post-layout graphical replacement.
- prompt-specific escaping wrappers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm overlays: avoid full scans when truncating huge text fields
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: text overlay renderers now clamp width/rows, but shared truncate_cells first counts every char before taking the visible prefix. Pathological launcher queries, candidate names, or overlay messages can still cause per-redraw full-string scans before clipping. Scope: rewrite truncate_cells to detect/build a bounded prefix in one pass and add focused coverage for huge fields.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm PANES_JSON reply: build directly
description: PANES_JSON replies still use format! only to append a newline to the panes JSON value. Reuse the direct JSON-line helper while preserving panes JSON coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser status: precompute truncated URL label
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: browser status metadata is precomputed, but browser_status_text_for_cols_with_metadata still calls truncate(url, 40) each loop. The URL is immutable for a browser process, so this is avoidable repeated status-row work. Scope: precompute the truncated URL label once in real_main, route the loop through a precomputed-label status formatter, and keep existing public/test helpers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: make kittwm architecture contract kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make the kittwm architecture/separation contract available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add architecture-scene-json/platform-contract-scene-json and architecture-kitty/platform-contract-kitty aliases that render the SDK ArchitectureContract layers and composition planes as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing architecture-json behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm architecture-scene-json emits a kittui Scene summarizing layer count, plane count, and first-party native surface count.
- kittwm architecture-kitty/architecture-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing architecture-json/platform-contract-json output remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from ArchitectureContract::current().
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native help overlay log: build directly
description: The native prefix help toggle path formats its boolean diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact debug text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show tiled title drag reorder affordance
description: UX audit follow-up: tiled title rows now support drag-to-reorder and expose title_drag metadata, but the visible title chrome still has no compact reorder handle marker. Add a bounded tiled title drag/reorder affordance marker in live title text so users can discover that tiled title rows are draggable without opening docs/status JSON. Keep floating markers and resized markers distinct. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/quality audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: force 8-bit character size in raw mode
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: raw mode now clears input/local/output line-discipline flags, but it does not normalize c_cflag character size to CS8. POSIX raw mode should clear CSIZE and set CS8 so high-bit UTF-8/Alt/control-sequence bytes are not truncated by a host tty configured for 7-bit input. Scope: add a raw_mode_cflag helper, apply it in RawMode::enter, and add focused tests preserving unrelated control flags.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm daemon native-stale test socket path: build directly
description: The daemon native stale-socket cleanup test still formats its per-process socket filename with format!. Reuse the direct test_socket_filename helper for that temp path while preserving native stale-file cleanup coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK event parser tests: build event lines directly
description: Replace test-only `format!(r#"{{\"kind\":\"{kind}\",...}}"#)` in `event_parser_handles_known_and_unknown_events` with a direct preallocated JSON-line helper. Scope only kittwm SDK event parser test string construction in `crates/kittwm-sdk/src/lib.rs`; preserve known/unknown event parsing coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Raw RGB transmission via kitty f=32 in kittui-kitty
description: upload_still_ex grows a CaptureFormat option: Png (current default) or Rgba32 (f=32). When Rgba32, skip PNG encode and emit raw RGBA bytes base64'd directly. Update kittui::Runtime::place to accept a CaptureFormat hint on Scene or via a RuntimeBuilder option. Add an exact-grammar test that asserts the f=32 path emits a=t,f=32,s=W,v=H,m=0,q=2 followed by base64 RGBA. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon semantic publish test request: build directly
description: The daemon semantic publish test still formats its SEMANTIC_PUBLISH JSON request with format!. Build that test request directly while preserving semantic snapshot publish/read coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Use null filter in remote app fallback JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: installed/local `kittwm apps --json` emits `filter:null` when no query is supplied, but no-remote-kittwm pooled-SSH fallback app JSON currently emits `filter:""`. Make fallback list/first/launch/error JSON use null for absent filters so scripts see the same shape across healthy remote kittwm and fallback modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kitwm launcher --select/--launch-selection: choose numbered preview row
description: Extend the boxed 'kitwm launcher' preview with selection semantics. Add --select N to highlight/print a selected candidate row and --launch-selection to spawn that selected row (PATH command directly, macOS app via open -a) and report pid. This makes the preview behave like a future floating menu selection model. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: add composite RGBA surface adapter
description:
Surface runtime gap: kittui-wm now has scene and RGBA frame NativeSurface adapters, but no small native composite surface that can combine child RGBA captures into a single frame for future child-frame present/runtime wiring.

Scope:
- Add a capture-only `CompositeFrameSurface` in `kittui_wm::native`.
- It should accept positioned RGBA child frames (or `RgbaFrameSurface` snapshots) and produce one `NativeFrame::Rgba` with simple source-over alpha blending into a fixed pixel canvas.
- Expose `SurfaceKind::Composite`, stable id/title metadata, capture-only capabilities, and frame size.
- Validate canvas dimensions and reject child placements that overflow impossible dimensions; clipping at canvas bounds is OK.
- Resize/input should return explicit unsupported errors.
- Add focused tests for metadata, composition, alpha blending, clipping, and invalid canvas rejection.
- Do not wire it into live kittwm defaults.

Acceptance:
- Runtime/composite code has a native capture surface for composing RGBA child frames.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Home and End key input to pages
description: kittwm-browser forwards Enter/Backspace/Tab/Delete/arrows but still ignores common Home/End terminal sequences, and bracketed variants can leak fragments into text fields. Parse Home/End CSI sequences and dispatch browser Home/End key events so web text editing behaves correctly.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch: route browser launches through SDK browser surfaces
description: Make kittwm-launch use the typed kittwm-sdk Browser surface path instead of treating browser launches as raw terminal/PTY commands. This keeps first-party browser surfaces on the SDK native surface vocabulary and reduces duplicate launch quoting logic. Scope to kittwm-launch plus SDK helper/tests; avoid kittwm session reservation/control-plane internals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add SDK helpers for kittwm dirty-frame status
description: Operator-requested kittwm render-performance/SDK cleanliness follow-up: native status reports dirty-frame metrics, but SDK consumers still have to inspect raw DirtyFrameStatus fields to find clean/skipped frames or changed tile counts. Add typed dirty-frame convenience helpers on DirtyFrameStatus/NativePaneDetail plus pane iterators on PanesStatus/Status, with tests/docs, so render-performance automation can query clean/dirty panes directly.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-wm: add RGBA frame surface adapter
description:
Surface runtime gap: external/render-pipeline RGBA frames can be placed through kitty transport, but kittui-wm lacks a simple NativeSurface adapter for pre-rendered RGBA streams.

Scope:
- Add `RgbaFrameSurface` in `kittui_wm::native`.
- It should wrap/update a caller-provided RGBA frame, expose `SurfaceKind::Composite` or an appropriate existing kind with capture-only capabilities, stable id/title metadata, frame size, and `capture_surface()` returning `NativeFrame::Rgba`.
- Validate/update frame length against width*height*4 and reject invalid dimensions/payloads.
- Resize/input should return explicit unsupported errors.
- Add focused tests for metadata, capture, update, and invalid payload rejection.
- Do not wire it into live session defaults.

Acceptance:
- Runtime/composite code has a safe capture-only adapter for pre-rendered RGBA frames.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK events iterator tests: build socket path directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("/tmp/kwei-{}-{}.sock", ...)` in `events_iter_ms_iterates_bounded_socket_batch` with the existing direct socket-path helper. Scope only kittwm SDK events iterator test socket path construction in `crates/kittwm-sdk/src/lib.rs`; preserve EVENTS iterator socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md pictures alias for images
description: Add --pictures as a friendly alias for kittui-md --images so users can inspect Markdown image references with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: keep raw footer below text overlays
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: the raw compositor renders launcher/picker overlays, then computes footer rows with `footer_row.max(12)` or `max(14)`. Launcher owns rows 2..=17 and picker owns rows 2..=15, so the status footer can overwrite the active overlay box/hints, causing visible flicker/corruption. Ensure the raw footer row is below the active overlay region (or otherwise outside overlay-owned rows) and add deterministic helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-terminal: use absolute status scene placements
description: Audit finding: kittwm-terminal status/events kitty rendering still uses unicode placeholder placements. Placeholder cells can leave text artifacts and fight image placement in narrow/kitty-native chrome paths. Use absolute decoration placement options without unicode placeholders and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add reset-weights aliases for kittwm pane balancing
description: Operator-requested kittwm tiling UX polish: pane resize weights are now visible as wt:<n>, but the daily-driver reset action is named only balance. Add reset-weights aliases (CLI and long option) that map to BALANCE_PANES so users can discover the “undo resize weights” action from the same vocabulary as reset-position/reset-positions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm ctrl-c debounce log: build directly
description: The raw compositor Ctrl-C debounce diagnostic still formats the count with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm info scene: build heading label directly
description: Small kittwm info scene performance cleanup: info_scene builds the heading layer label with format! from bounded socket/focus/layout labels. Build the info heading label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md plain table text honors Markdown alignment
description: Use MarkdownTable alignment metadata when building the table textbox text so plain output and component metadata preserve left/center/right padded cell layout, not just rich overlay rendering. Add renderer tests for aligned table text.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon restore-session queued test request: build directly
description: The daemon native command coverage test still formats a RESTORE_SESSION_JSON queued request with format!. Reuse the direct restore_session_json_request helper while preserving queued restore-session coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add remote forward graphical alias
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: graphical forwarding checks are exposed as forwarding/x11/gui/graphical, but users may naturally type the shorter `forward`. Add `kittwm remote HOST forward` and `doctor --forward` as aliases for graphical forwarding diagnostics and advertise them in help/completions/catalog.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: docs: document browser semantic action routing
description:
Documentation follow-up after browser semantic action routing lands. Update browser semantic and quickstart docs to explain which browser semantic actions are supported through DevTools, stale component behavior, and remaining limitations.

Scope:
- Document focus/activate/set/insert/select/scroll behavior once landed.
- Include examples using kittwm CLI or SDK helper methods against browser semantic components.
- Clarify stale-component errors and fallback screenshot behavior.

Acceptance:
- Docs match landed browser semantic action behavior.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support modified insert and delete in SEND_KEY
description: Keyboard/input UX gap: browser surfaces already expose Shift/Ctrl/Alt Insert and Delete helpers, but first-party `SEND_KEY` only accepts unmodified insert/delete. That blocks common copy/paste/editing shortcuts (for example Shift+Insert and Ctrl+Insert) from socket/CLI automation. Add shift/alt/ctrl insert/delete aliases to the daemon key parser using common xterm modifier sequences, route those labels to direct browser surfaces, and update focused parser/mapping/help coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-bar: emit kittui scene artifact
description:
SDK/kittwm-bar follow-up: now that the runtime has a top-bar model, improve `kittwm-bar` as a reusable chrome renderer without touching live session runtime.

Scope:
- Add `--scene-json` or equivalent render-artifact output to `kittwm-bar` using kittui primitives/affordances for the same one-line bar model.
- Keep `--json` model output and text output working.
- Add tests for scene/model shape.
- Do not modify `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm-bar` can produce a kittui render artifact suitable for future live chrome integration.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: theme native toast colors from config
description: Audit finding after toast geometry fixes: native toast scenes still use hardcoded Nord Chrome/Neon palettes instead of resolved kittwm chrome colors/config. This can make error/toast overlays visually mismatch the rest of themed chrome and look like random foreign UI. Use native_glass_chrome_colors() as the base toast palette while preserving error highlighting.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-renderer: skip unchanged native app placements
description: Reduce flicker and terminal graphics traffic by memoizing native app image placements in the live kittwm renderer. If a pane frame has no dirty upload and its absolute footprint did not change, skip emitting redundant kitty placement/embed bytes; if only the pixels changed at the same footprint, upload without re-placing. Keep semantics deterministic and preserve unplace cleanup for retired panes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: implement local shared-memory/file raw-frame transport
description: Implement adaptive local shared-memory and file-backed kitty graphics transfer for large local raw-frame uploads. Scope: add a transport-policy path for Runtime::place_raw_frame / kittui-kitty upload_still_rgba that can choose kitty t=s or t=f when the terminal is local, manage cleanup/fallback, and keep remote/tmux-safe defaults. Follow-up from bd-3c0dd1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward Tab input to pages
description: kittwm-browser raw input parser still drops Tab bytes, so users cannot move focus between fields/buttons in browser panes. Preserve text ordering and dispatch Tab key events through HeadlessBrowserApp.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Advertise graphical preflight in remote doctor suggestions
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote doctor output lists common local pooled-SSH commands but omits the graphical forwarding preflight. Add `kittwm remote HOST graphical` to the suggested command list so users diagnosing app launch from Ghostty/Kitty can discover the X11/waypipe preflight from the default doctor output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: verify bracketed paste and exact bytes through aliases and SDK
description: QA gap: exact bytes, send-file, paste-file, SDK send_bytes/paste_bytes, bracketed-paste wrapping, and base64 paths need coverage against real PTY mode state. Acceptance: tests prove paste wraps only when DEC ?2004 is enabled, exact bytes survive NUL/ESC/non-UTF8 where supported, and aliases/SDK produce equivalent protocol requests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md pager shows scroll position and viewport status
description: Improve kittui-md rich/interactive output by showing current offset, maximum scroll offset, viewport height, and total rendered document rows in the status line. Add pure tests for status formatting so pager progress remains clear as the viewer evolves.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Report remote kittwm startup health in doctor JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST status --json` should expose whether remote kittwm is healthy, not just whether it exists on PATH. Add remote doctor JSON fields for startup health/check result so scripts and users can distinguish healthy remote-kittwm mode from local pooled-SSH fallback mode.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kitwm apps --first / --launch-first: select or launch first filtered candidate
description: Add selection/launch primitive on top of searchable launcher candidates. 'kitwm apps --filter QUERY --first' prints the first matching PATH command or macOS app; '--launch-first' launches that candidate (PATH commands directly; macOS apps via 'open -a'). This is the command-line selection foundation for the future floating launcher menu. Parent: bd-031e54; decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm top bar: avoid format allocation for clock text
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: BarModel::render_i3bar builds the clock chip with format!(" {clock} ") on every render. This path is used by native top-bar text/scene labels and companion bar output. Scope: add a direct clock-text helper that preallocates and appends, preserve bounded render_i3bar behavior with focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: tolerate resize failures on layout changes
description: Follow-up to host terminal resize resilience: the terminal-size branch logs and tolerates hosted app resize failures, but the later `layouts != last_resized_layouts` path still calls strict `resize_native_panes(...) ?`. That path is used after layout/chrome reservation changes and can still crash the WM if a hosted app rejects a resize. Reuse the tolerant logged resize path there, keep updating layout state and repainting, and add focused coverage for the decision/log helper.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Advertise kittwm nudge in CLI help and completions
description: Operator-requested kittwm floating-control discoverability slice: the nudge alias exists, but top-level CLI help still lists move/raise/lower/resize without nudge and completion metadata only lists move/resize controls. Add nudge to user-facing CLI help/completion surfaces so precise floating pane placement is discoverable without reading docs or socket HELP.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-keymap: saturate scene row count
description: Audit finding: keymap_scene computes rows with km.bindings.len() as u16 + 5 before clamping. Pathologically large keymap files can wrap the u16 count before clamp, producing too-small scene metadata. Saturate/clamp using usize before casting and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Implement deterministic Scene normalization before hashing
description: DESIGN.md says Scene::id() must hash a deterministic normalized scene: drop empty/zero-opacity layers, flatten normal composites, snap pixel rects to 1/64 px, clamp/sort gradient stops, and remove zero-width/transparent structural no-ops. Current crates/kittui-core/src/scene.rs hashes the raw serde form directly, so semantically equivalent scenes can miss the cache and produce different kitty ids. Add a normalizer in kittui-core, test id stability/equivalence with property or fixture tests, and keep serde JSON compatibility stable.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: NativeSurface side-effect event hook
description:
Docs follow-up for NativeSurface event draining.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md, docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md, and/or docs/README.md to mention `NativeSurface` now includes side-effect event draining for PTY title/bell/OSC52/notification events, with capture-only adapters default-empty.
- Clarify daemon `EVENTS` publication semantics remain unchanged.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect the common NativeSurface side-effect event hook.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: policy-gated cached clipboard read
description:
Runtime policy gap: kittwm forwards nested-app OSC52 clipboard writes and emits `surface_clipboard_set`, but there is no explicit read policy or socket surface for retrieving the cached clipboard data.

Scope:
- Cache the latest OSC52 clipboard write observed through native surface events in daemon state.
- Add `CLIPBOARD_JSON` socket command that is denied by default unless `KITTWM_CLIPBOARD_READ=allow|1|true|yes` is set.
- When denied, return JSON with `allowed:false` and no payload.
- When allowed and cached, return JSON including selection, source window, payload_base64, payload byte length, and timestamp/seq metadata; do not read the host OS clipboard.
- When allowed but empty, return `available:false`.
- Add help/catalog entry and targeted daemon tests.
- Do not add SDK wrappers in this bead.

Acceptance:
- Clipboard reads are policy-gated and cache-only.
- Default behavior does not disclose clipboard payloads.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add placement contract iterators
description: Add SDK iterators over architecture placement contracts (`placement_contracts`, `ready_placement_contracts`) so apps can inspect all first-party app/chrome placement metadata without manually mapping native surfaces. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: make kittwm command catalog kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make the kittwm local command catalog available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add commands-scene-json and commands-kitty/commands-graphics commands that render local_command_entries as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing commands text and commands-json behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm commands-scene-json emits a kittui Scene summarizing local command categories/counts.
- kittwm commands-kitty/commands-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing commands and commands-json outputs remain unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from the local command catalog.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native status focus label: build combined title directly
description: Replace `format!("{}({title})", pane.window)` in `native_status_pane_focus_label(...)` with direct preallocated String construction. Scope only focused-pane footer/status label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing footer visibility and bounding tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-bar: trim reservation owner env
description: Burn-down finding while scanning kittwm chrome/config consistency: `kittwm_bar::apply_reservation_options` filters `KITTWM_WINDOW` using `value.trim().is_empty()` but then passes the original untrimmed value as the chrome reservation owner. Values like ` bar ` can leak whitespace into chrome reservation owner metadata/logs. Trim the owner value before passing it to `ChromeReservationRequest`, preserving the `kittwm-bar` fallback for blank/unset values; add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Fix selected chat bubble border rendering with graphics-oriented styling
description: The selected chat bubble currently renders a flat border overlay on top of the bubble. This should be replaced with a more graphics-oriented approach that modifies the /border highlighting and background/foreground colors to provide a more integrated visual effect.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: [broken-on-main] Nix build fails in native layout tests
description:
`nix build .#kittwm --no-link` reaches the kittui derivation but fails in the newly landed layout invariant tests from bd-f1c4d1. The failing tests are `session::native_pane_tests::native_layouts_apply_chrome_reservation_bands_and_gaps` and `session::native_pane_tests::native_layouts_keep_reserved_chrome_and_gaps_out_of_pane_bounds`; both construct dummy native panes, which tries to spawn `/nix/store/.../coreutils-9.10/bin/true` and fails in the Nix Darwin sandbox. Fix by making these layout-only tests avoid PTY child process construction (or mark with the existing Darwin sandbox ignore if unavoidable). Filed by kittui-dev-3 after Nix validation for stale bd-09be0e exposed the regression.

Additional Nix validation then progressed to `compose_batch_accepts_placement_origin_overrides`, which failed with `cache io error: No such file or directory` due compose batch integration tests sharing cache state across subprocesses in the Nix sandbox. Folded into this broken-on-main fix by isolating `KITTUI_CACHE_DIR` per compose batch test.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3
spoken_name: fix native layout test nix build

title: kittwm CPU raster cached-image error: build directly
description: Replace formatted cached-image unsupported error construction in `crates/kittui-render-cpu/src/rasterize.rs` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only `ImageRef::Cached` CPU rasterizer error text; preserve exact unsupported-image message.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Operator UI: live backend picker + window picker in kittui-wm
description:
Inside the running kittui-wm session, support hotkeys to spawn/attach backends (local Xvfb, XQuartz, Quartz capture target) and pick which windows to surface. Use the ratakittui design language: floating side panel listing 'available backends' and 'live windows' with arrow keys to navigate, Enter to surface, Mod+T to toggle Tiled/Floating per window.

Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-ed1c33

Title: Window picker: floating panel + Tab cycle to surface a Mac/X window

Inside the running kittui-wm session, hotkey (default Mod+Space) opens a kittui floating panel listing every window from QuartzServer::list_app_windows() + every X11 window across attached backends. Arrow keys navigate, Enter surfaces the window (calls QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)) and attaches as a new backend, or pins an existing one). Mod+W cycles surfaced windows. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-97c7a3

Title: Prove: picker selection visibly surfaces a chosen window

Drive the demo with a FakeServer holding 3 named windows; programmatically send Mod+Space, navigate to window 2, press Enter. Assert MultiCompositor now has window 2 attached as a Tiled backend and its capture is included in compose() output. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-9c76ac

Title: Doc: picker keybindings + screenshot in docs/wm.md

Document the Mod+Space picker, Mod+W cycle, Mod+1..9 quick-select. Capture a screenshot of the picker open in the kittui terminal. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md summary alias for stats
description: Add --summary as a friendly alias for kittui-md --stats so users can request the concise document summary with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm commands graphics: build category summary without Vec join
description: commands_scene builds command category diagnostics by allocating a temporary Vec<String> and joining it for the backdrop label. Build the small category summary directly into one String to reduce graphics scene allocation churn while preserving labels.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md anchors-json inspection mode
description: Add --anchors-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for heading anchors, emitting schema_version plus indexed level/anchor/text records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: bound graphical help overlay height
description: Audit follow-up: graphical help overlay width is now bounded, but height still uses all shortcut lines plus two rows at y=2. On short terminals, the kitty scene can extend below the visible area and corrupt/overlap bottom chrome. Pass terminal rows into graphical chrome scene construction and bound help overlay y/height to visible rows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: inline affordances: style-specific animated glare/pulse/reflection effects
description: Add style-specific phase-reactive visual layers for inline chip/badge/segment/divider/row scenes: glass gets periodic glare, neon gets pulse/glow, metal gets moving/reflection-like shimmer, chrome gets a crisp highlight. Must loop without hard cuts and use kitty native animation frames uploaded in one shot.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm architecture scene: build heading label directly
description: Small kittwm architecture scene performance cleanup: architecture_scene builds the heading layer label with format! from the static contract kind. Build the architecture heading label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: support native PTY scroll regions
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm scrolls the entire screen whenever output reaches the bottom row. Full-screen TUIs and pagers commonly set DEC scroll margins with `CSI top;bottom r` so only a viewport/body region scrolls while headers/status bars remain fixed. Without this, rendered panes and READ_TEXT snapshots corrupt status lines.

Scope:
- Track a vertical scroll region in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI top;bottom r`, including reset/defaults.
- Make newline-at-bottom scroll only the active region and keep content outside the region fixed.
- Clamp/reset margins sensibly across resize and alternate-screen creation.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test shows scrolling inside a region preserves header/footer lines.
- Test shows `CSI r` reset returns to whole-screen scrolling.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Use null filter in remote listing JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: pooled-SSH remote app fallback JSON now uses `filter:null` when no query is supplied, but window/display listing JSON still emits an empty string for absent filters. Make remote window/display listing JSON use null for absent filters while preserving strings when a query is supplied, so scripts get consistent semantics across app/window/display remote modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

Follow-up from bd-502737 and bd-8b5926. Add stable kittwm CLI wrappers for semantic snapshot/action/focus so users and scripts do not need raw `--attach -c` protocol strings.

Scope:

- Reuse existing socket/display targeting flags.
- Keep action payload JSON passed as a string/file-safe value and return daemon reply directly.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm --semantic-snapshot focused` prints semantic JSON.
- `kittwm --semantic-action focused COMPONENT ACTION JSON` and `--semantic-focus focused COMPONENT` call the corresponding socket verbs.
- Help text documents the wrappers.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: add kittui-ghostty PTY command mode

This should run a command in a pseudo-terminal sized to the requested cols/rows, capture the VT byte stream, feed it through libghostty-vt, and render a deterministic PNG. This improves evidence images for commands that depend on terminal dimensions/colors.

Acceptance:
- kittui-ghostty supports --pty-command COMMAND.
- PTY size follows --cols/--rows.
- Captured PTY bytes render through the existing libghostty-vt PNG path and compose with --scroll.
- Existing stdin, --demo, --command, and --timelapse-demo modes still work.
- Generate/post a fresh PTY-rendered headless Ghostty image in the session.
- Run focused validation.

title: Pump: per-backend worker thread for capture+encode

SharedServer wraps Arc<dyn XServer> so a backend can be shared between the synchronous path and the pump. Update MultiCompositor::attach_async to spawn a Pump. Parent epic: bd-031e54

The session loop can skip unchanged PNG uploads afterwards, but the expensive PNG render already happened. Cache and reuse the last PNG frame when the Ghostty PTY output channel is idle, clearing the cache on resize or new output; add deterministic cache helper coverage.

title: kittwm: add native pane balance command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes now have weights and resize controls, but there is no first-class way to rebalance a layout back to equal weights. A terminal WM needs a quick balance/reset operation after manual resizing.

Scope:

title: Encode kitty unicode-placeholder diacritics per spec
description:
placeholder_text in crates/kittui-kitty currently emits bare U+10EEEE characters with only a \x1b[38:2:r:g:b]m foreground color set to the low 24 bits of the image id. The kitty graphics spec (https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol/#unicode-placeholders) requires every placeholder cell to carry up to three combining diacritics encoding (row, column, most-significant-byte-of-image-id). Without these, kitty/Ghostty treat every cell as row 0 column 0 of the image and the placement does not anchor. Implement diacritic encoding from the spec's tables, support image ids whose top byte is non-zero, add exact-grammar unit tests, and snapshot the placeholder grid for a known fixture.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-xquartz: XQuartz-Xvfb backend for X11 on macOS
description:
New crate kittui-xquartz that spawns XQuartz in Xvfb mode (Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac) and connects via x11rb the same way kittui-xvfb::xvfb::XvfbServer does. Implements the XServer trait so the compositor sees XQuartz the same as any other backend. Documents the XQuartz install path (/opt/X11/bin/Xquartz, or via brew install --cask xquartz).

Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-dc80c6

Title: kittui-xquartz: spawn XQuartz in nolisten mode, attach via x11rb

New crate kittui-xquartz behind feature 'xquartz'. spawn() launches /opt/X11/bin/Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen $DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac or attaches to an existing :N. Implements XServer trait by delegating to kittui-xvfb's x11rb code path (extract that into a shared helper if needed). Detects XQuartz install at /opt/X11/bin or /Library/X11. Returns Unavailable if not found. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui: guard high-level placement on terminal capability support
description:
Large gap: kittui now detects terminal capabilities, but `Runtime::place` still emits kitty upload/placement/placeholder bytes even when `TerminalInfo` says kitty graphics or unicode placeholders are unsupported. That makes library/CLI hosts think output is safe when it is not.

Scope:
- Add a clear `KittuiError::UnsupportedTerminal` or equivalent for high-level placement.
- Make `Runtime::place`/`place_at` fail before rendering/uploading when `supports_kitty=false` or `supports_unicode_placeholders=false`.
- Preserve low-level raw APIs if needed, but document/guard high-level scene placement.
- Add targeted tests for both unsupported flags and an explicit supported override.

Acceptance:
- `Runtime::place` returns an error for unsupported kitty or unicode placeholders.
- Supported terminal overrides continue to place scenes.
- `cargo test -p kittui unsupported_terminal` and `cargo build -p kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm terminal renderer: avoid repeated counts while padding clipped text

title: kittwm: restore native sessions from manifest JSON
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: kittwm can now export a persistence-oriented `SESSION_JSON` manifest, but there is no native socket restore path. Session persistence remains half-built until a controller can hand the manifest back to the WM and have it recreate pane order, layout, titles, commands, weights, and focus.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON <json>` accepting the `SESSION_JSON` shape.
- Queue a native restore command that replaces current native panes with newly spawned PTYs from manifest commands.
- Restore layout axis, pane order, display titles, weights, and focused pane index.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON coverage and docs example.

Acceptance:

title: kittwm-browser: forward Ctrl-Tab input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards plain Tab and legacy Shift-Tab (`ESC [ Z`), but consumes kitty-keyboard Ctrl+Tab CSI-u (`ESC [ 9 ; 5 u`). Browsers and web apps use Ctrl+Tab for tab/navigation shortcuts. Forward it with the Ctrl modifier.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: enforce native surface frame bounds on layout resize
description:
Native WM resize/layout bug: switching native panes between columns and rows can flicker badly because a pane may capture/render at its previous full logical size during the resize transition, while the WM places it into a smaller layout. The WM must enforce each window's frame bounds regardless of backend timing: resize the surface to its new logical cell area, but also crop/pad captured frames to the allocated pixel footprint before placement. Scaling/zooming should remain a separate future feature, not implicit pane resize behavior.

Scope:
- Add a native-session frame fitting/cropping helper for RGBA frames using current cell dimensions (8x16 today) and allocated app_cols/app_rows.
- Before kitty placement, ensure frame dimensions exactly match the allocated native pane footprint in pixels; crop overflow and pad underflow with terminal background.
- Preserve normal PTY resize calls so apps are still informed of new logical cols/rows.
- Add focused tests proving oversized frames are cropped and undersized frames are padded without scaling.
- Document resize semantics: WM reports logical size to surfaces and enforces bounds; zoom/scaling is separate future behavior.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover crop and pad behavior.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: implement native PTY erase CSI modes
description:
Large terminal fidelity gap: native PTY rendering treats all CSI `J` erase-display modes as clear-screen and all CSI `K` erase-line modes as erase-to-end. Real terminal apps use `CSI 1K`, `CSI 2K`, `CSI 0J`, and `CSI 1J`; incorrect erase semantics can corrupt native pane rendering and automation READ_TEXT snapshots.

Scope:
- Implement CSI `K` modes 0/1/2: cursor-to-end, start-to-cursor, whole line.
- Implement CSI `J` modes 0/1/2: cursor-to-end-of-screen, start-of-screen-to-cursor, whole screen.
- Keep default mode semantics (`0`) unchanged.
- Add focused TerminalState parser tests.

Acceptance:
- Text snapshots reflect correct erase-line/display modes.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: route mouse input to browser surfaces
description: Follow-up to bd-037543 and broader kittwm usability: browser launches now create a direct HeadlessBrowserApp surface, but live mouse routing only emits terminal SGR mouse payloads when terminal apps request mouse modes. Browser/direct pointer surfaces do not request SGR, and Ghostty/native surfaces also need pointer events where their adapter supports them. Route host mouse events over the NativeSurface pointer hook for direct pointer surfaces, mapping app-local cells to CSS/pixel coordinates, while preserving terminal SGR routing for PTYs that request it. Add targeted helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: tolerate per-pane socket byte input failures
description: Socket input stability issue: live kittwm handles `SEND_BYTES_B64` / `PASTE_BYTES_B64` by calling `pane.app.send_bytes(...)?`. Direct browser panes reject non-UTF-8 bytes through the NativeSurface adapter, so one malformed/binary byte payload can propagate an error out of the native terminal loop and kill the WM. Make per-pane socket byte/paste/send-key delivery failures non-fatal: log a bounded diagnostic and continue processing/rendering other panes. Add focused helper coverage for the log line.
