title: kittwm SDK layout request: build directly
description: The typed kittwm SDK layout helper still formats the LAYOUT request string. Build the request directly into a small preallocated String while preserving protocol output and tests.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: dogfood kittui controls in settings/help panels
description: Dogfood opportunity: include actual kittui controls (buttons, toggles, chips, text inputs) in help/settings panels for renderer/theme/layout options. This validates component rendering and interaction inside kittwm. Acceptance: at least one in-session panel renders interactive kittui controls and routes keyboard actions through existing command paths.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm scrollback tests: build stress lines directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("line-{idx}")` in `terminal_state_batches_scrollback_pruning_after_overflow` with a direct helper in `crates/kittui-wm/src/native.rs`. Scope only scrollback pruning stress-line construction; preserve pruning assertions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add native pane balance command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes now have weights and resize controls, but there is no first-class way to rebalance a layout back to equal weights. A terminal WM needs a quick balance/reset operation after manual resizing.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `BALANCE_PANES` queued into the native PTY loop.
- Handle it by resetting all pane weights to 1, recomputing layout, and redrawing.
- Add a local prefix keybinding (`Ctrl-A b`) for balance.
- Include command in HELP/HELP_JSON and parser tests.
- Add/extend tests for balance/reset helper behavior.

Acceptance:
- Socket `BALANCE_PANES` queues and drains a typed command.
- Native session can reset pane weights to equal values locally and over socket.
- Targeted daemon/session tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: answer basic terminal DSR and CPR queries
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm parses app output but does not answer basic Device Status Report queries. Many TUIs probe `CSI 5 n` and `CSI 6 n` expecting terminal OK / cursor-position responses; without CPR, apps can block, mis-detect cursor placement, or fall back to degraded terminal paths.

Scope:
- Add a pending terminal-response path from `TerminalState` back to the PTY writer.
- Handle `CSI 5 n` with `ESC[0n`.
- Handle `CSI 6 n` with `ESC[row;colR` using current 1-based cursor coordinates.
- Ensure the reader loop flushes responses to the child PTY promptly while preserving existing socket/send paths.
- Add focused unit tests for DSR/CPR response bytes.

Acceptance:
- Tests prove `CSI 5n` and `CSI 6n` queue the correct response bytes.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch: avoid lowercasing full launch queries for URL detection
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: kittwm-launch auto backend detection calls query.to_ascii_lowercase() before checking URL prefixes. Pathological long launch queries/URLs allocate and scan the full string just to test a few ASCII prefixes. Scope: replace full lowercase allocation with bounded ASCII prefix matching for http/https/data/about targets and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: expose adaptive graphics transport diagnostics
description: Expose adaptive graphics transport diagnostics in kittui/kittwm. Scope: report the selected transport, compression mode, fallback reason, local-vs-remote/tmux classification, and override source through kittwm inspection surfaces and a testable policy selector. Follow-up from bd-3c0dd1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: add documented semantic role variants
description:
Semantic protocol gap: docs describe common semantic roles such as link, heading, paragraph, code, image/canvas/terminal/browser document, list/list item/tree/tree item/row/cell, but `kittwm-sdk::ComponentRole` still maps many of these to `Custom(...)` in adapters. Add first-class SDK role variants for these documented roles while preserving Custom for vendor-specific types.

Scope:
- Add additive `ComponentRole` variants for common documented roles not yet present: Link, Heading, Paragraph, Code, Image, Canvas, Terminal, BrowserDocument, List, ListItem, Tree, TreeItem, Row, Cell (adjust if some already exist).
- Update serde tests to verify snake_case shape.
- Do not change daemon behavior; adapter remapping can be a follow-up if needed.
- Keep `kittui-core` untouched.

Acceptance:
- SDK semantic role enum better matches semantic protocol docs.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: wire swap actions to compositor z-order
description:
Large gap: swap/raise-style keybindings in capture-backed kittwm still only update footer counters. Now that hit-testing has deterministic render order, the compositor should expose real z-order operations so keyboard actions can raise/lower the focused window.

Scope:
- Add compositor z-order overrides used by raw and Scene composition order.
- Add helpers to raise/lower the focused-or-first window one step or to top/bottom.
- Wire swap keymap actions in session: right/down raise one step, left/up lower one step while preserving footer text.
- Add targeted tests proving composition order and hit-testing change after raise/lower.

Acceptance:
- Swap keybindings affect actual render/hit-test z-order in capture-backed sessions.
- `raise_focused`/`lower_focused` wrap/clamp deterministically.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Include filter and limit in remote app JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm app fallback JSON reports host/mode and candidate arrays, but not the query or limit that produced them. Add `filter` and `limit` metadata so scripts consuming `kittwm remote HOST apps --json` can understand the remote pooled-SSH app discovery context.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: use dirty grid to skip unchanged raw frame uploads
description:
Follow-up from bd-510a36. Use the new kittui-wm dirty-grid helper to skip raw kitty frame uploads when a bounded RGBA frame is byte-identical at tile level. Keep behavior conservative: no partial updates and no visual changes when pixels differ.

Scope:
- Add opt-in `KITTWM_DIRTY_FRAMES=skip-unchanged` (or equivalent renderer policy) around the native raw-frame path.
- Maintain current full-frame delete+upload behavior whenever any tile changed or dimensions changed.
- Add tests proving identical frames skip upload and changed frames still upload normally.
- Keep tmux pure-terminal default unchanged.

Acceptance:
- Opt-in unchanged-frame skipping works for raw RGBA frames.
- No dirty-rectangle/overlay behavior is enabled by default.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm queued command empty-error reply: build directly
description: queue_native_pane_command still uses format! for ERR <empty_error> replies. Build that shared empty-error reply directly while preserving queued command validation coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch: bound plan status fields before formatting
description: Audit finding: kittwm-launch scene labels are clipped, but build_launch_plan still formats raw title/query into the status string before later clipping. Pathological launch queries can allocate huge status strings during plan rendering. Bound status-only title/query fields before formatting while preserving actual launch command/query behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm PTY renderer: skip redundant default background fills
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: render_terminal_rgba initializes the entire frame to the default terminal background, then still calls fill_cell_background for every cell including default-style cells. Large mostly-default terminal panes therefore rewrite the whole frame cell-by-cell before drawing glyphs. Scope: skip per-cell background fill when the cell background equals the initialized default background, preserving styled/reverse backgrounds and cursor rendering with focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Avoid allocation in kittwm title reorder affordance check
description: Render-performance/design-cleanliness follow-up: live kittwm title chrome calls native_title_reorder_affordance_enabled every frame, and it currently allocates with to_ascii_lowercase() just to match columns/rows/grid/tiled. Replace it with allocation-free eq_ignore_ascii_case checks and keep focused coverage for layout labels. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/render-performance audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add kittui-md frontmatter inspection alias
description: Add --frontmatter as a user-friendly alias for the existing --metadata-blocks inspection mode. Document the alias and test parsing/output-mode conflict behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Keep resized title marker scoped to tiled layouts
description: UX visual-state cleanup: after adding the fullscreen `▣` marker, the generic non-floating weight marker path can still show `↔` for fullscreen panes with non-default weights even though resize weights only affect tiled layout. Limit the `↔` resized marker to tiled/reorder title rows so fullscreen title chrome stays focused on fullscreen state. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-wm WindowTree split can over-consume tiny rects
description: Follow-up from bd-f1c4d1 duplicate-work stand-down. While independently testing core tiling invariants, I found a distinct edge case not covered by the landed native pane invariant tests: crates/kittui-wm/src/lib.rs::layout_node computes non-last split lengths with rounded proportional slices and tracks consumed as u32, then computes the last length as along - consumed. For tiny parent rects with more split children than available cells, earlier rounded slices can make consumed exceed along, causing debug underflow/panic and potentially invalid geometry. Repro shape: horizontal WindowTree split into 6 equal child windows inside CellRect::new(0, 0, 4, 3). Suggested fix: allocate split spans from remaining length/remaining weight with saturation and add deterministic tests for more children than cells plus 0/1/2/3 visible panes. Filed as follow-up per ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3 after bd-f1c4d1 landed at 887193d.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: forward Shift-PageUp and Shift-PageDown input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards plain PageUp/PageDown and shifted arrows/Home/End, but consumes Shift+PageUp/PageDown CSI (`ESC [ 5 ; 2 ~` / `ESC [ 6 ; 2 ~`). Web editors and scroll containers use these for page selection. Forward them with the Shift modifier.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Document macOS XQuartz and xterm prerequisites for kittwm proof
description: context: while working bd-a9ec5b and bd-2a0133 on ms-mac, the host lacked /opt/X11/bin/Xquartz and xterm, so the new XQuartz proof harness could only compile and skip. Suggested fix: document install prerequisites and/or provide a nix/devenv path for macOS XQuartz/xterm smoke testing. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-a9ec5b/bd-2a0133 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: fix kittwm frame deletion flicker
description: Operator reported visible flicker after recent old-frame deletion changes: every second frame appears lost when running kittwm. Investigate frame deletion/presentation path and make deletion non-destructive/stable. Filed from direct operator report during dev-2 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward F6 input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser now forwards F5 but still consumes adjacent function-key browser navigation such as F6 (`ESC [ 17 ~`), commonly used to focus the location bar/address controls in browsers. Forward F6 through DevTools key events.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm keymap config language with tmux-style Ctrl-A defaults
description: Expose a first keybinding configuration language for kitwm. v1 should parse a simple text format mapping key chords to WM actions, ship tmux-like defaults with Ctrl-A prefix (workspace-create, split-vertical-launcher, swap-right, focus hjkl, launch), and provide a visible 'kitwm keymap' subcommand to print the resolved default/configured bindings. This is the config foundation before wiring every action into runtime. Parent: bd-031e54; related to launcher/keybinding work.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: do not forward third Ctrl-C before quitting
description: Follow-up to the operator Ctrl-C report. The native terminal loop now traps Ctrl-C, but  forwards byte 0x03 to the focused pane before checking whether it is the third press that exits kittwm. That means the quit-triggering Ctrl-C can still be delivered to the app/inner shell, potentially killing the foreground process just as kittwm exits. Scope: count Ctrl-C before forwarding; forward only non-quit Ctrl-C presses; add targeted coverage for the byte handler/guard behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-renderer: avoid duplicate ANSI help overlay over graphics
description: When affordance-scene chrome is enabled, render_native_shell_view_affordance_scenes already includes a graphical help-overlay scene, but the live loop writes ANSI help overlay text again afterward. This duplicates text over kitty graphics and contributes to text/image fighting. Remove the duplicate ANSI write in the affordance chrome path and add coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm architecture JSON: append newline directly
description: Replace `format!("{}\n", serde_json::to_string(...))` in `architecture_contract_json_text()` with direct string construction: serialize once, push a newline, and return. Scope only `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; preserve architecture JSON output and add/retain narrow coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: theme tokens for graphical shell chrome
description: Dogfood/theming gap: graphical WM chrome needs shared theme tokens for background, translucent panels, focused/unfocused borders, title strips, key chips, warnings, and typography. Define reusable theme model for kittwm shell chrome and apply to top bar, pane chrome, overlays. Acceptance: one theme source drives chrome scenes; easy to adjust alpha/colors; docs/tests cover token usage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-cli: add render-only PNG command
description:
Large gap: kittui now has a true render-only PNG API in Rust/FFI/Python, but shell users still cannot ask the `kittui` CLI to render scene JSON to PNG bytes without emitting kitty placement escapes. That limits scripts and external tools that want previews/artifacts instead of terminal placement.

Scope:
- Add `kittui render <scene.json|-> [--out path]` to render a single Scene JSON to PNG bytes via `Runtime::render_png`.
- Write PNG to stdout by default or to `--out`; support global `--json`/`--dry-run` metadata with byte counts and output path.
- Reject scene-array batch input with a clear error for v1.
- Add integration tests for stdin scene -> PNG file and JSON metadata.

Acceptance:
- Render command output begins with PNG signature and contains no kitty escapes.
- It does not require terminal placement support.
- Targeted CLI tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md metadata JSON mode for outlines links and images
description: Add a kittui-md --metadata-json mode that parses Markdown and emits structured JSON for component counts, heading outline, links, images, and tables without rendering rich/plain components. Include tests for stable JSON shape.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm affordance control labels: build directly
description: Replace formatted control layer label construction in `crates/kittui-affordances/src/controls.rs` with direct preallocated helpers. Scope only `control_tab_<idx>`, `control_option_<idx>`, and `control_animation_<kind>` labels; preserve existing scene-label tests used by kittwm UI affordances.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Document kittui-md references mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include --references mode alongside plain/rich/outline/metadata-json/interactive, with an example command and one-line explanation. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: preserve FPS cap while chunking frame sleeps
description: Follow-up from bd-4c714b: replacing single `sleep(slack)` calls with `sleep(frame_sleep_chunk(slack))` improves responsiveness but can shorten the total frame-budget sleep on paths that do not loop, causing kittwm to exceed its FPS cap and waste CPU. Fix by adding a helper that sleeps the whole remaining budget in <=16ms chunks, use it in single-sleep paths, and keep the raw-loop chunk helper for already-looped sleeps. Add deterministic tests for both chunking and total sleep accounting.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm send-mouse queued reply: build directly
description: queue_native_send_mouse still uses format! for SEND_MOUSE_QUEUED command/window/event/col/row replies. Build that reply directly while preserving mouse queue coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: native pane status convenience accessors
description:
SDK ergonomics gap: `NativePaneDetail` contains many optional mode/status fields, but SDK clients must inspect raw Options directly. Add small ergonomic helpers for common pane capabilities/modes.

Scope:
- Add methods on `NativePaneDetail` for cursor tuple, app bounds, has_mouse_reporting/sgr/button/all motion, bracketed paste, application cursor keys, and dirty/transport presence where useful.
- Keep methods pure and additive; no daemon behavior changes.
- Add targeted unit tests over existing rich status JSON fixture.

Acceptance:
- SDK consumers have convenient typed accessors for native pane status/mode data.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm app list rows: build directly
description: Linux desktop app list rows still use format! for '<label> (<id>) — <file>'. Build the row string directly while preserving app listing row coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm info scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm info scene performance cleanup: info_scene builds the backdrop layer label with format! from the bounded workspace label and pane count. Build the info backdrop label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-keymap: avoid chord string allocation when counting duplicates
description: Audit finding: `kittwm keymap-kitty` and keymap check count duplicate chords by rendering every binding chord into a freshly allocated string. Custom keymaps with many/large key names can add avoidable allocation churn before rendering/checking. Count duplicates by borrowed chord slices instead of stringifying every chord.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: wrap semantic snapshot and action commands
description:
Follow-up from bd-6c4bc5 and bd-502737. Add SDK wrapper methods for the new semantic socket skeleton so clients can request semantic snapshots and receive explicit unsupported errors for semantic action/focus through typed APIs.

Scope:
- Add SurfaceHandle::semantic_snapshot() -> SemanticSurfaceSnapshot using SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT.
- Add SurfaceHandle::semantic_action(component, action, payload) and semantic_focus(component) wrappers.
- Enforce ReadSemanticTree and InvokeSemanticAction capabilities before socket I/O.
- Add tests proving command strings/capability denial using no-socket paths or a minimal test server if needed.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can call semantic snapshot/action/focus methods.
- Capabilities deny these operations before I/O when scoped out.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native pane border: build title-gutter label directly
description: Replace the `format!("pane-{idx}-title-gutter")` label in `native_pane_border_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated label helper. Scope only the title-gutter layer label in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing native shell affordance scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui affordance components: add animation option to UiComponent
description: Continue animation coverage for high-level `UiComponent` document/markdown affordances. Inventory: textbox, h1/h2/h3, title, banner, header, footer, and textchip carry ratakittui Chrome metadata but no animation option. Acceptance: add ComponentAnimation with 60fps/180-frame/3s defaults plus `animated()`/`animation()` builders that attach native pulse glow metadata to chrome, preserving static defaults and adding tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: clear dirty-frame state for retired pane images
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported slow/flickery: when a native pane image id disappears, the loop unplaces the kitty image and removes placement memo state, but `NativeDirtyFramePolicy` keeps the dirty grid for that image id. If a later pane reuses the same stable image id (for example after closing all panes or restoring a session), the first frame can be considered clean and skip upload even though the terminal image was deleted, causing a missing/disappearing frame. Fix by forgetting dirty-frame state whenever an image id is retired; add a deterministic regression test.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: add connect and window handle skeleton
description: Add a kittwm-sdk crate with connect_from_env, typed socket targeting, WindowHandle identifiers, create/replace-current request skeletons, and initial docs/tests over the existing socket transport.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK: reject blank surface spawn commands
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: `SurfaceSpec::native_pty_command` returns terminal commands and browser targets without checking for blank/whitespace-only values. A blank terminal command reaches `SPAWN_PTY` and is rejected only after daemon socket I/O; a blank browser target can produce a meaningless `kittwm-browser ''` command. Trim valid terminal commands/browser targets and reject blank values locally; add focused dry-run/spawn helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add SDK helpers for focused title-drag coordinates
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/design-cleanliness follow-up: SDK clients can ask whether the focused pane title is draggable, but still have to fetch focused_pane() and call title_drag_cell()/title_drag_cells_by() for the actual drag coordinates. Add focused_title_drag_cell() and focused_title_drag_cells_by() helpers on PanesStatus and Status, with tests/docs, so automation can start focused-pane title drags without manual focused-pane plumbing.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm picker overlay: avoid full entry row formatting before clipping
description: PickerOverlay::render formats each full entry row (including macOS window owner/title strings) before truncate_cells clips to the overlay width. Build picker rows directly into a bounded width buffer and stop at ellipsis to reduce overlay redraw allocation/scanning for huge window titles.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native pane move keybindings
description:
Large gap: native kittwm pane reordering exists over the socket (`MOVE_PANE`), but the local terminal WM keymap still cannot move the focused pane. Operators should be able to reorder panes without leaving the session.

Scope:
- Add native prefix keybindings: `Ctrl-A [` / `Ctrl-A ,` moves focused pane left/up, `Ctrl-A ]` / `Ctrl-A .` moves it right/down.
- Recompute layout, preserve focus on moved pane, and redraw.
- Update footer hint and native pane helper tests where useful.

Acceptance:
- Local move keybindings reorder the pane vector using existing move-target helper.
- Focus remains on the moved pane.
- Targeted native helper test and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm v1: Xvfb-backed WM rendering app windows as floating/tiling chrome
description: Operator direction: kittui-wm becomes a real terminal window manager that hosts Xvfb (or per-app Xephyr) in the agent process tree, captures each X window's framebuffer through SHM, composites them as floating or tiling kittui chrome inside the user's terminal, and routes kitty pointer/keyboard events back to the X server via XTestFake* so the hosted apps respond to clicks and keys. This is the substrate that turns kittui into a full WM over SSH: PATH inherits, env inherits, so anything spawned inside the nested X session picks up the operator's environment. v1 scope: (1) kittui-input parses kitty's mouse + key reporting; (2) kittui-xvfb spawns Xvfb, claims a DISPLAY, captures the root window via MIT-SHM and exposes per-window rects; (3) kittui-wm gains a Compositor that owns a kittui::Runtime + LifecycleTracker, places one Image scene per X window, and feeds pointer events back through XTest; (4) tiling and floating layouts implemented on top of LayoutNode::{Split,Stack,Tab,Floating}; (5) demo example that launches Xvfb + a small X app and renders it as a kittui window. Out of scope for v1: Wayland support, GPU passthrough, raw RGB(A) transmission, multi-display.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help overlay scene: build row labels directly
description: Replace `format!("help-overlay-row-{idx}")` in `native_help_overlay_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated label helper. Scope only help-overlay row separator labels in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing help overlay panel/row behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm CPU raster image read error: build directly
description: Replace `format!("failed to read {}: {e}", path)` in `crates/kittui-render-cpu/src/rasterize.rs` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only ImageRef::Path read error construction; preserve stable unsupported-image error text.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm commands JSON: append newline directly
description: commands_json_text still uses format! to add a trailing newline to the JSON command catalog. Serialize the JSON value then append the newline directly while preserving JSON output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: restore Ctrl-C handling with triple-Ctrl-C exit guard
description: User report: Ctrl-C is not being captured/handled correctly. Desired behavior: Ctrl-C should normally be forwarded to the focused internal app/PTY, and kittwm should exit only after three Ctrl-C presses in a short window because nested apps may need one or two Ctrl-C presses themselves. Audit current raw input/keymap path and restore/implement triple-Ctrl-C guard without starving apps of Ctrl-C. Acceptance: single/double Ctrl-C are delivered to focused pane; third Ctrl-C within timeout exits kittwm; timeout resets counter; focused tests cover forwarding and exit guard.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md --mode name selector
description: Add a --mode <name> selector for kittui-md so users and tools can choose any output mode by canonical flag or alias name without spelling the dedicated flag. Document it and add parser/conflict/unknown-mode tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: implement native PTY index and reverse-index controls
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not implement terminal index/reverse-index controls (`ESC D`, `ESC E`, `ESC M` and C1 NEL/RI equivalents). TUIs and prompts use reverse index to insert/scroll at the top of a scroll region; missing it can corrupt snapshots and fixed headers.

Scope:
- Add index/next-line/reverse-index helpers to native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `ESC D` (IND), `ESC E` (NEL), `ESC M` (RI) plus C1 `0x84`, `0x85`, `0x8d` if surfaced by vte execute.
- Reverse index should scroll the active region down when the cursor is at `scroll_top`; otherwise move cursor up.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with and without scroll regions.

Acceptance:
- Tests show reverse-index inside a scroll region preserves outside lines and scrolls region downward.
- Tests show next-line moves to column 0 on the next line.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK semantic token requests: build directly
description: SurfaceHandle semantic_snapshot and semantic_focus still format single-component semantic request strings. Build SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT through the surface token helper and SEMANTIC_FOCUS through the surface payload helper while preserving validation and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm chrome: avoid allocating debug strings for visual hashes
description: native_shell_chrome_scene_visual_hash currently builds format!("{scene:?}") for every chrome redraw-key check, allocating a full scene debug string. Stream Debug output directly into the hasher to preserve visual invalidation while removing the large temporary allocation.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm ghostty terminal: route Enter as keypress not literal newline
description: Operator report: in kittwm Ghostty-backed native terminal panes, pressing raw Enter in coding agents inserts a literal newline instead of being routed as an Enter keypress. Kittwm needs to distinguish raw Enter from text newline/paste-like input, preserve passthrough for variants such as Shift+Enter where supported, and pass a raw Enter as if the user hit Enter in the native app. Scope: inspect the kittwm input translation for Ghostty/PTy terminal panes, add focused tests, and fix Enter routing without regressing literal text/paste paths.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: support DEC origin mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap after scroll-region support: native kittwm does not honor DEC origin mode (`CSI ? 6 h/l`). TUIs that set scroll margins often enable origin mode so cursor addressing is relative to the scroll region; without it, writes land in the wrong rows and corrupt fixed headers/status lines.

Scope:
- Track DEC origin mode in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI ? 6 h` / `CSI ? 6 l`, resetting cursor to home on toggle.
- When origin mode is enabled, make `CSI H/f` row coordinates relative to `scroll_top` and clamped within `scroll_top..scroll_bottom`.
- Reset origin mode on full terminal reset if such helper exists; otherwise preserve existing behavior narrowly.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with scroll region + origin mode.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `CSI 1;1H` inside origin mode writes at scroll-region top, not absolute row 0.
- Tests show disabling origin mode restores absolute addressing.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm panes scene: build row labels directly
description: Small kittwm panes scene performance cleanup: panes_scene builds each pane row label with format! from bounded window/title labels, focus state, and app dimensions. Build pane row labels directly into preallocated Strings while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: App launcher inside kittui-wm: a kittui floating menu to spawn apps
description: Hotkey (Mod+Return by default) opens a kittui floating launcher: a text input + matching app list pulled from common locations ($PATH binaries via 'compgen -c' equivalent, /Applications/*.app, /opt/homebrew/bin, /nix/store/mlrgd5zljyrxl15ryn31w15w48f6yii5-cargo-1.94.1/share:/nix/store/c9vv24f9bdnhnms10nwijn6q8wjz00ak-just-1.51.0/share:/nix/store/cn0f42d8p48g718nh2zsgl96zjx207hi-nixfmt-1.2.0/share:/nix/store/7693fhfqg2hddwl3kbbpinrdqdp71nq2-pkg-config-wrapper-0.29.2/share:/nix/store/xvmhkpvfvmy4sfdkqwg9inq3qkpnx81b-libiconv-109.100.2/share:/Users/harryaskham/.nix-profile/share:/run/current-system/sw/share:/nix/var/nix/profiles/default/share/applications). Selecting an entry spawns the app under the currently-active backend (Xvfb/XQuartz for X apps; Quartz capture mode for native Mac apps). v1 supports launching X apps inside XQuartz. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: remap accessibility roles to first-class SDK roles
description:
Follow-up after SDK added first-class semantic roles: accessibility adapter still collapses headings to Label and many document/list/tree/media roles to Custom or SelectList. Remap obvious accessibility role strings to first-class SDK roles.

Scope:
- Update `accessibility_component_role` to use Heading, Link, Image, Canvas, List/ListItem, Tree/TreeItem, Row/Cell where role strings clearly indicate them.
- Preserve existing control mappings and `Custom("accessibility.*")` fallback.
- Update/add targeted `kittui-wm` accessibility tests.
- Do not add platform FFI or change action routing behavior.

Acceptance:
- Accessibility semantic snapshots use first-class SDK roles for obvious document/list/tree/media roles.
- Targeted accessibility tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK helpers for frame status labels
description: Render-performance SDK follow-up: live kittwm pane chips present frame state as frame:new, frame:clean, or frame:<changed>/<total>, but SDK consumers currently combine optional dirty_frame plus label/clean helpers manually. Add frame_status_label helpers on NativePaneDetail and focused Status/PanesStatus so SDK clients can mirror live pane chip text consistently. Filed from ongoing kittwm render-performance/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm split launcher selected log: build directly
description: The raw compositor split launcher selected diagnostic still formats kind/command/pid with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal graphics: build event summary without Vec join
description: terminal_events_scene_for_cols clips up to five event kind labels, collects them into a temporary Vec<String>, then joins with commas for a scene label. Build the bounded summary directly in one String to reduce graphics/status rendering allocation churn while preserving output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add monitor and screen remote display aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote display listing currently advertises `display`/`displays`, but users may naturally ask for screens or monitors when SSHing from Ghostty/Kitty into a remote desktop context. Route `kittwm remote HOST monitor(s)` and `screen(s)` plus `list monitor(s)`/`list screen(s)` to the existing remote display fallback path and advertise them in help/completions/catalog.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: compact cheat-sheet command
description:
Daily-driver UX source work: add a concise `kittwm cheat` / cheat-sheet command.

Scope:
- Implement source changes (not docs-only) for `kittwm cheat` or `kittwm cheatsheet` that prints a compact command + keybinding cheat sheet.
- It should be shorter than `quickstart` and optimized for repeated daily use.
- Include in-session keys, common aliases, pane controls, and inspection commands.
- Keep presentation-only; no daemon/session behavior changes.
- Avoid overlapping empty-workspace renderer hints.
- Add focused tests.

Acceptance:
- Users can run a short cheat sheet when they forget a command/key.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md mode listing output
description: Add --modes and --modes-json discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can list available output modes, aliases, and descriptions without rendering a document. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon queue command reply: build directly
description: queue_native_pane_command still uses format! for '<OK> command=N arg=...' replies. Build that reply directly while preserving focus/close/layout/move/nudge/rename queue behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm status scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm status scene performance cleanup: status_scene builds the backdrop layer label with format! from already-known numeric fields and bounded labels. Build the status backdrop label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm chrome JSON reply: build directly
description: CHROME_JSON replies still use format! only to append a newline to native_chrome_status_value. Build that JSON reply directly while preserving chrome status validation coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md tables-only mode for table inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --tables mode that prints only parsed Markdown tables with rows, alignments, column widths, and footprint metrics. This complements --outline and --references with a human-readable table audit. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm session manifest window ids: build directly
description: The session scene large-manifest stress test still formats synthetic window ids with format!. Build those test window ids directly while preserving saturated row-count coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Default remote terminal alias titles to host
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST shell`, `ssh`, `terminal CMD`, and `kittwm` open local kittwm panes for remote SSH work. Set a default pane title to the remote host when the user did not provide one, so multiple remote panes are easy to identify in Ghostty/Kitty host sessions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm runtime placement escapes: build directly
description: Replace `{mv}{p}` formatted placement escape concatenation in `crates/kittui/src/lib.rs` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only Runtime placement escape construction for scene/uploaded image placements used by kittwm graphics surfaces.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: tolerate pane termination failures on close
description: Pane lifecycle stability issue: socket/shortcut close paths still call `pane.app.terminate()?`. If a hosted app or PTY returns an error while closing, that error can propagate out of the native loop and exit kittwm, even though the user explicitly asked to close the pane. Make per-pane close termination best-effort: log a bounded diagnostic and remove the pane/continue. Add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: bound panes scene row rects on narrow terminals
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: panes graphics inherit narrow-aware info scene widths, but pane row rects still use a fixed 10-cell-style inset. On very narrow terminals those row rectangles can extend past the scene bounds and contribute to graphical wrap/flicker. Scope: reuse bounded info row geometry for pane rows and add focused narrow-width coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add heading anchors to kittui-md outlines
description: Generate stable slug anchors for Markdown headings in kittui-affordances, expose them in kittui-md outline/metadata-json/plain metadata output, and add targeted tests. Keep behavior deterministic and narrow.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: unified scene graph for WM chrome plus app surfaces
description: Dogfood architecture gap: WM chrome and app frames are still emitted through separate text/image paths. Move toward a unified per-frame kittui scene/composition graph that includes background, top bar, split borders, pane chrome, overlays, and app surface frames as layers. Acceptance: design/implementation path produces a single ordered visual composition for graphics mode, reducing placeholder/text burn-in and making z-order/translucency testable.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Show source path in kittui-md stats
description: Update kittui-md --stats to include source.path when a file path is provided, matching metadata-json source provenance. Keep stdin behavior clear and add targeted tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon HELP_JSON: build catalog directly
description: Small kittwm daemon CLI performance cleanup: native socket HELP_JSON still collects command entries into a Vec<serde_json::Value> before formatting. The help catalog is static, so build the JSON response directly into one String with serde_json string escaping for each field.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm keymap reload-failed log: build directly
description: The raw compositor keymap reload failure diagnostic still formats the error with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: plan semantic component surface protocol
description:
Architecture gap: kittwm can model pixel/terminal surfaces, but not semantic UI surfaces from toolkit/accessibility/framework adapters. Capture the component protocol and runtime model needed for future Qt/GTK/browser/accessibility/native SDK adapters to publish labels, buttons, radio groups, text inputs, menus, split panes, etc. as kittwm/kittui-native UI rather than pixels.

Scope:
- Add a durable docs plan for SemanticSurface / ComponentTree / action IDs / focus / layout / event routing / renderer mapping.
- Explain fallback hierarchy: semantic component surface when available, terminal/cell surface, pixel capture otherwise.
- Include implications for pure terminal, kitty graphics, kittui scene, and remote streaming renderers.
- Map follow-on beads for one proof semantic adapter and for SDK transport types.

Acceptance:
- In-repo doc section or new doc defines semantic surface model and component protocol enough to drive implementation.
- Clearly states that arbitrary GUI apps still need capture fallback unless framework/accessibility semantics exist.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fall back when remote kittwm app probe fails
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: local `kittwm remote HOST apps/launch` should be easy when the remote host has no working kittwm. Today the remote app script delegates as soon as `command -v kittwm` succeeds; if that binary is installed but broken at startup, the fallback PATH/.app/.desktop discovery never runs. Try remote kittwm first, but fall back to local shell discovery when it exits non-zero.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: refresh Ghostty text in pure-terminal fallback
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: the pure-terminal fallback renders pane text via `NativeTerminalApp::text_snapshot`, but the Ghostty backend only updates `last_text_snapshot` during graphical `capture_surface`. In pure-terminal mode the graphical capture loop is skipped, so Ghostty-backed panes can show stale or blank text. Add a lightweight Ghostty text refresh path that drains PTY output and renders a text snapshot without PNG generation, and call it before building the pure-terminal shell view; add targeted coverage for the refresh decision/helper.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: docs: add semantic surfaces documentation map
description:
Documentation integration gap: semantic surface docs are now spread across SDK plan, semantic surfaces, browser adapter, accessibility adapter, and quickstart. Add a small docs index/README section tying these together so contributors can find the semantic architecture and implementation status.

Scope:
- Add or update an in-repo docs index section listing semantic surface docs and their purpose.
- Update README/DESIGN or docs/wm.md with a concise semantic docs map.
- Include current implementation status: terminal fallback, SDK publishing/actions/events, browser DOM/ARIA extraction/publish/action routing, accessibility plan.

Acceptance:
- Semantic docs map exists and links to all relevant docs.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Pointer + key routing back into Xvfb via XTestFake*
description:
Route kittui-input events back to the active Xvfb-hosted window. Mouse hit-tests pick the topmost LayoutNode::Floating or tiling pane under the cursor and translate the local pixel coordinate into XTestFakeMotionEvent/XTestFakeButtonEvent; keys go through XTestFakeKeyEvent with the host's keymap. Mod state preserved across events.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SSH env arg: build directly
description: The pooled SSH argv builder still formats KEY=value environment arguments with format!. Build those env argument strings directly while preserving quoting behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon NUDGE_PANE queue arg: build directly
description: The native daemon NUDGE_PANE parser still uses format! to build the internal tab-separated queue argument. Build that queue arg directly while preserving NUDGE_PANE validation and queued command behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: define common native Surface trait and frame metadata
description: Introduce SurfaceId, SurfaceFrame, SurfaceMetadata, capability flags, and a common capture/input/resize/title interface for terminal, browser, X/Quartz, kittui-scene, and composite surfaces.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm apps inspection kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm apps inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add apps-scene-json and apps-kitty/apps-graphics commands that render launcher candidate discovery as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing apps text/json/first/launch-first behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm apps-scene-json [--filter Q] [--limit N] emits a kittui Scene summarizing default command resolution and candidate counts.
- kittwm apps-kitty/apps-graphics [--filter Q] [--limit N] places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing apps text/json/--first/--launch-first behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels using synthetic app candidate data.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal: add examples to help output
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm-terminal --help` now lists options, but it does not show concrete examples for common paths: default terminal spawn, titled command, replace current pane, status, and bounded events. Add a concise Examples section plus focused assertion coverage so operators can copy a working command quickly.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.

title: kittwm top bar clock chip labels should build directly
description: `BarModel::scene_with_prefix` still builds clock chip and clock foreground scene labels with `format!`. Build those labels directly into preallocated Strings while preserving exact scene label text. Scope: top-bar clock chip scene labels only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-sdk: expose button-specific mouse release events
description: Kittwm burn-down follow-up: daemon and CLI now accept button-specific SEND_MOUSE release-left/release-middle/release-right events, but kittwm-sdk::MouseEvent still exposes only generic Release. SDK clients cannot request the more precise release labels. Scope: add ReleaseLeft/ReleaseMiddle/ReleaseRight variants with protocol labels and tests, preserving generic Release.

title: Add SDK helpers for full frame chip labels

Add frame_chip_label helpers on DirtyFrameStatus/NativePaneDetail and focused Status/PanesStatus so telemetry consumers can mirror live pane chip text without prefix glue. Filed from ongoing kittwm render-performance/SDK audit.

Add a concise Examples section plus focused assertion coverage so operators can copy a working command quickly.

title: Add SDK helper for focused title-drag state
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/design-cleanliness follow-up: SDK clients can list title-draggable panes, but common automation still has to fetch focused_pane() and call is_title_draggable() to know whether the focused title row can be dragged. Add focused_is_title_draggable() helpers on PanesStatus and Status, with tests/docs, so focus/drag affordance state can be queried directly.

description: SEMANTIC_PUBLISH success replies still use format! for SEMANTIC_PUBLISHED window=<id>. Build that reply directly while preserving semantic publish coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: saturate help-topic scene row count
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: help-topic graphics compute rows with content_lines.len() cast to u16 plus padding. Very large help text can overflow/truncate row accounting before clamping, risking malformed scene metadata. Scope: use saturated row count accounting for help-topic scenes and add focused large-list coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-md metadata JSON records render width
description: Add render option metadata to kittui-md --metadata-json, starting with the width_cells value used to create components. This helps consumers interpret component widths and reproduce rendering. Add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Accept remote doctor --wayland forwarding flag
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST wayland` routes to the graphical forwarding doctor, but `kittwm remote HOST doctor --wayland` is not accepted even though users may naturally pair the wayland vocabulary with doctor/status. Accept `--wayland` as a graphical forwarding check flag and advertise it alongside x11/gui/graphical/forwarding.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: forward Ctrl-Enter input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards Enter and Shift+Enter CSI-u, plus many Ctrl-modified navigation/editing keys, but consumes Ctrl+Enter CSI-u (`ESC [ 13 ; 5 u`). Web editors and chat apps use Ctrl+Enter as an alternate submit/newline shortcut. Forward it with the Ctrl modifier.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native failure logs: build capture line directly
description: native_capture_failure_log_line uses format! after bounding the error string. Build the diagnostic line directly with a preallocated buffer while preserving the bounded message.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --capture window:<substr> / display:<n> / main / all (scriptable target)
description: Add a --capture flag that takes a target spec: 'window:<title-substr>' picks the first CGWindow whose owner_name or title contains the substring; 'display:<n>' picks the nth connected display (0=main); 'main' (default) main display; 'all' all displays. Lets the operator launch kitwm against a specific source without the interactive picker, e.g. 'kitwm --capture window:Safari' or 'kitwm --capture display:1'. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native keyboard focus log: build directly
description: The native keyboard focus-cycle diagnostic still formats the focused window with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui core: centralize standard animation contract
description: Continue animation coverage by centralizing the standard kitty-native animation contract. Inventory: multiple modules now independently spell the 60fps / 180-frame / 3000ms loop math (`InlineAnimationArgs`, controls, components, panel, overlay, table glyphs, examples). Acceptance: add canonical constants/helpers in kittui-core Animation, update representative callers to use them, and add tests so the period cannot drift.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Use shortcut scene row capacity for visible entries
description: UX/discoverability follow-up: graphical `kittwm shortcuts-kitty` computes enough scene rows for up to 18 rows, but still hard-caps visible shortcut entries at 12. After adding mouse/title-marker help, this leaves avoidable blank space and hides useful shortcuts. Use the computed scene row capacity to render more shortcut entries while still guaranteeing the title marker legend remains visible under the cap. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native footer: bound log path before status formatting
description: native_status_line_text formats the full KITTUI_WM_LOG path every native redraw and only later clips the footer to terminal width. Bound the diagnostic log-path payload before formatting so pathological paths cannot allocate/scan a huge footer string while preserving visible clipped footer behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: wrap native socket event stream
description:
SDK architecture follow-up: native kittwm exposes bounded JSON-lines `EVENTS [ms]`, but kittwm-sdk does not wrap it in typed APIs yet. Add a first typed event stream helper over the existing socket protocol.

Scope:
- Add SDK event structs/enums for the current native event stream: status, status_changed, pane_opened, pane_closed, pane_changed, focus_changed, layout_changed, and unknown fallback.
- Add `Kittwm::events_ms(ms)` or equivalent bounded API that sends `EVENTS <ms>` and parses JSON lines until END.
- Enforce `SubscribeEvents` capability before I/O.
- Add tests with a small Unix socket server or pure parser tests for current event JSON shape and capability denial.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can request bounded typed event batches from the existing socket protocol.
- Unknown event kinds do not break parsing.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-cli: expose inline affordance chrome commands
description:
Large gap: `kittui-affordances` ships reusable inline chrome patterns (`chip_chrome`, `divider_chrome`, `title_chrome`), but the CLI exposes only `panel`. Shell scripts cannot generate these common chrome scenes without writing Rust/FFI code, limiting kittui as a shell renderer and kittwm chrome preview tool.

Scope:
- Add CLI subcommands for inline affordance scenes:
  - `kittui chip --bg COLOR --border COLOR -w W [-h H]`
  - `kittui divider --left COLOR --right COLOR -w W`
  - `kittui title-bar --left COLOR --right COLOR -w W [-h H]`
- Build scenes through `kittui_affordances::{chip_chrome, divider_chrome, title_chrome}` and `Chrome::to_scene`.
- Support existing emit modes: `--scene-json`, `--json`, `--dry-run`, channels.
- Add targeted CLI tests and update README/DESIGN.

Acceptance:
- Each new command emits valid Scene JSON via `--scene-json`.
- Tests assert expected footprints/layers.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show localized names in SSH fallback app text rows
description: Installed/local app text rows now include localized desktop names, but pooled-SSH fallback text rows still only show generic name and categories. Include localized `Name[...]` values in fallback Linux desktop text details so interactive fallback output matches installed remote output more closely.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: saturate top-bar workspace width accounting
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: multiple constrained top-bar label helpers compute total chip columns with . An extremely long workspace label can overflow/truncate the u16 accounting in debug or misclassify constrained ordering in release. Scope: use saturating/usize-based width accounting for live graphical, scene, and standalone bar overlay label ordering; add focused long-label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm raw footer: bound redraw key note payloads
description: The raw compositor footer key includes launch/keymap note strings verbatim. Error/action notes can be long while the visible footer is clipped, causing key allocation churn and redraws for invisible tail changes. Bound note payloads in the redraw key while preserving visible footer text behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Alt-Home and Alt-End input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards Alt+Arrow and plain/Shift/Ctrl Home/End, but not Alt+Home/End CSI (`ESC [ 1 ; 3 H/F`). Some terminals/editors emit these for modified line/document navigation. Forward them with the Alt modifier instead of consuming them.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-renderer: skip unchanged raw compositor error repaints
description: When the raw compositor fails (capture/TCC/backend error), run_loop_with rewrites a full clear-screen error panel on every frame. Persistent failures therefore cause visible flicker and needless terminal writes while the user is trying to read or quit. Memoize the rendered error screen by message/log path so repeated identical errors do not repaint every tick, and reset the memo once a normal frame succeeds.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm fatal error log: build directly
description: The kittwm process fatal-error diagnostic still formats the error line with format!. Build that process log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm multi-backend accent colors use theme tokens
description:
Audit gap: kittwm multi-backend compositor still has a hardcoded backend accent palette even after shared overlay/chrome tokens. Move per-backend/source accent colors into reusable theme tokens/config so Xvfb/Quartz/remote surfaces and kittwm shell chrome stay visually coherent.

Scope:
- Replace or wrap the hardcoded `backend_color` palette in `kittui-wm::multi` with a shared theme/accent token API.
- Preserve deterministic cycling and existing visual distinction.
- Add tests for cycling and token-backed defaults.
- Consider future config/env overrides, but keep this bead focused on code structure and shared defaults.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Ctrl+Arrow word navigation keys
description: Kittwm burn-down/input parity finding: browser input routing forwards plain and Shift+Arrow, but xterm/kitty modified Ctrl+Arrow sequences such as CSI 1;5A/B/C/D are consumed as unknown CSI. Web editors and text fields use Ctrl+Arrow for word/paragraph navigation. Scope: parse Ctrl+Arrow CSI variants, dispatch DevTools Arrow* key events with Ctrl modifier, and add focused parser/key coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-cli: expose channelized bytes in JSON output
description:
Large gap: shell/external-platform callers can ask `kittui --json` for placement metadata, but the JSON omits the actual upload and placement byte channels. Scripts must either parse terminal escapes from non-JSON output or lose channel separation.

Scope:
- Add a global `--json-bytes` flag for render commands.
- When combined with `--json` or `--dry-run`, include `upload`, `placement`, and `embed` string fields alongside byte counts.
- Keep default JSON compact/backward compatible when `--json-bytes` is absent.
- Add CLI tests for the JSON channel fields.

Acceptance:
- `kittui box -w 2 -h 1 --json --json-bytes` includes upload/placement/embed strings.
- Existing `--json` output remains compact without upload/placement strings.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm split overlay-open log: build directly
description: The raw compositor split launcher overlay-open diagnostic still formats the query with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: document SDK and surface architecture plan
description:
Document the long-term kittwm SDK/surface architecture so future work keeps terminal, GUI, browser, and composite app surfaces separated from the default WM shell.

Scope:
- Add a durable design/plan document covering kittwm core runtime, surface handles, terminal engine extraction, GUI backend surfaces, SDK APIs, event streams, clipboard/bell/notification policy, and standalone first-party apps like kittwm-terminal/kittwm-launch.
- Include staged implementation milestones and bead backlog mapping.
- Capture current-state gaps and principles: built-in session can dogfood the same SDK; app surfaces must be composable; kittwm owns WM/session policy while surface engines own I/O/rendering.

Acceptance:
- Plan is committed under docs/.
- The doc is specific enough to drive implementation beads.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK pane state convenience accessors
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/design-cleanliness slice: pane metadata now includes focus, stack_top, and title_draggable, but SDK consumers still have to scan panes_detail manually for the focused/topmost/draggable panes. Add small typed convenience accessors on Status/PanesStatus so automation and WM tools can reason about focus/stack/drag state without duplicating scans.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm standalone daemon SPAWN success: build directly
description: The standalone daemon SPAWN success reply still uses format! for the pane/window/pid/layout/focused/argv line. Build that reply directly while preserving SPAWN/PANES tracking behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: avoid duplicate ANSI top bar over graphical chrome
description: In live kittwm graphics mode, the loop always writes ANSI top-bar text before placing the affordance-scene top-bar image. This duplicates terminal text under/over graphical chrome and contributes to text/image fighting and flicker. Gate the ANSI top-bar write to the non-affordance fallback path and add coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-terminal events-ms errors: build diagnostics directly
description: parse_events_ms uses format! to build missing/invalid milliseconds diagnostics. Build those small diagnostics directly into preallocated strings while preserving flag-specific error text.

description:
Docs drift: docs/kittwm-semantic-surfaces.md still describes browser DOM/ARIA and accessibility adapters as planned, even though browser extraction/publish/action/CLI inspection and accessibility safe core/action routing/role remaps have landed. Refresh the adapter status section without changing protocol design.

Scope:

- Mention landed browser DOM/ARIA/DevTools and accessibility safe core/action routing status.
- Clarify direct platform bindings and richer lifecycle remain follow-up work.

Acceptance:
- Semantic surfaces protocol doc no longer calls landed browser/accessibility adapter work merely planned.
- `git diff --check` passes.

title: kittwm-terminal graphics: avoid double allocation for short scene labels
description: terminal_scene_label_text preallocates bounded output, pushes chars, but still discards that buffer and calls text.to_string() for non-truncated labels. That does duplicate work/allocation in status/events graphics paths. Return the already-built bounded output when the label fits while preserving truncation semantics.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help session: include save/restore examples
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. The `kittwm help session` topic explains session save/restore flags, but it does not include copyable examples for saving to a file, restoring from a file, or using stdout/stdin. Add concise example lines plus focused assertion coverage so operators can discover session workflows directly from the main session topic.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Include schema summaries in kittui-md mode-search-json
description: Extend --mode-search-json results so each matching JSON output mode includes its top-level JSON keys and schema description, matching the targeted --mode-info-json surface. Document it and add output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daily help scene: build row labels directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: daily_help_scene still builds each row layer label with format! from kind, row index, and trimmed row label. Add a direct preallocated row-label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Show link and image titles in kittui-md references
description: Update the combined kittui-md --references output so link/image title attributes preserved by bd-59f51b are visible there too, not only in --links/--images/JSON. Add targeted tests and docs if wording needs adjustment.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm CLI events snapshot request: build directly
description: load_events_snapshot still formats EVENTS request strings before querying the daemon. Build EVENTS <ms> directly through the existing validated request helper while preserving timeout validation and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui inline chip full-width graphics and theme
description:
Inline chip visual correctness/theme follow-up.

User reports kitty graphics now render but are confined to the 1x1 placeholder cell. Expected behavior:
- `kittui inline chip --text abcdef` should render a pill background across text width + padding, anchored at the inline position, while printing visible text fallback.
- Image outside rounded chip border should be transparent.
- Defaults should be attractive/easy: Nord glass style by default; support simple theme/style/color overrides.

Scope:
- Change kitty inline placement to a cursor-anchored background image spanning the full chip footprint, behind visible text, instead of U=1 placeholder-only placement confined to one cell.
- Render a transparent-background rounded pill scene with fill/stroke/highlight using defaults like nord/glass.
- Keep `plain`, `ansi`, `tmux` fallbacks.
- Add minimal `--theme`, `--style`, `--bg-color`, `--border-color`, `--fg-color` support if tractable.
- Validate dry-run placement has no U=1 and spans full footprint; tests/build.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Let kittwm remote HOST apps accept positional queries
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: make listing remote apps easier by allowing `kittwm remote HOST apps firefox` as shorthand for `kittwm remote HOST apps --filter firefox`, while preserving `--first`, `--launch-first`, `--limit`, and `--json`. This keeps the top-level remote family easy for app list/launch workflows over pooled SSH.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kitwm record --apng: write single animated PNG instead of frame directory
description: Add --apng flag to 'kitwm record' that encodes all captured frames as a single animated PNG via kittui_render_cpu::encode_apng, plus --delay-ms for per-frame delay (default 33ms ~30fps). Output path becomes <out>/kitwm.apng instead of frame-NNNNN-winK.png stills. Makes the captured pipeline shareable as one file. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: prioritize active workspace in narrow top bars
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: constrained text and graphical top-bar paths only prioritize custom active workspace labels. If the active workspace is a numeric default such as 3, narrow terminals can still render inactive 1/2 first and omit the active workspace. Scope: prioritize the active workspace chip whenever the chip list is constrained, for both text render_i3bar and live graphical overlay labels; add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: wire native session socket SPAWN_PTY to visible panes
description:
Large gap: kittwm child apps inherit `KITTWM_SOCKET`, but the live native WM session does not consume socket spawn requests into visible panes. The daemon `SPAWN` path launches detached null-stdio processes, so the socket is not yet DISPLAY-like for terminal panes.

Scope:
- Add a live native-session socket command queue for `SPAWN_PTY <cmd>`.
- Start that queue server inside the no-arg native PTY session on the exported socket.
- Drain queued PTY spawn requests each frame and create visible native panes with the requested command.
- Keep existing standalone daemon `SPAWN` behavior intact; add tests for command queue parsing/draining.

Acceptance:
- `SPAWN_PTY htop` over the native session socket queues a visible pane spawn.
- Queue tests cover empty command errors and FIFO draining.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md code-blocks mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --code-blocks mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add placement contract role helpers
description: Add typed helpers on `SurfacePlacementContract` for common WM placement roles: app-surface, decoration, overlay, and z-order comparison. This lets SDK apps reason about placement contracts without string-matching plane names or manually comparing z-indexes. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm daemon PANES_JSON reply: build directly
description: Standalone daemon PANES_JSON replies still use format! only to append a newline to the panes JSON value. Reuse the direct JSON-line helper while preserving panes JSON coverage.

title: KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY socket: detach/reattach session daemon (graphical tmux)
description:
Split kittui-wm into a long-lived daemon process (server) and a thin viewer process (client), communicating over a Unix domain socket at KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock, analogous to tmux TMUX or X11 DISPLAY). The daemon owns all backends (Quartz capture, Xvfb, XQuartz, remote SSH), runs the Pump threads, holds the layout and focus state, and survives client disconnects. The client (kittui-wm-demo / kittui-wm attach) is just a renderer: connects to the socket, requests scene snapshots, sends pointer/key events back, redraws kitty graphics. Multiple clients can attach simultaneously to the same session (mirroring tmux pane sharing) - useful for pair-programming-over-SSH and for SSH-survives-disconnect. Wire format: length-prefixed JSON or bincode; v1 supports CMD_LIST, CMD_TICK (returns last frame + window metadata), CMD_INPUT (forwards InputEvent), CMD_RESIZE, CMD_QUIT_SESSION. kittui-wm-demo grows --attach/--detach/--kill modes. Session ids are operator-configurable (KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:1 -> /tmp/kittui-wm-1.sock). Makes the whole stack tmux-shaped: daemon stays running, hosted apps survive client disconnect, you can SSH in from anywhere and reattach to your existing kittui-wm session at the exact state you left it, multiple displays render the same session. Parent epic: kittui-wm v2.
