Add concise helper example lines plus focused assertion coverage so operators can discover the polished event helper workflows from the main events topic.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm launcher preview: build row text directly
description: Small kittwm launcher text-mode performance cleanup: the launcher preview loop still builds each candidate row with `format!("{marker} {:>2}. [{:<5}] ...")` before truncating. Add a direct row builder with preallocated capacity while preserving the text-mode launcher output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: support native PTY alternate screen
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm panes do not honor alternate-screen DEC private modes. Full-screen TUIs commonly enter `CSI ? 1049 h` and leave with `CSI ? 1049 l`; without support, their output contaminates the normal scrollback/snapshot and restoring the shell prompt view is impossible.

Scope:
- Track normal vs alternate screen buffers in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle DEC private `?1049h` / `?1049l` (and compatible `?47`/`?1047`) in CSI dispatch.
- Preserve current visible snapshot behavior while active; restore the normal buffer and cursor on leave.
- Resize both active and saved buffers sanely.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Entering alternate screen hides normal-buffer text from `text_snapshot`.
- Leaving alternate screen restores normal text/cursor.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm launcher graphics: saturate candidate row count
description: kittwm launcher preview scene computes rows with candidates.len() as u16 + 5 before clamping. Extremely large candidate lists can wrap before clamp and produce wrong scene metadata. Use saturated row accounting before clamping.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward F8 input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards F5-F7 but still consumes F8 (`ESC [ 19 ~`). Some browser/web-app workflows bind higher function keys. Forward F8 through the existing DevTools function-key path to keep input parity moving.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-launch unknown option diagnostic should build directly
description: `LaunchArgs::parse_from` builds unknown-option diagnostics with `format!("unknown option {other}\n\n{}", help_text())`. Build the diagnostic directly into a preallocated String while preserving the user-facing error text. Scope: kittwm-launch unknown option diagnostic only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: throttle raw compositor footer repaints
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: `run_compositor_session` rewrites the terminal footer every frame, and the footer text includes the changing frame counter/FPS. Even when all window image uploads are skipped, this forces constant ANSI text repaint at the bottom of the screen, adding flicker and terminal throughput. Throttle footer writes to state changes or periodic telemetry refreshes; clear the old footer row if its row moves; add deterministic helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui: separate render scene from terminal placement
description:
Large gap: kittui rendering and terminal placement are conflated. CompositionEntry carries a footprint, but Composer calls `runtime.place(&scene)`, so moving an existing rendered scene requires mutating the scene footprint and can change the scene/cache id.

Scope:
- Add a high-level `Runtime::place_at`/placement override API that renders/caches a scene using its scene-local footprint while placing it at a caller-supplied terminal footprint with matching dimensions.
- Update `Composer::apply` to honor `CompositionEntry.footprint` through the new API.
- Add regression tests showing the same scene id/image id can move to a different x/y and that Composer emits placement for moved entries without requiring a new scene.

Acceptance:
- Moving an entry in a Composition changes placement position but not scene id/image id.
- Existing `Runtime::place` behavior remains unchanged.
- Targeted kittui tests and build pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm launcher preview kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make the built-in kittwm launcher preview available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add launcher-scene-json and launcher-kitty/launcher-graphics commands that render existing launcher candidate preview data as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing launcher text and launch-selection behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm launcher-scene-json [--filter Q] [--limit N] [--select N] emits a kittui Scene summarizing query, selected row, and candidate rows.
- kittwm launcher-kitty/launcher-graphics places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing launcher text and --launch-selection behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from synthetic launcher candidates.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Shift-Insert and Shift-Delete input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards Insert/Delete and shifted navigation keys, but consumes Shift+Insert/Shift+Delete CSI (`ESC [ 2 ; 2 ~` / `ESC [ 3 ; 2 ~`). These are common paste/cut editing keys in terminal/browser workflows. Forward them with the Shift modifier.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm keymap check should stream duplicate action labels
description: `kittwm keymap check` reports duplicate chord actions with `actions.join(", ")`, allocating a joined string for each duplicate chord. Stream the labels directly to stdout instead while preserving the text output. Scope: keymap check reporting only; avoid keymap parser/chord-string work owned by other agents. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-renderer: do not toast persistent footer status
description: User reports opening a terminal shows random chrome near the bottom (three ovals/extra stuff). Graphical chrome currently turns every persistent footer/status line into both a footer scene and a floating toast, so normal help/status text appears as extra decorative bottom chrome. Restrict toast rendering to actual transient/error messages and keep persistent footer status as only the footer bar.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-cli: render kittwm session manifest previews
description:
Large integration gap: kittwm can save/restore `SESSION_JSON`, and `kittui wm-chrome` can render one window frame, but there is no kittui-side command that turns a native kittwm session manifest into a preview/export scene set. This limits shell/external-platform workflows that want to inspect or render the WM layout without attaching to a live terminal.

Scope:
- Add `kittui wm-session <session.json|-> -w/--width W -h/--height H`.
- Parse `SESSION_JSON` manifests and build one `WindowChromeTheme` scene per pane.
- Honor manifest `layout` (`columns`/`rows`), pane order, weights, titles, and focused flags.
- Route through the existing batch emit pipeline so `--scene-json`, `--json`, and placement output work.
- Add targeted unit coverage.

Acceptance:
- `kittui --scene-json wm-session manifest.json ...` emits an array of scenes labelled with kittwm chrome labels.
- Weighted rows/columns affect scene footprints.
- Targeted kittui CLI tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Respect desktop visibility in installed remote app parser
description: The pooled-SSH fallback shell honours Linux .desktop OnlyShowIn/NotShowIn/TryExec metadata, but the installed/local Rust app parser currently only filters Type, Hidden, NoDisplay, and Exec. Bring healthy remote kittwm app discovery closer to fallback parity by teaching the Rust parser to respect OnlyShowIn, NotShowIn, and TryExec, including simple argument-bearing TryExec values. Cover with focused parser tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm log: make usage errors actionable
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm log` argument errors currently collapse to a terse usage string (`kittwm log path | kittwm log tail [-f]`). Make the log usage error actionable with copyable examples and a `kittwm help log` pointer, plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-sdk: event envelope convenience accessors
description:
SDK event ergonomics gap: `KittwmEvent` variants carry `EventEnvelope`, but clients must match every variant just to inspect common metadata/detail fields. Add additive convenience accessors.

Scope:
- Add `KittwmEvent::envelope()` returning `Option<&EventEnvelope>` for all typed variants and `None` for Unknown, plus `raw()` if useful.
- Add small `EventEnvelope` detail helpers such as `detail_str`, `detail_bool`, `detail_u64` for common event payloads.
- Add targeted unit tests covering status/semantic/surface events and unknown fallback.
- Do not change parsing behavior or daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK event consumers can inspect common metadata/details without repetitive matches.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` event tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md preserves Markdown table column alignment
description: Pulldown-cmark exposes table column alignments but kittui-md currently drops them. Preserve alignment metadata in MarkdownTable, include it in metadata JSON, and add tests for left/center/right alignment.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm graphical launcher overlay: build title directly
description: Replace `format!("kittwm launcher query={}", overlay.query)` in test-only `launcher_overlay_scene_for_candidates(...)` with a direct preallocated title helper. Scope only graphical launcher overlay scene title construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Implement user Shader node rendering and pipeline cache
description: DESIGN.md/Future ideas mention Node::Shader with per-scene WGSL pipeline compilation, caching, and GPU execution, while CPU should return a clear GPU-only error. Current GPU encode accepts Shader in the type system but draws nothing, and CPU returns UnsupportedImage. Implement shader validation, uniform binding, per-scene pipeline cache, error reporting, and tests that Shader nodes either render through GPU or fail clearly under CPU-only mode.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: clamp viewport resize pixels to nonzero
description: Browser resize hardening follow-up: `browser_viewport_pixels(cols, rows)` maps cells directly to pixels, so a direct/edge-case `resize_surface(0, 0)` request can produce a zero-sized DevTools viewport. kittwm usually clamps app cells before resize, but the NativeSurface API should be defensive. Clamp browser viewport conversion to at least 1x1 cells before multiplying so DevTools receives nonzero dimensions; update focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-renderer: expose frame write byte telemetry
description: Add pane_frame_presented renderer byte telemetry (upload/placement/embed bytes) so kittwm flicker/performance debugging can distinguish heavy frame uploads from cheap placement moves and verify no placeholder embed text is emitted for absolute native placements.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Report selected remote app candidate in launch JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST launch QUERY --json` reports kind/method/name/pid, but not the selected candidate id or desktop file. Include candidate and desktop_file fields so pooled-SSH app launch diagnostics can explain exactly which PATH command, macOS app, or Linux desktop entry was selected.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add pooled-ssh remote app discovery and launch fallback
description: Operator request slice: from local kittwm, support `kittwm apps --remote HOST` using pooled SSH. If the remote has kittwm installed, delegate to remote `kittwm apps`; if not, auto-detect path commands remotely and support list/json/first/launch-first fallback. This bead covers app discovery/launch only; remote window/display/terminal forwarding should be tracked by follow-up beads.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: spike macOS AX semantic adapter
description: Implement a macOS Accessibility proof for kittwm semantic surfaces. Associate a captured app/window with an AXUIElement window, extract a bounded semantic snapshot mapping common AX roles to ComponentNode values, and report Accessibility permission diagnostics. Follow-up from bd-4a49aa.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm PTY renderer: reduce background-fill pixel index work
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: fill_cell_background is only called for in-grid cells, but recomputes the full row-major pixel index for every pixel in the cell. Styled backgrounds are common in TUIs, so this repeats avoidable multiplication in the renderer hot path. Scope: fill each cell row with an incrementing RGBA index/in-bounds write helper, preserving styled background rendering with focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Fix text rendering in kittwm
description:
Text elements (time, workspace labels, status indicators, etc.) are not appearing in the kittwm window manager chrome. Investigate rendering pipeline and ensure all text is properly positioned and visible in the compositor.

Acceptance criteria:
- Time display visible in kittwm bar
- Workspace labels visible in kittwm bar
- Other status/info text properly rendered
- Text is legible and positioned correctly
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: Add kittui-md notes alias for footnotes
description: Add --notes as a friendly alias for kittui-md --footnotes so users can inspect Markdown footnote references and definitions with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm overlay test tempdirs: build directly
description: Replace overlay test tempdir filename `format!` in `crates/kittui-overlay/src/lib.rs` with direct preallocated String assembly. Scope only kittui overlay runtime test tempdir path construction used by kittwm/overlay validation helpers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm SDK wait-ms requests: build directly
description: SurfaceHandle wait_text_ms and wait_output_ms still format WAIT_TEXT_MS/WAIT_OUTPUT_MS request strings. Build these through a direct preallocated wait request helper while preserving timeout clamping, needle validation, and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: fix split overlap and host-resize logical reflow
description: User report: splitting a terminal leaves the original pane at the same size, causing the new split to overlap/flicker. Resizing the host terminal scales the existing graphics instead of reflowing to a new logical split layout. Fix kittwm layout/render loop so pane frames are recomputed at the current host cell/pixel geometry, splits do not overlap, and host resize triggers logical rows/cols/layout updates rather than bitmap scaling. Acceptance: split panes occupy disjoint app/chrome regions; host terminal resize recomputes layout and re-captures at native resolution/DPI; targeted tests/smoke cover split and host-resize geometry.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: docs: update first-party kittwm app docs
description:
Documentation follow-up after kittwm-terminal/kittwm-launch maturity. Update docs to describe first-party standalone SDK apps, including kittwm-terminal status/events and kittwm-launch backend/dry-run/status behavior after it lands.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md or docs/README.md with first-party app commands.
- Mention kittwm-terminal --status / --events-ms.
- Mention kittwm-launch maturity behavior once landed.
- Keep docs concise and link to SDK plan.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect current first-party app capabilities.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK title drag handle coordinate helper
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/dragging cleanliness slice: pane metadata now exposes title_draggable and geometry, but SDK clients still have to compute a safe host-cell coordinate for title-row drag start manually. Add a small NativePaneDetail helper that returns a title drag handle cell when the pane reports title_draggable and geometry is available, with tests and docs.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: support extended SGR colors in native PTY renderer
description:
Large terminal-WM fidelity gap after basic SGR support: many modern shells and TUIs use 256-color and truecolor SGR sequences (`38;5;n`, `48;5;n`, `38;2;r;g;b`, `48;2;r;g;b`). Native kittwm currently preserves only the 16 ANSI colors, so many prompts/status bars still lose their intended colors.

Scope:
- Extend native `TerminalState` SGR parsing to handle semicolon and colon/subparameter forms for:
  - `38;5;n` / `48;5;n` 256-color foreground/background.
  - `38;2;r;g;b` / `48;2;r;g;b` truecolor foreground/background.
- Keep existing 16-color, reset/default, bold, and reverse behavior.
- Add parser/render tests for 256-color and truecolor cells.

Acceptance:
- Tests show 256-color and truecolor SGR are stored on cells and affect RGBA output.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native terminal renderer: avoid top-bar string build for empty workspace
description: native_shell_view builds native_top_bar_text through BarModel::render_i3bar even for the startup empty workspace where pane count/focus are stable. Avoid unnecessary focus/socket formatting in the empty-workspace fast path by using the static workspace/clock bar model directly, preserving output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm bar: graphical workspace chips and high-contrast themed clock
description: User reports the kittwm bar still appears as ASCII `| 1 | 2 |` text; workspace numbers should render as kittui chip/action surfaces for clickability, the clock/time contrast is too low, and the bar should use configured colorscheme with richer translucent styling/drop shadow. Implement focused top-bar/chrome scene improvements and keep pure terminal fallback usable.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm panic log: build directly
description: Replace the `log_kittwm_process_event(&format!("panic: {}", panic_payload_message(...)))` temporary in `kittwm` startup error handling with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only kittwm panic diagnostic log string construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; avoid active SSH/remote doctor/packaging work.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: tolerate focused pane keyboard byte failures
description: Keyboard input stability issue: live kittwm forwards parsed/raw host keyboard bytes to the focused pane with `pane.app.send_bytes(...)?`. If the focused pane has a dead PTY or a direct surface rejects byte input, that error can propagate out of the native loop and exit kittwm. Make focused keyboard byte forwarding best-effort: log a bounded per-pane diagnostic and continue the WM loop, while preserving prefix/shortcut handling. Add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-terminal graphics: preallocate scene label truncation
description: kittwm-terminal terminal_scene_label_text now bounds huge labels but still grows an initially empty String for every clipped status/event label. Preallocate the requested label width before pushing chars to reduce small redraw allocations while preserving label output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm launcher F12 overlay-open log: build directly
description: The raw compositor F12 launcher overlay-open diagnostic still formats the query with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: add inline script UI showcase bash script
description:
Add a first-party bash showcase script that demonstrates kittui as an inline-script UI substrate: title bar, partitioned panels, inline rows/chips/badges/segments/dividers, control-like affordances, footer, varied themes/styles, and animation flags with safe terminal fallback.

Acceptance:
- Script is executable and self-contained.
- Supports graphics/static/text modes and uses tmux-safe default fallback unless explicitly overridden.
- Validates with bash -n and a non-graphics smoke run.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: examples: publish synthetic semantic SDK snapshot
description:
Follow-up from synthetic semantic SDK app and SEMANTIC_PUBLISH. Teach the synthetic semantic SDK app example to publish its generated settings snapshot to the current/focused kittwm surface, not just print/query snapshots.

Scope:
- Add a `--publish-current` or `--publish WINDOW` flag to `kittwm_semantic_app`.
- Use `kittwm-sdk` `semantic_publish` wrapper.
- Keep default behavior as printing JSON.
- Add tests for argument behavior or publish target selection without requiring a live daemon.

Acceptance:
- Example can publish generated semantic snapshot to kittwm when connected.
- Existing `--query-current` and print behavior remain.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix kitty upload chunk syntax causing Ghostty ENOENT in showcase
description: Harry reported cargo run -p kittui-cli --example showcase in Ghostty prints placeholder glyphs plus kitty responses like Gi=<id>;ENOENT: image not found. Inspection found kittui-kitty encode_chunked emits upload commands as ESC_G,a=t,... with a leading comma before a=t; placement commands use ESC_Ga=p... and are parsed, so Ghostty reports placements for images whose uploads were ignored. Fix upload chunk control syntax, add regression coverage, and verify kittui-kitty tests. Filed from operator report.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm semantic scene labels: build translated labels directly
description: Replace `format!("semantic_{index}_{label}")` in kittui-wm semantic scene layer translation with a direct preallocated String helper. Scope only semantic layer label construction in `crates/kittui-wm/src/semantic.rs`; preserve semantic surface rendering coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward Backspace input to pages
description: kittwm-browser filters stdin to printable ASCII/newline and drops Backspace/Delete, making text fields hard to edit. Preserve text ordering and dispatch Backspace key events through HeadlessBrowserApp so browser panes respond to deletion.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add drag chip to footer status scene
description: UX follow-up: the terminal footer exposes active title-drag state as drag:move/reorder, but the affordance-scene footer labels only help/focus/state/terminal/close. Add a drag footer chip label so graphical scene artifacts can identify active-drag state without scraping text. Filed from ongoing kittwm footer visual-state audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Use gio launch before Exec in remote desktop fallback
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm Linux desktop app launch currently prefers gtk-launch, then falls back directly to parsed Exec. Add `gio launch <desktop-file>` as an intermediate launcher when available, so remote app launches preserve more desktop-entry semantics on Linux boxes where gtk-launch is absent or fails.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-terminal: avoid full scans in scene label truncation
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: kittwm-terminal status/events graphical labels are clipped, but terminal_scene_label_text still counts every character before taking the visible prefix. Pathological status or event-kind strings can cause full-string scans before clipping. Scope: rewrite the helper to use bounded prefix/lookahead construction and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: bindings: add render-many-to-directory helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: FFI/Python/TypeScript can return render-many manifests with base64 PNGs, and the CLI can write batch PNGs to a directory, but platform bindings do not provide the equivalent file-output helper. External hosts have to manually decode base64 manifests and invent naming/manifest conventions.

Scope:
- Add Python `Kittui.render_many_to_dir(scenes, out_dir, prefix="scene")` helper.
- Add TypeScript `Kittui.renderManyToDir(scenes, outDir, options?)` helper.
- Write deterministic files `prefix-00000.png`, etc. and a `manifest.json` with filenames.
- Keep using existing `render_many` / `renderMany` FFI manifest, no ABI change.
- Add binding tests and README updates.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS helpers decode `png_base64` and write files/manifest.
- Existing Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add primitive glow and scanline helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: Python/TypeScript helpers cover solid, gradient, and image primitives, but core effect primitives (`Glow`, `Scanlines`) still require hand-authored Scene JSON. These effects are useful for shell previews and kittwm/chrome-like external renderers.

Scope:
- Add Python `scene.glow_layer(...)`, `scene.glow_box(...)`, `scene.scanlines_layer(...)`, `scene.scanlines_box(...)` helpers.
- Add TypeScript `scene.glowLayer(...)`, `scene.glowBox(...)`, `scene.scanlinesLayer(...)`, `scene.scanlinesBox(...)` helpers.
- Keep helpers primitive-only and JSON-compatible.
- Add binding tests and README examples.

Acceptance:
- Python and TS users can build valid Glow and Scanlines scenes without hand-authoring schema.
- Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Explain missing display during remote app fallback launch
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm app launch requests `ssh -Y`, but if the remote session still has no DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY, Linux desktop fallback launch can fail opaquely through gtk-launch/gio/Exec. Add an explicit preflight/error in the desktop-launch fallback path so `kittwm remote HOST launch QUERY` tells users to run the forwarding doctor/check X11 forwarding instead of silently trying GUI launchers without a display.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add kittwm --nudge-pane long option
description: Operator-requested kittwm floating-control consistency slice: kittwm has long --move-pane/--resize-pane wrappers and a short nudge alias, but no matching --nudge-pane wrapper. Add --nudge-pane WINDOW DX DY so scripts that prefer long options can use the precise floating-pane nudge control consistently with other pane controls.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Release prep: README v0.3 + DESIGN.md update + version bump
description:
README leads with kitwm + a 30-second-quickstart. DESIGN.md gets a kittui-wm v2 section reflecting the daemon/client model. Workspace version 0.3.0. CHANGELOG entry summarising the v2 epic landings. Tag release once all P0/P1 children of bd-031e54 close. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-bc8121

Title: Backend manifest spec + persistent session file

Add a session file format (~/.config/kittui-wm/session.toml) describing which backends to spawn on startup, their config, default layouts, per-window chrome themes. kittui-wm loads it at startup; the operator picker writes back to it. Means the operator can configure 'on launch, attach Quartz to display 1, spawn XQuartz at :99 with xterm tiled top-left, mirror Safari floating bottom-right'.

Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

## Merged duplicate: bd-4b801d

Title: docs/wm.md v2: multi-backend mental model + operator workflow

Rewrite docs/wm.md to lead with the multi-backend mental model: any window is local/remote/Quartz/XQuartz, rendered as homogenous kittui chrome. Include backend matrix from the design conversation, the SSH-into-Mac story, the mixed X11+macOS session example, and the limits (no Cocoa hosting without v3 CGVirtualDisplay).

Parent epic: bd-031e54

Update docs/wm.md macOS section: brew install --cask xquartz, then cargo run --release -p kittui-cli --example kittui_wm_demo --features quartz,xquartz. Include a Nix fragment that adds /opt/X11/bin to PATH for the devshell on Darwin. Parent epic: bd-031e54
