## Merged duplicate: bd-19ccb5

Title: Doc: SSH operator walkthrough

docs/operator-walkthrough.md: step-by-step for the SSH-into-Mac use case. Includes generating an SSH key for kittui-wm, enabling Screen Recording for sshd, setting KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY, attaching, detaching, killing the session. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm keymap: parse key specs without Vec
description: Small kittwm keymap performance cleanup: KeySpec::parse currently collects split tokens into a Vec just to pop the final key. Parse the final key with rsplit_once and iterate modifier tokens directly to avoid transient Vec allocation while preserving keymap UX.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Accept --fallback on remote launch aliases
description: Remote fallback wrapper documentation implies `kittwm remote HOST launch QUERY --fallback` is equivalent to `kittwm remote HOST fallback launch QUERY`, but the launch parser currently rejects `--fallback` as an unknown remote launch flag. Accept `--fallback` in `parse_remote_launch_alias`, advertise it in help/catalog, and cover parser behavior so launch/open/run/start can force pooled-SSH app launch fallback directly.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-launch: mature standalone SDK launcher
description:
First-party app gap: `kittwm-launch` exists as a skeleton, but browser/app/terminal backend behavior and app discovery integration are still shallow. Mature it as a standalone SDK launcher.

Scope:
- Audit current `kittwm-launch` skeleton.
- Improve backend selection, app discovery replies, browser command behavior, status output, and error messages.
- Prefer typed SDK surfaces where available and explicit fallback where not.
- Add targeted tests for backend selection/request construction.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-launch is a more useful first-party SDK launcher.
- Existing CLI behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help inspect: include helper examples
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces, chrome-scene, or info-header work. The `kittwm help inspect` topic lists inspection commands, but does not include copyable first-party helper examples like `kittwm-top --json` or `kittwm-browser --semantic-snapshot ...`. Add concise helper example lines plus focused assertion coverage so operators can discover the polished inspection helpers from the main inspect topic.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm debug clock: build directly
description: The kittwm session debug timestamp helper still formats seconds/milliseconds with format!. Build that clock string directly while preserving exact output shape.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK helper for active title drag window
description: SDK follow-up: active title-drag pane/kind/cell helpers exist, but callers still unwrap the pane just to get the active window id for logging or UI labels. Add active_title_drag_window() on Status/PanesStatus as a small parity helper for active drag state. Filed from ongoing kittwm SDK/drag-state audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: honor application cursor-key mode in native panes
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not honor DECCKM application cursor-key mode (`CSI ? 1 h/l`). Full-screen TUIs commonly enable it and expect arrow keys as SS3 `ESC O A/B/C/D`; native host-key routing and socket status currently ignore the mode, so navigation can be misinterpreted.

Scope:
- Track application cursor-key mode in native `TerminalState`, default false.
- Handle `CSI ? 1 h` / `CSI ? 1 l`.
- Expose the mode from `PtyTerminalApp` and native pane status/JSON/text listings.
- Route parsed host arrow key events to the focused native pane as SS3 arrows when the pane has application cursor mode enabled, otherwise preserve normal CSI arrows/raw behavior.
- Add focused tests for mode tracking and payload selection.

Acceptance:
- Tests show the mode toggles via parser sequences.
- Tests show arrow payloads switch between CSI and SS3 forms.
- Targeted cargo tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Match Linux desktop Categories remotely
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm Linux desktop fallback now indexes Name, GenericName, and Keywords, but not `Categories=`. Include Categories in the candidate search payload so `kittwm remote HOST apps utility`, `network`, or `TerminalEmulator` style launcher/category queries work better over pooled SSH.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: make kittwm-bar reserve chrome and render kitty-native
description:
Make first-party kittwm-bar a real SDK/chrome app: it can reserve/clear top-bar drawable space through typed kittwm-sdk, and it can render its kittui title-bar scene directly as kitty graphics rather than only text/JSON/scene-json.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-bar supports a graphics output mode that places the existing kittui scene.
- kittwm-bar supports --reserve and --release/--clear-reservation using Kittwm::reserve_chrome / clear_chrome_reservation.
- JSON model includes extended reservation metadata from CHROME_JSON.
- Tests cover CLI parse/model shape without touching live session internals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add login remote shell alias
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote shell aliases include shell/sh/ssh/console/tty, but the help text explicitly describes a login shell pane. Add `kittwm remote HOST login [CMD]` as a discoverable pooled-SSH terminal alias and advertise it in help/catalog.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: make graphical top-bar text overlay follow configured colors
description: Operator loop: keep polishing kittwm usability. Review found live graphical top-bar text overlay still hardcodes Nord-ish ANSI foreground/background colors even though the kittwm bar scene/theme now follows KittwmConfig. This can produce mismatched or low-contrast chip labels/clock when users configure different colors or opacity. Scope: derive overlay ANSI colors from the resolved native chrome colors/config accent/fill/foreground, preserve high-contrast active chips, and add targeted unit coverage for the palette helper.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm graphical launcher overlay: build candidate rows directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("{}. [{}] {}", ...)` launcher graphical overlay row construction with a direct preallocated row helper. Scope only `launcher_overlay_scene_for_candidates(...)` row string construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing launcher/picker scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Fallback to desktop Exec when gtk-launch fails
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST launch QUERY` uses `gtk-launch` for Linux `.desktop` entries when available, and only falls back to `Exec=` when gtk-launch is missing. If gtk-launch exists but cannot launch the selected entry, still try the parsed `Exec=` command so remote app launch is more robust on minimal or misconfigured Linux desktops.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-terminal scene JSON: append newline directly
description: kittwm-terminal status/events scene JSON paths serialize a scene and then use format!("{json}\n") just to append a newline. Append the newline directly after serialization while preserving output and errors.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-ts: expose probe and unplace lifecycle APIs
description:
Large gap: the TypeScript koffi binding still does not expose FFI lifecycle/introspection functions `kittui_probe_json` and `kittui_unplace`, even though shell/CLI and C ABI users can inspect runtime transport/ABI details and delete uploaded images. JS/TS hosts need the same renderer lifecycle controls.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_probe_json` and `kittui_unplace` in `bindings/ts/src/index.js`.
- Add `probe()` returning parsed JSON and `unplace(imageId)` returning delete bytes.
- Accept decimal numbers and hex strings for image ids if practical.
- Update TypeScript declarations and README docs.
- Add fake-library tests that assert probe parsing and unplace forwarding.

Acceptance:
- `Kittui.probe()` returns an object parsed from FFI JSON.
- `Kittui.unplace(0x1234)` forwards image id and returns delete bytes.
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: ensure graphics image lifecycle does not leak or flicker
description: QA gap: recent flicker came from delete-before-reupload. Audit kitty image id/placement lifecycle for steady frames, moved panes, closed panes, overlays, host resize, and renderer switches. Ensure deletes only happen when safe and resources are reclaimed without visible blank frames. Acceptance: tests/smoke cover no steady-state delete, delete on pane removal, re-place on footprint move, and no overlap/flicker regressions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add SDK helpers for pane weight chip labels
description: UX/SDK follow-up: live kittwm pane status chips include `wt:<n>` when a tiled pane has a non-default resize weight, but SDK consumers only have boolean resized helpers and raw weight. Add NativePaneDetail and focused Status/PanesStatus helpers for the weight chip label so clients can mirror resized-pane visual state consistently. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm XWindow capture context: build directly
description: Replace `format!("capture X window {:?}", self.window.id)` in `crates/kittui-wm/src/native.rs` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only XWindowSurface capture context labels; preserve exact text and native capture behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: avoid full scans in CLI/status truncation helper
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs has a shared truncate helper used for window lists and graphical scene label payloads. It counts every character before taking the visible prefix, so pathological owner/title/status/socket strings can still cause full-string scans before clipping. Scope: rewrite the helper to use bounded prefix/lookahead construction and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm config action log: build directly
description: The raw compositor config-action diagnostic still formats the action message with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: reset app frame memos on native clear
description: Audit finding for disappearing frames/flicker: native-session clear paths write CSI 2J and reset chrome memo state, but app frame placement/upload memo state remains. If terminal clearing or layout/action redraw invalidates kitty graphics/placements without changing app pixels/footprints, the renderer can skip upload/placement and leave panes blank. Reset app placement/upload dirty memos on full native clears so the next frame re-emits app graphics deterministically.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm runtime GPU error labels: build directly
description: Replace `format!("gpu error: {e}")` construction in `crates/kittui/src/lib.rs` with a direct String helper. Scope only kittui runtime GPU render error wrapping used by kittwm graphics fallback/error paths.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-launch: respect narrow plan scene width
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: kittwm-launch --plan-kitty/--plan-scene-json clamps scene width to a 32-column minimum even when COLUMNS is smaller. On very narrow terminals that can emit a graphics plan wider than the row, causing wrap/flicker. Scope: preserve default and upper bound but respect positive narrow COLUMNS/override values; keep internal command rect inside the scene; add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm quickstart: show zsh and fish completion setup
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm quickstart` now points at completions setup, but its copyable setup example is Bash-only while `kittwm help completions` and `kittwm examples` include Zsh/Fish setup. Add concise Zsh and Fish completion setup examples to quickstart's More help section plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add kittui-md slugs alias for anchors
description: Add --slugs as a concise alias for kittui-md --anchors so users can inspect generated heading slug anchors with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: kitwm with no args opens a usable session, launches xterm, you can type into it
description: Integration: kitwm starts, app launcher opens via Mod+Return, type 'xterm', press Enter, xterm appears in the kittui terminal, type 'echo hello' inside it, the chars route through, the output is captured back. Final visual proof via tendril screenshot. This is the v2 acceptance criterion. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Match remote Linux desktop apps by display name
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: improve the no-remote-kittwm Linux `.desktop` fallback so `kittwm remote HOST list apps QUERY` / `launch QUERY` match user-visible desktop entry `Name=` values as well as desktop file IDs, while still launching via the desktop ID. This makes remote app requests easier when IDs differ from visible app names.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native footer tests: build huge log path directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("/tmp/{}", "x".repeat(...))` in `native_footer_visible_text_clips_huge_log_paths_to_terminal_width` with a direct preallocated log-path helper. Scope only kittwm native footer test stress-data construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve footer truncation coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-bar: fall back to chrome workspace when status workspace is blank
description: Follow-up to kittwm-bar status normalization: `status_workspace_label` normalizes `Status::workspace_id()`, but the SDK helper prefers the top-level workspace field before chrome metadata. If top-level workspace is blank/whitespace while chrome.workspace is valid, kittwm-bar falls back to env/default instead of the live chrome workspace. Normalize top-level and chrome workspace fields independently so blank top-level values do not mask valid chrome metadata; add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm wait/event requests: build directly
description: events_request and wait_ms_request still use format! to build daemon request strings after numeric validation. Build EVENTS and WAIT_*_MS requests directly into preallocated Strings while preserving bounds checks and needle trimming.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Ctrl-Home and Ctrl-End input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards Ctrl+Arrow and plain/Shift Home/End, but not Ctrl+Home/End (`ESC [ 1 ; 5 H/F`). Text editors and pages use these for document start/end navigation. Forward them with the Ctrl modifier.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Markdown tables via kittui box-drawing glyph atlas and relative placement anchors
description: Implement markdown table rendering that replaces unicode box drawing characters with kittui image cells: every box-drawing glyph gets a unique cell-sized kittui image so a rendered table is visually connected as a whole when emitted through kitty unicode replacement. Use kitty virtual placement plus relative non-virtual positioning anchors (P=/Q=/H=/V=) to position these cell images consistently, and support placing a background image or panel beneath the table in z-space. Include exact protocol/placement tests and visual proof fixtures.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native pane titles: preallocate title output
description: native_pane_title_text is built for pane chrome every native terminal redraw. It now avoids formatting full titles before clipping, but still grows from an empty String. Preallocate the pane title width before pushing marker/window/title text and padding to reduce reallocations in the redraw path.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add explicit animation frame update primitives
description:
Follow-up from bd-510a36. Add typed kittui-kitty helpers for kitty animation/full-frame update experiments without coupling them to the live WM renderer.

Scope:
- Add explicit helpers for frame upload/update/control commands needed by full-frame animation/ring-buffer experiments.
- Keep helpers protocol-focused and tested for grammar.
- Do not change kittwm default rendering.

Acceptance:
- kittui-kitty exposes typed animation/update primitives with tests.
- Existing upload_animation behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md rich footer reports heading outline entries
description: Plain kittui-md output now reports a heading outline, but rich/interactive footer metadata still only lists links/images. Add a compact rich outline section that mirrors the plain outline indentation and test the formatter/status behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: disable extended line discipline in raw mode
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: raw mode disables ICANON/ECHO/ISIG but leaves IEXTEN enabled. On POSIX terminals IEXTEN can make the host line discipline intercept implementation-defined controls such as Ctrl-V/LNEXT before kittwm can route them to native terminal apps. Scope: clear IEXTEN in raw_mode_lflag and add focused coverage while preserving the existing Ctrl-C triple-press behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: build graphical chrome after native capture
description: Operator loop: keep finding/fixing kittwm flicker and usability issues. Review found the graphical native renderer builds shell chrome before capture_surface runs for panes, while capture can update surface metadata/text/dirty-frame state. This can leave title/status chrome one frame stale even though pane status telemetry is now fresh. Scope: keep pure-terminal behavior unchanged, but for graphical rendering capture panes first, then build/write shell chrome from post-capture metadata before flushing the frame. Add/update targeted coverage where practical.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-wm attach: thin viewer client
description:
kittui-wm attach (or kittui-wm with no args + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY set) connects to the daemon socket and runs the existing run_loop as a renderer: receives scene snapshots from CmdTick replies, posts InputEvents on stdin parse, never touches backends directly. Multiple clients can attach to the same session simultaneously. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-624e22
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm CLI: reject empty send-text payloads locally
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: `kittwm --send-text`, `--send-line`, and their `type`/`line` aliases can accept an empty string argument and format a command without payload, leaving the daemon to reject it after a socket round-trip. Reject empty text payloads locally while preserving whitespace-only payloads as valid typed spaces, matching daemon behavior. Add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: keep SEND_KEY unsupported-key help aligned
description: CLI/automation UX issue: the `SEND_KEY unsupported key` error text still lists only the old key set and omits newer accepted keys/aliases such as `shift-tab`, `f5`-`f12`, arrow aliases, and page-up/page-down spelling. Users who mistype a key get stale guidance. Centralize the supported-key help string beside the parser and update focused coverage so the error advertises the real accepted vocabulary.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add keyboard nudge shortcuts for floating kittwm panes
description: Operator-requested kittwm UX uplift: floating panes can be dragged with the pointer and nudged externally, but the in-session shortcut overlay has no keyboard way to make small floating-position adjustments. Add C-a w/a/s/d as focused floating-pane nudge shortcuts, document them in the shared shortcut catalog/help overlay, and keep the behavior bounded to floating mode so tiled app input is not surprised.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Expose title drag handle coordinates in kittwm status
description: Operator-requested kittwm dragging/SDK metadata slice: SDK can compute a title drag cell from pane geometry, but non-SDK JSON consumers still have to duplicate that calculation. Expose title_drag_col/title_drag_row in PANES_JSON/STATUS_JSON for title-draggable floating panes and have SDK NativePaneDetail consume those coordinates when available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: preallocate bounded status truncation
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser uses bounded truncation in the frame status path, but the helper grows output from an empty String. Preallocate the bounded width so repeated status formatting avoids small reallocations while preserving clipping semantics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: disable output post-processing in raw mode
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: RawMode currently adjusts input and local termios flags but leaves output post-processing enabled. POSIX raw mode should clear OPOST so the host terminal cannot rewrite output bytes (for example newline processing) while kittwm is emitting alt-screen control sequences, kitty graphics, and precise cursor-addressed chrome. Scope: add a raw_mode_oflag helper that clears OPOST, apply it in RawMode::enter, and add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native reap log: build directly
description: reap_exited_native_panes formats the native pane reaped log line in the hot native loop. Build that log line directly while preserving the diagnostic text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-bar reservation errors: build directly
description: Small kittwm-bar CLI performance cleanup: reservation mode maps SDK errors with small `format!("prefix: {err}")` allocations. Add a direct prefix/error builder and use it for connect, clear, and reserve reservation errors while preserving diagnostics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Show fullscreen marker in kittwm pane titles
description: UX/visual-state audit follow-up: kittwm pane titles now show focus, tiled reorder/resized, and floating drag/top/moved markers, but fullscreen mode has no title-row marker beyond focus. Add a compact fullscreen title marker so the active pane title communicates fullscreen state even when footer/status text is clipped or hidden. Keep tiled/floating markers distinct and covered by focused tests/docs. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-runtime: skip unchanged pane status publications
description: The native render loop calls NativeSpawnQueue::update_panes every frame, taking the queue lock and diffing/emitting event checks even when the pane status snapshot is unchanged. Memoize the last published native pane statuses in the session loop and only call update_panes when the snapshot changes. This reduces idle/runtime overhead without changing event semantics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add explicit socket and display CLI flags
description:
Large DISPLAY-like usability gap: kittwm supports `KITTWM_SOCKET` / `KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY` env resolution, but CLI users must set environment variables to target a specific socket/display. First-class `--socket` and `--display` flags make the WM control plane easier and less error-prone for shell scripts and external controllers.

Scope:
- Add global CLI flags:
  - `--socket PATH` sets `KITTWM_SOCKET` / `KITTWM_SOCK` for this invocation.
  - `--display DISPLAY` sets `KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY` / `KITTWM_DISPLAY` for this invocation.
- Apply before any socket-resolving command (`--serve`, wrappers, save/restore, status, attach, etc.).
- Validate the flags are not both supplied.
- Update help/docs and add focused parse/normalization tests.

Acceptance:
- `--socket` and `--display` affect default socket resolution through existing daemon helper.
- Supplying both errors clearly.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render and publish native PTY cursor visibility
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm tracks cursor position but the rendered PTY surface does not draw a cursor and does not honor DEC cursor visibility (`CSI ? 25 h/l`). Shells/editors rely on a visible caret; controllers can inspect cursor metadata but users cannot see it in the pane render.

Scope:
- Track cursor visibility in native `TerminalState` and handle DEC private `?25h` / `?25l`.
- Expose `PtyTerminalApp::cursor_visible()` and include optional `cursor_visible` in native pane status JSON/text surfaces.
- Render a visible cursor in `render_terminal_rgba` when enabled (without changing plain text snapshots).
- Update docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Parser test proves cursor visibility mode toggles.
- Render test proves the cursor changes RGBA output at the cursor cell.
- Daemon status tests include cursor visibility metadata.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-python: expose runtime configure
description:
Large gap: FFI live runtime_configure is now real, but the Python binding still only configures at construction time. Long-lived Python hosts need to reconfigure renderer/transport/terminal capability without closing and reopening.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_runtime_configure` in Python ctypes binding.
- Add `Kittui.configure(config: dict)` returning None or self, using the same error handling/last_error path.
- Add fake-CDLL tests for success and failure.
- Update Python README API/example.

Acceptance:
- Python tests pass.
- configure forwards JSON config and errors include last_error.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm bar: graphical chip active state follows workspace label
description: After graphical bar chip work, the top-bar scene/model and ANSI overlay can still hardcode workspace 1 active. Make graphical workspace chips/overlay use the configured/current workspace label so action chip state is accurate when KITTWM_WORKSPACE or future workspace state selects another workspace.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm replace request: build directly
description: Small kittwm CLI request-builder performance cleanup: `resolve_replace_action` still creates the replace spawn socket request with `format!("SPAWN {}", argv_to_shell_words(...))`, allocating a final formatted wrapper. Build the `SPAWN ` request directly while preserving replace/browser behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add pointer dragging for kittwm floating panes
description: During bd-de7d38, raise/lower controls were added over existing stack movement, but true pointer dragging for floating panes remains unimplemented. Implementing drag will likely need explicit mutable floating pane positions in session state, drag start/move/end tracking, z-order interaction, status metadata, and SDK/docs coverage. Track this as a focused future kittwm UX slice rather than burying it under raise/lower vocabulary work. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-de7d38 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Finish v1 CLI surface from DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md lists a broader CLI than currently implemented: panel, image, place, scene emit, cache info/gc/clear, probe --force, --transport, --renderer, --upload-only/--placement-only/--embed-only, --dry-run, pixel and percentage sizing, richer colors, glow/scanline/shadow/label options, and animation curve validation. Some cache commands exist, but most common flags and subcommands do not. Fill out the v1 CLI surface in thin wrappers over Runtime/Composition and add integration tests for JSON/dry-run/raw output modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK helpers for moved floating kittwm panes
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/design-cleanliness uplift: floating panes now support drag/nudge/reset and expose floating_dx/floating_dy, but SDK users must manually filter pane details to find panes with non-default offsets. Add convenience helpers on NativePaneDetail/PanesStatus/Status for non-zero floating offsets so automation can quickly find panes that have been moved and decide whether to reset them.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: skip raw loop flush when frame emitted no output
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported slow/flickery: after unchanged image uploads, chrome repaints, and footer repaints are skipped, `run_compositor_session` still calls `handle.flush()` every frame on the raw compositor success path. For idle/static frames that emitted no terminal bytes, this is unnecessary syscall/TTY churn. Track whether the frame actually wrote output and only flush when needed; add deterministic helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kitwm daemon APPS_FIRST/APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST verbs
description: Expose the launcher selection primitive through the daemon protocol. Add APPS_FIRST QUERY to return the first matching launcher candidate, and APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST QUERY to launch it and report pid/kind/name. This lets 'kitwm --attach -c' exercise search+selection+launch against a running daemon. Parent: bd-031e54; extends bd-f92697/bd-68568a and decomposes bd-8d64b1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SPP proof reports zero kitty placements
description: Follow-up from bd-45e324 targeted SPP proof: sampled kittwm runs with KITTWM_NATIVE_RENDERER=kitty / affordance-scene and TERM/KITTY env still produced manifests with kitty_placements=0 for every frame. This means the current proof path cannot demonstrate end-user SPP/kitty-graphics placement usability for live kittwm, even though frames are non-blank. Investigate whether kittwm is falling back to text/ANSI, emitting tmux-wrapped or unsupported graphics, or libghostty-vt is not extracting the placement form used by kittwm.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm pump error labels: build directly
description: Replace formatted pump error string construction in `crates/kittui-wm/src/lib.rs` with small direct preallocated helpers. Scope only `pump_worker` capture/windows error labels; preserve exact error text.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-terminal default shell command: build directly
description: default_terminal_command maps SHELL through format!("{shell} -l") when no configured terminal command is present. Build the login-shell command directly into one String while preserving env/config fallback behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK shortcuts catalog helper
description:
Docs follow-up for typed SDK shortcut catalog helper.

Scope:
- After source lands, document `Kittwm::shortcuts()` / `shortcuts_json()` and `SHORTCUTS_JSON` typed access.
- Mention relationship to `kittwm --shortcuts-json`, text shortcuts, and `C-a ?`.
- Docs-only; no source/runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention typed SDK helper and catalog shape at a high level.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitty animation control grammar wrong: s={loops},c={count} misuses state/current-frame fields
description: Per kitty spec, on a=a (animation control) the s field is state (1=stop, 2=loop, 3=play N times) and c is the current frame to display; loop count goes in v. We emit s={loops},c={framecount} which sets state to a bogus value and forces current frame to last. Result: animations don't loop correctly and display wrong frame on start. Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: fix core tiling non-overlap invariants
description: User report: kittwm fundamental tiling is broken. Opening kittwm shows ugly chrome/empty boxes, opening terminals causes flicker/z-index fights, and multiple terminals draw over one another. Before fancy kitty renderer polish, fix the core tiling engine contract: panes must have disjoint outer bounds, disjoint app bounds, stable top-bar/footer reservations, no overlapping split allocations, and predictable focus/title geometry. Acceptance: add/strengthen deterministic layout tests for 0/1/2/3+ panes across column/row splits and resize sizes; fix any overlap/off-by-one issues in the tiling/layout helpers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm kitty animation control: build escape directly
description: Replace formatted temporary construction in `kittui-kitty` animation control escape assembly with direct preallocated String assembly. Scope only `animation_control_ex(...)` in `crates/kittui-kitty/src/lib.rs`; preserve exact animation control grammar tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md links-only mode for link inspection
description: Add a kittui-md --links mode that prints only parsed Markdown links with labels and URLs. This complements --references and --images for focused audits. Include output tests for populated and empty documents.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: daemon/client architecture in docs/wm.md
description: Long-form description of the daemon/client split: protocol, socket lifecycle, multi-client semantics, security (file permissions on the socket), comparison to tmux's server. Operator quickstart: kittui-wm serve & ; KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY=:0 kittui-wm attach. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket pane rename command
description:
Large gap: native kittwm panes have visible title rows and socket status records, but there is no scriptable way to name/rename panes. Titles fall back to the spawned command, which is poor WM chrome for long-running shells and external controllers.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `RENAME_PANE <window> <title>`.
- Track an optional display title per native pane and use it in title chrome/status snapshots.
- Keep app command title as fallback when no display title is set.
- Add queue parsing and pane title helper tests.
- Document the attach command in docs/wm.md and include it in HELP/HELP_JSON.

Acceptance:
- `RENAME_PANE native-2 editor` over the native socket updates title/status for that pane.
- HELP/HELP_JSON list RENAME_PANE.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm layout state: build action strings directly
description: Replace `format!("toggle.split -> {}", self.label())`, `format!("balance.windows -> {}", self.label())`, and `format!("layout ignored action {other}")` in `LayoutState::apply()` with direct preallocated String helpers. Scope only layout state action string construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing layout state behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm keymap: build chord strings directly
description: Small kittwm/keymap perf cleanup: keymap chord rendering currently maps KeySpec to a Vec<String> and joins, including in live native-session debug logging and keymap display helpers. Build chord strings directly with bounded preallocation to reduce transient allocations in input/debug paths.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: consume partial CSI fragments instead of leaking text
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: browser input parsing only treats Escape+[ as CSI when at least one byte follows it. If a terminal CSI sequence is split across stdin reads (for example ESC [), the ESC is ignored and the [ can be forwarded as printable text to the page. Scope: treat incomplete ESC/[ CSI prefixes and incomplete parameter fragments as control input to consume rather than leaking printable characters; add focused parser coverage without overlapping active Escape/Shift-Tab work.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm doctor: report HiDPI and gap tuning
description: UX follow-up: `kittwm config` reports HiDPI/native-cell/gap tuning, but `kittwm doctor` is the first diagnostic users run when rendering looks bad. Add the same effective display tuning summary to doctor text, JSON, and scene labels so Retina/4K and gap misconfiguration is visible from doctor without switching commands.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm overlays: reuse bounded truncate-cells buffer
description: session truncate_cells is used by raw footer/chrome/overlay text paths. It bounds scans but still grows from an empty String and discards the bounded buffer for fitting inputs via s.to_string(). Preallocate the requested width and return the built buffer to reduce overlay redraw allocations while preserving output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon native-read test socket path: build directly
description: The daemon native-read test still formats its per-process socket filename with format!. Reuse the direct test_socket_filename helper for that temp path while preserving READ_TEXT coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document kittui-md footnotes mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --footnotes mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Document or clean clippy warning baseline for kittui-cli
description:
Context: while validating bd-c6d587, `cargo clippy -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm --no-deps -- -D warnings` failed before/around the touched binary on a broad pre-existing clippy warning baseline across shared crates and kittui-cli files (`self_named_constructors`, `derivable_impls`, `needless_as_bytes`, `too_many_arguments`, etc.). Plain `cargo clippy -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` succeeds but emits many warnings. This makes warnings-as-errors smoke validation noisy and requires agents to distinguish baseline warnings from bead regressions.

Suggested fix: either clean the current baseline in a dedicated pass, add a documented clippy profile/allowlist for intentional warning classes, or document the expected validation command for small CLI UX beads so agents do not repeatedly rediscover the same failure mode.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-c6d587 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Doc: protocol-conformance.md row for f=32 raw RGB
description: Add f=32 to the spec coverage table in docs/protocol-conformance.md. Mark as 'supported' once the kittui-kitty test and the demo measurement both land. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon WAIT_MS target needle arg: build directly
description: The native daemon WAIT_*_MS parser still uses format! to rebuild the target/needle argument passed to the shared wait helper. Build that argument directly while preserving WAIT_TEXT_MS/WAIT_OUTPUT_MS behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Doc: WM-thread model + pump lifecycle in docs/wm.md
description: Add a section to docs/wm.md describing the UI thread vs Pump worker thread split, when frames go stale, how backpressure works (latest-frame-wins). Include a small ASCII diagram. Add a perf table updated with measured frame times from the demo against the FakeServer + Quartz pump. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native socket ignored-mouse log: build directly
description: The native socket SendMouse ignored diagnostic still uses format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact window/event/modes debug text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: wait for text across screen and scrollback
description:
Gap after adding native scrollback: `WAIT_TEXT` only searches the current screen. Fast commands can print a sentinel and scroll it off-screen before controllers poll, so automation still misses output despite scrollback being available.

Scope:
- Add socket commands `WAIT_OUTPUT <window|focused> <needle>` and `WAIT_OUTPUT_MS <window|focused> <ms> <needle>` that search current screen plus native scrollback.
- Add CLI wrappers `--wait-output WINDOW TEXT` and `--wait-output-ms MS WINDOW TEXT`.
- Align client read timeout handling with the explicit millisecond form.
- Update help/docs/tests.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `WAIT_OUTPUT` matches scrollback-only text while `WAIT_TEXT` remains screen-oriented.
- Targeted daemon/kittwm CLI tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daily help scene: build heading label directly
description: Small follow-up to daily help scene label cleanup: daily_help_scene still builds the heading label with format! from kind and heading labels. Add a direct preallocated heading-label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm overlays: bound text dialog rows on short terminals
description: Raw/native text overlays for quit confirmation, picker, and launcher always write rows up to 7/15/17. On short terminals cursoring below the viewport can scroll and leave stale/flickery overlay content. Bound overlay writes to LINES/terminal height and skip offscreen rows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm automation request: build directly
description: automation_request uppercases the daemon verb into a temporary and formats either a token-only or payload request. Build automation requests directly into a preallocated String while preserving window token validation, payload case, and empty-payload behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support DEC Special Graphics line drawing
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap: native kittwm does not honor DEC Special Graphics character set selection (`ESC ( 0` / `ESC ( B`). Many curses apps still use ACS line-drawing bytes (`lqkx`, etc.) to draw borders. Native panes currently render those bytes literally, so boxes appear as `lqqk` / `x  x` in both the kitty-rendered frame and `READ_TEXT` snapshots.

Scope:
- Track G0 DEC Special Graphics selection in native `TerminalState`, default ASCII.
- Handle `ESC ( 0` to enable DEC Special Graphics and `ESC ( B` to return to ASCII.
- Map common DEC line-drawing glyphs (`l,m,k,j,q,x,t,u,v,w,n,plus/minus/dot variants`) to Unicode box-drawing/symbol characters during printable output.
- Reset charset selection on terminal reset; preserve it across resize like other modes.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests.

Acceptance:
- Test proves DEC ACS border bytes produce Unicode box-drawing glyphs.
- Test proves `ESC ( B` returns printable bytes to ASCII.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm Cargo defaults should enable Xvfb only on Linux targets
description: Follow-up to bd-529f1e and Harry's request: the first fix made default kittui-cli features include both sck and xvfb, while doctor hides non-effective xvfb on macOS. Refine the Cargo/build setup so Xvfb support is enabled automatically for Linux targets via target-specific dependency/features, without globally enabling the kittui-cli xvfb feature on macOS default builds. Keep macOS SCK/Quartz default behavior. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm daemon spawn next window env: build directly
description: The standalone daemon SPAWN path still uses format! to predict the next KITTWM_WINDOW label for child env. Reuse the direct tracked_pane_window builder while preserving SPAWN/PANES tracking behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch: add standalone SDK app launcher skeleton
description: Add kittwm-launch as a standalone client that detects/accepts backend selection, spawns terminal/browser/GUI surfaces through SDK, and can replace current or create new windows.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: emit explicit pane resized events
description:
Runtime event gap: native event stream has pane_changed but no explicit resize event. Emit a stable `pane_resized` event when a pane's outer/app geometry changes between `PANES_JSON` publications.

Scope:
- Update daemon pane event publication only; no SDK parser changes in this slice.
- Event kind: `pane_resized`.
- Detail should include old/new geometry labels or objects for outer and app bounds where available.
- Preserve existing `pane_changed` behavior for backward compatibility.
- Add targeted daemon unit test for event JSON shape.

Acceptance:
- `EVENTS [ms]` subscribers can observe explicit pane resize geometry changes.
- Existing pane_changed/status events remain.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm cheat: show zsh and fish completion setup
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm cheat` points to completions setup but only includes the Bash command, while quickstart/examples/help completions now include Zsh and Fish setup too. Add compact Zsh/Fish setup hints to the cheat sheet plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: accept button-specific mouse release commands
description: Follow-up to bd-ff9376: kittwm now preserves button-specific SGR release events internally (`release-left`, `release-middle`, `release-right`), but the daemon `SEND_MOUSE` command validator still only accepts the generic `release` event. External controllers/tests cannot request the more precise release events. Update `native_mouse_event_known` and the help/error text to accept button-specific release commands; add focused daemon command coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-ffi: expose render_many_png batch API
description:
Large gap: the Rust facade now has `render_many_png`, but FFI consumers still only have single-scene `kittui_render_json`. External platforms wanting batch preview artifacts need either many FFI round-trips or custom loops.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_render_many_json(runtime, scenes_json, out_json)` returning JSON manifest with count, per-image index/bytes/footprint/png_base64.
- Bump ABI minor and update `kittui.h`/ABI snapshot.
- Use `Runtime::render_many_png` internally.
- Add targeted FFI tests for PNG base64 manifest and non-array rejection.

Acceptance:
- `kittui_render_many_json` returns parseable JSON for scene arrays.
- Invalid non-array input returns BadScene with last_error.
- Targeted FFI and ABI snapshot tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support DEC origin mode in native PTY
description:
Terminal-WM fidelity gap after scroll-region support: native kittwm does not honor DEC origin mode (`CSI ? 6 h/l`). TUIs that set scroll margins often enable origin mode so cursor addressing is relative to the scroll region; without it, writes land in the wrong rows and corrupt fixed headers/status lines.

Scope:
- Track DEC origin mode in native `TerminalState`.
- Handle `CSI ? 6 h` / `CSI ? 6 l`, resetting cursor to home on toggle.
- When origin mode is enabled, make `CSI H/f` row coordinates relative to `scroll_top` and clamped within `scroll_top..scroll_bottom`.
- Add focused parser snapshot tests with scroll region + origin mode.

Acceptance:
- Tests show `CSI 1;1H` inside origin mode writes at scroll-region top, not absolute row 0.
- Tests show disabling origin mode restores absolute addressing.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native socket move log: build directly
description: The native socket Move path formats its debug diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact window/direction/target text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fall back when remote kittwm listing fails
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: local `kittwm remote HOST windows/displays` should use remote kittwm when healthy, but should still be useful when the remote host has an installed-broken kittwm. The listing fallback currently delegates as soon as `command -v kittwm` succeeds. Try remote kittwm first, then continue to platform fallback listing when it exits non-zero.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Document kittui-md definitions mode
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --definitions mode in examples and mode descriptions. Validate with docs checks.

description: After bd-efe4f9 added LayoutMode::Grid, the SDK helper smoke test still exercises layout(columns) and split(rows) but not the new grid protocol label over the socket helper path. Add focused SDK coverage so grid remains wired through Kittwm::layout and documented protocol labels.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-bar: add examples to help output
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm-bar --help` now succeeds and lists options, but it does not show concrete examples for the common paths: plain text/offline bar, JSON model, kitty rendering, and reserving/clearing top chrome. Add a concise Examples section plus focused assertion coverage so operators can copy a working command quickly.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.

title: kittwm: support ctrl-arrow in SEND_KEY and browser surfaces
description: Keyboard/input UX gap: browser surfaces already have `send_ctrl_arrow_key`, but first-party `SEND_KEY` cannot express Ctrl+Arrow. That blocks common word/paragraph navigation in browser text fields and terminal TUIs from the socket/CLI automation path. Add `ctrl-left/right/up/down` (and `ctrl-arrow-*` aliases) to the daemon key parser using common xterm modifier sequences, route those labels to direct browser surfaces, and update focused parser/mapping coverage and help strings.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-doctor: respect narrow scene width
description: Audit finding: kittwm doctor kitty/scene output forces a minimum 32-column scene even when COLUMNS/KITTWM_DOCTOR_COLS is smaller. On narrow terminals this can overflow/wrap diagnostic graphics, unlike recent narrow-width fixes for kittwm bar/help/toast/status surfaces. Respect narrow positive widths while keeping the 120-column upper bound and default width.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

description:

Scope:

- Mention browser DOM/ARIA extraction/publish/action routing and CLI semantic inspection current state.
- Mention accessibility safe core/action routing exists, direct platform bindings remain follow-up.

Acceptance:
- Quickstart no longer mislabels landed browser/accessibility semantic functionality as future work.
- `git diff --check` passes.

title: Show non-default kittwm pane weight in live chrome

Add a compact weight chip for resized panes so tiled layout state is visible without inspecting JSON.

Continue focused kittwm UX/help polish without overlapping active native-surfaces, chrome-scene, or info-header work. description: Add --mode-category <category> and --mode-category-json <category> no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can list modes in one category such as render, inspect, json, discovery, or stats. Document it and add parser/output tests.

title: Add tests for kittwm replace command mapping
description: context: during bd-cddcf2/bd-d75324, the replace path was smoke-tested manually with KITTWM_WINDOW set, but the command parser/exec path has no automated test because it uses exec on Unix. Suggested fix: factor argv resolution (browser to kittwm-browser) and context validation into a pure helper with tests. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-cddcf2/bd-d75324/bd-4b9c96 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md schemas-json discovery mode
description: Add --schemas-json as a no-input discovery mode for kittui-md machine-readable outputs, emitting schema_version plus per-output top-level JSON keys and short descriptions. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md headings alias for outline
description: Add --headings as a friendly alias for kittui-md --outline/--toc so users can inspect document headings with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: visual regression goldens for graphical chrome states
description: QA gap for graphical shell: add visual/golden tests or artifact snapshots for empty workspace, one pane, split panes, focus change, overlay open, translucent chrome, and fallback terminal renderer. Acceptance: reproducible artifacts/goldens catch regressions in kittui-rendered chrome and dogfood surfaces.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native surfaces JSON: append newline directly
description: Replace `format!("{}\n", serde_json::json!(...))` in `native_surfaces_json_text()` with direct string construction: build the JSON value, convert once with `to_string()`, push a newline, and return. Scope only `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; preserve native surfaces JSON output and add/retain narrow coverage for the trailing newline.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: add base64 byte injection command
description:
Large terminal automation gap: native kittwm socket input is line-oriented. `SEND_TEXT`/`SEND_LINE` cannot safely carry embedded newlines, NULs, escape sequences, or arbitrary paste payloads; scripts must work around protocol framing. A terminal WM control plane needs an encoded byte-injection path.

Scope:
- Add socket command `SEND_BYTES_B64 <window|focused> <base64>` that decodes bytes and sends them to the pane.
- Add CLI wrapper `kittwm --send-bytes-b64 <window|focused> <base64>`.
- Reuse existing `SendBytes` native loop handling.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON coverage, tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Base64 payloads decode to exact bytes and queue/send via `NativePaneCommand::SendBytes`.
- Invalid base64 and bad targets return clear errors.
- Targeted daemon/kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: respect narrow status scene width
description: Kittwm burn-down finding:  /  uses the generic info scene width helper with a 40-column minimum. On narrower terminals the status graphics scene can exceed the row and its row rects assume a fixed 10-cell margin. Scope: give status scene width narrow-aware handling, bound row rects inside the scene, and add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: forward F10 input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards F5-F9 but still consumes F10 (`ESC [ 21 ~`). Some browser/web-app workflows bind higher function keys. Forward F10 through the existing DevTools function-key path.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native footer status tests: build long status directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("log: {}", "x".repeat(...))` in `native_footer_status_scene_label_is_bounded` with a direct preallocated status helper. Scope only kittwm native footer status label test stress-data construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve label bounding coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Prove: detach + reattach preserves session state
description:
Integration test: spawn daemon with a FakeServer backend, attach client A, send pointer events, detach. Spawn client B (new process), attach to same socket, verify it sees the same session state (windows, focus, layout) immediately. Asserts the daemon outlived A and B starts at the same state, not a fresh one. Parent epic: bd-031e54

Duplicate of bd-624e22
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm top bar: avoid full scans when truncating huge display labels
description: top_bar_display_label still calls chars().count() before truncating, so pathological workspace labels can force a full scan every top-bar text/scene render. Detect truncation with take(MAX+1) and build only the bounded display label.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm PTY renderer: iterate terminal cells directly
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: render_terminal_rgba walks every row/col and calls TerminalState::get_cell_at for each cell. The loop bounds are already within the grid, so this adds repeated bounds checks and index recomputation on every rendered cell. Scope: iterate state.cells in row chunks with enumerate-derived coordinates, preserving cursor/styled rendering and adding focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-workspace: publish active workspace to bar status
description: Follow-up to workspace UX: C-a number workspace switching updates in-process WorkspaceState/footer, but daemon STATUS/CHROME/PANES still report KITTWM_WORKSPACE/env default, so standalone kittwm-bar may keep showing workspace 1 after C-a 2. Publish current WorkspaceState into the native spawn queue/status model so external bar/chrome reflects on-demand workspace switches.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: document kittwm-bar chrome reservation workflow
description:
Document kittwm-bar as a first-party kitty-native chrome app after bd-a83efd: --kitty/--graphics, --reserve/--release, and extended CHROME_JSON drawable reservation fields.

Acceptance:
- docs/wm.md documents top/bottom/side/gap reservation metadata and kittwm-bar usage.
- kittwm help apps mentions kittwm-bar chrome/reservation workflow.
- Lightweight validation passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md preserves markdown link labels in surrounding paragraph text
description: The markdown renderer emits links as highlighted chips, but currently removes the link label from the surrounding paragraph text, producing gaps such as 'A paragraph with a  and inline code'. Preserve the label in paragraph/table text while still emitting LinkChip metadata and chip components.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive help toggle
description: Add a native interactive pager help toggle for kittui-md so users can press ? or h to view keybindings while browsing. Reuse the keybinding catalog, document the control, and add targeted parser/pager tests where practical.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: apply kittwm chrome reservations to native layout
description:
Wire the chrome reservation control-plane contract into the native kittwm PTY layout so reserved top/bottom/side bands and tile gaps affect actual drawable pane geometry. This complements dev-2's z-order/non-overlap work and avoids touching runtime placement internals.

Acceptance:
- Native layout helpers clamp pane/app footprints to reported terminal rows/cols minus reservation bands.
- RESERVE_CHROME_JSON changes can be read by the live loop via NativeSpawnQueue.
- Tests cover top/bottom/side/gap reservation geometry.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: bound help-topic scene row rects on narrow terminals
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: help topic graphics now inherit narrow-aware info scene widths, but their row rects still use a fixed 10-cell-style inset. On very narrow terminals those row rectangles can extend past the scene bounds and contribute to graphical wrap/flicker. Scope: use the bounded info row rect helper for help-topic rows and add focused narrow-width coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-renderer: clear graphical top-bar text row before overlay
description: Follow-up to narrow top-bar clock fixes: the live graphical top-bar ANSI label/clock overlay writes new labels but does not clear the row first. If the clock was previously visible and later skipped on a narrower terminal, stale clock/label text can remain over the graphical bar. Clear the top-bar text row before writing the overlay labels.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm browser URL percent-encoding: build directly
description: Headless browser target creation still percent-encodes non-unreserved URL bytes via format!. Build percent escapes directly while preserving Chrome DevTools target URL encoding behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm session restore tests should cover grid layout
description: After grid layout support landed, RESTORE_SESSION_JSON accepts layout=grid but the restore parser test still only exercises rows. Add focused daemon/session restore coverage for grid so saved four-pane manifests keep the new 2x2 layout mode across restore.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm daemon launcher: avoid lowercasing every filtered candidate
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: daemon-side filter_candidates lowercases every app candidate with item.to_ascii_lowercase().contains(&q). Earlier launcher paths were optimized, but this daemon helper can still allocate per candidate while filtering large app lists. Scope: use allocation-free ASCII casefold contains against a pre-lowercased query and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: render readable native terminal glyphs and reduce title flicker
description:
Native kittwm PTY panes currently render printable glyphs with synthetic pseudo-box strokes, making zsh/shell text unreadable despite interactivity. Replace the pseudo glyph renderer with a deterministic bitmap/vector glyph renderer for common terminal characters and reduce title-row flicker by avoiding unnecessary ANSI title redraws.

Scope:
- Draw readable ASCII glyphs in native PTY RGBA frames instead of pseudo boxes.
- Keep SGR foreground/background behavior and cursor rendering intact.
- Add support for common box-drawing glyphs from DEC Special Graphics.
- Avoid rewriting unchanged native pane title rows every frame; update on clear/focus/title/layout changes.
- Add focused renderer tests.

Acceptance:
- Renderer test demonstrates different readable glyph shapes for common text and not full-cell boxes.
- Title-row redraw cache is covered by a small unit test or refactor-friendly behavior.
- `cargo test -p kittui-wm ...`, `cargo test -p kittui-cli ...`, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm unknown command: include examples and cheat recovery
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. Unknown `kittwm <command>` errors currently point at quickstart, --help, and help topics, but not the daily copy-paste recovery pages (`kittwm examples`, `kittwm cheat`) that are useful after a mistyped command. Add those recovery links plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-sdk: typed shortcuts catalog helper
description:
SDK gap: kittwm now exposes a machine-readable shortcut catalog through `SHORTCUTS_JSON`, but SDK clients lack typed access.

Scope:
- Add typed `ShortcutCatalog` / `ShortcutEntry` structs matching `SHORTCUTS_JSON`.
- Add `Kittwm::shortcuts()` / `shortcuts_json()` helper reading `SHORTCUTS_JSON`.
- Keep capability policy consistent with existing read-only catalog helpers.
- Add focused Unix socket unit test verifying command string and parsing of launch/help/exit entries.
- No daemon/session/CLI changes.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can call `client.shortcuts()` or `client.shortcuts_json()`.
- Targeted SDK tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: stable behavior under tmux, ssh, and non-kitty terminals
description: QA gap: daily-driver behavior differs under tmux, remote/ssh, non-kitty terminals, and explicit renderer overrides. Build a matrix/checklist or automated doctor smoke for KITTWM_NATIVE_RENDERER auto/terminal/kitty, tmux detection, remote detection, and fallback paths. Acceptance: documented/tested expected renderer/transport choices and no accidental kitty graphics passthrough in tmux-safe mode.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Guard summary authoring against unquoted heredoc command substitution
description:
Context: while finishing bd-7f64a0, I accidentally authored `.cacophony/agent/.../summary/pending/summary.md` with an unquoted shell heredoc. Markdown backticks around commands such as `kittwm-top --help` were executed by the shell, corrupting the summary with command output before I noticed and rewrote it. The session-recording profile already warns against this, but the failure mode is easy to trigger under time pressure.

Suggested fix shape: provide a first-party summary writer helper or lint that detects shell-expanded summary corruption before commit/reintegration, or make the recommended command easier to copy safely. A simple `caco summaries validate-local` check that flags missing bead IDs, empty code commit fields, or embedded test output in prose would have caught this immediately.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-7f64a0 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: bindings: add primitive scene builder helpers
description:
Large external-platform renderer gap: TypeScript has a few primitive scene helpers, but Python has none and TS lacks an easy valid solid scene builder. Platform users still have to hand-author verbose Scene JSON for common shell-renderer previews.

Scope:
- Add Python module-level `scene` helpers mirroring the TS helper surface.
- Add first-party valid solid-box helpers in both Python and TypeScript.
- Helpers should build JSON-compatible primitive-only Scene objects (no affordances in kittui core).
- Add binding tests and README examples.

Acceptance:
- Python and TypeScript users can create a valid solid Scene without hand-authoring the schema.
- Python and TS tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native resize weight logs: build directly
description: The native keyboard resize grow/shrink diagnostics still format window and weight with format!. Build those log lines directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: tolerate per-pane capture failures during render
description: Render-loop stability issue: live kittwm still calls `NativeSurface::capture_surface(&mut pane.app)?` for each pane. If one hosted surface (notably a browser DevTools capture after timeout/broken Chrome) returns an error, the entire WM exits. Make per-pane capture failures non-fatal: log a bounded line, clear that pane's dirty-frame metrics for the frame, continue rendering chrome/other panes, and retry next frame. Add focused helper coverage for the log line.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: consume split SS3 prefixes
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser now parses complete SS3 navigation sequences, but a split/incomplete SS3 prefix (`ESC O`) can still become Escape plus literal `O` text in a page when bytes arrive across reads. Consume incomplete SS3 prefixes the same way split CSI prefixes are consumed, avoiding stray text leakage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: treat motion mouse modes as click-capable
description: Follow-up bug-hunt finding after legacy mouse payload support: `native_mouse_event_payload` still requires `modes.basic` for press/release/scroll events. Terminal apps can enable button-event (`?1002`) or all-motion (`?1003`) tracking without separately enabling `?1000`; those modes should still receive button press/release (and scroll) events. Treat any mouse tracking mode (`basic || button_motion || all_motion`) as click-capable while preserving motion gating; add deterministic coverage for button-motion-only and all-motion-only modes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Fix macOS kittui-cli link failure for missing libiconv
description: While validating bd-c3214b on ms-mac, cargo build -p kittui-cli and cargo test -p kittui-cli --bins compiled successfully but failed at link time with ld: library not found for -liconv. cargo check -p kittui-cli passes, so this appears to be host/toolchain linker configuration or an existing native dependency issue rather than a source compile error from the config change. Command evidence: cargo build -p kittui-cli -> cc ... -liconv ... ld: library not found for -liconv. Filed from bd-c3214b validation.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm completions: make extra shell error actionable
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm completions bash extra` currently reports that completions accepts one shell, but it does not include the supported shell names, a copyable successful example, or `kittwm help completions`. Make the extra-argument error actionable like the missing/unsupported shell errors, and add focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native socket layout log: build directly
description: The native socket Layout path formats its debug diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm startup chrome minimal i3bar style
description:
Fix kittwm first-open chrome so the default empty workspace does not dump a large unicode-placeholder/graphics field. Default startup should show only a minimal i3bar-style header bar: workspace slots and right-aligned clock/status, e.g. `| 1 | 2 | 3 | ... 22:47`.

Acceptance:
- Default kittwm empty workspace no longer renders the large empty workspace graphics/placeholder scene by default.
- Top/header bar model renders a minimal i3bar-style text row with three workspace slots and right-aligned time/status.
- Preserve richer empty workspace/help surfaces behind explicit commands/tests where possible.
- Add focused tests for the minimal bar string/model and startup empty workspace output behavior.
- Generate/post a headless Ghostty progress image of the minimal bar/proof.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive clear-status key
description: Add a native clear-status key to kittui-md --interactive so users can dismiss reload success/failure messages while keeping the position footer visible. Expose it through keybinding discovery, document it, and add targeted pager/footer tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launcher: keep launcher visible over raw app graphics
description: User reports launcher is not working/rendering. In raw compositor mode the launcher is ANSI text drawn after kitty graphics, but app frame graphics can cover terminal text. Make the launcher modal path explicitly hide/unplace raw app graphics while the launcher is active and force a full app re-upload/redraw after it closes, so launcher text is visible and input can be used reliably.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm daemon apps JSON arrays should build directly
description: The kittwm binary apps JSON helper was already changed to build arrays directly, but the daemon-side `json_string_array` in `crates/kittui-cli/src/daemon.rs` still maps items to formatted Strings, collects a Vec, and joins with commas. Make the daemon helper build directly into one String while preserving JSON escaping/output. Scope: daemon apps JSON string arrays only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm PTY metadata surface ids: build directly
description: PTY and Ghostty terminal NativeSurface metadata still formats '<prefix>:<pid|unknown>' ids. Build those terminal metadata surface ids directly while preserving PTY metadata coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward F12 input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards F5-F11 but still consumes F12 (`ESC [ 24 ~`). Browsers and web apps commonly use F12 for developer-tools or app shortcuts. Forward F12 through the existing DevTools function-key path.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm standalone daemon SPAWN errors: build directly
description: The standalone daemon SPAWN error paths still use format! for registry-poisoned and spawn-failed replies. Build those error replies directly while preserving exact text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: keep native footer on-screen
description: Audit after user reports random bottom chrome/scrolling: native_shell_view places the footer at rows + 1, and writers then use row + 1, which targets below the terminal and can force scrolling/flicker. Clamp the native shell footer row to the last visible row while preserving empty-workspace no-footer behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add win remote window aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote window listing advertises `window`/`windows`, but users may naturally type the shorter `win` or `wins` when asking for remote desktop windows over pooled SSH. Route `kittwm remote HOST win(s)` and `list win(s)` to the existing remote window fallback path and advertise them in help/completions/catalog.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-ghostty: add kittwm proof PTY screenshot mode
description:
Harry reported that kittwm proof PNGs have been text-note images instead of screenshots. Add a first-party headless Ghostty proof mode that runs kittwm/kittwm commands inside a PTY with kittwm-friendly terminal env and renders the captured VT stream to PNG, so agents can attach real rendered feature evidence instead of note cards.

Bound the title scene to the pane width and clamp the status chip inside the footprint to avoid overdraw/wrap on narrow splits.

title: kittui-md interactive pager: keyboard scrolling for rich view

title: kittwm-terminal args: avoid rest Vec before quoting
description: TerminalArgs parsing collects trailing PROGRAM [ARGS...] into a Vec only to pass it to shell_words. Build the quoted command from the remaining iterator directly while preserving -- separator and bare-program parsing behavior.

title: kittwm-browser: forward Alt-Backspace input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards Backspace and Ctrl+Backspace CSI-u, plus many Alt-modified navigation/editing keys, but consumes Alt+Backspace CSI-u (`ESC [ 8 ; 3 u` / `ESC [ 127 ; 3 u`). Forward it with the Alt modifier so modified delete/backspace input is not swallowed.

title: stabilize kittwm graphical flicker and jank
description:
Investigate and reduce severe kittwm flicker/jank in graphical native mode. User reports kittwm is fundamentally unusable due to flicker and jank.

Acceptance:
- Identify high-impact cause(s) in native render path.
- Land a targeted stabilization fix with tests where possible.
- Preserve native-first graphical kittwm while making it usable; terminal fallback remains available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native launch log: build directly
description: The native terminal launch diagnostic still formats focused window and pane count with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
