title: inline affordances: add shared --animated/--fps/--frames flags
description:
Inventory of inline kittui elements that should support animation:
- `kittui inline chip`: one-line prompt/status chip; graphical kitty/prompt modes render via `inline_chip_scene`.
- `kittui inline badge`: compact label/count variant sharing `InlineChipArgs` and `inline_chip_scene`.
- `kittui inline segment`: prompt/status segment variant sharing `InlineChipArgs` and `inline_chip_scene`.
- `kittui inline divider`: rule/divider rendered by `inline_divider_scene`.
- `kittui inline row`: composed row of chip/badge/segment/divider items via `inline_row_scene` and inherited style/theme.
- Non-inline root commands such as `kittui chip`, panel/title/divider demos may have separate animation knobs, but the requested scope is inline elements.

Current animation substrate:
- `Scene.animation` already causes all frames to be rasterized/uploaded once through kitty native animation.
- CPU renderer currently animates `Node::Glow` intensity by phase, so style-specific inline animation can be expressed by adding phase-reactive glow layers plus an animation descriptor.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui: default RuntimeBuilder to detected terminal info
description:
Large gap: Rust/library callers that construct `Runtime::builder().build()` get `TerminalInfo::default_kitty()` instead of `TerminalInfo::detect()`, while the CLI separately detects terminal capabilities. This makes library/FFI hosts less reliable than the CLI.

Scope:
- Change `RuntimeBuilder::build()` to use `TerminalInfo::detect()` when no explicit terminal descriptor is supplied.
- Preserve `RuntimeBuilder::terminal(...)` as an exact host override.
- Add targeted tests proving detected transport/capabilities are used by default and explicit overrides win.

Acceptance:
- In a controlled `TMUX` environment, default runtime transport is tmux passthrough.
- With an explicit terminal override, runtime uses the override even if env detection would disagree.
- `cargo test -p kittui runtime_builder` and `cargo build -p kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: typed scrollback and wait helpers
description:
SDK automation gap: CLI/socket expose READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON, WAIT_TEXT_MS, and WAIT_OUTPUT_MS but `kittwm-sdk` only wraps `READ_TEXT_JSON`. Add typed SDK helpers for scrollback and waits so automation clients do not need raw protocol strings.

Scope:
- Add `ScrollbackSnapshot` typed response for `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON`.
- Add `SurfaceHandle::read_scrollback()`.
- Add `SurfaceHandle::wait_text_ms(ms, needle)` and `wait_output_ms(ms, needle)` plus convenience defaults if desired.
- Gate with existing read capability.
- Add targeted SDK unit/socket tests for command formatting and JSON parsing.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK automation clients can read scrollback and wait for screen/output text without raw protocol strings.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native socket key injection
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: native kittwm can inject text over the socket, but controllers still cannot send non-text terminal keys such as Ctrl-C, Escape, Tab, arrows, Home/End, or PageUp/PageDown. A terminal WM control plane needs key injection without relying on host TTY synthesis.

Scope:
- Add native socket command `SEND_KEY <window|focused> <key>`.
- Support common keys: enter/return, tab, escape/esc, backspace, delete, arrows, home/end, pageup/pagedown, and ctrl-a through ctrl-z spellings (`ctrl-c`, `C-c`).
- Queue bytes and handle in the native PTY session via existing focused/window lookup semantics.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON/error coverage and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- `SEND_KEY` validates window/key and returns queued replies or clear errors.
- Native loop sends the mapped bytes to the target pane.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm terminal DSR cursor response: build directly
description: TerminalState::device_status_report still uses format! for CSI cursor position responses. Build the ESC[row;colR response directly while preserving device-status response coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md definitions-json inspection mode
description: Add --definitions-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown definition-list entries, emitting schema_version plus indexed term/definition records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm picker select status: build directly
description: The picker overlay selected status string still formats the selected label with format!. Build that status string directly while preserving exact output.

title: kittwm launcher scene: build row labels directly
description: Small kittwm launcher scene performance cleanup: launcher_scene still builds each row layer label with format! even though row index, kind, bounded name, and selected state are already known. Build row labels directly into preallocated Strings while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm launcher scene: build heading label directly
description: Small kittwm launcher scene performance cleanup: launcher_scene still builds the heading layer label with `format!("kittwm-launcher-heading:selected={selected}")`. Build that label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: reset idle pacing after input or resize
description: Follow-up to adaptive browser capture pacing: after kittwm-browser enters idle cadence, user input or terminal resize should immediately return capture polling to the active interval even if the next captured frame is byte-identical. Otherwise static pages can feel sluggish because the loop remains on the idle sleep after input. Scope: treat input/resize as activity for the idle counter and add focused pacing tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: clear picker overlay rows on close
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: the raw compositor session tracks `launcher_overlay_was_active` and clears overlay rows when the launcher closes, but the picker overlay uses the same terminal-row overlay area and does not participate in that close cleanup. Closing the picker can leave stale box-drawing/text rows burned into the terminal until later repaint. Track picker overlay activity too and clear the shared overlay area when either overlay transitions from active to inactive; reset overlay/chrome/footer memo keys so the underlying content repaints. Add deterministic transition helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: bindings-ts: expose render_many manifest
description:
Large gap: FFI and Python now expose one-call render-many manifests, but TypeScript still only has single-scene `render`. JS platform preview/artifact tools need parity with Python and the FFI batch renderer.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_render_many_json` in the TypeScript koffi binding.
- Add `Kittui.renderMany(scenes)` returning a typed parsed manifest with base64 PNG entries.
- Add TypeScript declarations and fake-lib tests for success/failure.
- Update README example.

Acceptance:
- `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` passes.
- Type declarations expose manifest/image fields.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser semantic publisher: avoid cloning socket path per tick
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: BrowserSemanticPublisher::maybe_publish clones its socket PathBuf on every due check before publishing. The socket path is immutable metadata, so this is avoidable allocation in the browser loop/semantic publisher path. Scope: borrow the socket path through as_ref(), keep publish semantics unchanged, and add focused coverage if practical.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: ci: restrict workflows to tagged releases
description: Correct kittui CI/release triggers so self-hosted Nix validation and updater asset builds run for tagged semver-style releases (patch/minor/major) and manual dispatch only, not every main commit/PR. Then tag the current patch version to trigger release.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add link and image index fields to kittui-md metadata JSON
description: Add stable index fields to kittui-md metadata-json link and image records so downstream tools can reference parsed references without relying on array position. Add targeted tests and docs.

title: Add kittui-md interactive outline toggle

title: kittwm: align prefix keybindings for float fullscreen and split toggles
description: Operator request: make `Ctrl-A t` toggle floating mode, keep `Ctrl-A Enter` for terminal spawn, make `Ctrl-A f` toggle fullscreen, and keep/use `Ctrl-A e` to toggle the current split between vertical and horizontal. Audit the actual default keymap plus visible help/docs so users can discover these actions correctly. Preserve the in-progress mouse-routing bead separately.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native failure logs: build resize line directly
description: native_resize_failure_log_line uses whole-line format! for native pane resize diagnostics. Build the line directly with a preallocated buffer while preserving app/layout/error fields.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: skip unchanged pure-terminal redraw frames
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: the pure terminal renderer path in `run_native_terminal_loop` calls `render_native_shell_view_terminal` and writes the full rendered shell every frame, even when neither pane text nor chrome changed. In tmux/non-kitty fallback this can produce visible flicker and unnecessary throughput. Fix by memoizing the last rendered terminal string and only writing when the frame changes or a static clear is required; add a deterministic test for the skip decision.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm architecture graphics: bound row rects on narrow scenes
description: kittwm architecture graphics now inherit narrow-aware scene widths, but layer/plane/surface row rects still use fixed 10px margins and width-20 geometry. On tiny widths those rects can extend outside the scene footprint. Reuse bounded row geometry for architecture cards.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: make kittwm session inspection kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make SESSION_JSON inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI. Add session-scene-json and session-kitty/session-graphics commands/flags that render session manifest metadata as labelled kittui Scene layers, preserving existing --session-json/save/restore behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm session-scene-json / --session-scene-json emits a kittui Scene summarizing layout, focus, pane count, and pane commands from SESSION_JSON.
- kittwm session-kitty/session-graphics / --session-kitty places that scene through kittui Runtime at chrome z-index.
- Existing --session-json, --save-session, and --restore-session behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover scene labels from synthetic SESSION_JSON.

title: Add shell and ssh aliases for kittwm remote terminal

Document them in SSH help/doctor examples so terminal-only requests are easy from a local kittwm session.

title: fix inline chip graphics visibility

Bug: `kittui inline chip --text abcdef` prints placeholder/text but no graphics are drawn.

Observed output is placeholder + text, e.g. `Uabcdef`, but no pill-shaped graphics background appears.

Scope:

Add active_title_drag_cell() and active_title_drag_cells_by() helpers on Status/PanesStatus. Filed from ongoing kittwm SDK/drag automation audit.

title: examples: add synthetic semantic SDK app
description:
Follow-up from the semantic surface plan, renderer bridge, SDK semantic types, socket skeleton, and CLI wrappers. Add a first-party synthetic semantic SDK app example that builds a semantic component tree (settings/form UI) using kittwm-sdk protocol types and can print the snapshot JSON or send it to a future semantic surface endpoint.

Scope:

- Reuse kittwm-sdk semantic protocol types; do not depend on kittui-wm internals.

Acceptance:

title: kittwm events graphics: build summary label without Vec join
description: kittwm events_scene_for_cols clips up to six event kind labels, collects them into a temporary Vec<String>, then joins with commas for a scene label. Build the bounded event summary directly in one String to reduce graphical event/status allocation churn while preserving labels.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: publish architecture separation contract
description: Add a first-party kittwm architecture contract artifact documenting clean boundaries between SDK/control-plane, tiling/drawable layout, surface capture/renderer, decoration renderer, and kitty graphics compositor. Expose it via kittwm architecture-json/platform-contract-json and docs so future implementation work can align without overlap. Avoid touching active session reservation/control-plane internals.

title: kittwm top bar: avoid merged allocation when clipping narrow bar

creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: clamp terminal fallback footer row on-screen
description: Audit finding: render_native_shell_view_terminal clamps footer row with rows + 1, then writes row + 1, so malformed/external shell views can still render below the terminal and scroll/flicker. Clamp terminal fallback footer rows to the last visible row, matching native_shell_view's on-screen footer policy.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-renderer: include visual scene hash in chrome memo keys
description: Audit finding: graphical native chrome memo keys only include placement, dimensions, scene id, and layer labels. Paint/color changes from config/theme can leave labels unchanged, causing write_native_shell_affordance_chrome to skip re-rendering and keep stale chrome colors. Include a stable visual hash of the scene debug data in the key so color/style changes invalidate chrome placement/cache decisions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Fix kittwm crash on open with many rows/cols
description:
kittwm crashes when opened with terminal windows displaying too many rows or columns visible. Need to identify the root cause (likely memory allocation, rendering buffer overflow, or layout calculation issue) and fix the crash. Should handle edge cases with extreme grid dimensions gracefully.

Acceptance Criteria:
- kittwm no longer crashes when opening windows with excessive row/col counts
- Terminal renders correctly or degrades gracefully (e.g., scrolling, compression)
- Tested with pathological cases (e.g., 1000+ cols, 500+ rows)
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

description:

title: kittwm-renderer: clamp native size overrides to host terminal
description: Audit finding: KITTWM_NATIVE_COLS/ROWS overrides are trusted even when larger than the actual host terminal. Clamp native terminal size overrides to host terminal cells while preserving minimum 1x1 behavior.

title: Respect XDG data dirs in remote desktop discovery
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm Linux desktop fallback scans common application directories but ignores `XDG_DATA_HOME` and `XDG_DATA_DIRS`. Use the remote host XDG data directory configuration when discovering `.desktop` entries so `kittwm remote HOST apps/launch` finds apps installed in non-standard prefixes over pooled SSH.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: avoid full URL scans in status truncation
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: browser status rendering calls truncate(url, 40), but truncate first counts every character before taking the visible prefix. Pathological long URLs can cause per-frame full-string scans even though only a small status prefix is displayed. Scope: rewrite the browser truncate helper to use bounded prefix/lookahead construction and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kitwm --fps N + live fps in footer

title: Add layered kittui configuration resolution
description: Operator direction: every user-facing kittui option should be settable via explicit CLI/API flag, KITTUI_* environment variable, or ~/.config/kittui/config.yaml, with precedence flag/API override > env override > YAML default. This lets scripts establish an env/config scope and call kittui many times or batch operations without repeating every flag, while apps can still force overrides. Design and implement a typed Config model, YAML loader (respecting XDG_CONFIG_HOME), KITTUI_* env parsing, source/precedence diagnostics for --json output, validation/error messages, and tests covering precedence and malformed config.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

Pathological PATH entries or app names can inflate scene JSON/kitty payloads and redraw allocation churn. Clip apps scene label fields while preserving useful launcher diagnostics.

title: kittui-cli: allow compose batch placement origins
description:
Large gap: `kittui compose` supports arrays of scene JSON for shell/platform batch rendering, but rejects `--x/--y` placement overrides for batches. That makes it awkward for shell scripts to build reusable scene groups and place the whole group at a runtime origin.

Scope:
- Allow `kittui compose <array>|- --x/--y` by treating overrides as a group origin offset.
- Preserve each scene's relative offsets from the batch's minimum x/y.
- Keep single-scene override semantics unchanged.
- Add CLI tests replacing the old rejection test with batch offset assertions.

Acceptance:
- Batch compose with `--x` and/or `--y` succeeds in dry-run JSON.
- First/min batch scene is placed at the requested coordinate and other scenes preserve relative spacing.
- Existing single-scene override test still passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: deterministic pane ids and focus after rapid open/close
description: QA gap: rapid spawn/close/focus/move operations should keep deterministic pane ids and sane focus. Add tests for opening multiple panes, closing focused/non-focused panes, closing last pane, restoring empty workspace, and then spawning again. Acceptance: focus never points past pane list, ids do not collide, last-pane close returns to empty workspace, next spawn is predictable.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: send focus events when pane move changes focus
description: Bug-hunt finding while improving kittwm usability: `NativePaneCommand::Move` reorders panes and assigns `focused = to` directly. Unlike Focus/FocusNext/FocusPrev/Close paths, it does not call `native_set_focus`, so PTY apps with focus reporting enabled do not receive focus-out/focus-in when the move operation changes which pane is focused. This can leave editor/shell focus state stale. Preserve the current focus behavior (moved pane becomes focused) but send focus events through the existing focus helper after reorder; add deterministic helper coverage for locating the old focused pane after a move.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm launcher overlay failure log: build directly
description: The raw compositor launcher overlay failure diagnostic still formats errors with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.

title: kittwm: trim daemon workspace id env
description: Follow-up to bd-1fd95c: top_bar::workspace_label now trims KITTWM_WORKSPACE, but daemon.rs has a separate `native_workspace_id()` helper with the same old bug: it filters on `value.trim()` but returns the original untrimmed value. That can leak spaces into daemon chrome/status JSON even when the top bar is fixed. Align daemon workspace id handling with the trimmed top-bar behavior and add focused daemon helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm daemon semantic fallback ids: build directly

title: kitwm: single-command launcher that spawns daemon + attaches
description: Add a top-level bin 'kitwm' (or 'kittyui-wm') in kittui-cli that, when invoked with no args, does: 1. detect KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY or pick a free socket, 2. check whether a daemon is already listening; if yes attach to it, 3. if no, fork a daemon process that survives the parent and attach to it. With --serve, only run daemon; with --attach, only attach; with --kill, signal daemon shutdown. This is the user-facing entrypoint. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm chrome scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: chrome_scene still builds the chrome backdrop label with format! from workspace, owner, reservation, gap, and tilable rows fields. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: add native automation CLI wrappers
description:
Large terminal-WM automation gap: native kittwm has socket commands for SEND_TEXT/SEND_LINE/SEND_KEY/READ_TEXT/WAIT_TEXT, but scripts still have to wrap them as `kittwm --attach -c ...`. First-class CLI wrappers make kittwm usable as a terminal automation tool without spelling socket protocol verbs directly.

Scope:
- Add CLI flags:
  - `--send-text <window|focused> <text>`
  - `--send-line <window|focused> <text>`
  - `--send-key <window|focused> <key>`
  - `--read-text <window|focused>`
  - `--wait-text <window|focused> <needle>`
- Send the corresponding native socket command via default socket resolution and print the reply.
- Add help text and focused request-builder tests.
- Update docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Flags generate the expected socket requests without changing payload case.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native font tests: build temp roots directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("kittui-...-{}-{}", std::process::id(), Instant::now().elapsed().as_nanos())` temp directory names in kittui-wm native terminal font tests with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only font-discovery test temp root construction in `crates/kittui-wm/src/native.rs`; preserve terminal font discovery/scoring coverage.

The `kittwm help panes` topic lists pane lifecycle and layout commands, but it does not include copyable examples for common workflows like spawning a pane, splitting next to focus, focusing next, and balancing panes. Add concise example lines plus focused assertion coverage so operators can discover pane workflows directly from the main panes topic.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.

Desired behavior: start with one workspace and create/show more only on demand when navigating to them via C-a 1, C-a 2, etc. Fix initial/default workspace chip model and bar clock/chip geometry so startup chrome does not show non-existent workspaces or hide the clock.

title: kittwm: wire keymap focus to raw-frame compositor focus
description:
Large gap: raw-frame kittwm chrome can now display focus, but focus actions are still cosmetic counters in the session. If no pointer click has focused a window, every raw frame can appear focused because the raw-frame metadata defaults per-window. Keyboard focus actions should drive the compositor's real focused window.

Scope:
- Add compositor helpers for deterministic focus: default focus, focus_next, focus_prev over current backend windows.
- Make raw-frame metadata mark exactly one focused frame by default.
- Wire session keymap focus actions (`focus.right/down` next, `focus.left/up` previous) to compositor focus helpers while preserving footer state text.
- Add targeted compositor tests for default single focus and focus cycling.

Acceptance:
- `raw_frames` marks only one frame focused before any pointer event.
- `focus_next`/`focus_prev` wrap deterministically.
- Keymap focus actions update compositor focus in capture-backed sessions.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm terminal ANSI styles: build SGR strings directly
description: ansi_fg_bg uses format! to create terminal SGR style strings for pure-terminal chrome each frame. Build the foreground/background SGR string directly into a preallocated buffer while preserving exact escape output.

title: kittwm-browser: bound status metadata fields before formatting

Pathological env/socket paths can allocate large status strings every browser frame. Clip browser status metadata fields before formatting the status row.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm SDK: validate semantic component/action tokens locally
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: SDK semantic helpers format raw component/action strings into `SEMANTIC_FOCUS` and `SEMANTIC_ACTION` commands. The CLI already requires component/action to be single protocol tokens; SDK calls with blank or whitespace-containing component/action values fail only after daemon socket I/O and padded valid tokens are sent unnormalized. Add SDK-side token validation for semantic component/action parameters and update focused semantic wrapper tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm raw frame chrome: preallocate title row text
description: raw_frame_chrome_text is used for raw compositor frame chrome and redraw keys. It already clips incrementally but grows from an empty String each frame. Preallocate the visible chrome row width before pushing marker/title/mode/padding to reduce redraw allocations while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Prove: Ctrl-C delivery + triple-quit
description: Integration test: drive a FakeServer that records key events. Send one InputEvent containing Ctrl-C, assert FakeServer received it. Send three Ctrl-C events within 500ms, assert run_loop returns. Send three Ctrl-C with 1.5s gaps between each, assert run_loop does NOT return and FakeServer received three Ctrl-Cs. Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: avoid clock chip overlap with custom workspace chips
description: Kittwm burn-down follow-up: after custom workspace labels became visible in top-bar chips, the graphical BarModel scene still right-aligns the clock chip without considering how far workspace chips extend. A long custom workspace label can overlap the clock chip, causing confusing top-bar chrome. Scope: make scene clock placement conditional on available space after workspace chips, preserve existing default clock rendering, and add geometry tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser status: avoid allocating unchanged short labels
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: truncate() returns a new String even when the input is already within the limit. Status URL/window/socket labels are often short, so initialization and test helpers allocate unnecessarily. Scope: switch truncation to return Cow<str>, only allocating when clipping is needed, and keep status formatting behavior covered.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: add native socket event stream for pane and window changes
description: Add a WATCH/EVENTS-style native socket stream so SDK clients can subscribe to pane/window/focus/layout/status changes instead of polling STATUS_JSON/READ_TEXT. Include JSON event schema, client timeout handling, and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm PTY renderer: cache rasterized font glyphs
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: draw_terminal_font_glyph calls fontdue::Font::rasterize for each visible glyph every frame. Terminal panes redraw many repeated characters at the same cell height, so repeated rasterization is avoidable renderer CPU work. Scope: cache rasterized font glyph metrics/bitmaps by character and pixel size, preserve bitmap fallback for missing glyphs, and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm bars: saturate overlay label width accounting
description: Live and standalone bar text overlays still cast chip/clock text char counts to u16 while accumulating row widths. Pathological custom workspace labels or time strings can wrap the count and produce wrong clock-fit decisions. Saturate label width accounting before column/clock placement.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: composite surface ingests RGBA SurfaceFrame
description:
Small runtime adapter follow-up: `CompositeFrameSurface` can compose manually pushed RGBA children, but callers with a captured `SurfaceFrame` need to manually unpack it.

Scope:
- Add `CompositeFrameSurface::push_surface_frame(x, y, &SurfaceFrame)` that accepts `NativeFrame::Rgba` and uses its dimensions/payload as a child.
- Return an explicit unsupported error for `NativeFrame::Png` so callers do not silently drop scene/browser PNG frames.
- Add focused tests for RGBA SurfaceFrame ingestion and PNG rejection.
- Do not change live session defaults.

Acceptance:
- Composite adapter can ingest RGBA NativeSurface captures directly.
- Tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm PTY scrollback: avoid shifting buffer on every overflow line
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: TerminalState::push_scrollback_line keeps a Vec<String> at SCROLLBACK_MAX_LINES by draining from the front whenever len exceeds the cap. Once full, every new scrolled line shifts the full buffer, which is expensive during output floods and can make kittwm feel slow/flickery. Scope: prune scrollback in batches while preserving the hard cap after each push, and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm kitty placeholders: build foreground color directly
description: Replace per-row `format!("\x1b[38:2:{r}:{g}:{b}m")` in kitty placeholder text generation with a direct precomputed ANSI color helper. Scope only `crates/kittui-kitty/src/lib.rs` placeholder foreground color construction; preserve placeholder text grammar tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-renderer: re-place raw app frames on focus chrome changes
description: User reports switching between two splits makes both panes disappear and never redraw. In the raw compositor path, focus changes only repaint ANSI chrome; if kitty app placements are lost/covered, unchanged app frames are not re-placed because their pixels and footprint did not change. Re-emit app placement on chrome/focus changes so switching focus restores visible app graphics without forcing an upload.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add SDK helpers for mouse title-drag shortcut entries
description: SDK discoverability follow-up: shortcut catalogs now include mouse title-drag entries for tiled reorder and floating reposition, but SDK clients must still hard-code drag_tiled_title / drag_floating_title ids. Add ShortcutCatalog helpers for those entries so tools can surface mouse drag affordances without duplicating catalog ids. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm kitty query escape: build directly
description: Replace `format!("{ESC}_Ga=q,i={query_id}{ESC}\\")` in kitty graphics capability query construction with a direct preallocated String helper. Scope only `crates/kittui-kitty/src/lib.rs` query capability escape construction; preserve exact query grammar tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add keyboard previous-focus shortcut for kittwm panes
description: Operator-requested kittwm focus-state UX uplift: live keyboard focus can advance with C-a Tab / C-a n, and the socket/SDK expose focus_prev, but the in-session shortcut list has no simple previous-focus chord. Add C-a p for previous pane focus, advertise it in shortcuts/footer surfaces, and validate it uses the existing focus event path.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add SDK helper for focused topmost kittwm pane state
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/focus-state cleanliness uplift: Status/PanesStatus expose focused_pane() and topmost_pane(), but clients must manually compare window ids to detect when floating focus and visual topmost stack state diverge. Add focused_is_topmost helpers so automation/UI code can directly reason about focus-vs-stack state.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Implement Image node rasterization and kittui image CLI
description: DESIGN.md defines Node::Image plus CLI `kittui image --src PATH -w W -h H --fit ... --tint ...`. Current CPU renderer returns UnsupportedImage for Node::Image and GPU encode drops image nodes; the CLI has no image subcommand. Implement PNG/JPEG image decoding behind the intended feature split, shared Fit geometry, CPU raster copy/tint path, GPU texture/atlas or documented CPU fallback path, image cache storage, and CLI coverage with tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md interactive math toggle
description: Add a native math toggle to kittui-md --interactive so users can press m to inspect parsed math expressions without leaving the pager. Reuse existing math metadata, expose the keybinding, document it, and add targeted pager/math-screen tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: preserve split UTF-8 input across reads
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards UTF-8 text only within a single stdin read. If a multi-byte UTF-8 character is split across reads, the incomplete prefix is dropped and the continuation bytes are discarded, so non-ASCII input can still be lost. Preserve incomplete UTF-8 text bytes across reads while still dropping invalid bytes before control/navigation actions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm workspace state: build label directly
description: Replace `format!("{}/{}", self.current + 1, self.count)` in `WorkspaceState::label()` with direct preallocated String construction. Scope only workspace state label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing workspace state behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: docs: document new NativeSurface adapters
description:
Docs follow-up after native surface adapter work landed.

- Clarify these are runtime/composite primitives, not wired into default live kittwm behavior yet.
- Mention scene surfaces render through CPU renderer to PNG; RGBA/composite surfaces expose raw RGBA frames.

Acceptance:

Pathological backend errors can cause repeated huge terminal writes and redraw-key allocation in an already degraded path. Scope: bound error message/log-path text used for redraw keys and visible error panel, preserving normal error text and adding focused coverage.

title: kittwm help input: include send/paste examples
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. The `kittwm help input` topic lists text/key/mouse/semantic commands, but it does not include copyable examples for common paths like sending a line, pasting text, sending a key, and invoking a semantic action. Add concise example lines plus focused assertion coverage so operators can discover input workflows directly from the main input topic.

title: kittwm native footer: bound status scene label text

Long log paths or action text can bloat graphical scene metadata and hashing despite terminal clipping. Clip only the diagnostic layer-label payload while preserving the rendered footer text path.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md rich view wraps textbox content across rows
description: Rich kittui-md currently draws textbox content as one truncated line even though UiComponent computes multi-row heights. Add a wrapping text overlay helper so textboxes, banners, and headings can use their available rows, with pure unit tests for wrapping and truncation behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add save and restore session CLI flags
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: native kittwm now exposes `SESSION_JSON` and `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON`, but users/controllers still have to manually quote JSON through `kittwm --attach -c`. That makes persistence fragile in shell scripts and undermines the save/restore workflow.

Scope:
- Add first-class CLI flags:
  - `kittwm --save-session PATH` writes `SESSION_JSON` from the running socket (`-` for stdout).
  - `kittwm --restore-session PATH` reads JSON (`-` for stdin), compacts/validates it as JSON, and sends `RESTORE_SESSION_JSON <json>`.
- Reuse default socket resolution.
- Add help text and focused tests for restore command generation.

Acceptance:
- Save/restore avoids manual shell quoting hazards.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: skip unchanged PNG uploads
description: kittwm-browser still writes a kitty PNG upload every loop even when the captured frame bytes are unchanged. Memoize a frame content key and skip redundant uploads while still placing on first render/resize. This reduces idle graphics churn and flicker in the browser helper.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Show active title drag marker in pane chrome

Add a compact active-drag title marker on the dragged pane and include it in the title marker legend so the active interaction is visible in pane chrome too. Filed from ongoing kittwm drag UX audit.

title: Show dirty-frame tile ratio in kittwm pane chips
description: Operator-requested kittwm render-performance visual polish: pane chips currently show only changed tile count for dirty frames (for example frame:7), which hides the denominator that makes render cost understandable. Show changed/total tile ratio for dirty frames (for example frame:7/9) while keeping clean/new behavior unchanged, with focused tests/docs.

title: add kittui-ghostty PTY timelapse command mode
description:
Add a PTY-backed timelapse command mode to portable kittui-ghostty. It should run a command in a pseudo-terminal, capture the VT byte stream, replay it into libghostty-vt in line/chunk increments, and emit deterministic PNG frames plus a manifest. This gives progress/evidence images for real command output over time without desktop capture.

Acceptance:

- PTY size follows --cols/--rows.
- Emits frame-*.png and manifest.json by replaying captured PTY bytes incrementally.
- Existing stdin, --demo, --command, --pty-command, --scroll, and --timelapse-demo modes still work.
- Generate/post a fresh PTY timelapse montage in the session.
- Run focused validation.

title: Surface project config validation blockers in caco commands

title: Investigate persistent agent claim visibility drift
description: Context: while working bd-883864 and bd-67a477, first-party caco bd claim returned assignee kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2 and status in_progress, but the lead agent repeatedly saw the same bead as open/unassigned until I retried claim. The bead show output also warned assignee_missing for this persistent agent id. This makes coordination noisy and can cause duplicate assignment concerns. Suggested fix: reconcile board/inventory display for persistent Pi agent ids or make claim/read surfaces converge faster and explain assignee_missing. - Keep existing valid commands unchanged.
- Add focused tests for unknown command and unknown topic error text.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Mistyped commands teach the next command to run instead of dead-ending.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.

title: bindings-ts: expose runtime config and placeAt
description:
Large gap: the TypeScript koffi binding still only exposes the old FFI constructor and `place(scene)`, while the C ABI now has `kittui_runtime_new_config` and `kittui_place_json_at`. JS/TS hosts cannot configure terminal capabilities/transport or move scene-local renders without mutating JSON.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_runtime_new_config` in `bindings/ts/src/index.js` and use it when options include runtime/terminal config beyond `cacheDir`.
- Wire `kittui_place_json_at` and expose `placeAt(scene, x, y)`.
- Update TypeScript declarations and README docs.
- Add binding tests that use a fake koffi library to assert constructor selection and placeAt calls without requiring a compiled cdylib.

Acceptance:
- TS tests cover configured constructor JSON and `placeAt` x/y forwarding.
- Existing `place`/`placeMany` remain compatible.
- `npm test` in `bindings/ts` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

description:

Make the help path operator-friendly: print usage/details without an error and return success, with a small test if practical.

title: Per-window macOS capture via CGWindowListCreateImage
description:
Extend QuartzServer with list_app_windows() returning every titled macOS app window (CGWindowID + bounds + owning PID + title), and capture_window(window_id) backed by CGWindowListCreateImage. Adds WindowSpec variants so a single QuartzServer can expose either the full display, one display, or one specific app window. Keeps the existing main-display path as the default. Tests verify the public API on the agent's macOS host.

Parent epic: bd-031e54
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm session scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm session scene performance cleanup: session_scene builds the backdrop layer label with format! from bounded kind/layout/focus labels, schema, and pane count. Build the session backdrop label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.

title: kittwm-browser: make DevTools port detection timeout real
description: Browser startup reliability issue: `read_devtools_port` loops while `started.elapsed() < 10s`, but each `stderr.read(&mut byte)` is a blocking pipe read. If Chrome starts oddly or never prints the DevTools URL while keeping stderr open, the timeout is ineffective and kittwm-browser launch can hang indefinitely. Move stderr reading to a helper thread/channel with `recv_timeout`, kill Chrome on timeout, and add focused parser/timeout-behavior coverage where possible.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: route ctrl-letter SEND_KEY labels as browser shortcuts
description: Keyboard/input UX bug: daemon `SEND_KEY` accepts `ctrl-a..ctrl-z`, but direct browser panes do not map those labels. They fall through to `send_bytes`, which sends a UTF-8 control character as text instead of a DevTools Ctrl+letter key event, so browser shortcuts like Ctrl+A/C/V/F do not work through the first-party named-key path. Add browser Ctrl-letter routing, preserve terminal byte behavior, and add focused parser/mapping/DevTools-param coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-terminal status text: build directly
description: render_status_text still uses format! for the typed terminal status line. Build the status line directly into one preallocated String while preserving the SDK status output text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-shortcuts: bound row rects on narrow scenes
description: Audit finding: shortcuts scene rows respect narrow COLUMNS, but shortcuts_scene_row_rect uses fixed quarter/10px margins and max(1) width, so at 1-column or very narrow widths the row rect can extend past the scene footprint. Bound shortcut row rects to the scene width to avoid narrow-terminal graphics overflow.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Include X11 window classes in remote fallback listings
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm window listing fallback uses `xdotool` titles when `wmctrl` is unavailable, but omits window classes. Include `xdotool getwindowclassname` output so `kittwm remote HOST windows QUERY` can match app/class names as well as titles on minimal X11 remotes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Document latest kittui-md utility modes
description: Update README kittui-md docs to include the new --links, --footnotes, --images, --html, and --tables utility modes if any are missing after recent work. Validate with docs checks and kittui-md build.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: tolerate per-pane socket mouse input failures
description: Socket input stability issue: live kittwm handles `SEND_MOUSE` by calling direct pointer delivery or SGR byte delivery with `?`. A browser DevTools pointer failure or terminal byte-send error can therefore propagate out of the native terminal loop and kill the WM. Make per-pane socket mouse delivery failures non-fatal: log a bounded diagnostic and continue processing/rendering other panes. Reuse the socket input failure log helper and add/update focused coverage for mouse operations.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-terminal: respect narrow status scene width
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: kittwm-terminal --status-kitty/--events-kitty clamps scene width to a 24-column minimum even when COLUMNS is smaller. On very narrow terminals that can emit a graphics placement wider than the row and cause wrapping/flicker. Scope: preserve the upper bound/default but respect positive narrow COLUMNS values; keep internal card rects inside the scene; add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-md references-only mode for links images and footnotes
description: Add a kittui-md --references mode that prints only external/document references: links, images, footnote references, and footnote definitions. This complements --outline and --metadata-json with a human-readable references scan. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: support legacy terminal mouse payloads
description: Bug-hunt finding while improving kittwm usability: `native_mouse_event_payload` returns `None` unless the nested app enabled SGR mouse mode (`?1006`). Some terminal apps enable basic mouse tracking (`?1000`) without SGR; kittwm currently focuses the pane but drops the click/scroll/release payload. Add a legacy X10-style mouse payload fallback for basic/button/all-motion modes when SGR is disabled, with coordinate bounds checks, while keeping SGR preferred when available. Add deterministic coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Complete FFI ABI surface and generated header workflow

Current kittui-ffi exposes runtime_new/free, place_json, string_free, abi_version, and a hand-maintained header/test scaffold. Implement the remaining ABI functions, preserve panic-safe boundaries, generate/update kittui.h through an xtask/cbindgen workflow, and add ABI snapshot validation.
