title: kittwm native toast: build backdrop label directly
description: Replace the `format!("toast-backdrop:{}", ...)` label construction in `native_toast_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only kittwm native toast backdrop label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing toast geometry and long-message bounds.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: expose native PTY scrollback snapshots
description:
Large terminal-WM gap: native kittwm panes expose only the current screen via READ_TEXT. Once output scrolls off-screen, controllers and users cannot inspect prior output without polling fast enough. A terminal WM needs a native scrollback buffer and a socket/CLI surface for shell automation.

Scope:
- Track bounded native PTY scrollback lines when the screen scrolls.
- Expose a `PtyTerminalApp::scrollback_snapshot()` helper.
- Carry scrollback side-data through native pane status without serializing it in status JSON.
- Add socket commands `READ_SCROLLBACK <window|focused>` and `READ_SCROLLBACK_JSON <window|focused>`.
- Add a `kittwm --read-scrollback WINDOW` CLI wrapper.
- Update help/docs and focused tests.

Acceptance:
- A parser test proves lines pushed off screen enter scrollback.
- Daemon tests prove text and JSON scrollback replies work.
- Targeted kittui-wm/kittui-cli tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Fix remote kittwm ghostty_render_state_row_cells_new symbol lookup failure
description: Operator reported on helsinki while SSHed in: running kittwm failed before startup with: kittwm: symbol lookup error: kittwm: undefined symbol: ghostty_render_state_row_cells_new. This is a dynamic linker/deployment compatibility failure, so Rust startup fallback cannot catch it. Investigate whether the installed kittwm is loading a stale libghostty-vt/native library or needs packaging/runtime path fixes. Filed as a follow-up from bd-306ec8.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native socket restore-failure log: build directly
description: The native socket RestoreSession failure diagnostic still formats the error line with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact error text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: add place_json_at placement override
description:
Large gap: Rust and CLI now expose scene-local rendering with placement overrides (`Runtime::place_at`, `kittui compose --x/--y`), but FFI hosts can only call `kittui_place_json`, which always uses the scene's embedded footprint. Non-Rust platforms still have to mutate scene JSON just to move a rendered asset.

Scope:
- Add `kittui_place_json_at(runtime, scene_json, x, y, out)` to render/cache a scene and place it at a host-supplied terminal position while preserving scene-local dimensions/identity.
- Reuse `Runtime::place_at` internally.
- Bump ABI minor and update `kittui.h` / ABI snapshot tests.
- Add targeted FFI tests proving the emitted bytes place at the requested coordinate.

Acceptance:
- FFI test calls `kittui_place_json_at(..., 5, 6, ...)` and observes cursor placement for row 7 col 6.
- Existing `kittui_place_json` behavior remains unchanged.
- `cargo test -p kittui-ffi --test abi_snapshot` and `cargo build -p kittui-ffi` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm unknown command errors: build suggestions directly
description: friendly unknown command/help-topic errors use format! to create the base message and suggestion block. Build the diagnostics directly into one String with write!/push_str while preserving helpful suggestions and quickstart/topic hints.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm completions: make unsupported shell error actionable
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm completions <shell>` rejects unsupported shells with the allowed shell names, but it does not include a copyable example or point to `kittwm help completions`. Make the error actionable with a concise try/help hint plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native pane border: build focus-accent label directly
description: Replace the `format!("pane-{idx}-focus-accent-rail")` label in `native_pane_border_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated label helper. Scope only the focus-accent-rail layer label in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing native shell affordance scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm keymap unbound-prefix log: build directly
description: The raw compositor keymap unbound-prefix diagnostic still formats the chord spec with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help topic tests: build stress text directly
description: Replace the test-only `(0..128).map(format!).collect::<Vec<_>>().join("\n")` stress help text construction in `help_topic_scene_rows_saturate_large_help_text` with direct String appends. Scope only `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; avoid active SSH/packaging/window listing work.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: allow button-specific releases in CLI send-mouse
description: Follow-up to bd-b10a22 and bd-ff9376: daemon SEND_MOUSE now accepts button-specific release events, but the public kittwm CLI `--send-mouse` validator and help text still only mention/accept generic `release`. This means users cannot send `release-left`, `release-middle`, or `release-right` from the CLI even though the socket API supports them. Update CLI validation/help text to include button-specific releases and clarify legacy/SGR mouse support; add focused CLI helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: make kittwm-terminal events kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm-terminal event inspection available as kittui/kitty-native UI, not only text. Add event scene-json and kitty graphics modes for bounded SDK event batches, preserving existing --events-ms text behavior.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-terminal supports --events-scene-json MS and --events-kitty/--events-graphics MS.
- Event scene labels summarize count, timeout, and event kinds for inspection artifacts.
- Existing --events-ms text behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover parser/model/scene labels without a live kittwm socket.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: composite surface captures RGBA child surfaces
description:
Runtime adapter follow-up: after `push_surface_frame`, composites still require callers to separately call `capture_surface()` on child surfaces.

Scope:
- Add `CompositeFrameSurface::push_surface_capture(x, y, &mut impl NativeSurface)` that captures a child surface and ingests it through `push_surface_frame`.
- Return the captured `SurfaceFrame` or otherwise expose enough result to inspect what was captured.
- Accept RGBA child captures and reject PNG captures via the existing explicit unsupported path.
- Add focused tests using `RgbaFrameSurface` for accepted capture and `KittuiSceneSurface` for PNG rejection.
- Do not wire into live kittwm defaults.

Acceptance:
- Composite code can capture-and-ingest RGBA NativeSurface children in one helper.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: frame lifecycle and clear policy audit
description: Audit and fix disappearing frames between redraws: stable image ids, placement ids, delete/unplace timing, dirty-frame skipped-upload moves, and terminal clear behavior. Ensure redraw never clears app/chrome cells after image placement and only deletes surfaces when panes close or image id changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward Ctrl-PageUp and Ctrl-PageDown input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards Ctrl+Arrow/Home/End and plain/Shift PageUp/PageDown, but consumes Ctrl+PageUp/PageDown CSI (`ESC [ 5 ; 5 ~` / `ESC [ 6 ; 5 ~`). Browsers and web apps use these for tab/document navigation. Forward them with the Ctrl modifier.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: interrupt frame-budget sleeps when input arrives
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: native/raw compositor frame-budget sleeps are chunked for responsiveness, but each chunk uses std::thread::sleep. Input that arrives at the start of a chunk still waits for the chunk before the loop can process it. Scope: make remaining-frame-budget sleeps poll stdin for each chunk and wake as soon as input is ready; keep chunk sizing tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-wm: SurfaceCapabilities accessor helpers
description:
NativeSurface metadata ergonomics follow-up: after adding extended capability booleans, callers still manually inspect fields.

Scope:
- Add small accessor methods on `SurfaceCapabilities` for common checks: `can_capture`, `can_send_text`, `can_send_bytes`, `can_receive_focus_events`, `can_emit_surface_events`, `can_resize`, `has_title`, `can_restore`.
- Keep methods pure and simple; do not change field names or serialization (none today).
- Add focused tests covering PTY and capture-only adapters.
- Do not change runtime behavior.

Acceptance:
- Callers have stable method names for extended capability checks.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Audit and fix layout mathematics
description: Conduct a comprehensive audit of all layout calculation mathematics in kittwm. The current math implementation is incorrect and is likely the root cause of the tiling and rendering issues. Review and fix coordinate calculations, window positioning, sizing, and transformation matrices. Acceptance criteria: Document all math functions, identify errors, fix them, and add unit tests to verify correctness.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: kittwm accessibility snapshot: build fallback root id directly
description: Replace `format!("{}.root", association.surface)` fallback accessibility semantic root id construction with a direct preallocated String helper. Scope only kittui-wm accessibility snapshot fallback id construction in `crates/kittui-wm/src/accessibility.rs`; preserve accessibility snapshot coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm SDK nudge payload: build directly
description: SurfaceHandle::nudge still uses format! to build the '<dx> <dy>' payload for NUDGE_PANE. Build that payload directly while preserving SDK surface nudge behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: SDK session helper status
description:
Docs follow-up after typed SDK session helpers landed. Document `SessionManifest`, `SessionPane`, `Kittwm::session`, and `Kittwm::restore_session` alongside existing CLI `--save-session` / `--restore-session` workflows.

Scope:
- Update docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md and docs/wm.md or docs/README.md with concise current-state notes.
- Mention capability gating at a high level (read session vs restore/control).
- Keep docs-only and do not overlap with SDK event parser work.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect typed SDK session save/restore helpers.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui proof CLI command and Rust harness
description:
Add 'kittui proof' subcommand that, for each combination of (transport, animation on/off, unicode-placeholder vs absolute placement, single vs multi-placement, with/without subcell offset), prints labeled output of the exact escape bytes (hex + raw) and an optional --emit mode that prints them straight to the terminal so the operator can paste/run in Ghostty/kitty for visual verification. Mirror in Rust integration tests that snapshot the protocol bytes for each combination so 'cargo test -p kittui-cli proof' fails on any drift.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: clean up Chrome when post-connect init fails
description: Browser lifecycle follow-up: `HeadlessBrowserApp::launch` cleans up Chrome/user-data-dir if spawning/port/target/connect fails before the websocket is returned, but failures after connect during `Page.enable`, viewport setup, or document readiness currently return early via `?` before the app is constructed. That can leave a headless Chrome process and profile directory behind. Wrap post-connect initialization so any failure kills/waits the child and removes the user-data-dir; keep successful launches unchanged. Add focused helper coverage for cleanup behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: docs: NativeSurface pointer input hook
description:
Docs follow-up for NativeSurface pointer input hook.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md, docs/kittwm-sdk-plan.md, and/or docs/README.md to mention `NativeSurface::send_surface_pointer` and the current XWindowSurface implementation.
- Clarify PTY/socket SGR mouse routing remains separate and live session defaults are unchanged.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs reflect pointer hook status and current limitations.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md exposes structured footnote metadata
description: Footnote references and definitions now render visibly, but MarkdownDocument and --metadata-json do not expose structured footnote data. Add footnote metadata for definitions, include it in JSON/plain/rich metadata where appropriate, and add tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-workspace: add C-a number workspace switching
description: User follow-up: kittwm should create/show additional workspaces on demand as the user navigates to them via C-a 1, C-a 2, etc. Current default keymap has c/n/p but no numeric workspace switch action. Add explicit workspace.switch.N keymap actions for digits, grow workspace state on demand, and keep status labels usable.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm browser letter key code: build directly
description: Headless browser keyboard input mapping still uses format! for KeyX code strings. Build those browser key code strings directly while preserving browser key event mapping coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: show custom workspace label in top bar chips
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: KITTWM_WORKSPACE can be set to a custom label, but the text and graphical top-bar chip overlay always render only workspaces 1, 2, and 3. If the active workspace label is custom, no chip is marked active and the label is invisible in the top bar. Scope: include the configured/custom workspace label as the active chip when it is not one of the default numeric chips, preserve compact output, and add top-bar coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm SDK: reject empty text input helpers locally
description: Follow-up to CLI text-input validation: SDK `SurfaceHandle::send_text` and `send_line` still format empty payloads into daemon commands, which are rejected only after socket I/O. Add SDK-side validation that rejects truly empty text locally while preserving whitespace-only text as valid typed spaces, matching the CLI/daemon behavior; add focused local-rejection and socket-command coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native socket direct-mouse log: build directly
description: The native socket SendMouse direct-pointer success diagnostic still uses format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact window/event/col/row/events sent/total text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm bench: measure capture pipeline (captures/s, p50/p95/p99, bytes/s)
description: Add 'kitwm bench [--seconds N] [--capture SPEC]' that runs raw_frames() in a tight loop for N seconds (default 3), records per-iteration latency, and prints: captures/s, mean/p50/p95/p99/max latency, total bytes captured, throughput MB/s. Supports --json. No raw mode, no rendering. Gives operators a real perf number for their host without spelunking /tmp/kittui-wm.log. Parent: bd-031e54.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daily help scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: daily_help_scene still builds the daily-help backdrop label with format! from kind label, line count, and command count. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm commands: add daily guide footer
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm commands` now groups command categories cleanly, but its footer only points to `kittwm --help-json`. Add concise next-step footer links to `kittwm examples`, `kittwm cheat`, and `kittwm help topics` so the catalog can hand operators off to daily-driver docs and focused help, plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available and active-claim verification recovered to zero.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Complete remote kittwm alias shell completions
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: after adding `kittwm remote HOST kittwm` / `desktop` for the healthy remote-installed path, ensure shell completions advertise those aliases alongside status/list/apps/launch/windows/displays/terminal/shell/ssh. This keeps the remote front door discoverable from Ghostty/Kitty host terminals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm SDK: normalize app discovery queries
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: SDK `Kittwm::app_first` and `app_launch_first` interpolate raw query strings into `APPS_FIRST` / `APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST` commands. Blank/whitespace-only queries are rejected only by the daemon, and padded valid queries are sent unnormalized. Trim valid app queries and reject blank queries locally; add focused app-discovery helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: ci: add nix self-hosted multi-arch workflow
description: Add Nix-based GitHub Actions CI for self-hosted Linux x86_64, Linux aarch64, and macOS runners, following the repo-family pattern used by related projects. Keep scope to CI/workflow files.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm info scene: build backdrop label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: info_scene still builds the info backdrop label with format! from workspace label and pane count. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm XWindow surface ids: build directly
description: Replace `format!("xwindow:{}", window.id.0)` in `crates/kittui-wm/src/native.rs` XWindow metadata with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only X11/XWindow native surface id construction; preserve `xwindow:<id>` metadata output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Define kittui clippy warning policy for targeted validation
description: Context: while validating bd-c647fc after a crash revive, `cargo clippy -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm -- -D warnings` failed before reaching the touched code because existing default clippy warnings in dependency crates are not clean under the current toolchain. Non-strict `cargo clippy -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` completed with warnings, and targeted tests plus build passed. Friction shape: dev instructions mention clippy, but the repository currently has no clean strict-clippy baseline, so agents can mistake pre-existing warnings for bead failures. Suggested fix: either add a documented non-strict clippy smoke policy, a scoped allow/baseline, or a dedicated cleanup batch for the existing warnings. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-c647fc session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kitwm --pick-window: live Mac window picker that captures the chosen one
description: Add --pick-window flag to kitwm. On macOS with --features sck, lists CGWindowList::list_app_windows() in a kittui-rendered menu, lets the operator type to filter, arrows to navigate, Enter to surface that window as the capture target. Then runs the regular kitwm session against QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)). Visible new capability per overnight loop directive. Parent: bd-ed1c33.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch app replies: build directly
description: Small kittwm-launch CLI performance cleanup: app launch replies still use `format!` for APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST/APPS_FIRST response text. Build those replies directly into preallocated Strings while preserving app launch and `--status` output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: throttle pane_frame_presented event spam
description: Operator loop: kittwm is unusably slow/flickery; continue finding and fixing issues. After adding frame byte telemetry, the render loop still publishes pane_frame_presented on every pane every frame. Even when unchanged frame uploads are skipped, this can flood the in-process event queue and socket EVENTS consumers at 30fps, adding overhead and noise. Scope: suppress or coalesce unchanged clean frame events, while still publishing events for uploads, placement moves, skipped-upload transitions, and periodic/diagnostic changes. Add targeted unit coverage for the event decision/coalescing helper.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-xquartz: spawn XQuartz in nolisten mode, attach via x11rb
description:
New crate kittui-xquartz behind feature 'xquartz'. spawn() launches /opt/X11/bin/Xquartz -nolisten tcp -screen $DISPLAY 1024x768x24 -ac or attaches to an existing :N. Implements XServer trait by delegating to kittui-xvfb's x11rb code path (extract that into a shared helper if needed). Detects XQuartz install at /opt/X11/bin or /Library/X11. Returns Unavailable if not found. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-72e4e6
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm launcher match-score huge strings: build directly
description: The launcher match-score allocation-avoidance test still builds huge candidate/query strings with format!. Build those stress inputs directly while preserving match-score coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-update: explain missing GitHub latest release
description: User reported `kittui update` / `kittwm update` failing with raw `GET .../releases/latest status code 404`. This happens because the updater checks GitHub Releases, not main, and no published latest release/assets exist yet. Improve the update UX by adding a clear no-release hint for GitHub latest-release 404s.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: spike Linux AT-SPI semantic adapter
description: Implement a Linux AT-SPI proof for kittwm semantic surfaces. Find an application/window accessibility tree, extract roles/names/states/actions into SemanticSurfaceSnapshot, and degrade cleanly when AT-SPI is unavailable or sandboxed. Follow-up from bd-4a49aa.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm CLI: validate send-key tokens locally
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: CLI `--send-key` and the `key` alias format raw key strings into `SEND_KEY` commands. Blank or whitespace-containing key names are rejected only after daemon socket I/O, and padded valid keys are sent unnormalized. Add CLI-side token validation for send-key: trim valid keys and reject blank/multi-word keys locally, matching SDK and daemon expectations; add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: hide raw graphics behind picker overlay
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: the raw compositor now hides app graphics while the launcher text overlay is active, but the picker overlay uses the same terminal text overlay style and does not hide raw app graphics. That can let kitty graphics/placeholder cells conflict with picker text, causing flicker or obscured picker rows. Treat either launcher or picker as an active text overlay for raw graphics hiding, and keep restore/clear behavior consistent. Add deterministic helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-launch unknown backend diagnostic should build directly
description: `parse_backend` still builds unknown-backend diagnostics with `format!`. Build the diagnostic directly into a preallocated String while preserving the user-facing expected backend list. Scope: kittwm-launch unknown backend diagnostic only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-bar: bound standalone overlay workspace chip labels
description: Standalone kittwm-bar --kitty overlay writes every workspace chip label without checking terminal width. Long custom labels can wrap/corrupt the row even though live graphical overlay is bounded. Clip/stop overlay chip labels before the row edge, preserve row clear and clock-fit guard, and add tests with a long custom workspace.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK app lookup requests: build directly
description: Typed SDK app discovery helpers still format APPS_FIRST and APPS_LAUNCH_FIRST request strings. Build these request strings directly into preallocated buffers while preserving validation and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon SESSION_JSON: build panes directly
description: Small kittwm daemon performance cleanup: SESSION_JSON builds a Vec<serde_json::Value> for panes before serializing. Stream the static session JSON shape directly into one String with serde_json string escaping to reduce transient allocation while preserving the socket response.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm WAIT argument errors: build directly
description: WAIT_TEXT/WAIT_OUTPUT argument errors still use format! for ERR <verb> requires window and needle. Build that shared reply directly while preserving wait validation coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: doctor daily-driver readiness hints
description:
Daily-driver UX source work: improve `kittwm doctor` with actionable daily-driver recommendations.

Scope:
- Implement source changes (not docs-only) so `kittwm doctor` text output includes a short "Daily driver readiness" / "Next steps" section.
- Include checks/recommendations around tmux-safe renderer behavior, socket target env, quickstart/help/info commands, and whether a daemon/socket is reachable if practical.
- Keep existing JSON doctor stable unless a small additive field is straightforward.
- Add focused tests for text formatting/recommendations.
- Avoid overlapping lifecycle aliases (`start`/`stop`) handled by current agent.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm doctor` helps a user understand what to do next to make kittwm usable.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-ffi: expose batch placement origin API
description:
Large gap: Rust and CLI now support batch placement origins, but the FFI/TypeScript platform bindings still only expose batch placement at embedded scene coordinates. External platforms using kittui as their renderer need the same group-origin primitive.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_place_many_json_at(runtime, scenes_json, x, y, out)` using `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin`.
- Bump FFI ABI minor and update `kittui.h` plus ABI snapshot tests.
- Expose `Kittui.placeManyAt(scenes, x, y)` in TypeScript bindings/types.
- Add targeted FFI and TypeScript tests.

Acceptance:
- FFI batch-at-origin returns bytes for a scene array and rejects invalid input consistently with existing batch API.
- TypeScript `placeManyAt` calls the new symbol and returns a string.
- `cargo test -p kittui-ffi place_many_json_at -- --nocapture`, ABI snapshot, and `npm test --prefix bindings/ts` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add explicit kittwm raise/lower pane controls
description: Operator-requested kittwm UX/design-cleanliness slice: floating panes now click-to-raise and expose stack metadata, but users/SDK clients still have to know that `move focused last` is the raise operation. Add explicit `kittwm raise [WINDOW]` and `kittwm lower [WINDOW]` aliases plus SDK convenience methods so floating stack actions are discoverable and design vocabulary matches WM behavior. Keep implementation mapped to existing MOVE_PANE first/last semantics and add focused parser/SDK tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm reserve-chrome invalid-json reply: build directly
description: RESERVE_CHROME_JSON invalid-json errors still use format! to append the parse error. Build that reply directly while preserving reserve-chrome validation coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: graphical empty workspace landing surface
description: Dogfood graphics gap: empty workspace should be a kittui-rendered landing surface, not mostly text. Render a graphical empty state with top bar, subtle background/translucent panel, quick actions, and hints for terminal/help/examples. Acceptance: launching kittwm with no panes presents a polished kittui scene in graphics mode; keyboard shortcuts still work; terminal fallback remains concise.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: cache idle PTY terminal RGBA frames
description: Bug-hunt finding while kittwm is reported unusably slow/flickery: `TerminalSurface::capture` clones terminal state and renders a full RGBA frame every tick, even if no PTY output or resize changed the terminal since the previous capture. Dirty-frame upload skipping happens later, after the render cost. Add a terminal state revision and cache/reuse the last RGBA frame while the revision is unchanged; invalidate on PTY output and resize; add deterministic helper/state coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm OSC52 clipboard forwarding: build host sequence directly
description: Replace `format!("\x1b]52;{selector};{payload}\x07")` in `crates/kittui-wm/src/native.rs` with a direct preallocated OSC 52 helper. Scope only host clipboard OSC forwarding construction; preserve exact forwarded OSC 52 output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Avoid duplicate focused footer status chip computation
description: Render-performance follow-up: after adding focused status to the footer, native_shell_view computes the focused pane status chip once for the footer and again while building pane chrome. Reuse the pane chrome status string for the focused footer state instead of recomputing it. Filed from ongoing kittwm footer/render-state audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm SDK wait default requests: build directly
description: SurfaceHandle wait_text and wait_output still format WAIT_TEXT/WAIT_OUTPUT request strings. Build these through the direct surface payload request helper while preserving needle validation and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm completions: cache static word list
description: Small kittwm CLI performance cleanup: completion generation now uses borrowed static words, but completion_words still rebuilds/sorts/dedups a Vec on every bash/zsh/fish generation. Cache the sorted static completion word list once with OnceLock and iterate the slice for all shell completion emitters.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: saturate session scene row count
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: session graphics compute rows with panes.len() cast to u16 plus padding. A huge session manifest can overflow/truncate row accounting before clamping, risking malformed scene metadata. Scope: use saturated row count accounting for session scenes and add focused large-list coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: size text overlays from detected terminal width
description: Resize follow-up: live text overlays use `overlay_inner_width`, which only reads static `COLUMNS`. In a resized host terminal, `COLUMNS` can be stale while `TIOCGWINSZ` knows the current width. Make overlay sizing fall back to the detected host terminal columns before the preferred width so launcher/picker/help overlays fit the resized canvas; add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add SDK helpers for active title drag kind value
description: SDK follow-up: active title-drag kind booleans are available, but clients that want the raw/typed active title_drag_kind still have to unwrap active_title_drag_pane(). Add active_title_drag_kind() and active_parsed_title_drag_kind() helpers on Status/PanesStatus. Filed from ongoing kittwm SDK/drag-state audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native socket focus-prev log: build directly
description: The native socket FocusPrev path formats its debug diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact text.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm markdown footnote markers: build directly
description: Replace `format!("[^{label}]")` in `crates/kittui-affordances/src/markdown.rs` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only markdown footnote reference marker construction; preserve footnote rendering tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: reorganize CLI help for daily-driver use
description:
Daily-driver UX: `kittwm` help is too hard to read.

Scope:
- Rework the `kittwm` printed help into a concise, grouped command tree with common daily-driver flows first.
- Keep all existing commands/flags working; this is presentation only.
- Include clear sections for starting kittwm, inspecting a running WM, pane control, input/automation, semantic/session surfaces, and diagnostics/backends.
- Include examples for common workflows (launch terminal, inspect panes/status, read text, wait for output).
- Add/adjust focused test to assert major section headings and key commands are present.
- No daemon/socket/runtime behavior changes.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm --help` is scannable and teaches basic usage without reading docs.
- Targeted kittwm binary tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings: add primitive group/clip/composite scene helpers
description:
Platform renderer substrate gap: Python/TypeScript primitive scene helpers cover rectangles, gradients, images, glows, and scanlines, but not the core scene-composition primitives (`group`, `clip`, `composite`, `mask`). External platforms and shell-adjacent scripts need these to build reusable kittui/kittwm chrome and effects without hand-authoring raw JSON.

Scope:
- Add Python scene helpers for `group`, `clip`, `composite`, and `mask` nodes/layers.
- Add TypeScript scene helpers and d.ts typings for the same primitives.
- Keep helpers primitive-only; do not add high-level widgets to core.
- Update README/tests for both bindings.

Acceptance:
- Python unit tests cover JSON shape for new helpers.
- TypeScript tests cover JSON shape for new helpers.
- `PYTHONPATH=bindings/python python3 -m unittest discover bindings/python`, `npm test --prefix bindings/ts`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm architecture scene: build surface labels directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: architecture_scene still builds first-party native surface row labels with format! from surface metadata. Add a direct preallocated surface-label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-launch missing query diagnostic should build directly
description: `LaunchArgs::parse_from` still builds the missing-query diagnostic with `format!("missing launch query\n\n{}", help_text())`. Build the diagnostic directly into a preallocated String while preserving the user-facing help text. Scope: kittwm-launch missing query diagnostic only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-terminal: match spaced Fira Code Nerd Font names
description: Follow-up to Nerd Font discovery: scoring only matches filenames containing contiguous "firacode" and "regular". Common Nerd Font packages may install names like "Fira Code Regular Nerd Font Complete Mono.otf" or use hyphen/space-separated forms. Normalize candidate names before matching so user-installed Fira Code Nerd Font variants are found reliably.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm PTY capture: avoid cloning scrollback for rendering
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: after cache misses, TerminalSurface::capture clones the full TerminalState for rendering. That includes scrollback and pending buffers even though render_terminal_rgba only needs cells, cursor, and dimensions. Large scrollback makes changed-frame capture unnecessarily expensive. Scope: add a lightweight render snapshot containing only renderer-needed fields, use it for capture/rendering, keep existing render tests passing.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add document counts to kittui-md metadata JSON
description: Add a top-level counts object to kittui-md --metadata-json/--json with component, heading, heading_anchor, link, image, table, footnote, definition, math, html, metadata_block, and code_block counts. Add targeted tests and docs.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK shortcuts tests: build socket path directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("/tmp/kwshortcuts-{}-{}.sock", ...)` in `shortcuts_helper_sends_expected_socket_command` with the existing direct socket-path helper. Scope only kittwm SDK shortcuts-helper test socket path construction in `crates/kittwm-sdk/src/lib.rs`; preserve shortcuts helper socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-kitty: make zlib auto compression threshold-based
description: Implement threshold-based kitty zlib compression decisions for raw graphics frames. Scope: make KITTUI_KITTY_COMPRESSION=auto size/cadence aware rather than unconditional zlib, add KITTUI_ZLIB_MIN_BYTES, and cover small/large raw frame decisions with tests. Follow-up from bd-3c0dd1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Make kittwm remote HOST terminal open forwarded terminal panes
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm remote HOST terminal` should not just point users at kittwm-terminal; it should invoke the existing `kittwm-terminal --remote HOST` path, forwarding optional title/command args, so app/window/display/terminal requests all have top-level `kittwm remote HOST ...` aliases.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui: add batch placement origin API
description:
Large gap: the CLI can now move JSON scene batches as a group, but the core Rust renderer API still only exposes `place_batch(&[Scene])` at embedded scene coordinates. External platform bindings and shell host libraries need the same group-origin primitive without mutating every scene footprint themselves.

Scope:
- Add `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin(&[Scene], x, y)` to place a scene batch with its minimum x/y remapped to a runtime origin while preserving relative spacing.
- Factor batch concatenation through placement results so `place_batch` remains unchanged.
- Add targeted `kittui` library tests for group-origin placement and empty batches.
- Update CLI compose batch origin handling to use the new library API instead of cloning/mutating scene footprints.

Acceptance:
- Core API returns batch footprints at the requested origin with relative offsets preserved.
- Empty batches still succeed.
- CLI compose batch origin tests continue to pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: clean kittwm first-launch UX
description:
Docs follow-up for clean first-launch kittwm UX.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md and docs/README.md as needed to describe empty workspace default, top bar, terminal-launch shortcut, Ctrl-A ? help overlay, and env knob for startup terminal compatibility if added.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:
- Docs match the new default launch behavior.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-top unknown option: build diagnostic directly
description: kittwm-top option parsing formats unknown-option diagnostics together with help text. Build the diagnostic directly into one preallocated String while preserving the help output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Structure kittwm footer status density and truncation
description: During bd-49529a, the footer gained a mode indicator, but the footer is now carrying shortcuts, mode, and log path in one long line. Future kittwm UX polish should consider a structured footer/status model with priority/truncation rules (for example mode/focus first, hints second, log path last) and tests across narrow terminals, rather than concatenating a long string. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-49529a session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native top bar: parse clock without token Vec
description: native_top_bar_time_from_text collects all whitespace tokens into a Vec just to inspect the trailing clock/UTC fields when rebuilding graphical top-bar state. Parse from the end with split_whitespace().next_back() and keep the same fallback behavior to reduce per-frame top-bar allocations.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm keymap launcher selected log: build directly
description: The raw compositor keymap launcher selected diagnostic still formats kind/command/pid with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: route host mouse reports into native panes
description:
Large native WM gap: the kittwm native terminal loop enables SGR mouse reporting on the host terminal, but stdin bytes are forwarded mostly raw to the focused PTY. Host mouse reports are not translated to pane-local coordinates or routed to the pane under the pointer, so mouse-aware TUIs cannot be used naturally in native panes and panes may receive inappropriate outer-terminal escape bytes.

Scope:
- Parse host SGR mouse input in the native terminal loop using `kittui_input`.
- Route mouse events to the pane whose app area contains the pointer, translating host cell coordinates to pane-local 1-indexed coordinates.
- Reuse native mouse event payload encoding and only inject when the target pane has compatible mouse reporting modes.
- Focus the clicked/scroll-target pane when routing a press/scroll event.
- Preserve existing keyboard/prefix behavior.
- Add focused unit tests for coordinate translation/routing helpers.

Acceptance:
- Tests prove host coordinates map to pane-local mouse payloads and reject title/outside areas.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md grid alias for tables
description: Add --grid as a friendly alias for kittui-md --tables so users can inspect Markdown table/grid layout with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-wm: expose surface events on NativeSurface
description:
Surface runtime gap: side-effect events (title/bell/clipboard/notification) are currently available on PTY internals but not part of the common `NativeSurface` abstraction.

Scope:
- Add a default `take_surface_events(&mut self) -> Vec<SurfaceEvent>` method to the `NativeSurface` trait.
- Override it for `PtyTerminalApp` by delegating to its terminal surface event drain.
- Leave capture-only adapters default-empty.
- Add focused tests showing PTY/native surface trait drains events and capture-only adapters return empty.
- Do not change daemon event publication semantics or live session behavior beyond using/keeping equivalent drain paths if touched.

Acceptance:
- Common native surface interface can drain semantic side-effect events.
- Existing side-effect behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Agent checkouts should explain or recover stale git index.lock files
description:
Context: while finishing bd-d15cf7 in the kittui managed checkout, two `git commit` attempts failed because `.git/index.lock` existed even though `git status` worked and no repo-local git/cargo/rustc process was using this checkout. The lock was zero bytes and had to be removed manually after preserving evidence. This is likely host/restart or agent tooling friction rather than a product bug.

Suggested fix shape: add a first-party diagnostic/recovery path that can distinguish an active writer from a stale zero-byte lock in an isolated agent checkout, explain the owner if known, and provide a safe lifecycle command or clearer guidance rather than forcing workers to inspect process lists manually.

Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-d15cf7 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-xvfb: spawn Xvfb, claim display, SHM capture
description:
New crate kittui-xvfb that owns an Xvfb subprocess (or attaches to an existing :N display), connects via XCB, lists toplevel windows, and captures their framebuffer regions via MIT-SHM. Emits per-window (id, rect, rgba_bytes) snapshots and damage rectangles. Test exercises the spawn + connect path under a Linux runner and gracefully skips on macOS.

Parent epic: bd-8bfc05
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui panel test: update animation default expectations
description: Follow-up after standardizing panel animation defaults: `crates/kittui-cli/tests/panel_command.rs` still expects legacy 8-frame/800ms metadata for `kittui panel --animate`. Update smoke coverage to assert the new standard 180-frame/3000ms compatibility behavior and include `--animated` where appropriate.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm bar: use configured colorscheme and configurable shadow alpha
description: Follow-up to user report: top-bar chip scene currently uses mostly static Nord tokens. Make bar scene styling derive from KittwmConfig colorscheme/background where available and expose a simple KITTWM_BAR_SHADOW_ALPHA override for drop-shadow/translucency tuning. Preserve high contrast clock defaults and pure text fallback.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: bindings-python: expose render_many manifest
description:
Large gap: FFI now exposes render-many manifests, but Python still only exposes single-scene `render`. Python automation needs the one-call batch render manifest for preview/artifact pipelines.

Scope:
- Wire `kittui_render_many_json` in Python ctypes binding.
- Add `Kittui.render_many(scenes) -> dict` returning parsed manifest with base64 PNG entries.
- Add fake-CDLL tests for success and failure detail.
- Update Python README API/example.

Acceptance:
- Python tests pass.
- `render_many` accepts dict/JSON mixed scenes and returns parsed manifest.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md references-json inspection mode
description: Add --references-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for combined Markdown references: links, images, footnote references, and footnote definitions. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help: mention kittwm-top in app helpers
description: Follow-up to `kittwm-top`: the focused `kittwm help apps` / app helper text still mentions `kittwm-launch` and `kittwm-bar`, but omits the new first-party SDK-backed `kittwm-top` process viewer. Add `kittwm-top` to the app helper discoverability text and focused tests so users can find the dual-mode process viewer from CLI help.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-ffi: make render_json return PNG bytes
description:
Large gap: the FFI/header/Python binding call `kittui_render_json` a render-only PNG API, but the implementation currently calls `Runtime::place` and returns terminal escape bytes. External platforms needing previews/non-terminal embedding get the wrong bytes.

Scope:
- Add public `Runtime::render_png(&Scene) -> Vec<u8>` that renders without terminal capability checks, placement, upload state, or kitty escapes.
- Make `kittui_render_json` parse a scene and return PNG bytes from `Runtime::render_png`.
- Add Rust/FFI tests that assert PNG signature and no kitty escapes for render_json.
- Re-run Python binding tests to ensure its new render method now matches real ABI semantics.

Acceptance:
- `kittui_render_json` returns bytes beginning with the PNG signature.
- Render-only path does not require kitty terminal support.
- Targeted kittui/kittui-ffi/Python tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Respect desktop environment filters in remote app fallback
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: when remote kittwm is absent/broken, `kittwm remote HOST list apps` falls back to Linux `.desktop` discovery. Respect `OnlyShowIn=` and `NotShowIn=` using the remote desktop context from XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP/DESKTOP_SESSION so the pooled-SSH app list better matches the remote host launcher.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: add native surface placement contract helpers
description: Add SDK helpers to build `SurfacePlacementContract` directly from `NativeSurfaceContract` / `ArchitectureContract` by surface name or kind. This lets apps inspect chrome surfaces like kittwm-bar as placement contracts even when they are not spawned via `SurfaceSpec`. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add SDK helpers for full pane status chip labels
description: UX/SDK follow-up: SDK now exposes pid/weight/frame chip fragments, but clients still need to stitch them together with the command label to mirror the full live kittwm pane status chip. Add NativePaneDetail and focused Status/PanesStatus helpers for bounded command labels and full pane status chip labels. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/SDK audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: semantic publish/action surfaces should survive pane lifecycle
description: QA gap: semantic snapshots/published component trees/actions may become stale across pane close, replace, restore, focus changes, and browser app restarts. Audit state cleanup and stale-component behavior. Acceptance: closed/replaced panes do not serve stale semantic trees; focused semantic actions route only to current pane; tests cover publish then close/replace.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: add compositor-ordered placement contracts
description: Add SDK helpers to return first-party placement contracts in compositor/z-index order, with app/decorations/overlays grouped according to the architecture contract. This helps apps and diagnostics reason about kitty graphics composition without hard-coded sorting. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm apps: make --limit parse errors actionable
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm apps --limit nope` currently fails with a terse `--limit expects integer` error. Make the limit parse error actionable with a copyable app-listing example and the `kittwm help apps` pointer, plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-terminal: size status scene from terminal width
description: Resize follow-up: `kittwm-terminal` status scene sizing uses `KITTWM_TERMINAL_STATUS_COLS` or static `COLUMNS`, then falls back to 56. In a hosted/resized PTY, environment values can be stale even when terminal detection knows the current width. Mirror the kittwm-bar fix: fall back to `TerminalInfo::detect().columns` before the fixed default, while preserving explicit valid overrides and caps; add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: docs: refresh accessibility adapter status in docs map
description:
Docs drift: docs/README.md still says accessibility-tree adapters are planned even though the safe adapter core, mapping, action routing, and role remaps have landed. Refresh the docs map/status bullet.

Scope:
- Update docs/README.md accessibility status only.
- Mention safe adapter core/action routing/role remaps are landed; direct macOS AX/Linux AT-SPI platform bindings remain follow-up.
- Keep docs-only.

Acceptance:

- `git diff --check` passes.

title: kittwm: mouse coordinate routing across chrome, top bar, and app area
description: QA gap: host SGR mouse reports must be translated correctly through reserved top bar, pane chrome, and app bounds. Add coverage for clicks/drag/scroll in top bar, border/title chrome, pane app area, and outside panes. Acceptance: app receives only app-area coordinates in pane-local cells when mouse modes are enabled; chrome/top-bar clicks do not corrupt app input.

title: kitwm keymap defaults: Ctrl-A d launcher alias inspired by collective WM bindings

Add viewport scroll selection to kittui-ghostty CLI so headless command-output screenshots can capture the top or bottom of scrollback. This improves progress/evidence images for real kittwm help/cheat output rendered through libghostty-vt.

title: kittwm graphical overlay panel: build backdrop label directly
description: Replace `format!("{id}-backdrop:{title}")` in test-only `graphical_overlay_panel_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated backdrop-label helper. Scope only graphical overlay panel backdrop label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-launch scene JSON: append newline directly
description: Small kittwm-launch CLI performance cleanup: `render_launch_plan` scene JSON mode still appends a newline with `format!("{json}\n")` after serialization. Append the newline directly to the serialized String while preserving scene JSON output.

title: List and launch remote macOS apps without remote kittwm
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: when the remote host lacks kittwm, `kittwm remote HOST list apps QUERY` and `kittwm remote HOST launch QUERY` should discover macOS `.app` bundles as well as PATH commands, and launch app bundles through `open -a` when selected. This improves local kittwm pooled-SSH fallback behaviour for macOS remote hosts.

title: Add kittui-md code-blocks-json inspection mode
description: Add --code-blocks-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for Markdown fenced/indented code blocks, emitting schema_version plus indexed language/text records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add batch/many render entrypoints that share resolved config and runtime state
description: Operator direction: common usage should support setting config/env once, then calling kittui many times or in a batch in that scope. Add well-documented batch entrypoints that resolve config once, reuse Runtime/cache/probe state, render/place many scenes efficiently, and expose this through CLI (e.g. JSONL or manifest input), Rust library, and FFI/TS binding surfaces. Ensure per-item overrides remain possible and tests verify repeated calls do not re-probe/re-upload unnecessarily.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make layout actions rebuild tiled slots
description:
Large gap: capture-backed kittwm exposes `layout.toggle-split` and `layout.balance`, but these still only update footer state. The compositor/layout already supports tiled slots, so layout actions should rebuild actual tiled rectangles for current windows.

Scope:
- Make the session own a mutable layout snapshot derived from the provided initial layout.
- Add a helper that rebuilds tiled slots for current compositor windows in row or column orientation using the current layout bounds or visible window rect bounds.
- Wire `ToggleSplit` and `BalanceWindows` to rebuild actual `Layout` slots while preserving footer state.
- Add tests for layout tiling helpers.

Acceptance:
- Toggle split changes real tiled window footprints in capture-backed sessions.
- Balance recomputes equal slots for current windows.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK reset-position alias for floating kittwm panes
description: Operator-requested kittwm SDK/design-cleanliness uplift: CLI exposes reset-position for dragged/nudged floating panes, while the SDK helper is named reset_floating_offset. Add a reset_position alias on SurfaceHandle and document it, so SDK clients have the same UX vocabulary as the CLI while retaining the explicit offset method.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm PTY renderer: bound rasterized font glyph cache
description: Kittwm burn-down follow-up: bd-c867d7 added a rasterized terminal glyph cache keyed by character and pixel size, but the cache is unbounded. Long sessions or unusual Unicode output can accumulate many glyphs/sizes. Scope: add a conservative cache cap/prune behavior and focused tests so the optimization cannot grow without bound.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm picker: avoid joining all entries for overlay redraw keys
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: picker_overlay_key builds a full joined string of all entries every frame while the picker is active. macOS window titles or other picker entries can be long, causing repeated allocation/scan churn just to decide whether the static picker overlay changed. Scope: key picker redraws from active/selected/count state instead of joining full entries, preserving visual updates for actual picker state changes and adding focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm native status chips: preallocate bounded command label
description: native_pane_status_chip_text uses bounded_ellipsis for pane command labels on redraw. The helper bounds scans, but currently collects into a String without an explicit bounded capacity. Preallocate the bounded command-label width and push chars directly to reduce reallocations while preserving ellipsis behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: fix nix build PTY shell resolution
description: nix run/build failed in kittui-cli lib test native_pane_statuses_include_dirty_frame_metrics because PtyTerminalApp fell back to bare sh when /bin/sh was unavailable in the pure build environment. Fix by resolving sh/bash on PATH before falling back. Filed from operator report during dev-2 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm graphical overlay panel: build footer label directly
description: Replace `format!("{id}-footer-hints")` in test-only `graphical_overlay_panel_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated footer-label helper. Scope only graphical overlay panel footer label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm daemon queue action reply: build directly
description: queue_native_pane_action still uses format! for '<OK> command=N' replies. Build that reply directly while preserving focus/balance/reset queue behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm markdown metadata labels: build directly
description: Replace `format!("metadata:{}\n{source}", kind.as_str())` in `crates/kittui-affordances/src/markdown.rs` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only markdown metadata/frontmatter component text construction; preserve metadata block rendering tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-md stats mode summarizes document counts
description: Add a kittui-md --stats mode that prints concise human-readable counts for source bytes/lines, components, headings, links, images, tables, footnotes, definitions, math, HTML, and code blocks. Include output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Update kittwm remote doctor suggestions for easy SSH aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: `kittwm doctor --remote HOST` should recommend the new top-level `kittwm remote HOST ...` commands for list/launch/windows/displays/terminal rather than only older lower-level `--remote` forms, so users discover the easiest pooled-SSH workflow when the remote lacks kittwm or has a broken install.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add kittui-md components-json inspection mode
description: Add --components-json as a focused machine-readable output mode for generated Markdown UI components, emitting schema_version plus indexed kind/rect/text records. Document it and add parser/output tests plus a proof-gallery smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui affordance controls: add animation option to ControlComponent
description: Continue animation coverage into `kittui-affordances` reusable control components. Inventory: button, checkbox, radio, radio_group, text_input, text_area, select_list, menu, slider, progress, tabs, and split_pane lower through `ControlComponent::to_scene` but have no animation option. Acceptance: controls expose an animation builder/options with 60fps/180-frame/3s defaults, `to_scene` emits a loop-closing Scene.animation and labelled phase-reactive layer, gallery components can opt in later, and unit tests cover representative controls.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm --attach -c CMD: one-shot daemon command for scripting
description: Add -c/--command flag to 'kitwm --attach' so operators can fire a single daemon command and read the reply without entering the interactive REPL. Useful for scripting: 'kitwm --attach -c WINDOWS | jq...'. Exit code maps to daemon ERR/OK. Parent: bd-031e54 (extends bd-5b8dd6).
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal: build shell words directly
description: Small kittwm terminal client performance cleanup: shell_words currently maps each arg to a String, collects a Vec, then joins. Build the command line directly into one String while preserving quoting behavior for `kittwm-terminal -- PROGRAM [ARGS...]`.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native mouse routing should borrow parsed input events
description: Native terminal input handling currently clones every parsed `InputEvent` before `native_route_mouse_event`, but that function only inspects mouse fields and never needs ownership. Borrow the parsed event instead to remove an allocation/copy from the input hot path. Scope: native terminal mouse routing only; avoid socket mouse/key routing work owned by other agents. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm markdown heading anchors: build duplicates directly
description: Replace `format!("{base}-{}", *count)` in markdown unique heading anchor generation with direct preallocated String assembly. Scope only `unique_heading_anchor(...)` in `crates/kittui-affordances/src/markdown.rs`; preserve stable duplicate heading anchors.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Add kittui-md mode categories catalog
description: Add --mode-categories and --mode-categories-json no-input discovery outputs for kittui-md so users and tools can list supported mode categories and counts before filtering by category. Document it and add parser/output tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm compose error log: build directly
description: Replace `dbg.log(&format!("compose err: {msg}"))` in the kittwm raw compositor frame loop with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only compose-error diagnostic log string construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing diagnostic text and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm X11 titles: build directly
description: Replace `format!("x11:{}", w.id.0)` in kittui-wm X11 scene/raw-frame title construction with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only `crates/kittui-wm/src/lib.rs` X11 title string construction; preserve title output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm chrome reserved reply: build directly
description: RESERVE_CHROME_JSON success replies still use format! for CHROME_RESERVED <json>. Build that reply directly while preserving reserve-chrome validation coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Support placement_id (p=) and subcell offset (X=, Y=) on placement_command
description:
placement_command currently emits a=p,U=1,i=,c=,r= with no placement-id and no subcell pixel offset. The spec allows multiple placements of the same image via p= and subcell origin via X=/Y= for fractional positioning. Extend placement_command (or add a new builder) to accept placement_id and pixel offsets; thread through Runtime::place; expose via CLI.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm commands: group repeated category headings
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm commands` describes itself as a grouped local command catalog, but its text renderer emits repeated category headings when entries of the same category are separated in the source list (for example HELP and DIAGNOSTICS appear multiple times). Make the human command catalog group each category once in first-seen order while preserving JSON order/data, and add focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: guard raw kitty frame memory and tmux graphics
description:
Runtime memory/safety bug: native kittwm's kitty-graphics path uploads full raw RGBA frames every tick. In tmux this can explode memory because passthrough graphics payloads are large, and the raw-frame hot path does not explicitly delete prior image data before replacing an image id. Add immediate safeguards: explicit image deletion before raw frame re-upload, and default native kittwm to the pure terminal renderer when running under tmux unless the user opts into graphics.

Scope:
- Update `Runtime::place_raw_frame` to emit a kitty image delete for an existing raw-frame image id before uploading the replacement frame.
- Preserve stable image ids/placements and current direct-Ghostty behavior as much as possible.
- Detect `$TMUX` in native kittwm and default to `KITTWM_NATIVE_RENDERER=terminal` behavior unless `KITTWM_NATIVE_RENDERER=kitty|graphics` is explicitly set.
- Document why tmux graphics are guarded and how to override.
- Add tests for raw-frame delete-before-upload grammar and renderer selection.

Acceptance:
- Raw frame replacement includes delete grammar for already-placed images.
- tmux defaults to terminal renderer; explicit graphics override works.
- Targeted tests, `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm`, and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm PTY renderer: skip redundant box-glyph pixel bounds checks
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: draw_box_drawing_glyph clips rectangles to the cell bounds, but each pixel still goes through set_rgba_pixel with a slice bounds check. Box drawing glyphs are common in TUIs, so this repeats avoidable checks in the renderer hot path. Scope: add/use an in-bounds set-pixel helper for clipped box glyph rectangles, keep checked set_rgba_pixel for generic callers, and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: local command catalog
description:
Daily-driver UX: add a local command catalog that does not require a running WM.

Scope:
- Add `kittwm commands` for a grouped text catalog of local subcommands/aliases.
- Add `kittwm commands-json` for machine-readable local command catalog JSON.
- Catalog should include common lifecycle, help, inspect, action, pane-control, app/session/diagnostic commands and aliases.
- Keep socket `HELP_JSON` unchanged (that catalogs daemon verbs); this catalogs local CLI commands.
- Add focused tests for text and JSON outputs.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Users/tools can discover local CLI aliases without starting kittwm.
- Targeted kittwm tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: CLI shortcut list for clean first launch
description:
Clean first-launch UX follow-up: Ctrl-A ? shows in-session help, but there is no non-interactive CLI wrapper for the same native shortcut list.

Scope:
- Add `kittwm --shortcuts` (or similar) to print the native shortcut list used by Ctrl-A ?.
- Reuse/share the existing help list where practical.
- Add targeted CLI test for output containing terminal launch, help toggle, and exit shortcuts.
- Do not change live session input behavior.

Acceptance:
- Users can discover shortcuts without launching or attaching to kittwm.
- In-session help and CLI help stay aligned from shared data.
- Targeted tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-commands: bound row rects on narrow scenes
description: Audit finding: commands-kitty / commands-scene-json still use fixed 10px row margins with (width - 20).max(1), so narrow command catalog scenes can have row rects escaping the footprint. Reuse bounded info indicator geometry for command row rects and add coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-cli: add scene-json/stdin compose pipeline
description:
Large gap: kittui is intended to be a core renderer for shell scripts/external platforms, but `kittui compose` is path-only and the CLI cannot emit reusable scene JSON for pipelines.

Scope:
- Teach `kittui compose -` to read a scene JSON document from stdin.
- Add a scene JSON output mode for simple scene-generating commands (`box`, `gradient`, `glow`) so shell scripts can pipe generated scenes into `compose`.
- Ensure JSON is the actual serializable `Scene` shape consumed by compose, not just byte-count metadata.
- Update README/DESIGN examples where they currently imply `compose -`/scene pipelines.

Acceptance:
- `kittui box --scene-json ... | kittui compose - --dry-run --json` works.
- Tests cover stdin compose and scene-json validity for at least one generated scene.
- Existing placement/dry-run behaviour remains compatible.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm cache probe test tempdirs: build directly
description: Replace probe-cache test tempdir filename `format!` in `crates/kittui-cache/src/probe.rs` with direct preallocated String assembly. Scope only kittui cache probe test tempdir path construction used by kittwm runtime GPU-probe/cache validation helpers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm events scene tests: build stress kinds directly
description: Replace the test-only `(0..128).map(format!).collect::<Vec<_>>()` event-kind stress vector in `events_scene_rows_saturate_large_event_lists` with direct String construction. Scope only `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; avoid active SSH/help/packaging work owned by other agents.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm daemon HELP should build command list directly
description: The process-daemon `HELP` reply builds its command summary with `map(...).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(" | ")`. Build the reply directly into one String like the native socket help path, avoiding the temporary vector and joined command string. Scope: `daemon_help_reply` only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm live bar: clip active workspace overlay label instead of dropping it
description: Live graphical top-bar overlay currently stops before writing a chip that would exceed terminal width. With a long custom active workspace label, this can drop the active label entirely on narrow terminals. Clip the chip label to available row width instead of dropping it when possible, matching standalone kittwm-bar behavior, while still avoiding wrap/corruption.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: suppress repeated pane capture failure log spam
description: Follow-up to capture-failure resilience: after making per-pane capture failures non-fatal, a persistently broken surface can fail every frame and write the same capture-failure log line at frame rate. That can flood `/tmp/kittui-wm.log`, add IO overhead, and obscure useful diagnostics. Track per-pane capture failure state, log only on first failure in a streak, and reset the state after a successful capture. Add focused helper/unit coverage where practical.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: align client timeout with WAIT_TEXT_MS
description:
Gap after adding `WAIT_TEXT_MS`: the socket parser allows waits up to 60000ms, but the shared client helpers still use a fixed 10s read timeout. Long explicit waits can still fail client-side before the daemon replies.

Scope:
- Compute client read timeout from the outgoing command.
- For `WAIT_TEXT_MS <window> <ms> ...`, use `ms + small margin`, capped safely above the daemon maximum.
- Keep normal commands at the existing default timeout.
- Add unit tests for timeout selection.

Acceptance:
- `WAIT_TEXT_MS ... 60000 ...` gets a client timeout longer than 60 seconds.
- Normal commands keep the 10 second timeout.
- Targeted daemon tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add native pane resize keybindings
description:
Large gap: native kittwm pane weights can be controlled over the socket, but the local terminal WM keymap still cannot resize panes directly. A terminal WM should let the operator grow/shrink the focused pane without leaving the session or using an external client.

Scope:
- Add prefix keybindings in the native PTY loop: `Ctrl-A +`/`Ctrl-A =` grows the focused pane, `Ctrl-A _`/`Ctrl-A <` shrinks it.
- Recompute weighted layouts and clear/redraw after key-driven resize.
- Update the native footer hint to mention resize keys and current focused weight.
- Add a small pure helper test if needed.

Acceptance:
- Focused pane weight changes with local keybindings and remains clamped to at least 1.
- Layout is recomputed immediately after key-driven resize.
- `cargo test -p kittui-cli session::native_pane_tests::native_adjust_weight_clamps_to_one -- --nocapture` and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Replace CGDisplayCreateImage with ScreenCaptureKit on macOS 12.3+
description: QuartzServer::capture currently uses CGDisplayCreateImage, which is soft-deprecated on macOS 14 and returns a black/dimmed frame on macOS 14/15/26 even when Screen Recording is granted. Operator reported black screen + ~1 FPS on macOS 26.4. Replace with ScreenCaptureKit (SCStream + SCStreamConfiguration) running on its own thread with latest-frame-wins, falling back to the legacy path only on macOS < 12.3. Add a non-black-frame assertion to the round-trip test. Affects both whole-display and per-window capture paths in CaptureTarget.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Expand kittui-wm from placeholder substrate to split/stack/tab layout semantics
description: DESIGN.md says kittui-wm currently reserves WindowTree/WindowGeometry but full split/stack/tab semantics and per-window scene generation should arrive after tmux showcase exercises diff-driven composition. Current crate description/source mark kittui-wm as a placeholder scaffold. Implement basic split, stack, and tab layout models, z-order handling, conversion to Composition entries, and tests for deterministic geometry output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: route accessibility semantic actions
description: Route semantic focus/actions through platform accessibility objects for AX/AT-SPI adapters. Support focus, activate, toggle, set value, insert text, select, and stale-component/permission errors. Follow-up from bd-4a49aa.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: typed surface side-effect events
description:
Follow-up after native surface side-effect events landed: teach `kittwm-sdk` typed event parsing about `surface_title_changed`, `surface_bell`, `surface_clipboard_set`, and `surface_notification` instead of leaving them as unknown events.

Scope:
- Add `KittwmEvent` variants for the four surface side-effect event kinds.
- Update `KittwmEvent::kind()` and parser mapping.
- Add targeted event parser tests for title, bell, clipboard, and notification detail payloads.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK event subscribers get typed variants for native side-effect events.
- `cargo test -p kittwm-sdk event -- --nocapture` and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add mouse drag reorder for kittwm tiled pane titles
description: UX audit follow-up: floating panes can be repositioned by title dragging, while tiled panes require keyboard/socket MOVE_PANE controls to reorder. Add bounded tiled-title drag behavior so dragging a tiled pane title and releasing over another pane reorders the tiled stack, aligning mouse interaction with the existing move-pane model. Include focused tests and documentation. Filed from ongoing kittwm UX/quality audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm daemon default socket path: build directly
description: The kittwm daemon default/display socket path helpers still use format! for hot startup socket path construction. Build the /tmp/kittwm-USER.sock and /tmp/kittui-wm-ID.sock paths directly while preserving env/display behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: add JSON native socket status and panes
description:
Large gap: native kittwm socket control is now scriptable, but STATUS/PANES are human-oriented text. Shell and external controllers need machine-readable JSON for robust automation.

Scope:
- Add native session socket commands `STATUS_JSON` and `PANES_JSON`.
