- Include pending command count, pane count, focused window, layout axis, and per-pane window/title/focused records.
- Keep existing text `STATUS` and `PANES` behavior.
- Add targeted queue reply tests and document commands in docs/wm.md.

Acceptance:
- `STATUS_JSON` returns parseable JSON with `pending`, `panes`, `focus`, and `layout`.
- `PANES_JSON` returns parseable JSON with `panes_detail` records.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm daemon: normalize chrome reservation owner
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: `native_reserve_chrome_json_reply` clamps chrome reservation dimensions but stores `owner` exactly as provided by clients. kittwm-bar trims its env-derived owner, but other clients can still send whitespace-padded or blank owner strings, leaking noisy owner metadata into CHROME_JSON/status/events. Normalize `reservation.owner` daemon-side by trimming non-empty values and dropping blank values; add focused daemon queue coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-session: bound graphical scene labels
description: Audit finding: `kittwm session-kitty` / session scene JSON bounds geometry and row count, but serializes raw kind/layout/focus/window/title/command values into scene labels. Pathological session manifests can inflate scene JSON/kitty payloads and redraw allocation churn. Clip session scene label fields while preserving useful diagnostics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm info scene: build chrome label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: info_scene still builds the chrome summary layer label with format! from top_bar_rows and tilable_rows. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Add first-class text rendering contract for kittui scenes
description: During bd-a1d081, the root cause was that kittui `Scene`/`Node` currently has no first-class text node, so live kittwm graphical chrome has to combine kitty image backgrounds with ANSI text overlays. That makes z-index ordering fragile and forces duplicate text overlay code for top bar, pane titles, and status chips. Consider designing a first-class text layer/node or a documented text-overlay compositor contract so chrome scenes can carry readable text without relying on terminal z-plane hacks. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-a1d081 session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm cheat: include completions quick reference
description:
Continue focused kittwm CLI/help UX polish without overlapping active native-surfaces or chrome-scene work. `kittwm cheat` is the compact daily reference and now includes helper workflows, but it does not mention shell completion setup or the new `kittwm help completions` topic. Add a concise completions quick-reference line to the cheat sheet plus focused assertion coverage.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: model clipboard bell and notification surface events
description: Promote OSC clipboard, bell, title, and notification side effects into semantic SurfaceEvent values with runtime policy for host forwarding, OS clipboard, denial, and shell display.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add SDK helper for focused skipped-frame uploads
description: Operator-requested kittwm render-performance/SDK follow-up: focused dirty-frame helpers expose clean state and changed tile ratios/fractions, but clients that need to distinguish skipped uploads from zero-change measured frames still have to fetch focused_pane(). Add focused_frame_upload_skipped() on PanesStatus and Status, with tests/docs, to complete the focused dirty-frame helper set.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: add semantic snapshot publish path
description:
Semantic runtime follow-up: the SDK can build semantic snapshots and the daemon can expose a synthetic snapshot for PTY panes, but there is no runtime queue/publish path for first-party semantic SDK apps to publish their own component trees. Add a small design/implementation slice for a semantic snapshot publish verb and storage model.

Scope:
- Define/add native socket command skeleton for SEMANTIC_PUBLISH <window|focused> <snapshot-json> or an equivalent surface-specific publish path.
- Validate schema_version/surface/root minimally using SDK protocol types.
- Store latest semantic snapshot per pane/window in daemon state and have SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT prefer published semantic tree over terminal text fallback.
- Add tests for publish/readback and invalid JSON rejection.

Acceptance:
- First-party SDK examples have a daemon endpoint to publish semantic snapshots.
- Existing PTY text-area fallback still works when no snapshot is published.
- Targeted tests and git diff --check pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: bound empty workspace scene geometry
description: Audit finding: native_empty_workspace_scene clamps panel width to a minimum 24 columns even if terminal cols is smaller, and its hero/accent/action rects use fixed margins that can extend outside tiny panels. Bound empty workspace scene footprint and internal rects to available columns/rows so first-launch/empty-workspace graphical surfaces cannot overflow narrow terminals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Make kittwm focused pane title marker more legible
description: Operator-requested kittwm UX polish slice: pane title focus state is currently a subtle leading asterisk/space in terminal chrome. Make the focused pane marker more legible in the live title text while keeping the representation compact, bounded, and safe for narrow panes. This is a small focus-state visual uplift for tiled/floating window-manager use.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Include command in direct remote terminal default title
description: After remote aliases learned to default titles like `HOST: htop`, direct `kittwm-terminal --remote HOST -- htop` still defaults to just `HOST`. Align direct remote terminal behavior with the alias path by using `HOST: COMMAND` for command-explicit remote panes, while keeping login shells titled `HOST` and preserving explicit `--title` overrides.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm graphical overlay panel: build row labels directly
description: Replace `format!("{id}-row-{idx}:{row}")` in test-only `graphical_overlay_panel_scene(...)` with a direct preallocated row-label helper. Scope only graphical overlay panel row label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing scene labels and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm PTY surface: cache text and scrollback snapshots by revision
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: PtyTerminalApp::text_snapshot and scrollback_snapshot rebuild full strings every time the native loop publishes pane status or renders pure-terminal fallback. TerminalSurface already tracks a revision for frame caching, so unchanged terminal content can reuse snapshot strings. Scope: cache text/scrollback snapshots by TerminalState revision, invalidate on revision changes, and add focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Window picker: floating panel + Tab cycle to surface a Mac/X window
description:
Inside the running kittui-wm session, hotkey (default Mod+Space) opens a kittui floating panel listing every window from QuartzServer::list_app_windows() + every X11 window across attached backends. Arrow keys navigate, Enter surfaces the window (calls QuartzServer::with_target(Window(id)) and attaches as a new backend, or pins an existing one). Mod+W cycles surfaced windows. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-6a36e1
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Advertise remote term alias
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: parser already accepts `kittwm remote HOST term ...` as a short alias for `terminal`, but help/completions/catalog primarily advertise `terminal`. Make `term` discoverable so users can quickly open remote terminal commands over pooled SSH from local kittwm.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: add native pane control CLI wrappers
description:
Large terminal-WM usability gap: native kittwm now has first-class CLI wrappers for inspection and automation, but core pane control (spawn/focus/layout/move/resize/rename/close/balance) still requires raw `kittwm --attach -c ...` protocol strings. A terminal WM should expose these as stable CLI verbs/flags for shell scripts and external controllers.

Scope:
- Add CLI wrappers for native pane control:
  - `--spawn-pty <cmd>`
  - `--focus-pane <window>` / `--focus-next` / `--focus-prev`
  - `--close-pane <window|focused>`
  - `--layout <columns|rows>`
  - `--move-pane <window|focused> <dir>`
  - `--resize-pane <window|focused> <amount>`
  - `--balance-panes`
  - `--rename-pane <window> <title>`
- Route through default socket resolution, preserve command/title payload case, validate simple tokens where useful.
- Add help text, request-builder tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Wrappers generate expected native socket requests.
- Targeted kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm send-text queued reply: build directly
description: queue_native_send_text still uses format! for SEND_TEXT_QUEUED/SEND_LINE_QUEUED replies. Build that shared reply directly while preserving send-text/line queue coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add exec remote terminal alias
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote terminal panes now accept `cmd` and `command`, and users may also naturally type `exec` when they want to execute a command on a remote host in a pooled SSH pane. Add `kittwm remote HOST exec ARGS...` as an alias for the existing remote terminal command path, including help/catalog/parser coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: forward kitty Shift+Enter to pages
description: Kittwm burn-down/input parity finding: browser input routing forwards Enter and Shift-Tab, but kitty keyboard Shift+Enter (CSI 13;2u) is currently treated as an unknown CSI and consumed. Web editors and coding-agent UIs often distinguish Enter from Shift+Enter. Scope: parse CSI 13;2u as Shift+Enter, dispatch DevTools Enter key events with Shift modifier, and add focused parser/key coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-top process rows: build directly
description: render_process_row in kittwm-top still formats the entire process table row, including temporary pid/cpu/rss strings. Build each process row directly into a preallocated String while preserving table output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: prototype portable libghostty-vt integration
description:
Investigate and prototype portable libghostty-vt integration for kittui/kittwm headless terminal capture. Use Nix flake deps and Cargo, not macOS Ghostty.app symbols. Goal is a small Rust-facing proof surface that can feed terminal bytes into libghostty-vt and inspect terminal text/render state, documenting feasibility for future headless Ghostty canvas capture and kittwm native terminal-emulator app integration.

Acceptance:
- Add a narrow crate or docs/prototype that uses pkg-config against nixpkgs libghostty-vt (portable package) rather than Ghostty.app.
- Wire Nix dev/build deps enough for the prototype to build on supported platforms.
- Include focused validation and notes on limitations: libghostty-vt provides VT/parser/render-state/kitty-graphics metadata, not the full GUI renderer; full headless pixel canvas likely needs a renderer on top or Ghostty upstream surface API.
- Avoid CI workflow edits, SDK placement helper churn, Runtime/browser/bar/session internals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Include counts in remote listing JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote window/display JSON currently reports host/kind/filter/mode/source and raw lines, but scripts still need to count results themselves. Add a count field to the JSON envelope so local kittwm pooled-SSH fallback diagnostics are self-describing.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native surfaces JSON: append newline directly
description: Replace `format!("{}\n", serde_json::json!(...))` in `native_surfaces_json_text()` with direct string construction: build the JSON value, convert once with `to_string()`, push a newline, and return. Scope only `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm.rs`; preserve native surfaces JSON output and add/retain narrow coverage for the trailing newline.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm PANES mouse label should build directly
description: `native_pane_mouse_label` builds a temporary Vec of enabled mouse modes and joins it with commas for every PANES row. Build the label directly into one small String while preserving `-` for no enabled modes. Scope: daemon PANES text reporting only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: docs: document browser semantic publishing workflow
description:
Documentation follow-up after browser semantic extractor and publish loop landed. Update semantic docs/quickstart to explain browser DOM/ARIA semantic extraction and best-effort publishing from kittwm-browser.

Scope:
- Document how kittwm-browser publishes DOM/ARIA semantic snapshots when KITTWM_SOCKET/KITTWM_WINDOW are present.
- Explain screenshot fallback, debounce/duplicate behavior, and limitations.
- Link to browser semantic adapter plan docs.
- Include quick commands for launching browser and reading SEMANTIC_SNAPSHOT.

Acceptance:
- Docs describe current browser semantic publishing behavior and limitations.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: publish pane status after native frame capture
description: Operator loop: continue finding kittwm flicker/performance issues. While reviewing the native loop, pane status snapshots are published before native frame capture updates per-pane dirty_frame metrics and surface metadata. That makes STATUS/PANES/EVENTS subscribers see one-frame-stale dirty_frame/title data, which weakens the telemetry we just added for flicker diagnosis. Scope: publish pane statuses after graphical native capture/frame decisions while preserving pure-terminal behavior, and add targeted coverage for the status-change helper if needed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Add kittui-md widgets alias for components
description: Add --widgets as a friendly alias for kittui-md --components so users can inspect generated UI component records with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Markdown renderer proof gallery for components, links, and image-backed tables
description: Add a proof/gallery command or example that renders a markdown document exercising all new UI components: h1/h2/h3, title/banner/header/footer, textboxes, textchips/link chips, code blocks, lists, and image-backed tables. Include screenshots or protocol output fixtures showing connected table borders, highlighted links, and a z-ordered background image underneath a table. This should become the acceptance harness for the markdown renderer epic.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK mouse input request: build directly
description: SurfaceHandle send_mouse still formats SEND_MOUSE request strings. Build the request directly into a preallocated buffer while preserving mouse event labels and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK focus-close test socket path: build directly
description: The SDK surface_focus_close_send_expected_socket_commands test still formats its temp Unix socket path inline. Reuse the direct test_socket_path helper while preserving focus/close socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm should support directed split launches and true 2x2 layouts
description:
Harry asked whether there is a command to launch a pane into a particular horizontal/vertical split to create a true 2x2 setup. Current socket/CLI controls support global `LAYOUT columns|rows`, `MOVE_PANE`, resize/balance, and session restore as a flat pane list, but not a directed split target such as "split focused vertically and launch this command there" or nested 2x2 layout construction.

Desired outcome:
- Add a first-party CLI/socket/SDK path for directed split launch, e.g. `kittwm split --axis columns -- <cmd>` / `SPLIT_PANE <window|focused> <columns|rows> <cmd>` or equivalent.
- Support building/restoring a true 2x2 nested layout with four panes, not just a flat all-columns/all-rows layout.
- Make it practical to script a demo with native ghostty terminal, first-party browser, local Quartz/app launcher, and remote X11 launcher.
- Document copy-paste examples in `docs/wm.md` / `kittwm examples`.

Filed from operator request while preparing the XQuartz/kittwm test instructions.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: disable byte-mangling input flags in raw mode
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: raw mode now clears CR/LF translations and software flow control, but still preserves BRKINT, PARMRK, and ISTRIP. Those host line-discipline flags can interrupt on break, parity-mark bytes, or strip the eighth bit before kittwm routes input to native terminal apps, corrupting Alt/UTF-8/control-sequence passthrough. Scope: clear BRKINT/PARMRK/ISTRIP in raw_mode_iflag and update focused tests while preserving unrelated flags.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm SDK: ignore blank top-level workspace before chrome fallback
description: Follow-up to kittwm-bar workspace fallback cleanup: typed SDK `Status::workspace_id()` and `PanesStatus::workspace_id()` prefer the top-level `workspace` string even when it is blank/whitespace, so callers can miss a valid `chrome.workspace` fallback. Normalize workspace strings inside the SDK helpers: trim returned labels, ignore blank top-level values, then fall back to trimmed non-empty chrome workspace. Add focused SDK decoding coverage for both status shapes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm keymap graphics: preallocate bounded shortcut labels
description: kittwm keymap graphical scenes build key-spec/chord labels for bindings. The helpers grow from empty strings and repeatedly count chars while the maximum visible label width is already known. Preallocate bounded label buffers and track used width to reduce graphical keymap rendering churn while preserving clipping.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm kitty upload still test: use literal expected payload
description: Remove the remaining non-interpolating `format!` expected payload in `kittui-kitty` upload still grammar tests. Scope only `upload_still_emits_exact_grammar` in `crates/kittui-kitty/src/lib.rs`; preserve exact output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm float toggle result status: build directly
description: The float-toggle result status still formats window/mode and failure suffixes with format!. Build those status strings directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm app launcher: avoid lowercasing every candidate during filtering
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: the main kittwm app/command filter in kittwm.rs still lowercases every candidate string on each query, allocating and scanning full item text. The raw WM launcher path was fixed separately; this shared kittwm command/app filter has the same input-latency shape for large PATH/app lists or pathological names. Scope: replace per-candidate lowercase allocation with allocation-free ASCII case-insensitive matching, preserving exact/prefix/contains ordering and adding focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-bar: kitty output should include visible chip labels and clock overlay
description: Standalone kittwm-bar --kitty can place the graphical chip scene without any visible text because kittui scenes carry chip geometry/labels but no text node renderer. Append a high-contrast ANSI overlay for workspace chip labels and the clock after the absolute no-placeholder placement, matching live kittwm behavior. Keep text/json/scene output unchanged and add deterministic tests for the overlay bytes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: docs: update native kittwm socket controls
description:
Large gap: native kittwm socket/control-plane docs are stale. The code now supports move, resize, balance, weights, and pane geometry in PANES_JSON, but docs only mention spawn/focus/status/basic layout. Users and external controllers need the command catalog and metadata model documented.

Scope:
- Update docs/wm.md native session/socket section with MOVE_PANE, RESIZE_PANE, BALANCE_PANES, RENAME_PANE, HELP_JSON, and PANES_JSON geometry/weight fields.
- Update README native status bullets/attach examples for move/resize/balance.
- Keep examples concise and accurate.

Acceptance:
- Docs mention the new native socket commands and local resize/balance keybindings.
- Docs mention PANES_JSON includes weight and geometry fields.
- `git diff --check` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm info scene: build chrome label directly
description: Small kittwm info scene performance cleanup: info_scene builds the chrome row layer label with format! from top-bar and tilable-row counts. Build the info chrome label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: disable all CR/LF input translations in raw mode
description: Follow-up to the Ghostty Enter report: clearing ICRNL preserves ordinary Enter as carriage return, but raw mode should also clear INLCR and IGNCR so the host terminal never rewrites or drops carriage-return/line-feed bytes before kittwm routes them. Scope: update raw input flags, preserve unrelated input flags, and add focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: send focus events after keyboard pane move
description: Follow-up to bd-c5d787: the socket `NativePaneCommand::Move` path now sends focus events after reorder, but the keyboard/internal `native_move_focused` helper still removes/inserts the focused pane and assigns `*focused = to` directly. PTY apps with focus reporting can still miss focus transitions when pane movement is triggered through kittwm keybindings. Update `native_move_focused` to preserve the old focused window through the reorder and call `native_set_focus` for the moved pane, matching the socket move path; extend deterministic move focus coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: fix core tiling non-overlap invariants
description: User report: kittwm fundamental tiling is broken. Opening kittwm shows ugly chrome/empty boxes, opening terminals causes flicker/z-index fights, and multiple terminals draw over one another. Before fancy kitty renderer polish, fix the core tiling engine contract: panes must have disjoint outer bounds, disjoint app bounds, stable top-bar/footer reservations, no overlapping split allocations, and predictable focus/title geometry. Acceptance: add/strengthen deterministic layout tests for 0/1/2/3+ panes across column/row splits and resize sizes; fix any overlap/off-by-one issues in the tiling/layout helpers.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-launch: preallocate bounded scene label truncation
description: Audit finding: kittwm-launch `bounded_label` is used in plan scene/status paths, but it builds clipped output via iterator collect from an empty/unknown-capacity string. Preallocate the bounded width and build directly to reduce small allocations in plan rendering while preserving label semantics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: make kittwm-launch plan kittui/kitty-native
description:
Make kittwm-launch dry-run/planning inspectable as kittui/kitty-native UI. The launcher already uses typed SDK SurfaceSpec for terminal/browser; expose that plan as a labelled kittui scene and optional kitty graphics so first-party helper UX is not text-only.

Acceptance:
- kittwm-launch supports --plan-scene-json and --plan-kitty/--plan-graphics for LaunchPlan.
- Plan scene labels include backend/mode/query/command for inspection artifacts.
- Existing --dry-run/--status behavior remains unchanged.
- Tests cover parser and scene labels without a live kittwm socket.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-terminal: add explicit options to help output
description:
Continue the focused kittwm UX/help polish. `kittwm-terminal --help` currently succeeds and explains modes in prose, but it lacks a compact Options section for flags like `--replace`, `--new-window`, `--title`, `--command`, status modes, and event modes. Add a concise options list plus a small assertion so operators can scan the command without reading the prose.

Filed from the persistent kittwm UX improvement loop after no ready dispatchable beads were available.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-sdk: typed exact bytes paste and mouse helpers
description:
SDK automation gap: socket/CLI expose exact byte input, bracketed paste payloads, and mouse injection (`SEND_BYTES_B64`, `PASTE_BYTES_B64`, `SEND_MOUSE`) but SDK clients only have text/line/key helpers. Add typed SDK helpers for these remaining input paths.

Scope:
- Add `SurfaceHandle::send_bytes_b64` or `send_bytes(&[u8])` using base64 internally.
- Add `SurfaceHandle::paste_bytes(&[u8])` using `PASTE_BYTES_B64`.
- Add a typed/simple mouse helper for known event labels and pane-local col/row.
- Gate with existing SendInput capability.
- Add targeted SDK unit/socket tests for command formatting and capability denial.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can use exact bytes, paste bytes, and mouse events without raw protocol strings.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: make wait-text timeout configurable and client-safe
description:
Large automation reliability gap: `WAIT_TEXT` waits up to 5s, but kittwm socket clients use a 2s read timeout. The first long wait path added to the WM control plane can therefore fail client-side before the daemon replies. Scripts also cannot tune the wait duration.

Scope:
- Raise client read timeout enough for WAIT_TEXT by default.
- Add `WAIT_TEXT_MS <window|focused> <ms> <needle>` for explicit timeouts.
- Add `kittwm --wait-text-ms <ms> <window|focused> <needle>` CLI wrapper.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON coverage, tests, and docs examples.

Acceptance:
- Default `WAIT_TEXT` no longer exceeds client read timeout.
- Explicit millisecond timeout validates arguments and queues/waits correctly.
- Targeted daemon/kittwm tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: relative placement (P=,Q=,H=,V=) not implemented
description: Kitty placement grammar supports anchoring relative to another placement via P=<src image id>, Q=<src placement id>, H=<col offset>, V=<row offset>. PlacementOptions has none of these fields, so 'relative placement' is just missing. File as separate work since it's additive grammar + new tests. Parent: bd-42d7c1.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kitwm --serve + --status: minimal Unix-socket daemon protocol (PING/STATUS)
description: kitwm --serve binds a Unix socket at /tmp/kitwm-$USER.sock (override via KITWM_SOCK env), spawns an accept thread, and answers single-line text requests: 'PING' -> 'PONG'; 'STATUS' -> 'pid=N uptime=Xs sock=...'. kitwm --status connects to that socket, sends 'STATUS', prints the reply, exits non-zero if no daemon. Lays the protocol foundation for bd-aaea73 attach client without committing to a wire format yet. RAII Drop on the server unlinks the socket. Parent: bd-031e54 (decomposed from bd-fb5d9d).
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser status tests: build stress strings directly
description: Replace test-only `format!` stress URL/socket construction in `browser_status_is_stable_by_default_and_frame_opt_in` with direct preallocated helpers. Scope only kittwm-browser status test stress-data construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/bin/kittwm_browser.rs`; preserve truncation and bounded status coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm top-bar overlay ANSI helpers: build directly
description: native graphical top-bar overlay ANSI color helpers still use format! for foreground/background SGR sequences. Build those escape strings directly into preallocated buffers while preserving exact overlay output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: render kittwm-bar as live graphical component by default
description: Dogfood graphics gap: kittwm-bar exists as a scene artifact but the live top bar still often behaves like text. Integrate the kittwm-bar model as the default live graphical top bar in graphics mode, including workspace, state, time, and display/chrome metadata. Acceptance: live top bar is a kittui-rendered scene/component by default outside pure-terminal fallback; --scene-json artifact matches live rendering labels/metadata.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native footer: build status line directly
description: native_status_line_text bounds the log path but still uses format! for the full footer status each redraw. Build the visible status line directly into one preallocated String while preserving empty-workspace behavior and bounded log path output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm architecture scene: build plane labels directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: architecture_scene still builds composition-plane row labels with format! from plane name and z-index. Add a direct preallocated plane-label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: kittui-rendered split borders and gutters
description: Dogfood graphics gap: split pane borders/gutters should be kittui-rendered graphical surfaces, not ASCII/text chrome. Implement kittui scene/component borders for split boundaries, focused/unfocused states, and gutters that align with pane app_bounds and top-bar reservation. Acceptance: split panes show graphical kittui-rendered borders/gutters in graphics mode; borders do not overlap app content; terminal fallback remains explicit/automatic.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm capture: avoid lowercasing every Mac window title during matching
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: --capture window:<substr> lowercases every window title and owner while looking for a match. Large/window-title-heavy desktops or pathological titles can allocate/scan full strings during capture target selection. Scope: reuse allocation-free ASCII case-insensitive contains matching for owner/title checks, preserving existing matching semantics for ASCII input and adding focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm doctor scene: build heading label directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: doctor_scene still builds the doctor heading label with format! from transport and compression debug values. Add a direct preallocated label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: Launch remote Linux desktop entries via Exec fallback
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: when the remote host lacks kittwm and `kittwm remote HOST launch QUERY` selects a Linux `.desktop` entry, use `gtk-launch` when available but fall back to the desktop entry `Exec=` command if gtk-launch is missing. This makes pooled-SSH app launch work on minimal Linux remotes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: typed chrome JSON helper
description:
SDK gap: daemon exposes `CHROME_JSON` and CLI has `kittwm --chrome-json`, but `kittwm-sdk` lacks a typed convenience helper for it.

Scope:
- Add `Kittwm::chrome()` / `chrome_json()` returning `ChromeReservationStatus` by sending `CHROME_JSON`.
- Reuse existing `ChromeReservationStatus` type and accessors.
- Keep capability policy consistent with existing status/panes helpers (inspection-only, no mutation).
- Add a focused Unix socket unit test that verifies the helper sends `CHROME_JSON` and parses workspace/top_bar_rows/tilable_rows.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- SDK consumers can call `client.chrome()` or `client.chrome_json()`.
- Targeted SDK tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui primitives: add standard animation flags to box/gradient/glow
description:
Continue animation coverage for primitive `kittui` scene commands after inline and top-level affordances. Inventory:
- `kittui box` has legacy `--animate frames@cycle_ms`; add the standard `--animated --fps --frames` contract while preserving legacy behavior.
- `kittui gradient` is static; add the standard contract and a labelled glow/sheen animation layer.
- `kittui glow` is static despite containing a phase-reactive glow node; add the standard contract.
Acceptance: box/gradient/glow support 60fps/180-frame/3s defaults, scene JSON exposes the animation, and smoke tests cover the new flags.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm PTY renderer: fast-path blank default cells
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: after initializing the whole RGBA frame to the default background, render_terminal_rgba still computes terminal_cell_colors for every blank default-style cell before discovering there is no glyph/background work. Most terminal panes are mostly blank/default cells, so this is avoidable per-frame CPU work. Scope: add a blank/default-cell fast path before color computation while preserving styled blank backgrounds, glyphs, and cursor rendering with focused coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: docs: document accessibility semantic action routing status
description:
Documentation follow-up after accessibility action routing lands. Update accessibility semantic docs and docs map with current platform-neutral action routing core status and remaining platform-specific bindings work.

Scope:
- Document supported accessibility semantic actions and error behavior.
- Update docs/README status for accessibility action routing.
- Clarify what remains platform binding work vs safe core.

Acceptance:
- Docs match landed accessibility routing core.
- git diff --check passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: add SurfaceSpec composition plane helpers
description: Add SDK convenience methods on `SurfaceSpec` to derive its architecture composition plane and z-index from the current first-party native surface contract. This lets apps ask a typed surface request where it belongs in the kitty graphics compositor without manually calling `native_surface_contract`. Scope SDK-only; avoid kittwm.rs inspection, helper binaries, Runtime/browser/bar/session changes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-cli: support compose JSON scene arrays
description:
Large gap: kittui is intended to be a shell/external-platform renderer, but `kittui compose -` accepts only a single Scene JSON document. Hosts that generate many scene assets must invoke the CLI repeatedly instead of sending one JSON array and getting one batch placement result.

Scope:
- Teach `kittui compose <path>|-` to accept either one Scene or an array of Scenes.
- For arrays, use the existing runtime batch placement path and emit concatenated upload/placement/embed streams.
- Add JSON/dry-run output for batch compose with count, image ids, footprints, byte counts, and optional channel strings under `--json-bytes`.
- Reject `--x/--y` placement overrides on batch input for now with a clear error.
- Add targeted integration tests for stdin array batch compose.

Acceptance:
- `printf '[scene, scene]' | kittui compose - --dry-run --json` returns batch JSON with count 2.
- Non-JSON batch compose emits non-empty terminal bytes.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm CLI: add paste-bytes-b64 command
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: the daemon and SDK support `PASTE_BYTES_B64`, and the CLI exposes `--send-bytes-b64` plus `--paste-file`, but there is no direct CLI `--paste-bytes-b64` for already-encoded paste payloads. Add a CLI flag mirroring `--send-bytes-b64`, with the same local nonempty/valid-base64 validation, and update help/tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: typed chrome reservation status
description:
SDK follow-up after runtime exposes chrome reservation metadata in STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON.

Scope:
- Add typed SDK structs/fields/accessors for chrome reservation metadata (workspace id, top_bar_rows, tilable_rows if available).
- Preserve backwards-compatible parsing when fields are absent.
- Add focused SDK serde/accessor tests.
- Do not change daemon/runtime behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can inspect top-bar reservation metadata without raw JSON access.
- Tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add kittui-md snippets alias for code blocks
description: Add --snippets as a friendly alias for kittui-md --code-blocks so users can extract Markdown code snippets with familiar terminology. Document it and add parser/conflict tests plus a smoke check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Use kind-specific footer drag scene chip labels
description: UX follow-up: graphical footer scene exposes an active drag chip, but it does not distinguish floating move from tiled reorder. Emit kind-specific status-chip-drag-move / status-chip-drag-reorder labels based on the footer drag segment so render artifacts can identify active drag semantics without text scraping. Filed from ongoing kittwm footer drag-state audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: Preserve SSH target in remote app and listing output
description: Remote doctor now preserves the user-typed SSH target for copy-paste command hints, but remote app/window/display JSON and text still identify only the remote `hostname`. Add `KITTWM_REMOTE_TARGET` to app/listing pooled SSH env, expose `target_host` in JSON, and show target host in human fallback headers so SSH config aliases remain visible in diagnostics.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: pace idle captures after unchanged frames
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: kittwm-browser now skips unchanged uploads/placements, but it still captures the browser every fixed 250ms while idle. Headless capture can be expensive even when no terminal output is emitted. Scope: add adaptive idle capture pacing after consecutive no-output frames, with env overrides for active/idle intervals and focused tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittui-ffi: add batch place_many_json API
description:
Large gap: Rust and CLI now support batch scene placement (`Runtime::place_batch`, `kittui compose [scene...]`), but FFI/TS hosts still need to loop one scene per FFI boundary crossing. External platforms need a batch ABI to use kittui as an efficient renderer backend.

Scope:
- Add `kittui_place_many_json(runtime, scenes_json, out)` accepting a JSON array of Scene documents.
- Use `Runtime::place_batch` internally and return concatenated upload+placement+embed bytes.
- Bump ABI minor and update `kittui.h` / ABI snapshot tests.
- Add targeted FFI tests for two-scene batch placement and bad non-array input.

Acceptance:
- FFI test passes an array with two scenes and receives non-empty bytes with kitty escapes/placeholders.
- Non-array input returns `BadScene` or equivalent and sets last_error.
- `cargo test -p kittui-ffi place_many` and `cargo test -p kittui-ffi --test abi_snapshot` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md table glyph atlas: render box drawing as connected image cells
description: Next proposal: complete the table glyph layout by generating a real cell-sized box-drawing glyph atlas and rendering markdown tables through image cells anchored with relative placement. Include a z-ordered background image/panel below tables. Builds on bd-aa77c5/bd-04718f.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Shift+Arrow selection keys
description: Kittwm burn-down/input parity finding: browser input routing forwards plain arrows and Shift+Enter/Shift+Tab, but kitty/xterm modified arrow sequences such as CSI 1;2A/B/C/D are consumed as unknown CSI. Web editors and text fields use Shift+Arrow for selection. Scope: parse Shift+Arrow CSI variants, dispatch DevTools Arrow* key events with Shift modifier, and add focused parser/key coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: forward Tab input to pages
description: kittwm-browser raw input parser still drops Tab bytes, so users cannot move focus between fields/buttons in browser panes. Preserve text ordering and dispatch Tab key events through HeadlessBrowserApp.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add Linux desktop metadata to local apps JSON
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: pooled-SSH fallback app JSON exposes Linux desktop metadata (generic names, keywords, categories, comments), but installed/local `kittwm apps --json` currently emits only desktop ids/files/labels. Add the same metadata fields to the Rust Linux desktop parser/search and local JSON so healthy remote kittwm output is closer to fallback parity.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-renderer: bound help overlay internal layer rects
description: Audit follow-up: graphical help overlay footprint is now bounded, but its internal decorative layers still use hard-coded x offsets (8/10px) and widths derived from rect.width - 16/20. On very narrow terminals, layer rects can extend outside the bounded scene footprint even though the footprint itself fits. Bound internal help overlay row/key-chip rects to the panel width.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-sdk: typed cached clipboard helper
description:
SDK gap after runtime/CLI clipboard policy work: `CLIPBOARD_JSON` exists but kittwm-sdk has no typed helper.

Scope:
- Add typed structs for `CLIPBOARD_JSON` replies and `Kittwm::clipboard()` / `clipboard_json()` helper.
- Gate helper with existing Clipboard capability if available; otherwise add/use a narrow read capability without changing daemon behavior.
- Preserve default-deny semantics: denied JSON parses without payload.
- Add mock transport tests for denied, allowed-empty, and allowed-cached replies.
- Do not change daemon or CLI behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can inspect cached clipboard policy state without raw protocol strings.
- Payload fields are optional/available only when daemon returns them.
- Targeted kittwm-sdk tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: tolerate socket pane spawn failures
description: Pane lifecycle stability issue: live kittwm handles socket `SPAWN_PTY` by calling `spawn_native_pane(...)?`. If the requested command cannot spawn or a backend launch fails, that error can propagate out of the native loop and exit kittwm. Make socket pane spawn failures non-fatal: log a bounded diagnostic and continue with existing panes/layout. Add focused helper coverage for the spawn failure log line.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-ffi: return channelized batch placement JSON
description:
Large gap: platform bindings can batch-place scenes, including at a runtime origin, but only receive one concatenated byte string. CLI users can request channelized JSON (`upload`, `placement`, `embed`, image ids, footprints), while FFI/TypeScript hosts cannot inspect, schedule, or separately write the upload/placement/embed channels.

Scope:
- Add additive C ABI `kittui_place_many_json_channels(runtime, scenes_json, x, y, out)` returning JSON for a batch placed at a group origin.
- Use `Runtime::place_batch_at_origin` and include `count`, `image_ids`, `footprints`, `upload_bytes`, `placement_bytes`, `embed_bytes`, and raw `upload`/`placement`/`embed` strings.
- Bump FFI ABI minor and update `kittui.h` plus ABI snapshot tests.
- Expose `Kittui.placeManyChannels(scenes, x, y)` in TypeScript and add tests.

Acceptance:
- FFI returns parseable JSON with channel strings and metadata for scene arrays.
- TypeScript returns a parsed object.
- Targeted FFI, ABI snapshot, and TypeScript tests pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native split log: build directly
description: The native terminal split diagnostic still formats axis and pane count with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact Debug axis output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: chrome JSON socket and CLI wrapper
description:
CLI/control-plane gap: chrome reservation metadata is embedded in STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON, but operators and scripts do not have a narrow command/wrapper to inspect just the WM chrome reservation.

Scope:
- Add native socket `CHROME_JSON` returning the same chrome reservation object plus workspace id.
- Add `kittwm --chrome-json` CLI wrapper mapping to `CHROME_JSON`.
- Add HELP/HELP_JSON entries.
- Add targeted daemon and CLI command tests.
- Do not change live rendering or SDK types.

Acceptance:
- `kittwm --chrome-json` can inspect top-bar reservation metadata without raw `--attach -c` or full STATUS_JSON parsing.
- Existing STATUS_JSON/PANES_JSON remain unchanged.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm keymap alternate duplicate key: build directly
description: The kittwm keymap duplicate-count test still formats the alternate pathological key with format!. Build that alternate key directly while preserving chord-identity duplicate coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm STATUS_JSON reply: build directly
description: STATUS_JSON replies still use format! only to append a newline to the status JSON value. Reuse the direct JSON-line helper while preserving status JSON coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm help command-tree needles: build directly
description: The kittwm help command-tree catalog test still formats expected command needles with format!. Build those expected strings directly while preserving command-tree coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Advertise remote forwarding alias consistently
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: the parser accepts `kittwm remote HOST forwarding` as an alias for the graphical/X11 forwarding doctor check, but help/completions only advertise `x11` and `graphical`. Make the alias visible consistently so users can discover the forwarding preflight command from Ghostty/Kitty host terminals.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-sdk: typed wait match results
description:
SDK ergonomics follow-up: `SurfaceHandle::wait_text[_ms]` and `wait_output[_ms]` currently return raw daemon reply strings like `MATCH_TEXT window=... bytes=...`. Add typed parsing helpers/results while preserving existing raw methods if useful.

Scope:
- Add a `WaitMatch` struct with match kind/text-vs-output, window, and byte count.
- Add typed helpers such as `wait_text_match_ms`, `wait_output_match_ms`, `wait_text_match`, `wait_output_match` or adjust existing helpers only if backwards-compatible.
- Parse successful daemon replies; continue returning daemon errors through existing Error path.
- Add targeted unit tests for parser and Unix socket command formatting.
- Do not change daemon behavior.

Acceptance:
- SDK automation clients can get typed wait-match metadata without parsing strings.
- Existing wait helper behavior remains compatible.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser: forward Alt-Insert and Alt-Delete input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards plain/Shift/Ctrl Insert/Delete and other Alt-modified navigation keys, but consumes Alt+Insert/Alt+Delete CSI (`ESC [ 2 ; 3 ~` / `ESC [ 3 ; 3 ~`). Forward them with the Alt modifier so modified editing keys are not swallowed.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm top bar workspace shadow label should build directly
description: `BarModel::scene_with_prefix` still builds workspace-chip shadow scene labels with `format!`, even though the prefix and bounded display label are already available. Build that label directly into a preallocated String while preserving scene labels. Scope: top-bar workspace chip shadow label only. Filed from dev-4 burndown session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm: add native socket focus and close pane commands
description:
Large gap: the live native kittwm session socket can report panes and spawn new PTY panes, but it cannot control them. A DISPLAY-like terminal WM needs scriptable focus/close control, not just introspection.

Scope:
- Extend the native session socket queue with control actions in addition to PTY spawns.
- Add socket commands:
  - `FOCUS_PANE <window>` to focus a visible native pane by window token.
  - `CLOSE_PANE <window|focused>` to close a visible pane (when more than one pane exists).
- Drain and apply those actions in the native session loop, reflowing panes and updating status snapshots.
- Add queue parsing/action tests and small focus-selection tests.
- Document commands in docs/wm.md.

Acceptance:
- Queue tests cover FOCUS_PANE and CLOSE_PANE command parsing/draining.
- Session helper tests cover applying focus/close actions to pane metadata.
- `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittwm` passes.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-sdk: spawn browser surfaces via first-party browser app
description:
SDK transport gap: `SurfaceSpec::Browser` exists but `Kittwm::spawn_surface` returns an explicit unsupported error. Add a pragmatic first-party transport path that spawns `kittwm-browser TARGET` as a terminal-backed surface, matching `kittwm-launch --browser`, while keeping richer dedicated browser surface protocol as future work.

Scope:
- Add a helper on `SurfaceSpec` for browser targets if missing.
- Update `Kittwm::spawn_surface` so `SurfaceKind::Browser` queues `SPAWN_PTY kittwm-browser <target>` with safe command quoting/reuse existing conventions.
- Preserve unsupported behavior for `SurfaceKind::Other`.
- Add SDK unit/socket tests for browser spawn command formatting and docs comments noting this is a first-party PTY-backed browser transport.

Acceptance:
- SDK clients can request browser surfaces without raw protocol strings.
- Terminal surface behavior remains unchanged.
- Targeted `kittwm-sdk` tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add focus and state chips to footer status scene
description: UX follow-up: graphical footer status scene still exposes generic help/terminal/close decorative chips even though the live footer now prioritizes focus and state. Add focus/state footer chip labels in the affordance scene so render-artifact consumers can identify the same high-priority footer concepts without scraping the backdrop text. Filed from ongoing kittwm footer visual-state audit.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm SDK browser surface command: build directly
description: browser_surface_command still formats the kittwm-browser command wrapper. Build the command directly into a preallocated String while preserving shell quoting output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui/kittwm use mcp-cli and updatable-cli crates
description:
Refactor kittui/kittwm CLI entrypoints to use `mcp-cli` and `updatable-cli` crates, following patterns from tendril/ring-mods/cacophony.

Goal:
- Enable `kittui update` and equivalent update plumbing for release artifacts from self-hosted GitHub runners.
- Align CLI structure with shared mcp/updatable command helpers used elsewhere.

Scope:
- Audit existing workspace and local examples for `mcp-cli` / `updatable-cli` usage.
- Add dependencies/config as needed.
- Wire update command into kittui and/or kittwm CLI without regressing existing commands.
- Preserve current clap UX and existing subcommands.
- Add focused tests/validation for command recognition/help where practical.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK semantic wrapper test socket path: build directly
description: The SDK semantic_wrappers_send_expected_socket_commands test still formats its temp Unix socket path inline. Reuse the direct test_socket_path helper while preserving semantic wrapper socket command coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui UI component + markdown rendering layer
description:
Operator request: kittui has boxes, gradients, strips, etc., but needs a first-class UI component layer and a markdown renderer built on it. Scope includes textbox, headings (h1/h2/h3), title/banner/header/footer, text chips, link chips, tables, and markdown-to-kittui rendering. The renderer should use kitty virtual placement plus relative non-virtual positioning anchors where appropriate so composed UI pieces can share connected layout/z-space semantics. Parent/area: kittui renderer + kittwm operator UI.

Operator clarification: this epic should produce a full kittui Markdown viewer, not just a library renderer. The viewer should run as a normal terminal app for rich kitty-graphics Markdown rendering, and also work as a kittwm-native app when launched inside kittwm context.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: dogfood surface handles in built-in native session
description: Refactor the built-in native session to manipulate surfaces through the common handle/trait path where practical, preserving current shortcuts, socket commands, and rendering behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-browser semantic publisher: reuse payload string allocation
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: BrowserSemanticPublisher::record_payload stores changed payloads with payload.to_string(), replacing the previous String allocation. Semantic payloads can be large and change repeatedly; we can reuse the existing allocation by clearing and pushing into the stored String when present. Scope: avoid allocating a fresh last_payload String on every changed semantic payload while preserving unchanged-payload debounce behavior and tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-4

title: kittwm-browser: forward Shift-Home and Shift-End input
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards plain Home/End and shifted arrows, but consumes Shift+Home/End CSI (`ESC [ 1 ; 2 H/F`). Web editors use these for line selection. Forward Shift+Home/End through DevTools modifiers while preserving existing Home/End parsing.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-browser: forward Escape input to pages
description: Audit finding: kittwm-browser forwards text, arrows, Tab/Enter, Delete, Home/End, and PageUp/PageDown, but a lone Escape key is dropped. Escape is commonly needed to dismiss browser dialogs/menus and blur page UI. Ctrl-] remains the terminal exit chord, so forward lone ESC as a browser Escape key event while preserving CSI parsing for arrow/navigation sequences.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: share kittwm-bar model with live top bar
description:
Top-bar integration gap: `kittwm-bar` and live native session now render similar bar state separately. Extract/share the small bar model and scene builder so the SDK app and live session stay aligned.

Scope:
- Move the kittwm-bar model/render/scene helpers into a reusable library module in `kittui-cli` (not kittui-core).
- Update `kittwm-bar` binary to use the shared module.
- Update live native session top-bar text/scene construction to use the same shared model/scene builder where practical.
- Keep SDK socket loading in the binary; live session can construct the model from in-process pane count/socket/time.
- Preserve existing CLI outputs and live behavior.
- Add focused tests for shared model text/scene and existing binary/session tests.

Acceptance:
- One shared implementation defines top-bar text and scene labels.
- `kittwm-bar --scene-json` and live scene chrome use aligned scene labels/model text.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK semantic action request: build directly
description: SurfaceHandle semantic_action still formats SEMANTIC_ACTION request strings. Build the request directly into a preallocated buffer while preserving component/action validation, JSON serialization, and protocol output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Add application remote app aliases
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote app workflows support `apps` and `app`, but users may naturally type `applications` or `application` from a desktop launcher mindset. Add `kittwm remote HOST applications QUERY` as a list alias and `kittwm remote HOST application QUERY` as a first-match selection alias, including JSON support and help/catalog coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-renderer: mouse hit-test and keyboard focus invariants
description: Audit/fix mouse and keyboard interactivity after final renderer changes. Top bar/chrome/help should consume or route appropriately; app area should forward local coordinates; focus visuals should match focus state and never lag a frame behind input routing.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native surfaces scene: build row labels directly
description: Small kittwm scene performance cleanup: native_surfaces_scene still builds each native surface row label with format! from surface metadata, plane, and z-index. Add a direct preallocated row-label helper while preserving scene output and focused label tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm SDK config test yaml path: build directly
description: The SDK kittwm_config_loads_partial_yaml_over_nord_defaults test still formats its temp YAML path inline. Build that test path directly while preserving partial config load coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui panel test: update animation default expectations
description: Follow-up after standardizing panel animation defaults: `crates/kittui-cli/tests/panel_command.rs` still expects legacy 8-frame/800ms metadata for `kittui panel --animate`. Update smoke coverage to assert the new standard 180-frame/3000ms compatibility behavior and include `--animated` where appropriate.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm daemon tracked pane labels: build directly
description: PaneRegistry::track_spawn still uses format! for daemon window/layout labels (daemon-N and tile:N). Build these labels directly while preserving daemon spawn tracking behavior.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm native terminal renderer: avoid transient format strings
description: render_native_shell_view_terminal assembles the pure-terminal redraw frame but still allocates temporary format! strings for each chrome/snapshot line before copying them into the frame buffer. Write ANSI sequences directly into the preallocated String to reduce redraw allocations while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: add kittui-ghostty CLI timelapse mode
description:
Extend the portable kittui-ghostty CLI with a reusable timelapse/frame mode. It should emit deterministic PNG frames and a manifest from demo steps without relying on desktop capture or Ghostty.app, so agents can generate progress evidence from the command line.

Acceptance:
- kittui-ghostty supports --timelapse-demo --out-dir DIR to emit multiple render-state PNG frames and manifest.json.
- Existing single-frame stdin/--demo --out behavior still works.
- Add focused validation and post a fresh montage image artifact in the session.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-launch connect error: build directly
description: Small kittwm-launch CLI performance cleanup: connection failure diagnostics still use a multi-line `format!` to add KITTWM_SOCKET guidance. Build the connect error directly into one preallocated String while preserving the user-facing message.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm native surfaces text: write rows directly
description: Small kittwm native-surfaces text-output performance cleanup: native_surfaces_text pushes formatted temporary Strings for readiness and each surface row. Write those rows directly into the existing output buffer while preserving CLI output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: Replace substring-based protocol tests with exact-grammar regressions
description:
kittui-kitty unit tests assert .contains(...) on small substrings, which passed the previous malformed leading-comma upload chunks for months. Convert each public function to use exact-grammar regression tests (golden strings for fixed inputs, structural parsers for the rest) covering: upload still single chunk, upload still multi-chunk, upload animation 3 frames + control, placement direct + tmux + unicode, placeholder grid with diacritics for a 4x3 footprint and an image id with non-zero top byte, delete by id, delete by placement-id. Each test must also fail if a stray comma or missing q= regresses.

Parent epic: bd-3dc8c7
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev
spoken_name: replace substring tests with grammar regressions

title: kittwm raw footer: build keymap action note directly
description: Replace the raw compositor footer keymap action note `last_keymap_action.as_ref().map(|a| format!(" — action={a}"))` with a direct helper that appends into a preallocated String. Scope only footer keymap-note construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve prefix-active and footer text/key behavior.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kitwm daemon APPS verb: expose launcher candidates through --attach
description: Bridge the launcher candidate source into the daemon protocol. Add APPS and APPS_JSON daemon verbs so 'kitwm --attach -c APPS' lists default launcher + candidates and 'kitwm --attach -c APPS_JSON' returns the same machine-readable structure as 'kitwm apps --json'. This makes the running daemon expose launcher data to clients before the floating menu lands. Parent: bd-031e54; extends bd-7e7f3d/bd-13d80f.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-kitty: add a=q query encoder and parser
description: Add pure kitty graphics a=q capability query encoder/parser helpers in kittui-kitty, with exact grammar/parser tests. No terminal I/O or render-loop integration. Follow-up from bd-02ef7b.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui inline row composition command
description:
Add inline row composition to avoid multiple process launches in prompts/statuslines.

Scope:
- Add a command such as `kittui inline row ...` or a practical equivalent that can render multiple inline chips/segments in one invocation.
- Goal use case: prompt/status script builds session/window/host chips without spawning kittui once per segment.
- Support at least JSON/config input or repeated simple flags; keep syntax pragmatic and testable.
- Preserve current single-chip command.
- Add tests for multiple components and width/ordering.
- Validate targeted kittui tests/build and diff check.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm headless Chrome profile prefix: build directly
description: Replace test-only `format!("kittui-headless-chrome-{}-", std::process::id())` in `crates/kittui-wm/src/native.rs` with a direct preallocated helper. Scope only headless Chrome profile prefix construction for native kittwm tests; do not exercise browser/Chrome.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm standalone daemon STATUS: build directly
description: The standalone daemon STATUS reply still uses format! for its pid/uptime/socket/panes/focus line. Build that status line directly while preserving STATUS/STATUS_JSON coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: tolerate command-initiated pane resize failures
description: Follow-up to host/layout resize resilience: many command-driven paths (spawn, close, layout, move, resize, balance, restore, and keybinding split/layout actions) still call `resize_native_panes_for_layout_with_reservation(...) ?`. If a hosted app rejects resize, those user actions can still exit kittwm. Make the common reservation-aware resize helper tolerant of per-pane resize failures, reusing the existing logged/tolerant machinery where callers provide logs and otherwise continuing silently; add focused coverage that the helper returns success on resize failures.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm daemon HELP_JSON reply: build directly
description: Standalone daemon HELP_JSON replies still use format! only to append a newline to the commands JSON value. Reuse the direct JSON-line helper while preserving help JSON coverage.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-bar: preallocate fitted chip text
description: Audit finding: standalone `kittwm-bar` overlay chip fitting already bounds huge labels, but clipped chip text is collected into an unpreallocated String before wrapping. Preallocate the bounded label width and build the clipped chip directly to reduce redraw allocation churn.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: do not change focus on mouse wheel scroll
description: Bug-hunt finding while improving kittwm usability: `native_mouse_event_name_and_position` marks scroll-up and scroll-down as `should_focus = true`, so merely scrolling over an unfocused pane steals focus. This is surprising in multi-pane terminal workflows and can make keyboard input go to the wrong pane after a scroll. Keep click-to-focus for button presses, but route wheel events to the pane under the pointer without changing focus; add deterministic coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm nudge parse context: build directly
description: The nudge pane request parser formats dx/dy parse error context with format!. Build those context strings directly while preserving exact errors.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Name remote app fallback mode accurately
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: remote app fallback now supports PATH commands, macOS .app bundles, and Linux .desktop entries, but user-facing output and JSON still call the mode shell-path-or-macos. Rename that mode string to include Linux desktop support so users understand what local pooled-SSH fallback is auto-detecting.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm: replace ASCII footer/status hints with graphical status components
description: Dogfood graphics gap: footer/status/action hints are currently text/ASCII. Replace runtime status hints (prefix active, last action, launch pid, logs, fps/transport) with graphical kittui status components/chips in graphics mode. Acceptance: no ASCII footer/status in graphics mode; status data remains visible and testable; terminal fallback stays compact.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm kitty placement fields: build command fields directly
description: Replace formatted temporary construction in `placement_command_ex` with direct String assembly for kitty graphics placement fields. Scope only `crates/kittui-kitty/src/lib.rs` placement command field construction; preserve exact kitty placement grammar tests.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm SSH cache-dir context: build directly
description: The pooled SSH control-path setup still formats the cache directory error context with format!. Build that context string directly while preserving exact output.
creator: ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm: invalidate Ghostty PNG cache after text-only refresh
description: Follow-up bug-hunt finding after bd-9144f2: `GhosttyTerminalApp::refresh_text_snapshot` drains PTY output and updates the terminal/text snapshot without rendering a PNG, but it does not clear `last_png_frame`. If kittwm later switches from pure-terminal fallback back to graphical capture, `capture_surface` can see no new output, reuse the old cached PNG, and display a stale graphical frame. Invalidate the cached PNG whenever text-only refresh drains output; add deterministic helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm apps scene: build row labels directly
description: Small kittwm apps scene performance cleanup: apps_scene builds path and macOS row layer labels with format! even though the row kind and bounded label are already known. Build app row labels directly into preallocated Strings while preserving scene output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm-renderer: skip blank graphical help overlay content
description: Audit finding: native_help_overlay_scene with no lines produces decorative backdrop/heading/control layers but no useful help content. If the shortcut catalog is ever empty or filtered out, this can render a blank modal chrome surface and flicker. Return a minimal 1x1 empty scene with no layers for empty help-overlay input so callers do not place blank chrome.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm: shell completions command
description:
Daily-driver UX: add shell completion generation.

Scope:
- Add `kittwm completions bash|zsh|fish` cooked-mode subcommand.
- Generate static completions from the local command/alias catalog plus common flags.
- Keep implementation dependency-free and presentation-only.
- Add focused tests for each supported shell and unsupported shell error.
- No daemon/session runtime changes.

Acceptance:
- Users can install shell completions from kittwm itself.
- Targeted tests/build and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm SDK: reject blank wait needles locally
description: Kittwm burn-down finding: SDK wait helpers (`wait_text*`, `wait_output*`, and JSON variants) format raw needle strings into daemon WAIT commands. The daemon trims and rejects blank needles, so blank/whitespace-only SDK calls fail only after socket I/O and padded valid needles are sent unnormalized. Add SDK-side wait needle validation that trims valid needles and rejects blank needles locally; add focused helper coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm-browser: native kitty placement and row clamp
description: Improve kittwm-browser as a first-party kitty-graphics-native surface: clamp browser drawing/status rows to reported terminal dimensions, use cursor/absolute kitty placement options instead of placeholder grids, and add deterministic tests for row math/placement contract. Coordinate with kittui-dev; avoid kittwm session reservation/control-plane overlap.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-wm daemon: socket server + KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY
description:
New binary kittui-wm-daemon (or kittui-wm serve subcommand) that listens on a Unix socket at $KITTUI_WM_DISPLAY (e.g. /tmp/kittui-wm-0.sock). Owns all backends + Pumps. Wire format: length-prefixed bincode. v1 messages: CmdListWindows, CmdTick, CmdInput(InputEvent), CmdResize { cols, rows }, CmdQuit. Daemon survives client disconnects. Parent epic: bd-031e54

---

Duplicate of bd-624e22
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittwm-renderer: report actual frame write byte counts
description: Follow-up to frame write byte telemetry: live native app events should report actual emitted upload/placement/embed byte lengths. Superseded during dev-2 rebase: main already contains equivalent NativeFrameWriteBytes telemetry from the landed frame telemetry slice, so dev-2 dropped redundant code.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittui-cli: implement cached image place subcommand
description:
Large gap: DESIGN documents `kittui place --id ...` as the shell workflow for re-placing cached/uploaded images, but the CLI does not implement it. Scripts can render an image id, but cannot later move/re-anchor it without going through Rust APIs.

Scope:
- Add a `kittui place --id ID --x X --y Y --cols C --rows R` subcommand that emits placement + placeholder bytes for an existing image id.
- Support decimal and `0x...` image id parsing.
- Honor existing output controls: `--json`, `--json-bytes`, `--dry-run`, `--placement-only`, and `--embed-only` where applicable.
- Add targeted CLI tests and update README/DESIGN if needed.

Acceptance:
- `kittui place --id 0x1234 --x 2 --y 3 --cols 4 --rows 2 --json --json-bytes` includes empty upload plus placement/embed channels.
- Non-JSON output emits placement+embed by default.
- Targeted tests and `cargo build -p kittui-cli --bin kittui` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: kittui-md pager supports arrow and page keys
description: Extend the kittui-md interactive pager input helper to understand common terminal escape sequences for Up/Down, PageUp/PageDown, Home/End, in addition to the existing letter controls. Add pure input tests using byte cursors so the behavior is covered without a real TTY.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Let remote Sway listing fallback work without jq
description: Operator SSH workflow follow-up: no-remote-kittwm window/display fallback supports Sway/wlroots via `swaymsg` only when `jq` is installed. Add a `python3` JSON parsing fallback for `swaymsg` outputs so minimal Linux remotes can still list displays/windows over pooled SSH when jq is absent.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittui-wm: advertise NativeSurface extended capabilities
description:
NativeSurface capability metadata gap: the trait now supports exact-byte input, focus notifications, and side-effect event draining, but `SurfaceCapabilities` still only advertises coarse text input/capture/resize/title/restore.

Scope:
- Extend `SurfaceCapabilities` with explicit booleans for exact byte input, focus notifications, and side-effect event draining.
- Keep defaults false for compatibility.
- Set PTY terminal metadata to advertise exact bytes, focus notifications, and surface events.
- Keep X/Quartz/browser text input capabilities unchanged unless they truly support the new hooks.
- Capture-only adapters should keep the new flags false.
- Add focused tests for PTY and capture-only metadata.
- Do not change socket or live session behavior.

Acceptance:
- NativeSurface metadata accurately advertises the new hooks.
- Targeted kittui-wm tests and `git diff --check` pass.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev

title: Advertise remote app open and run aliases

Make these natural app-launch aliases discoverable for users SSHing from Ghostty/Kitty into hosts with or without remote kittwm.

title: kittwm daemon apps JSON: build string arrays directly
description: Small kittwm daemon CLI performance cleanup: daemon app discovery JSON still formats string arrays with map(format!).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(", "). Mirror the kittwm CLI cleanup by streaming daemon app JSON arrays directly into a preallocated String while preserving escaping/output.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-2

title: kittwm focus state: build label directly
description: Replace `format!("{}#{}", self.last_direction, self.moves)` in `FocusState::label()` with direct preallocated String construction. Scope only focus state label construction in `crates/kittui-cli/src/session.rs`; preserve existing focus state behavior and tests.

title: kittwm CLI: include byte paste in quickstart

Add a concise byte/paste example to the quickstart so first-run operators can discover `--paste-bytes-b64` or `--paste-file` without going to the longer examples page; update focused quickstart coverage.
creator: ms-mac:kittui:ms-mac-kittui-kittui-dev-3

title: kittwm native socket spawn log: build directly
description: The native socket spawn command path formats its debug diagnostic with format!. Build that log line directly while preserving exact text.

title: kitwm doctor: one-shot diagnostics report (backends, displays, terminal, log, version)
description: Add 'kitwm doctor' subcommand that prints a self-contained health report: OS, binary version, available backends (sck/quartz/xvfb feature flags), display enumeration via Quartz (count + bounds), terminal probe (kitty graphics support, TERM, COLORTERM), /tmp/kittui-wm.log presence & last-modified, screen-recording permission hint. Output as plain text + JSON via --json. Useful for bug reports and quickly confirming a fresh checkout has the right toolchain. Parent: bd-031e54.

NativeSurface ergonomics gap: callers repeatedly match `NativeFrame`/`SurfaceFrame` to inspect format and payload metadata.

- Keep helpers pure and allocation-free where possible.
- Add focused unit tests.
- Do not alter adapter behavior or live session defaults.
